Specifically, Saber-LightSaber is identified as the most suitable option for Class 0 devices, while ML-DSA-87 combined with ML-KEM-768 achieves the highest overall scores for Class 1 and Class 2 devices. Our findings improve feasibility and efficiency by up to 30–40% compared to static baseline configurations. With these findings, we recommend EAS-PQC as a useful tool for the assessment of post-quantum security in industrial IoT systems.

title: Hybrid Frequency Regulation in Multi-Area Power Systems with Renewable Energy Sources, Electric Vehicles, and Cyber-Attack Mitigation: a MATLAB/Simulink Implementation Study
authors: Prakriti Kumar Srivastava, Pankaj Rai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Modern power systems are confronted with more than ever before in terms of frequency stability challenges as the utilization of renewable energy sources (RES) is beginning to rise in large volumes alongside low inertia nature. This paper will provide an extensive hybrid frequency control plan to multi-area interconnected power systems that will incorporate proportional-integralderivative (PID) controllers at the fractional-order, aggregators of electric vehicles (EV) to provide virtual inertia, and cyber-resilient architectures to avert denial-ofservice (DoS) and false data injection (FDI) attacks. Simulink was used to model and simulate the proposed system with realistic nonlinearities such as governor dead bands, generation rate limits, and communication weaknesses. The outcome of simulations shows that the hybrid FOPID controller (with EV aggregators) reduces the frequency overshoot by 67 %, and the settling time is 3.5 times shorter than that of the standard PID controllers. In the case of hybrid cyber-attacks, the Kalman filter-based detection and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> control switching preserve a nominal performance of 93 %. The experiment confirms the methodology with different system inertia (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H=5-8$</tex> seconds), levels of RES penetration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30-50 \%$</tex>), and attack strengths, and offers a practical idea of the next-generation implementation of smart grids.

title: Temperature Compensation in RF Reflectometry for Crop Quality Assessment
authors: Adeoluwa Oyinlola, Temitope Odedeyi
abstract: This study examines the impact of temperature on RF reflectometry (RF-R) measurements in four root crops: cassava, carrot, sweet potato, and potato. Using a dual-pin RF probe and a vector network analyzer, reflection coefficient, S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>, was measured across 401 frequency range of 30 kHz to 200MHz as samples cooled from 40 °C to 25 °C. A linear relationship between S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> and temperature was observed across all crops. Temperature correction factors (TCFs) were derived to normalise measurements, with the aim of improving the accuracy of RF-R-based quality characterisation in food and crop analysis, particularly under field conditions.

title: Trustworthy Big Data Pipelines: Fairness-Aware and Privacy-Preserving Knowledge Discovery Framework
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Prasanta Parida, Manish Gupta, Ankam Sreejyothsna, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: It can be indicated that the given paper deals with the design, implementation, and evaluation of reliable big-data pipelines that would also be fair and privacy conscious when knowledge is being discovered. Modern data pipelines are mediating an increasing set of high-stakes decisions across a variety of domains (healthcare, finance, public policy), but that are susceptible to privacy leakage, representational and procedural bias, and opaquitas that erode trust between the stakeholders. Our proposed conceptual framework (i) models the data pipeline as a process where each stage is handled in an accountable manner (data acquisition, cleaning, entity matching, model training, and deployment); (ii) incorporates interventions that are cautious of fairness (preprocessing, in-pipeline auditing, model selection, and post-processing); and (iii) incorporates provable privacy mechanisms (differential privacy, secure multi-party computation, and privacy-related generation of synthetic data) without impairing analytics utility. The framework also suggests the provision of instrumentation to support constant surveillance, provenance, and human-in-the-loop governance so as to enforce remedial measures and legal regulations. The paper formulates research problems and evaluation standards and an experimental roadmap to compare tradeoffs between privacy and fairness metrics and downstream utility by synthesizing recent contributions in the areas of privacy-preserving generative modelling, pipeline-sensitive fairness studies, and operational trustful AI practices. The work is based on the increasingly common demands regarding the pipeline-level implementation of fairness and the comprehensive consideration of privacyinterfairness dependencies in machine learning systems.

title: Enhancing Manifold Convexity in Deep MRI Image Clustering Using Adversarial Learning
authors: Sawsan Hilal, Nairouz Mrabah, Zyad Atef Allam, Riadh Ksantini
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Till today, no cure has been found for some diseases such as cancer and early detection remains the only reliable way to increase the survival chances for patients. Recently, machine learning has been used to aid in the diagnosis of large numbers of patients, especially using medical image data such as Magnetic Resonance Imaging images. However, due to the difficulty of obtaining large and labelled datasets, clustering techniques have been favored in this field of research with particular interest in deep clustering which has proven capable of understanding and accurately grouping highly complex data. One of the most recent developments in deep clustering came in the form of the dynamic auto-encoder (DynAE), which solves a historic problem that faced autoencoders (AEs) prior. However, it suffers from feature twist, a phenomenon which causes the cluster boundaries to flatten and become coarse and more complex which in turn hinders the clustering performance. This study proposes a new model (AdversFAE) using adversarial learning to combat feature drift in the auto-encoders by utilizing adversarial constrained interpolation in the clustering phase to escape feature twist. The proposed model was tested on six datasets from the MedMNIST v2 collection of medical datasets. The results showed that the proposed model outperformed the convolutional dynamic autoencoder (ConvDynAE) in terms of accuracy and F1_score. Interestingly, AdversFAE achieved an accuracy of 85.9%, 66.9%, and 53.5% on PneumoniaMNIST, BloodMNIST, and RetinaMNIST respectively compared to $62.1 \%, 46.3 \%$, and 31.5% achieved by ConvDynAE on the same datasets. The proposed changes proved effective in improving the convexity of cluster boundaries and hence the clustering performance.

title: Opacity-Informed UAV Navigation with 3D Gaussian Splatting and Model Predictive Control
authors: Jiahao Tu, Zhongrui Hu, Chenghao Yao, Jingyi Lu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated UAV navigation system that combines 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for environment mapping, A* algorithm for global path planning, and Model Predictive Control (MPC) for trajectory tracking with obstacle avoidance. The 3DGS-generated Gaussian sphere map provides rich geometric information including opacity and scale parameters, which are utilized to construct a collision cost function embedded into the MPC optimization framework. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves significant improvements in both path planning and trajectory tracking performance. The A* algorithm with Gaussian opacity-aware cost function reduces the integrated occupancy probability by 26.7% compared to traditional grid-based methods. The MPC controller with collision cost achieves a position RMSE of 0.030m, representing a 66.3% improvement over the baseline MPC without collision cost. The integrated system operates at an effective control frequency of 75Hz with an average single-step computation time of 13.3ms, meeting real-time requirements for UAV navigation in GNSS-denied environments.

title: Research on Leak Source Localization in Complex Pipelines Based on Acoustic Emission Technology
authors: Gao Zhigang, Lu Ronghao, Zhong Weicheng, Yuan Xumin, Zhang Lanzhu, Li Ke
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Pipelines are widely employed in the petroleum, natural gas, and chemical industries due to their high efficiency and low cost. However, leakage frequently occurs at components such as valves and flanges in complex pipeline structures due to sealing failures or impact fractures, posing significant safety risks. This study conducts experimental research on leak source localization in complex pipelines using acoustic emission (AE) technology. Lead break tests are performed to analyze the propagation velocity and attenuation characteristics of AE signals. A multi-level analysis combining time-domain, frequency-domain, and wavelet transform methods reveals that the amplitude and frequency band distribution of leakage signals attenuate progressively during propagation. To enhance signal quality, a parameter optimization method based on vector weighted averaging is proposed and validated within the framework of variational mode decomposition (VMD). Effective signal modes are selected using sample entropy and correlation coefficients, followed by signal reconstruction through a wavelet thresholding denoising algorithm, resulting in a significant improvement in localization accuracy. Furthermore, the impact of signal attenuation on localization errors is investigated, and an optimized sensor placement scheme tailored for complex pipelines is proposed and experimentally verified.

title: Performance Evaluation of Non-Invasive Attention Classification Using Physiological and Ocular Metrics
authors: Akshit Munjal, Divneet Singh Kapoor, Kiran Jot Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The correct identification of cognitive attention states is critical for intelligent systems to perform optimally in applications like e-learning, driver monitoring, and humancomputer interaction. In this paper, the authors present a machine learning-based approach to classify three types of attention states: Sustained, Alternating, and Divided Attention. The classification is done by employing non-invasive physiological parameters and ocular responses. The data was collected using a Gazepoint eye tracker and a Galvanic Skin Response sensor. Relevant features like GSR values, fixations, and mean fixation duration were extracted. The dataset was normalized using the z-score method. The SMOTE technique was used to handle class imbalance. The performance of the proposed system was validated using four classifiers: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and K-Nearest Neighbors. The experimental results showed that the KNN algorithm produced the best results with an accuracy of 96.13 %, followed by XGBoost with 96.00 %, and then the Random Forest algorithm with 95.86 %. The Logistic Regression algorithm produced the lowest accuracy of 49.04 %. The features were also ranked with the GSR values contributing the most to the classification at 70.5 %, followed by the fixation count at 16.0 %, and then the mean fixation duration at 13.5 %.

title: Multi-Agent Paradigms for Privacy Preservation in Federated Learning: A Review
authors: Rahul H.Bhole, Sachin R. Sakhare
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In the recent years Federated Learning has emerged as a groundbreaking approach to train machine learning models using edge devices by ensuring that data is localized and central data aggregation is reduced. Despite the fact that this inherently provides a layer of privacy, it cannot be exempt from potential breaches or leaks during the training and aggregation phases. In this review, we examine how intelligent agents can augment privacy measures by integrating multi-agent systems with federated learning, emphasizing the innovative intersection between these two fields. In this paper, we examine the core functionalities of agents, from monitoring data exchanges to verifying node authenticity to proactively responding to anomalies, and provide an assessment of what is available in the state-of-the-art methodologies for using these agents. We highlight the challenges and potential future directions in harnessing multi-agent systems to enhance privacy in federated learning. In our opinion, our work illustrates the promise and complexity of this interdisciplinary crossroads and provides a foundation for future progress in developing robust, privacy-respecting federated learning infrastructure capable of meeting the needs of our students.

title: A Scalable Agentic Artificial Intelligence Architecture for Autonomous Decision-Making and Self-Optimization in Complex Cyberphysical Systems
authors: Satpalsing Devising Rajput, Kiran Veernapu, Minakshi Sati, Suresh Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Modern Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) must be autonomously adaptable to deal with growing complexity and uncertainty in the environment. In this paper, the author proposes a scalable agentic AI architecture that will allow CPS to sense, think, and act in autonomy. The framework combined both local edge intelligence and global coordination to solve this by balancing the distributed agents with the hierarchical reinforcement learning. By contrast with the traditional centralized models, our architecture employs the use of selfmonitoring and feedback loops in the optimization of performance in real time and without human intervention. Assessment of various CPS conditions reveals major gains in decision quality, latency, and resiliency, providing a solid base to the future of the industrial automation and intelligent infrastructure.

title: SMS Spam Detection Using Hybrid BERT + XGBoost Model
authors: K. Jeevitha, B. Jayasurya, N. Prakash, E. Loganathan, P. Anitha, R. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: AIM: The aim of the study is to develop a SMS spam detection system using a Hybrid BERT + XGBoost model that can predict the spam messages from SMS communication. Materials and Methods: In this research, there are two groups. Group 1 refers to a baseline TF-IDF + Random Forest model while Group 2 refers to a proposed and advanced BERT + XGBoost Model algorithm. Each group consists of 200 samples, with the SMS providing more accurate and flexible spam classification. The total dataset is 10,962 samples. The power value is 97 % the significance threshold is 0.05, with a confidence interval of 99 %. Result: The BERT + XGBoost model is a faster prediction method and significantly higher accuracy than the TF-IDF. BERT + XGBoost algorithm attains a validation accuracy range from 98.68 % to 99.12 %, but the baseline TF-IDF + Random Forest model reaches an accuracy range from 96.65 % to 97.63 %. Statistical analysis indicates a significant improvement in performance, with the proposed model demonstrating higher accuracy compared to the baseline approach, and a significant improvement in the statistical analysis (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> less than 0.001) in the detection of all sms spam categories. Precision, recall, and F1 scores are noticeably higher withBERT + XGBoost Model. Conclusion: In this work, it is observed that BERT + XGBoost achieves a greater improvement in spam detection compared to the Random Forest model methods. It provides an effective security for spam messages in text classification.

title: Synthetic Twins: Toward Sustainable, Tunable 3D Printed Models of Human Tissues
authors: Gianmarco Dolino, Damiano Coato, Alice Berardo, Emanuele Luigi Carniel, Paolo Gargiulo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The concept of the digital twin has already transformed engineering and medicine by enabling virtual replicas of organs and tissues for simulation, diagnostics, and treatment planning. Yet, many biomedical applications require physical counterparts that replicate not only the anatomy but also the mechanical behavior of living tissues. To address this gap, we introduce the Synthetic Twin: a sustainable, physical analog of human tissue, fabricated via advanced 3D printing with tunable microstructure that reproduce native mechanical properties. Unlike conventional phantoms, which depend exclusively on material composition, Synthetic Twins leverage microstructural engineering (e.g., fiber reinforcement, layering, porosity modulation) to replicate a wide spectrum of healthy and pathological conditions. As a proof-of-concept, we present the design and characterization of 3D printed fiber-reinforced cartilage phantoms, demonstrating how microstructural tuning enables anisotropic behavior that closely resembles native tissue. Using mechanical testing combined with Digital Image Correlation (DIC), we evaluated samples with single fiber orientations (superficial, middle, deep) as well as a novel multilayered construct mimicking the stratification of native cartilage. Results show that microstructural design governs stiffness and Poisson’s ratio in a depth-dependent manner, with multilayered constructs displaying the highest fidelity. This work demonstrates that Synthetic Twins can provide reproducible, customizable, and ethically sustainable alternatives to animal and cadaveric models, with applications in diagnostics, surgical training, and personalized healthcare.

title: Cyber Physical System Based Distributed Real Time Draining System for Smart Cities
authors: Avishek Golder, Simarpreet Kaur, Komal Sharma, Gaurav Kumar Bharti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: In this era of technology, almost everything is now automated. Cyber physical systems play an important role in making our lives smarter and contributes the development of a smart city. A smart drainage system significantly contributes in building a smart city. The residents are currently facing issues due to the accumulation of excess water in the drain and water logging caused by the inadequate flow of drain water. To date, none of the existing research has addressed the simultaneous monitoring, cleaning, and decontamination of underwater drains within manholes. In this paper, our aim to solve this issue by using cyber physical systems. Our proposed system will automate the process of drainage cleaning and solve the problem of manual drain cleaning process. It will decrease the problems faced by manual drainage cleaning such as disease, death of workers, time consuming process etc.

title: Incorporating Edge Computing in Wireless Sensor Networks for Efficient Data Processing
authors: Himanshu Rai Goyal, Yogendra Kumar, Zaid Alsalami, A. Kakoli Rao, Amit Srivastava, P. William
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Edge Computing with Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) to address the challenges posed by traditional centralized data processing architectures. WSNs, pivotal in applications like environmental monitoring, healthcare, and smart cities, face issues of latency, bandwidth inefficiency, and scalability due to the vast data they generate. Edge Computing offers a paradigm shift by processing data near its source, thus enhancing responsiveness and operational efficiency. We propose a novel framework that employs localized computing at the edge to optimize network performance. Through simulations and real-world experiments, we demonstrate how this integration reduces latency, conserves bandwidth, improves security, and enhances scalability. Our findings indicate significant improvements in network management and efficiency, confirming the benefits of Edge Computing in transforming WSNs into more robust, efficient, and scalable systems.

title: Low-Power Wide-Area Networks (LPWAN) for IoT Applications: Engineering the Innovative Practices
authors: Narender Chinthamu, Krishna Kant Dixit, Mudit Mittal, Arun Pratap Srivastava, Akhilesh Kumar Khan, Rajeev Sobti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Low-Power Wide-Area Network (LPWAN) technologies within the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem. As IoT applications proliferate, the need for efficient, low-power communication solutions that can operate over extensive areas becomes critical. LPWAN technologies, characterized by their long range and low power consumption, are well-suited to meet these demands. This study examines various LPWAN technologies, including both cellular and non-cellular systems, and assesses their performance, design specifications, and suitability for different IoT applications. Through a comprehensive review and real-world use case analysis, this research aims to provide insights into the optimization of LPWAN technologies to enhance IoT connectivity. The paper also discusses potential challenges and future research directions in the deployment of LPWAN solutions, contributing to the broader understanding and development of IoT infrastructure.

title: Identification of vulnerable lines in power system considering the randomness of wind power
authors: Ning Wang, Lixia Zhang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To deal with the uncertain challenges brought by the high proportion of wind power integration, this paper proposes a fragile line identification method considering the randomness of wind power. Firstly, based on the scene generation technology combining Latin hypercube, K-means clustering and Monte Carlo sampling, the random scene model of wind power is constructed; Furthermore, the network structure vulnerability, load impact vulnerability and branch breaking vulnerability are proposed, and the final vulnerability ranking is obtained by TOPSIS method. The simulation in IEEE 39 and IEEE 118 bus system shows that this method can effectively identify the vulnerable lines of the system in the random wind power scenario, and analyses the impact of wind power penetration on the vulnerability, which provides a theoretical basis for power grid security assessment.

title: TAC-DHER: Tactical-Consistency-Aware Confidence-Gated Dynamic Hindsight Experience Replay for Cooperative UAV Air Combat
authors: Wenxin Li, Yongxin Feng
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Dynamic-target cooperative unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) air combat poses substantial challenges to reinforcement learning due to continuously evolving tactical objectives, sparse rewards, and poor reuse of failed episodes. To tackle these issues, a tactical-consistency-aware confidence-gated dynamic hindsight experience replay method, termed TAC-DHER, is proposed. TAC-DHER replaces purely geometric goals with joint tactical goals that jointly encode mission mode, cooperative role, engagement geometry, resource status, and safety constraints. During failed-trajectory reconstruction, tactical soft matching is introduced to replace the rigid goal correspondence used in the original Dynamic Hindsight Experience Replay (DHER), thereby expanding the set of high-value failed segments that can be relabeled. During replay, confidence gating and adaptive scheduling are further incorporated to ensure reliable sample injection and stable training. Experiments in a 2 -vs- 2 heterogeneous cooperative UAV air combat environment show that TAC-DHER consistently outperforms the original DHER across multiple dynamic combat scenarios, including standard dynamic-target settings, constrained training configurations, riskconstrained cooperation, and extremely sparse-reward conditions. Ablation studies confirm the complementary contributions of tactical soft matching, confidence gating, and adaptive replay scheduling, while trajectory cases further demonstrate that TACDHER induces more stable cooperative suppression and targetelimination behaviors.

title: Architecture of an IoT–AI Digital Ecosystem for Air Quality Monitoring and Environmental Assessment
authors: Ali Abbasov, Elchin Aliyev, Ramin Rzayev, Asgar Almasov
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: This paper presents an Internet of Things (IoT)– Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based digital ecosystem for continuous air quality monitoring and environmental assessment in urban and ecologically sensitive regions of Azerbaijan. The system integrates distributed sensor networks, cloud storage, and intelligent analytics within a unified framework. Real-time and historical data are processed using machine learning models and fuzzy inference to estimate pollution levels, detect anomalies, and generate short-term forecasts. The platform incorporates automated data validation, adaptive model updating, and spatial– temporal analysis of pollutant dynamics. The approach was evaluated using multi-year datasets from urban monitoring stations and benchmarked against conventional statistical methods. Results demonstrate improved prediction accuracy and greater robustness under varying meteorological and emission conditions. The proposed architecture enables timely identification of high-risk pollution zones and supports evidence-based environmental management. Its modular and scalable design allows adaptation to regions with similar climatic and urban characteristics.

title: Propagation Characteristics and Parameters Optimization of Laser Ultrasonic Waves in C130 Post Insulators
authors: Bichuan Xu, Zhenhua Xu, Tao Lai, Yanglin Li, Fangji Liu, Hui Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Laser ultrasonic non-destructive testing (NDT) technology has the unique advantages of ultrasonic testing method and optical method, which improves the temporal and spatial resolution while realizing non-contact and non-destructive testing. In order to improve the excitation efficiency and defect detection accuracy of laser source ultrasonic non-destructive excitation, it is necessary to optimize the parameters of laser source to improve the energy conversion efficiency and reduce the testing cost. This paper mainly researched the propagation characteristics of laser ultrasonic waves under different conditions and simulated the process of laser ultrasonic action on C130 high-strength aluminum post insulators by using the finite element analysis software COMSOL Multiphysics 6.3. The parametric relationship between width of laser pulse 10-40ns, radius of beam 0.3-0.6mm, and resultant ultrasonic waves characteristics was quantitatively established. Simulation results revealed that a pulse width of 10ns combined with a beam radius of 0.3mm achieved optimal ultrasonic excitation efficiency. This result provided an important basis for the analysis of the internal ultrasonic waves propagation of C130 high-strength aluminum ceramic post insulators in the future.

title: A Unified AI-Based Approach for Performance Optimization and Threat Detection in Telecommunication Systems
authors: Prashant Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The rapid growth in telecommunication systems is resulting in increased complexity in these systems, thus creating problems in optimizing their performances and detecting intrusions in real-time. Conventional machine learning techniques and rule-based systems face difficulties in dealing with high-dimensional data and class imbalance problems. In this paper, an intelligent system called EATIO-Net (Ensemble Attention-Based Telecom Intrusion and Optimization Network) is presented for optimizing the performances of telecommunication systems and detecting intrusions simultaneously. Various data preprocessing techniques are applied in EATIO-Net, including outlier removal using IQR and Z-score methods, categorical encoding, normalization, class balancing using SMOTE, and text preprocessing using stopword removal and lemmatization techniques. Three deep learning models, named “Attention-based Multi-Layer Perceptron” (Attention-MLP), “TabNet,” and “CNN-LSTM hybrid,” are implemented on a balanced data set consisting of 159,926 training samples, 39,982 testing samples, and 78 features, where the ensemble technique is applied for improving the robustness of the predictions made by the models. The accuracy of the proposed framework is validated by the results obtained, where 98% accuracy, 97% precision, recall, and F1-score are achieved, making it suitable for modern high-speed telecommunication environments.

title: CS-MSENet: Centered Linear Attention and Edge Enhancement for UAV Detection
authors: Haibo Wang, Qingsong Meng, Lixin Qiao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the challenges of low resolution and cluttered backgrounds for small objects in UAV aerial imagery, this paper proposes a small object detection method based on an improved YOLO26m model. By introducing the Centered Squared Linear Self-Attention (CS-LSA) mechanism, the model achieves efficient global feature modeling while maintaining linear computational complexity. Furthermore, a Multi-scale Spatial Edge-enhancement Network (MSENet) is integrated to effectively sharpen the model's sensing precision for the boundary features of tiny targets. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved YOLO26m model strikes an optimal balance between detection accuracy and real-time performance, providing reliable technical support for intelligent inspection on resource-constrained embedded UAV platforms.

title: Hyperparameter-Invariant Deep Spiking Neural Networks: A Triple Synergy Approach
authors: Donglin Xiong, Jianwei Zhao, Jintao Li, Yun Li
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) promise energyefficient neuromorphic computing but suffer from extreme sensitivity to hyperparameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tau, \theta)$</tex>, necessitating costly trial-and-error tuning. To address this issue, we integrate reset-free dynamics (Accumulator-LIF) with Layer Normalization and SelfAttention, yielding hyperparameter invariance: across extensive search of neuronal parameters (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau, \theta$</tex>), the accuracy variance is strictly zero (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma=0.000 \%$</tex>). Under this tuning-free paradigm, we develop an SNN architecture reliably to achieve 77.20 % on CIFAR-100, and a stable average accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.73 \% (\pm 0.16 \%$</tex> across random seeds) on CIFAR-10, peaking at 94.03 %. We formalize this via the Triple Synergy Mechanism: (1) Reset-free dynamics linearize gradients, enabling scale-invariant normalization; (2) Softmax's relative encoding decouples sparsity from accuracy; (3) Adaptive attention compensates residual variations. Extensive validation shows 6.85 % robustness advantage under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> noise, stable feature representations (Procrustes distance: 4.49 vs 23.10), and flat loss landscapes (std: 0.021 vs 0.098). This work eliminates hyperparameter tuning, clearing a major hurdle for plug-and-play SNN deployment on edge devices and embodied intelligent systems.

title: Neuro-Symbolic AI for Cognitive Interaction in Humanitarian Voice-Based Interfaces
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Myasar Mundher Adnan, Ankam Sreejyothsna, Manish Gupta, B. Sireesha, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The proliferation of voice-based interfaces (VBIs) offers a transformative opportunity for humanitarian assistance, enabling interaction with vulnerable populations irrespective of literacy or digital literacy levels. However, conventional deep learning-based VBIs often struggle in these high-stakes, low-resource contexts, exhibiting critical limitations in reasoning, contextual understanding, and operational transparency. This paper posits that Neuro-Symbolic AI (NeSyAI), which integrates the statistical pattern recognition of neural networks with the explicit, logical reasoning of symbolic AI, presents a seminal framework for overcoming these challenges. We propose a novel architecture for humanitarian VBIs that leverages neural networks for robust speech recognition and intent classification, while a symbolic reasoning engine manages task decomposition, contextual grounding, and ethical constraint validation. This synthesis facilitates not only improved accuracy in noisy, multilingual environments but also engenders crucial cognitive interactions—such as explainable decision-making, adaptive dialogue management, and trust-building through verifiable reasoning chains. The paper delineates the theoretical underpinnings of this approach, outlines a prototypical system design for a disaster response scenario, and discusses the significant implications for developing ethically aligned, cognitively resonant, and deployable humanitarian technologies.

title: Lightweight Thermal Fault Diagnosis in Induction Motors: A Comparative Study of CNNs and Depthwise-Separable CNNs
authors: Jimmy Palomino-Huilcapi, Mahmoud Kassas, Mohammad A. Abido
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This paper investigates lightweight thermal fault detection and diagnosis (FDD) for induction motors, utilising Infrared Thermography (IRT) and compact deep neural networks. We use consistent pipeline to compare a standard convolutional neural network (CNN) with a depthwise–separable convolutional neural network (DS–CNN) across depths $d \in\{2,3,4\}$, employing purely geometric augmentation to maintain radiometric integrity, and evaluate using two protocols: a fixed $50 / 50$ train–test split that simulates limited training data and K-fold cross-validation. Experiments are performed on a publicly available motor thermography dataset featuring eleven different operating and fault conditions. At a consistent depth of $d=4$, the CNN reaches an accuracy of $96 \%$, while the DS–CNN attains $95 \%$, using approximately $25 \%$ of the parameters—similar results are shown in the cross-evaluation. Analysis of the confusion matrix reveals clear class separation overall, with some residual confusion mainly in low-severity inter–turn short–circuit fault (ITSC) cases. The results show that using DS–CNN preserves essential thermography cues, while significantly reducing the size of the model.

title: When Inertia Drives Transparency: The Role of Business Environmental Uncertainty in Inert and Alert Firms’ Sustainability Disclosure
authors: Made Irma Lestari, Pandu Dwi Luhur Pambudi, Emny Harna Yossy, Netnapha Chamnisampan, Steven Walker Simangunsong, Arinda Cinta Lestari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The turbulence of business environmental uncertainty in Indonesia provides a timely context for discussing corporate sustainability. This study aims to examine the impact of business environmental uncertainty on sustainability disclosure across two groups of firms: alert and inert. Using the full population data of 12 industry sectors in Indonesia, consisting of 363 listed companies and a total of 1,089 firm-year observations (570 alert and 519 inert), we tested differences using the Chow Test and dummy-interaction regression. Multiple linear regression was applied to identify variations in effects. Results show that business environmental uncertainty significantly influences sustainability reporting in both groups. While both alert and inert firms demonstrate positive significant effects, the impact is stronger in inert firms. This finding aligns with legitimacy and signaling theory, suggesting that environmental uncertainty compels inert firms, being less adaptive to rely more heavily on sustainability disclosure as a defensive strategy. Conversely, alert firms, being more responsive, do not depend as strongly on disclosure for legitimacy. This study contributes novelty by distinguishing firms based on adaptive capacity, thereby enriching legitimacy, signaling, and organizational adaptation theory.

title: Co-Designing Quantum Codes with Transversal Diagonal Gates via Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Xi He, Sirui Lu, Bei Zeng
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: We present a multi-agent, human-in-the-loop workflow that co-designs quantum error-correcting codes with pre-scribed transversal diagonal gates. It builds on the Subset-Sum Linear Programming (SSLP) framework, which partitions basis strings by modular residues and enforces <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex>-marginal Knill-Laflamme (KL) equalities via small LPs. The workflow is powered by GPT-5 and implemented within TeXRA, a multi-agent research assistant platform where agents collaborate in a shared LATEX-Python workspace synchronized with Git/Overleaf. Three specialized agents formulate constraints, sweep and screen candidate codes, exactify numerical solutions into rationals, and independently audit all KL equalities and induced logical actions. Focusing on distance-two codes with nondegenerate residues, we catalogue new nonadditive codes for dimensions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K \in\{2,3,4\}$</tex> on up to six qubits, including high-order diagonal transversals, yielding 14, 116 new codes. From these data, the system abstracts closed-form families and constructs a residue-degenerate ((6, 4, 2)) code implementing a transversal controlled-phase <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{diag}(1,1,1, i)$</tex>, illustrating how AI orchestration can drive rigorous, scalable code discovery.

title: Predicting Waterborne Related Diseases in Rural Jharkhand Using Machine Learning Until 2030
authors: Shweta Bharti, Ravi Kumar Burman, Abhishek Kumar, Muskan Pandey, Usha Desai, Biresh Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: This paper demonstrates an artificial intelligence enabled predictive model to forecast the risk of waterborne diseases in rural districts of Jharkhand, India which are estimated through the year 2030. The research combines the heterogeneous data such as hospital-reported cases of Integrated Disease Surveillance Programme (2017-2025), rainfall and temperature of the India Meteorological Department and NASA POWER, water supply of the Jal Jeevan Mission, and demographical forecast of the Census of India. The preprocessing of the data complicated the imputation of missing data, normalization and feature engineering of lag variables, rolling averages, rate of positivity, and seasonal encodings. Exploratory Data Analysis predicts high risk areas, monsoon related peaks, connected with health and climate. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model achieving 81.8% accuracy, macro F1-score of 0.771, AUROC of 0.802 on the 2024-2025 test set. Forecasts for 2026-2030 indicated elevated average high-risk probabilities (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.47$</tex>) compared with the historical mean (0.41). The proposed system will deliver probabilistic early warnings that will be used to conduct surveillance and facilitate climate-sensitive in the under-resourced area in the field of public health.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Compact UWB Antenna for Breast Tumor Detection
authors: V. N. Koteswara Rao Devana, M. Hemantha Rao, K. Moulya Sri, K. Nagarjuna, D. Tarun Deva Harish, Ch. V. Ravi Sankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This paper describes the design and numerically evaluating a small Ultra-Wideband (UWB) radiator for utilization in detecting breast tumors. The proposed antenna is made on a FR4 substrate and works over 3.33 GHz to 13.23 GHz. This covers the entire UWB spectrum set aside for imaging applications. CST Studio Suite 2025 with a time-domain solver is used to design the antenna and verify its performance to make sure to get an adequate broadband characteristic. The simulated results show that the reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}$</tex>) stays below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 0 ~ d B}$</tex> across the entire operating band, which means that the impedance matching is very good. The antenna has stable radiation properties and a pattern that stays uniform over the UWB range. Also, the radiation efficiency ranges from 60 % to 82.3 %, and the highest gain achieved is 5.22 dBi. To ensure safe operation near biological tissues, a Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) analysis is done, and the results show that the values are within internationally accepted safety limits. This shows that the radiator is suitable for biomedical microwave imaging applications.

title: Enhancing Reliability in Luxury Marketing Using AI, Big Data, and Financial Analytics: Infocom Technologies and Computational Strategies in the Digital Age
authors: Navleen Kaur, Smriti Sethi, Mehpreet Kaur, Naman Kalra, M Kashish
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: To strike a balance between exclusivity and accessibility, the digital transformation of luxury marketing has brought computational tactics that make use of big data, financial analytics, machine learning, and artificial intelligence (AI). This study investigates how financial modelling, neural networks, and AI-driven customisation improve luxury brand experiences while preserving status. The study illustrates important technological strategies through case studies of Gucci and Louis Vuitton, such as influencer analytics, AI-driven demand prediction for restricted online releases, financial forecasting for trends in the luxury industry, and virtual reality-enhanced shopping experiences. The results shed light on how luxury firms can use financial strategies and computational methods to successfully manage digital transition without sacrificing their basic principles. Furthermore, 203 luxury consumers' survey answers provide insights on how consumers view digital personalization, influencer partnerships, accessibility, and exclusivity in the premium sector. To assess the connections between digital strategy and customer behaviour, statistical studies are used, such as regression models, correlation analysis, and chi-square testing.

title: Generalized Semi-analytical Modelling for Eddy Current Testing Based on Reflection and Transmission Coefficients at Heterogeneous Interfaces
authors: Zhengshuai Liu, Yong Li, Wenlong Gao, Bingjie Su, Zhenmao Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: In an effort to clarify the influence of the ferrite core and defect on the propagation of the electromagnetic field in eddy current testing, the reflection and transmission characteristics at their boundaries are thoroughly investigated based on the extended truncated region eigenfunction expansion (ETREE) method. Building upon this, generalized expressions of reflection and transmission coefficients are formulated at two types of heterogeneous interfaces, namely homogeneous-homogeneous interfaces and homogeneous-heterogeneous interfaces, which are subsequently applied to the theoretical modeling of eddy current testing involving a ferrite-core probe and a multi-layer conductive medium with a defect. The accuracy of the theoretical model as well as the reflection and transmission coefficients at both homogeneous-homogeneous interfaces and homogeneous-heterogeneous interfaces is validated through experimental results.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Explainable AI Framework for Predicting Customer Churn in Telecom Networks
authors: Shivakumar Nethani, Sharfunisa. N, Kalneedi Rambabu, N Subbu Krishna, Manjula Mallya M, Deepali Yewale
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Customer churn in telecommunications imposes substantial revenue risk and necessitates predictive models that are both discriminative and operationally interpretable; existing high-capacity learners often deliver opaque outputs or implausible prescriptive recommendations. This study aimed to develop and validate a latency-aware, neurosymbolic explainable AI pipeline that concurrently maximizes churn discrimination, calibration and actionable interpretability. A large-scale experimental study was conducted using a 2.15M-record telecom corpus; the architecture fuses gradient boosters (LightGBM/CatBoost/XGBoost), an FT-Transformer for tabular interactions, and a GNN head for call/SMS relational signals within a calibrated stacking meta-learner. Preprocessing combined iterative Bayesian imputation, Boruta selection and SMOTE balancing. Explainability comprised TreeSHAP, Integrated Gradients/DeepSHAP, TCAV concept scores and constrained DiCE counterfactuals; fidelity, stability and sparsity metrics plus bootstrap/DeLong statistical tests were employed. The stacked hybrid achieved superior discrimination and calibration (AUC-ROC = 0.993, 95% CI [0.990,0.995]; PR-AUC = 0.972; Brier = 0.028) and concentrated churn risk (lift@10% = 3.15). Ablations show SMOTE and Boruta materially drive PR-AUC and F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> gains. The explainability stack produced high local fidelity (TreeSHAP) and coherent concept alignment (TCAV ρ ≈ 0.72) with sparse, plausible counterfactuals (mean Δ features ≈ 2.2; plausibility ≈ 0.92). Integrating neurosymbolic concept explanations with hybrid predictive stacking yields both state-of-the-art churn performance and operationally actionable, audit-ready explanations, supporting deployable retention interventions and governance.

title: Optimization of Low-Cost Flex Sensor Performance Using Machine Learning
authors: Carla Zuccarini, Bhargavi Rakesh Savani, William Luu, Ava Hedayatipour
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Flex sensors are pivotal in wearable technology, robotics, and gesture recognition due to their affordability and flexibility. However, their performance is often hindered by non-linear behavior, environmental sensitivity, and variability. This paper explores the enhancement of low-cost flex sensor performance through machine learning regression models. A system was designed using an Arduino-based setup for automated data collection, integrating a servo motor for precise angle control and a voltage divider for resistance measurement. Several regression models, including Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, Gaussian Process Regression (GPR), a Stacked Ensemble, and an uncertainty-aware fusion model, were evaluated based on Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> scores. Results showed that GPR achieved the highest prediction accuracy with an MAE of 0.82° and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.9981, outperforming traditional methods. These findings demonstrate that applying advanced machine learning techniques significantly enhances the predictive reliability of flex sensors, making them more viable for precision applications in wearable and embedded systems.

title: Hybrid Lexical-Topic Feature Framework for Machine Learning-Based Suicidal Ideation Prediction
authors: Maya Gharat, Manivel Kandasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Suicidal ideation is a significant public health issue, and the early detection of such ideation from text data could potentially allow for early intervention and the saving of lives. However, the automatic detection of suicidal ideation from text data is difficult because of the subtle and varied ways in which people express their distress through text. To tackle this issue, this paper proposes a hybrid lexical-topic feature framework for machine learning-based suicidal ideation prediction, using the TF-IDF n-gram approach and Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic features to model the lexical and topic-level semantic structures of user-generated text. The experiments are performed on the publicly available Suicide Watch dataset, which includes posts that have been annotated for the presence of suicidal ideation. Various machine learning classifiers are trained and tested using standard evaluation metrics. The hybrid feature-based approach outperforms the individual feature-based approaches, emphasizing the significance of modeling the lexical and topic-level semantic structures of usergenerated text for the detection of subtle suicidal ideation cues.

title: Research on Early Fault Detection Method of 10kV Cable Based on Adaptive Fusion of Multimodal Features
authors: Zikang Li, Haoran Liu, Xiaomin Lu, Jifeng Zhang, Ziyi Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: One-sided acquisition of cable operation information leads to low accuracy in early fault detection of 10kV cables. To address this issue, a research method for early fault detection of 10kV cables based on adaptive fusion of multimodal features is proposed. A high-frequency current sensor is used to acquire signals, which are then processed by wavelet threshold denoising, standardization, and sliding window segmentation of the 10kV cable operation signal. Based on the local signal segments processed by the sliding window, multimodal features in the time domain, frequency domain, and time-energy domain are extracted to reflect the cable operation status. The multimodal features are fused through adaptive weight calculation and input into the LOF model to achieve early fault detection of 10kV cables. Experiments show that the proposed method achieves a high fault detection accuracy of 98.32%. Compared with the comparative method, it significantly improves the detection sensitivity under various cable load rates, with an average improvement of over 10%, and significantly reduces the number of misclassified samples, thus improving the accuracy of early fault detection of 10kV cables.

title: Automated Solar Cell Surface Defect Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: R. Santhosh Kumar, Bachu Hemanth, Anagani Sai Deepthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Renewable energy resources, especially solar energy, play a crucial role in fulfilling the increasing demand for energy while addressing environmental concerns. Nevertheless, solar cell efficiency reduces with time owing to surface defects caused by environmental and usage factors. Electroluminescence imaging helps to highlight these defects, but manual processing of these images is a time-consuming, errorprone, and inefficient process. This paper presents a completely automated deep learning-based system for the detection and localization of solar cell surface defects. The system includes a series of preprocessing tasks such as distortion correction, segmentation, and perspective transformation, followed by convolutional neural network for defect detection. To handle the issue of imbalanced data, methods such as minority oversampling and data augmentation are used. The proposed system is trained and tested on a real-world electroluminescence image dataset, with an overall classification accuracy of 87.4% for the detection of common solar cell surface defects such as cracks, scratches, and impurities. Moreover, a Faster Region-Based Convolutional Neural Network (Faster R-CNN) model with a Feature Pyramid Network (RFPN) is designed to facilitate efficient detection and localization of surface defects. The system minimizes the time required for manual inspection, improves quality control, and facilitates the automation of solar cell manufacturing. Experimental results show that deep learning approaches are a trustworthy and scalable solution for solar cell surface defect detection in the photovoltaic industry.

title: Optimisation of Phase Processing And Noise Reduction In Speech Enhancement Using CSM and Adaptive Filters
authors: Savita More, Jagdish Helonde, Prakash G. Burade
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The significance of noise cancellation: Noise cancellation is very important to many applications, such as teleconferencing, VoIP [1], hearing aids, and automatic speech recognition. Noise in the environment, particularly room noise, is often a significant contributor to poor intelligibility of speech. The present work is focused on combating such interference through the use of adaptive filtering methodologies, like Recursive Least Squares (RLS) and Normalized Least Mean Squares (NLMS), with the aim of enhancing speech intelligibility for the listener. Furthermore, much research has been done on phase-relevant processing for improving the perceptual quality of generated speech. The traditional way of spectrogram-masking and phase reconstruction often ignores the problems with consistency constraint, which is likely causing audible artefacts as well as complicating the reconstruction process. To achieve this goal, we introduce CSM, a new framework for imposing consistency-based constraints to optimize spectrogram estimation at the complex-valued level. Embed the phase estimation into speech enhancement models for better time frequency domain representations, e.g., \ common ratio mask (CRM). Experimental results show that the CSM can effectively improve speech quality and accelerate model convergence. Our approaches might provide adaptation algorithms of speech for denoising/mic array-based speech denoising for noise reduction applications.

title: News Under the Microscope: Exposing Fakes with Classic Machine Learning
authors: Naeema Salah Alazemi, Nabil M. Hewahi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The rapid spread of misinformation through online news platforms poses significant societal challenges, making fake news detection an urgent area of study. This paper presents a machine learning-based framework for classifying fake and real news articles using classical algorithms combined with TFIDF vectorization. Utilizing the publicly available Fake and Real News Dataset from Kaggle, we apply thorough text preprocessing including lemmatization, stopword removal, and vectorization to transform raw news content into structured features.We evaluate five supervised learning models: Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Multinomial Naïve Bayes, Random Forest, and a Voting Classifier. On a held-out test set and via 5-fold cross-validation, Random Forest and SVM deliver near perfect performance, while Naïve Bayes trails but remains competitive given its speed. We further analyze computational cost and potential overfitting, and discuss why tree ensembles excel for this dataset. Finally, we position classical models relative to recent transformer-based approaches and outline conditions where lightweight models are preferable.While transformer-based architectures such as BERT and DistilBERT have achieved strong results in fake news detection, they are discussed in this paper only for contextual comparison. Our findings demonstrate that well-engineered classical models remain highly competitive, achieving near-perfect accuracy at a fraction of the computational cost typically associated with deep learning approaches.

title: Shapley-Driven Federated Edge Intelligence for Fair and Secure Model Aggregation in Industry 4.0 Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Natrayan L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Industry 4.0 is the collaboration of multiple sectors in the industry where the IoT (Internet of Things) based devices are deployed to collect information in a particular sector. Federated Learning system efficiently utilizes the network load by transferring the gradient of the information sensed rather than the raw content of the sectors. An edge node helps in processing the information of the local model that is deployed by the global model. The gradients from the local models are aggregated, and the gradients are transmitted to the global model. In the aggregation, the unequal data distribution and non-iiid data handling become more challenging. Hence, this work proposes the Shapley-based game theoretic approach to the aggregation of the gradients in the global model of the FL system. The coalition utility function is used for the sector-wise probability distribution, and the Shapley aggregation is done using it. The proposed work shown 95.12% accuracy due to the collaboration of the sector-wise distribution with Jain's Fairness Index of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>.

title: Dynamic Trust AI: Uncertainty-Driven Decision Adaptation for Trustworthy Human–AI Collaboration in High-Risk Imbalanced Systems
authors: Nour Ahmad Kaymih, Hajar Dib, Noor Aldeen Alawad
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: In many highly imbalanced domains, such as detecting credit card fraud, setting a threshold on the output of a classifier is a common and critical task. However, the common static threshold and uniform treatment of model predictions generally result in low sensitivity when detecting rare events.Dynamic Trust AI is a lightweight framework that is model agnostic, and it transforms the model’s uncertainty into dynamic, instance-specific decision controls. The framework uses predictive entropy to determine dynamic decision thresholds at runtime.On the European credit card fraud dataset, Dynamic Trust AI achieves a recall of 0.880 and an F1 score of 0.880, outperforming the baseline model (recall 0.720, F1 0.800). Moreover, the model achieves a PR-AUC of 0.849, indicating that it reduces the number of false negatives while maintaining precision.These results show that Dynamic Trust AI can significantly improve the reliability of AI models and the human–AI teaming in safety-critical settings.

title: VaST-Conv: Variable-Strided Tiled Convolution Framework for Resource-Efficient CNN Acceleration
authors: Bindu G Gowda, Pradyumna G, Madhav Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Convolution is the backbone of modern computer vision, yet traditional CNNs incur high computational cost, limiting deployment on constrained hardware. This work introduces the VaST-Conv strategy, where the input feature map is tiled, and each tile is convolved with potentially different stride values to balance resolution and computation. Non-uniform striding improves flexibility and reduces MAC operations, with outputs aligned through interpolation for seamless processing. The approach is evaluated on emotion classification and YOLO-based object detection, along with FP16 MAC hardware cost analysis. Results show that variable-strided tiled convolution provides a scalable and efficient alternative to conventional CNN processing.

title: LLM-Enhanced Braille-to-Speech Learning Assistant for Visually Impaired Users Using AraT5
authors: Alya Alahmed, Al Anood Saleh, Fatna Belqasmi, Manar Alkhatib
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: Braille literacy remains essential for visually impaired individuals to access written information and achieve independence in education and daily communication. However, learning Braille can be challenging due to the lack of immediate feedback when interpreting Braille dot patterns. Traditional assistive technologies often focus only on recognizing Braille characters and converting them into speech without providing contextual explanations that support learning.This paper proposes an intelligent Braille-to-Speech learning assistant that integrates rule-based Braille decoding with the Arabic transformer language model AraT5. The system converts Braille symbols into text, generates contextual explanations using a large language model, and provides auditory feedback through text-to-speech synthesis.Experiments conducted using a Kaggle Braille dataset demonstrate strong performance, achieving 93% Braille decoding accuracy, 0.84 token-level F1 score, ROUGE-1 score of 0.79, ROUGE-L score of 0.76, and BLEU score of 0.71. These results indicate that combining symbolic Braille decoding with large language models significantly improves accessibility and enhances interactive Braille learning for visually impaired users.

title: Efficient and Secure Watermarking for Encrypted Images Using LHE and DWT
authors: Ch. Praveen Kumar, Arla Gopala Krishna, Sanjay Kumar, Pandu Sowkuntla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Multimedia storage and image sharing over cloud platforms have increased the need for secure and privacy-preserving image authentication. Traditional watermarking methods either operate in the plaintext domain or rely on Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE), which incurs high computational cost and limits real-time applicability. To address these issues, this paper proposes an efficient watermarking framework that integrates Lightweight Homomorphic Encryption (LHE) with the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT), enabling watermark embedding and extraction directly in the encrypted domain without decryption. The host image is encrypted using LHE and decomposed using DWT, where the watermark is embedded in the low-frequency LL sub-band to ensure high imperceptibility and robustness against common attacks. Compared to FHE- based methods, LHE significantly reduces computational time and memory usage. Experimental results on grayscale images demonstrate high visual quality in terms of PSNR and SSIM, along with reliable watermark extraction. The proposed method is suitable for privacy-sensitive applications such as medical imaging, cloud storage, and digital rights management.

title: Green Goals Toning Fintech: A Literature Review
authors: Rachna, Manjit Kour
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: To enhance the financial sectors, it is essential to integrate policies, frameworks, practices, and strategies that will address both economic demands and social needs. The Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) pillars of sustainable finance fulfill businesses' responsibilities towards the environment and social welfare. Technological advancements are also being used to achieve the firm's sustainable objectives. Fintech is enhancing the functionality of financial products and services, but evolving needs have increased the call for a more sustainable approach, leading to the emergence of Green Fintech. Green Fintech merges financial technology with environmental and social requirements to promote sustainability. It is an intelligent advancement achieved by digitization and prioritizing sustainability as the main motivators. It creates eco-friendly technologies to enhance corporate conduct and optimize financial processes, along with green strategies to support brand sustainability.In order to understand the conceptual meaning of Green Fintech and other terms associated with it, the theories of a number of researchers have been reviewed in this paper. This paper also considers the Difficulties and Future Possibilities of Fintech practicing Green, which can be considered by experts, researchers, and the general public for further study.

title: An Intelligent AI-Based Multimodal Platform for Adaptive Interview Simulation and Candidate Assessment
authors: Kanukula Anshu, Yugandhar Manchala, Battula Bhoomika Naga Sai, Mungi Vverendhra Kumar, Gedipudi Abijith, Nirmal Keshari Swain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This research work introduces an AI-based mock interview performance analysis system developed using Streamlit to simulate and analyze actual interview situations. The system combines computer vision, audio processing, and natural language processing to analyze candidate answers from different perspectives, such as communication effectiveness, facial expressions, and emotional undertones. The system allows users to get real-time feedback to aid them in preparing for interviews through sentiment analysis, speech rate tracking, and visual analysis. For academic and professional interview preparation, the system provides an interactive and holistic self-analysis tool that accepts text, audio, and video inputs with real-time insights analysis.

title: Cross-Dataset Performance Evaluation of Multiple Faster R-CNN Models for Generalized Pothole Detection Application
authors: Krish Patel, Anshul Patel, Harsh Kapadia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The progressive deterioration of road infrastructure, specifically pothole formation, presents critical risks to vehicular safety and logistics efficiency in rapidly developing regions like India. Although deep learning-based object detection has advanced significantly, the robustness of these models when subjected to the domain shifts encountered in real-world deployments remains insufficiently characterized. This study presents a rigorous three-experiment framework to evaluate the cross-dataset generalization of Faster R-CNN variants Namely ResNet50, ResNet101, and ResNeXt101 equipped with Feature Pyramid Networks (FPN). Performance baselines were established on a primary composite dataset, followed by source-aware hyperparameter optimization to stabilize feature representation. Extensive cross-domain evaluations on multiple independent target datasets reveal a measurable generalization gap, with performance degrading by over 20 % in high-noise environments. While the ResNeXt101-FPN architecture demonstrates the highest resilience across unseen domains, the ResNet50 variant provides a superior balance of accuracy and inference speed for edge deployment. These findings quantify the impact of environmental domain shift and emphasize the necessity of crossdataset validation for the reliable deployment of automated road monitoring systems.

title: A Bibliometric Analysis of Artificial Intelligence enabled Predictive Analytics in Customer Retention and Credit Risk Management
authors: Richa Sharma, Aanchal Tyagi, Shaleen Suneja, Preshni Shrivastava, Nidhi Agarwal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Customer retention has become an important aspect in today's competitive world as it helps avoid losses and increase long-term value which are the main goals of organizations. The organizations are eager to learn about the creditworthiness and retention of their customers. Artificial intelligence driven predictive analytics can help organizations in this regard. The study is aimed at conducting a bibliometric analysis of the research on artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled predictive analytics in customer retention and credit risk management domain. For the purpose of this study, 211 publications have been retrieved from Scopus database through a structured search of titles, abstracts, and keywords in September 2025. Further, VOSviewer software was used to map bibliographic coupling, Keyword trends and co-authoring networks in the dataset. The results reveal consistent growth in publications since 1997, followed by an increase in 2016 and recent years. The results revealed that the key themes are Fraud detection, machine learning, data mining and consumer behavior modelling. India and the US have emerged as the top two countries in terms of output and influence, despite the fact that institutional contributions are still dispersed. The results suggest for more coordinated institutional efforts with greater attention to ethical and regulatory concerns.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Handcrafted Feature - Based Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Automated Mango Grading
authors: E. Loganathan, M. Bhavatharani, C.A. Kandasamy, A. Yoganathan, P. Kalyanasundaram, S. Nivetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Mango grading is an essential process for quality assessment and prevention of post-harvest losses. However, manual grading is the one of the most time-consuming processes. This proposed system uses a CNN-based deep learning model to extract deep feature and combines with the handcrafted features such as color, size, texture, and surface defect attributes using ensemble machine learning. The experiment consists of two groups. Group1 uses a traditional Random Forest model and Group 2 uses a Hybrid Ensemble model. The Experiments were performed on mango images originally taken from a Kaggle dataset. The mango grading image samples are 1200. The result Indicate that traditional Random Forest model achieved an accuracy of 88.64% while the proposed hybrid ensemble models achieve a higher accuracy of 94% and also hybrid models produce a better precision of 97.0%–97.9% and overall recall value for all grades of mangoes. Additionally, proposed hybrid models produce a low overall error or RMSE of 0.87 compared to Random Forest models and have less variability or error. Statistical analysis of the proposed hybrid model shows the overall accuracy significant value of P < 0.001 compared to traditional Random Forest models. This proposed hybrid model can give a reliable and efficient automated grading process in mangoes that helps in real-word applications.

title: PosePart-MLP: 3D Human Pose Estimation via Hierarchical MLP and Cross-Part Attention
authors: Junna Gao, Guohui Wang, Hongxue Yang, Lei Ma, Jianxing Yang, Chuanhao Ma, Dake Guo, Siyuan He
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Estimating 3D human pose from 2D joint coordinates is an important task in computer vision, but traditional methods often produce significant errors on distant body joints due to the articulated structure of the human skeleton and its high degree of freedom. Existing MLP-based models typically process joint features independently, failing to unify the modeling of local joint dependencies and global pose structure. To address these issues, this paper proposes PosePart-MLP, a novel end-toend network for 3D human pose estimation from 2D inputs. Our method first introduce a multi-level feature extraction module to fuse global spatial context and local joint features. We then divide human skeleton into a hierarchical structure based on body topology, and employ a hierarchical MLP pose block (HMPB) module to model intra-region dependencies and inter-region feature interactions. Finally, we use a prediction head to generate the final 3D pose. Experimental results on the Human3.6M dataset demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches, and exhibits significantly enhanced robustness on distant body joints.

title: Unsteady Motion Parameter Extraction for Shipborne High-Frequency Surface Wave Radar Using Distributed Reference Sources
authors: Yuze Wang, Yuhan Xia, Ruizhe Zhou, Xianzhou Yi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Shipborne high-frequency surface wave radar (HFSWR) operating under unsteady motion conditions faces critical challenges in motion parameter extraction, which is a prerequisite for reliable ocean surface current inversion. The integrated navigation system suffers from significant translational parameter degradation during non-stationary platform motion, necessitating alternative approaches. This paper proposes a cascaded motion parameter extraction framework based on distributed reference sources for shipborne HFSWR under unsteady motion. The proposed architecture decouples rotation and translation parameter extraction into two independent stages. First, rotational parameters (yaw, pitch, and roll) are estimated via multi-source maximum-likelihood search using the measured steering vectors of reference signal sources relative to the shipborne receiving array. Second, translational parameters (east and north displacements) are extracted through a differential reference source architecture that eliminates the clock synchronization error between shore-based transmitters and the shipborne radar. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves yaw root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.2°, pitch RMSE of 0.8°, and roll RMSE of 1.7° for attitude estimation, and east/north RMSE of 1.7 m and 2.5 m for translation estimation, with a trajectory recovery ratio of 95.3%.

title: Adaptive Power Electronics: Neuromorphic Control of Converters for Renewable Microgrids
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Hassan M. Al-Jawahry, Sorabh Lakhanpal, Kanchan Yadav, B. Sireesha, Shashi Prakash Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Adaptive power-electronics control is essential for robust, efficient integration of distributed renewable resources into future microgrids. This paper proposes a neuromorphic control framework for power converters that leverages event-driven spiking neural networks (SNNs) and lightweight on-chip learning to deliver low-latency, energy-efficient, and fault-tolerant regulation of voltage and current in heterogeneous renewable microgrids. The proposed approach (i) maps converter dynamics to compact SNN architectures that exploit temporal encoding of measurements, (ii) employs local, online adaptation rules inspired by biological plasticity for rapid set-point tracking and disturbance rejection, and (iii) integrates with conventional hierarchical microgrid controls (droop and secondary regulation) to ensure stability and power sharing. Analytical stability arguments are developed for the neuromorphic-controller-in-the-loop using Lyapunov-inspired energy metrics and are complemented by a suite of hardware-aware performance bounds that quantify latency, spike-rate energy, and robustness to parameter drift. Simulation and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) emulations against representative photovoltaic and battery-sourced microgrids demonstrate that the neuromorphic controller attains comparable regulation accuracy to model-predictive and adaptive-neural controllers while reducing active control-compute energy and response latency under abrupt load changes and communications degradations. The results indicate that neuromorphic control offers a promising pathway to embed adaptive intelligence directly within converters, enabling resilient, distributed renewable microgrids with reduced communications dependence and improved edge energy efficiency.

title: Smart Control System with NodeMCU-Controlled Relays and MobileNet-SSD for Person Detection
authors: Sunil Kumar G., Surya Kavitha T., Vamsidhar A., Sankara Rao P., Sridhar G. V., Ravi Tejesvi V.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The inception of intelligent context-aware systems has been made possible over the past decade by the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with Internet of Things (IoT) devices. This study describes a real-time, vision-based human detection system to autonomously operate numerous electrical appliances utilizing AI and IoT hardware. The proposed system uses a laptop webcam to continuously record video data, which is then analyzed by OpenCV in Python using a pretrained Mobile-SSD deep learning model to identify human presence. Upon recognizing a person, the control commands were delivered via serial-communication to an ESP8266-based NodeMCU microcontroller integrated on a Kiwi-IoT V1 development board. This board connects with a 4-channel relay module that operates a 12V DC fan, serial lamp and a submersible pump. A 3-cell LiPo battery powers the system, and an L298 motor driver controls the power distribution. For smart environments such as homes, farms and industrial areas, this proposed integrated architecture provides a low-cost, portable solution by enabling smooth interaction between AI-based detection and physical control of electrical equipment.
