This approach enables arbitrary partition connectivity and achieves rapid performance prediction with minimal training data requirements. Furthermore, to mitigate accuracy degradation arising from physical approximations, a neural network-based residual correction module is integrated to systematically refine the predictive responses. This hybrid physics-informed approach reduces computational complexity by orders of magnitude while maintaining high fidelity, providing a scalable and efficient solution for advanced EM design.

title: Role of AI in Fostering Professors’ Well-Being and Job Satisfaction
authors: N Janani, E Nafeza, Mithilesh, Venus Claver Dennen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The academic industry is changing as AI solves academics' biggest working issues. This research investigates how artificial intelligence improves academics' well-being and work satisfaction via technological interventions and supportive mechanisms. The study examines how AI can speed up administrative processes, decrease workplace stress, and improve learning environments. Artificial intelligence automates scheduling, grading, and student administration, freeing professors to focus on academics and research. The most noteworthy results show how artificial intelligence can adapt professional development, offer data-driven teaching tactics, and provide flexible learning environments. The technology's complete support networks reduce burnout, improve resource management, and diversify the workplace. Artificial intelligence's predictive analytics may potentially uncover issues and provide proactive assistance. The research found that strategic artificial intelligence may improve academic work cultures, improving educator job satisfaction, career development, and well-being.

title: Integrating Machine Learning and Data Analytics for Strategic Marketing Optimisation in the FMCG sector for consumer retention and loyalty
authors: Anshul Srivastava, Anamica Singh, Namita Sahay, Rita Mansukhlal Kotecha, Shambhavi Mishra, Vineeta Singh, Chirayu Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper will examine the revolution in marketing analytics, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of marketing campaigns and improve customer retention in the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) industry. This paper integrates the machine learning algorithm and data analytics for customer retention and to optimise marketing decision-making. The capability to make data-driven marketing decisions has become a critical success factor in a market where customer loyalty is short-lived and consumer preferences keep changing. The purpose of the study is to close the gap between raw marketing data and actionable insights and, more specifically, how analytics can expand its personalisation, optimize the engagement touchpoints, and generate long-term loyalty. The primary data are collected using structured surveys and statistical models that include multiple regression, correlation using Python. The paper discusses the ways of brands relying on descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics to learn customer behaviour, support personalised marketing, and Customer Lifetime Value. It further points out how information-based approaches drive customer loyalty, brand loyalty, and competitive edge, providing a guide to the optimisation of campaigns and developing sustainable consumer relationships.

title: Deep Learning Empowers Internet of Things Device Energy Consumption Optimization: Design of Intelligent Gateway Resource Scheduling Based on CNN-LSTM Model
authors: Junxu Wei, Huqiang Liu, Xiaoyun Huang, Rui Zheng, Yongjun Qi
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: With the surge in IoT (Internet of Things) devices, energy efficiency management challenges for smart gateways are becoming increasingly prominent. Traditional static resource scheduling strategies struggle to adapt to dynamic loads. This paper proposes a hybrid intelligent scheduling model that integrates a convolutional neural network (CNN) with a long short-term memory (LSTM). First, the CNN extracts spatial correlation features from multi-dimensional monitoring data. Then, the LSTM learns the temporal evolution of the load. Finally, based on the predicted results, CPU (Central Processing Unit) frequency scheduling rules are dynamically generated to achieve on-demand resource allocation. Experimental results show that this strategy reduces energy consumption by approximately 30% under typical loads, significantly outperforms the delay violation rate of comparable methods, and improves resource utilization stability. This study validates the effectiveness and advantages of deep learning-driven proactive scheduling in energy optimization and service assurance for IoT gateways.

title: Unsupervised Learning for Health Insights: AI-Enabled K-Means Clustering in Chronic Disease Prediction
authors: Mukesh Joshi, Mamta Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Chronic diseases like diabetes, renal failure, high BP, and various cardiovascular disorders are the major causes of illness around the world, it requires regular monitoring and personalized healthcare system. The rapid increase in the volume of patient data generated from EHR i.e., electronic health records, various wearable devices, and from different clinical trials has created novel opportunities for designing data-driven disease management system. The present study explores the role of K-Means clustering technique i.e., an unsupervised machine learning technique, in evolving an active system for Chronic Disease Management (CDM). In this approach we do the clustering of patients that was based upon the various parameters like age, BMI, glucose level, blood pressure, and cholesterol. In addition, K-Means facilitates the identification of discrete group of patients with same risk (of health) profiles. These clusters could allow and guide various healthcare providers in tailoring various preventive strategies, optimization of different health care plans, and allocation of various resources more efficiently. The proposed research demonstrates how K-Means technique could boost early finding of diseases, improved patient stratification, and effective decision-making from healthcare analytics. Finally, the integration of cluster-based analysis for CDM system offers a capable method to achieve tailored, predictive, and preventive healthcare system.

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Amplitude and Phase Measurement Deviations Across Three Commercial VNAs for Common RF Components
authors: Liwen Liang, Kwok L. Chung, Jialiang Ni, Zhilie Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: As the core high-precision test instrument in RF and microwave engineering, the Vector Network Analyzer (VNA) provides full-vector S-parameter amplitude and phase measurements that directly determine the reliability of electromagnetic performance evaluation for RF devices such as antennas and microstrip lines. Most existing studies focus on measurement comparison between two VNAs using standard metrology devices, with insufficient research on multi-brand VNA measurement consistency for commonly used engineering RF devices. This paper selects three commercial VNAs (CEYEAR 3671D, KEYSIGHT E5063A, ROHDE & SCHWARZ ZNL20) to test the S-parameters of two patch antennas and one microstrip line under uniform SOLT calibration, and quantitatively evaluates measurement deviation of each instrument via the RMSE method. Results show that quantifiable amplitude and phase deviations exist across different VNAs, with phase deviation significantly larger than amplitude deviation; each VNA has unique performance strengths in different frequency bands. This study provides direct reference for instrument selection and measurement uncertainty analysis in RF testing.

title: Modified Lin-Kernighan-Helsgaun Based on Binary α
authors: Xianzhi Huang, Jiarui Zhang, Qunfeng Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: The Lin-Kernighan-Helsgaun algorithm is one of the most effective heuristics for the traveling salesman problem, and its performance largely depends on the candidate edge sets built from 1-tree based <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bold symbol{\alpha}$</tex> values. However, computing these <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> values requires repeated 1 -tree construction and updates, which becomes a major preprocessing bottleneck on medium- and large-scale instances and complicates integration with other metaheuristics. This paper proposes a lightweight candidate-set construction method that replaces the classical 1tree <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> computation with Minimum Spanning Tree (MST)guided binary <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> strategy. A minimum spanning tree is first built as a global structural backbone, and multiple MST-guided initial tours are generated to identify frequently used edges. Each edge is then assigned a binary label indicating whether it appears in at least one initial tour, and candidate edges are selected by sorting incident edges using the binary label and edge cost. Experiments on TSPLIB instances show that the proposed method achieves solution quality comparable to classical The Lin-Kernighan-Helsgaun while reducing preprocessing time on many medium and large-scale instances, although the total runtime can occasionally be dominated by the downstream local search, demonstrating an effective trade-off between efficiency and performance for large-scale settings and hybrid frameworks.

title: Research on Digital Modeling of the Impact of Ocean Environment for Shipborne TT&C Antennas
authors: Ji Guo, Xing Li, Qian Sun, Tian Wang, Tianpeng Xu, Liang Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: This paper focuses on the multi-dimensional impacts of complex marine environment on the performance of shipborne Tracking Telemetry and Command (TT&C) antennas. Key factors are introduced, such as wind, waves, swells, and electromagnetic, with the action mechanisms of those factors presented. A novel simulation architecture of marine environment combined with TT&C antennas has been constructed, based on digital modelling. The research focuses on key technologies including the coupling of multiple factors in complex environments, and adaptive modeling and prediction. The effectiveness of the architecture is verified, through simulation and measured data. The degradation regularities of antenna performances, such as pointing accuracy, and gain attenuation, are analyzed, with providing the references for the anti-interference design, performance optimization, and operation and maintenance support of TT&C antennas.

title: Performance Driven Integration of Phase Change Materials in Indian Building Envelopes: A Climate-Sensitive Design Framework
authors: Mukesh S. Khedkar, A I. Deshmukh, Vaishnavi D. Kute, Pankaj D. Kute, Rahul B. Ingle, Amir Sohel F. Amdani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The research investigates building envelope performance assessment through climate-specific Phase Change Materials (PCMs) in tropical India. The research uses real-world data from eight actual deployments across five different climatic zones which extend beyond traditional simulation-based methods. The research evaluates PCMs through their different types and envelope integration methods and performance indicators which include Peak Indoor Temperature Reduction (PITR) and Cooling Load Reduction (CLR) and Thermal Lag (TL) and Comfort Hours Gained (CHG). The research demonstrates climate-specific advantages through its findings which show bio-PCMs and eutectics work well in composite and moderate climates yet paraffin-based PCMs achieve maximum PITR in hot-dry zones. The integration method between roof and wall and ceiling and hybrid systems determines thermal performance results with hybrid systems delivering the most consistent improvements. The research provides additional design trade-off information through a radar plot and material-property correlation analysis. The research provides strategic guidelines to enhance PCM implementation for passive thermal management in tropical buildings that aim to reduce energy consumption.

title: Cross-Domain Multi-Agent Digital Twin for Adaptive Traffic Signal Control and Intelligent Campus Mobility
authors: Alfredo Alan Flores-Saldivar, Jorge de Jesus Lozoya Santos, Juan Carlos Tudon Martinez, Edson Fernando Acosta Argueta, Yuxian Li
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion poses increasing challenges for intelligent mobility systems, particularly as robotic platforms require reliable infrastructure-aware information for decision-making. This paper presents a crossdomain digital twin framework that integrates empirically grounded traffic demand modeling, multi-agent simulation, adaptive signal control, and synchronized 3D visualization within a unified architecture. Signalized intersections are modeled as interacting vehicle and traffic light agents operating over a graph-based network. Infrastructure-level control is formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), enabling adaptive and interpretable signal phase allocation based on observed queue states. Simulation outputs-including congestion dynamics, delay metrics, and signal evolution-are exported and synchronized with a Unity-based environment, forming a spatially and temporally consistent digital twin. Comparative evaluation against a fixed-time baseline demonstrates reductions in waiting time and queue length, together with increased throughput under realistic demand conditions. The framework further establishes a structured interface through which robotic agents, such as neighborhood electric vehicles (NEVs), can consume predictive infrastructure information to support navigation decisions. The results demonstrate that integrating adaptive multi-agent control with digital twin synchronization provides measurable traffic-level improvements while enabling scalable cross-domain coordination between infrastructure intelligence and robotic autonomy.

title: A lightweight deep learning and gradient boosting approach for Oral cancer classification
authors: S. Annie Kesviya, Manikandan J, R. Brindha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Oral cancer is a major health problem in the world and more so in less developed nations where access to early diagnostic facility is small or absent. Oral cancer presents itself in its early life stages with no symptoms and thus it is not diagnosed and survival is largely compromised. Conventional methods of diagnosis are based on manual histopathological examination that besides being time-consuming, is prone to errors and prone to biases on personal grounds. Recent growth in artificial intelligence, especially in deep learning and machine learning, are the opportunities that can enhance the accuracy and efficiency of medical image analysis. Most of the solutions in the literature are based on single-model architectures, and they can often not simultaneously extract features well and classify well. To overcome these constraints, we will present a new hybrid neural network structure called Oral Net-CB and this network is specially created to detect oral cancer at an earlier stage and precisely. The last processing pipeline is a complete preprocessing system based on methods like histogram equalization, contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), and Gaussian filtering to boost the quality of the image and decrease noise. DenseNet121 is utilized to extract deep features because it is densely connected and it can extract dense spatial features efficiently. To classify, we apply Cat Boost, which is a high-performance gradient boosting algorithm that is most appropriate in case of class imbalance and in working with categorical data. Oral Net-CB with a publicly available data of oral cancer images is applied. The model had a classification accuracy of 94.3% that was relatively better and accurate than the current model.

title: Integrated energy-based assessment of settlement-induced crack patterns of Pietà dei Turchini church in Naples
authors: Andrea Montanino, Antonino Iannuzzo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The preservation of historical masonry buildings is a crucial challenge, particularly in light of modern monitoring and digital technologies, given the exceptional value of Italy’s and Europe’s architectural heritage. Masonry, however, is a highly peculiar material whose mechanical behaviour differs markedly from that of conventional structural materials. As a result, standard material models and finite element codes cannot reliably describe the stability of such constructions. Moreover, due to its unilateral response, cracking is common; therefore, monitoring systems must be suitably designed, utilising appropriate electronic devices and advanced sensing technologies.The aim of this work is to illustrate the combined use of two numerical methods developed within the framework of Heyman’s model for normal, rigid, no-tension materials: the Continuous Displacement for Fractures (CDF) and the Continuous Airy-based for Stress Singularities (CASS) methods. A concluding discussion highlights the complementarities between the two approaches through their application to a case study involving the lateral wall of the Church of Santa Maria Incoronatella della Pietà dei Turchini, offering insights into suitable monitoring system solutions that can be coupled with these models to effectively support the preservation of historic masonry heritage.

title: Federated Reinforcement Learning Model for Smart Grid Load Distribution
authors: Hare Krishna Mishra, Gautam Kumar, Ashish Kumar Suman, S.B.G. Tilak babu, D. Galingana, Randolph M. Balleras
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The current study will present a Federated ActorCritic Reinforcement Learning (FA2C) framework of smart grids to offer intelligent and privacy-responsive load allocation. The proposed solution enables different distributed energy nodes to obtain the best load management policies without necessarily sharing uncoded consumption data thus ensuring security and scalability. The implementation with the assistance of the popular simulation tool of smart grid modeling, the and gridLAB-D, to facilitate realistic experimentation is done through the framework. The grid state provided by gridLAB-D is high-resolution, and the FA2C agents can associate with the dynamic grid situations and optimize the effectiveness of distribution. The results of the experiment demonstrate that the federated design works effectively in terms of load balancing, reduction of peak demand pressure, and user privacy. Overall, the FA2C-GridLAB-D framework offers a good guideline to decentralized, intelligent and secure energy management of smart grids.

title: Optimizing food bank logistics: A mixed integer linear programming approach for efficient humanitarian aid distribution
authors: Jairo Enrique Parra Herrera, Diego Rossit, Daniel Rossit
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Food security stays a global challenge, as highlighted by the UN’s Sustainable Development Goal 2: Zero Hunger. Food banks reduce waste by addressing food surplus and shortages, but their operations require efficient planning. This study aims to develop a Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model to optimize food bank logistics, minimizing costs across the supply chain. The model considers transportation costs, supply and processing capacities, demand, and product types. Food is collected from suppliers, joined into packages at plants, and distributed via centers to recipients. Implemented in Python with Google OR-Tools and evaluated on data from Bogotá, Colombia, preliminary results show possible, cost-effective solutions. Decomposition of costs reveals that supplier-to-plant transportation dominates the overall costs $(97-98 \%$ of the total), while downstream stages (plants–distribution centers and plants–clients) together account for less than 3%. Fixed technology costs remain negligible ($0.2 \%$). This underlines that system-wide efficiency hinges almost entirely on upstream transportation, and interventions should target this stage. This research highlights MILP’s potential to improve logistics, reduce costs, and improve efficiency. Future work will incorporate dynamic demand, real-time data, and larger datasets.

title: Performance of Quantum Machine Learning Algorithm for Prediction of Dementia Disease
authors: R. Santhosh Kumar, S. Aisha Siddika, M. Ram Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Dementia is a brain disorder which affects the human memory, behavior and cognitive abilities. The person affected with dementia is unable to perform daily activities. So, there is a need of a model which identifies the Dementia. Many researchers have designed machine learning and deep learning models which uses MRI, clinical data, and medical imaging. The existing models take much time to provide the decision. The Quantum mechine learning algorithm will greatly reduce the execution time. A Quantum Dementia Classification (QDC) model is designed to predict the Dementia disease. The QDC model achieved the accuracy of 95 %. Early detection of Dementia disease can help the doctor to provide more appropriate treatment. The objective of this study is to develop an efficient Quantum Dementia Classification (QDC) model for accurate and early prediction of dementia using clinical and cognitive features. Index Terms-Dementia, Quantum Machine Learning, Quantum Dementia Classification, Prediction.

title: Optimization of Process Parameters in Milling Operation using VMC
authors: Ishan Jain, Aditiya Choudhary, Piyush Pant, Chitresh Nayak, Ajay Sharma, Narender Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Nowadays, cutting-edge modernization have led to the adoption of Computer controlled machines in several industries to boost output and quality. The computer controlled machines outrival conventional equipment in terms of accuracy and finishing. By adjusting the framework, CNC milling machining can be used to its greatest potential. Thus, this work reviews the process parameter optimization for CNC milling with carbide tools. The review shows how feed, speed, and DOC affect MRR, SR, and tool wear. Experimenters have been using Taguchi for upgradation. Surface and profile graphs are used to investigate the effects of milling on tool wear, MRR, and SR. It was determined that the 316 L stainless steel process parameters may be improved.

title: Website Heuristics and Their influence on Generation Z’s Purchasing Behaviour
authors: Mythili. R, Kiruthiga V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the digital era, website design and usability significantly influence customer behavior, especially among the technologically adept Generation Z. This research examines the influence of website heuristics—namely navigation, aesthetics, interaction, trustworthiness, and personalization—on the purchase behavior of Generation Z in Chennai City. The study uses a mixed-method approach, integrating quantitative surveys and qualitative interviews to examine the preferences, expectations, and behaviors of this group. Research reveals that Generation Z prioritizes websites with intuitive navigation, aesthetically pleasing designs, engaging functionality, and extensive personalization. Trustworthiness, including safe payment methods and clear rules, is a pivotal component affecting purchasing choices. The research emphasizes the alignment of these heuristics with the psychological and cultural characteristics of Generation Z, including their inclination towards immediate satisfaction, social validation, and digital proficiency. Enterprises focusing on this demographic may use this information to optimize website design, elevate user experience, and increase online sales. This study offers practical advice for e-commerce platforms and digital marketers to enhance their strategies for Generation Z, hence fostering sustainable company success in Chennai's aggressive digital economy.

title: Simulated DPN-Like Gait Abnormality Recognition Using Wearable IMU and Plantar Pressure Sensing Framework: A Pilot Study
authors: Jiayuan Zhao, Yan Wang, Ruixiang Hu, Hui Wang, Xiangyu Zhan, Aihui Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy (DPN) is associated with sensory-motor decline and altered gait, motivating objective, low-cost gait assessment tools. This paper presents a wearable multimodal sensing framework that combines an outsole-mounted plantar-pressure sensor array and an acquisition unit with on-board IMU to recognize DPNlike gait abnormalities in a pilot study. A dual-branch CNNBiLSTM network is developed to extract complementary spatial-temporal features from synchronized 14-channel sequences (8 pressure +6 -axis IMU) sampled at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 ~ H z}$</tex>. On a subject-wise leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) evaluation, the proposed approach achieves 75.13 % accuracy with AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=$</tex> 0.8403 in distinguishing normal from DPN-like gait. Under a window-level random split, it reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 0 0 \%}$</tex> accuracy (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=$</tex> 0.90) and outperforms single-modality baselines. These results suggest the potential of multimodal wearables for objective gaitabnormality recognition, while clinical validation on real DPN cohorts remains future work.

title: Predictive Analytics to Assess the Cost of Educational Exclusion in Rural India
authors: Sanjana Chauhan, Pankaj Sinha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This study estimates the socioeconomic costs of educational exclusion in rural India, focusing on how policy gaps affect access, attainment, and long-term economic outcomes. Using a mixed-methods approach with household surveys and Sarpanch interviews, the study finds that income disparities and gender play critical roles in school attendance. Girls face compounded barriers due to financial constraints, cultural norms, and safety concerns. Major obstacles include school costs, distance, inadequate infrastructure, lack of toilets, and mismatch between home and instructional languages. Delayed funds, shortage of trained teachers, and low parental awareness further exacerbate exclusion. Dropouts often end up in low-wage work or family enterprises, reinforcing intergenerational poverty. Despite challenges, strong community support for education exists. Policy recommendations include direct cash transfers, mother tongue instruction, improved infrastructure, gender-sensitive programs, and digital inclusion, alongside systemic reforms to reduce bureaucratic hurdles and ensure secure funding. A holistic approach is essential to foster equitable educational opportunities for rural children.

title: Memory Bank Compression for Continual Adaptation of Large Language Models
authors: Thomas Katraouras, Dimitrios Rafailidis

title: FusionGuard-E2P: A Deviation-Guided and Imbalance-Aware Multimodal Framework for Insider Threat Detection
authors: Rubina Akter, Anik Islam, Hadis Karimipour, Fattane Zarrinkalam, Benjamin Tan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Insider threat detection (ITD) remains a significant challenge in organizational cybersecurity. This is due to subtle behavioral patterns, pronounced class imbalance, and the need to integrate heterogeneous data sources. Traditional methods, relying on audit logs and rule-based heuristics, often fail to capture latent behavioral intent and complex feature interactions. To address these limitations, this article introduces FusionGuard-E2P, a lightweight multimodal deep learning (DL) framework. It integrates textual embeddings with structured behavioral and psychometric features for robust ITD. The proposed architecture uses a convolutional feature interaction module to process fixed-length text embeddings. It combines these with tabular features via a multilayer perceptron (MLP), avoiding inappropriate sequential modeling of tabular data while effectively capturing nonlinear feature interactions. To further mitigate class imbalance, a deviation-aware generative adversarial network (GAN)-based augmentation strategy is introduced to enhance minority-class representation. Extensive experiments on the CERT 4.2 and CERT 5.2 datasets show that this framework achieves competitive performance in both binary and multiclass settings. It maintains strong generalization and computational efficiency. These findings highlight the effectiveness of combining multimodal feature learning with imbalance-aware optimization for practical ITD.

title: Quantum Machine Learning for IoT: Trends, Challenges, and Technology Readiness
authors: Chirag Sharma, Kashvi Sood, Sandeep Kumar Sood
abstract: Quantum Machine Learning (QML) merges Quantum Computing (QC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to solve problems that are intractable by classical computers. At the same time, domains such as finance, healthcare, manufacturing, and transportation domains are transitioning into Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled cyber physical systems generating streams of heterogeneous data with high dimensionality and volume. This work presents a unified scientometric and qualitative review of QML across these sectors. A PRISMA-based screening is followed by a Keyword Co-occurrence Cluster Analysis (KCCA), a Keyword Burst Analysis (KBA), and a Citation Burst Analysis (CBA) using CiteSpace on a Scopus-derived corpus of literature. An evidence-based Technology Readiness Level (TRL) framework is introduced to assess the technology’s maturity. Our findings indicate that the volume of publications has increased after 2022, with healthcare dominating the volume and manufacturing the impact. Furthermore, a clear trend towards the development of hybrid quantum classical architectures is present across all domains. Security applications such as anomaly detection are the most developed QML entry point to IoT systems. All four domains are at a TRL of 3-4, indicating that QML research is still at the concept-validation stage. Challenges include hardware noise, barren plateaus, absence of standardized benchmarks, and encoding heterogeneous data from IoT devices for training QML applications.

title: HMFM: A Robust Hierarchical Multimodal Fusion Module to Enhance Map Learning With Additional Information_supp1-3698777.pdf
authors: Shitao Chen
abstract: High-definition (HD) maps, rich in geometric and semantic information, are essential for autonomous driving systems. Recent online map learning methods, predicting maps directly from onboard sensor data, offer promising alternatives to traditional manual approaches. However, they are limited in long-range detection and complex environments due to onboard sensors’ inherent constraints. While some works incorporate external priors, they often lack generality due to tight coupling with specific architectures and poor robustness to imperfect data. To address these gaps, we propose the Hierarchical Multimodal Fusion Module (HMFM), a robust, generalizable plug-and-play module that fuses onboard data with external information at two levels. At the feature level, the Masked Attention Module (MAM) selectively enhances features to boost fusion efficiency and minimize interference. At the Bird’s Eye View (BEV) level, the Alignment Module (AM) resolves spatial discrepancies; then, the Unified Attention Module (UAM) adaptively weighs external priors to effectively mitigate inaccuracies from imperfect data. We also introduce a distillation framework to distill benefits from additional information into the model without affecting runtime efficiency. Extensive experiments validate HMFM’s generality, leveraging satellite and SD maps for significant gains across baselines (e.g., +22.0 mIoU on HDMapNet, +12.3 mAP on MapTR). Furthermore, the distillation framework boosts the original HDMapNet by 2.8 mIoU without affecting runtime efficiency. The code and data processing pipeline for this work are publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/xjtu-cs-gao/HMFM</uri>

title: Improving Convergence for Semi-Federated Learning: An Energy-Efficient Approach by Manipulating Over-the-Air Distortion
authors: Jingheng Zheng, Hui Tian, Wanli Ni, Yang Tian, Ping Zhang
abstract: In this paper, we propose a hybrid learning framework that combines federated and split learning, termed semi-federated learning (SemiFL), in which over-the-air computation is utilized for gradient aggregation. A key idea is to strategically adjust the learning rate by manipulating over-the-air distortion for improving SemiFL’s convergence. Specifically, we intentionally amplify amplitude distortion to increase the learning rate in the non-stable region, thereby accelerating convergence and reducing communication energy consumption. In the stable region, we suppress noise perturbation to maintain a small learning rate for improving SemiFL’s final convergence. Theoretical results demonstrate the antagonistic effects of over-the-air distortion in different regions, under both independent and identically distributed (IID) and non-IID data settings. Then, we formulate two energy consumption minimization problems, one for each region, which implements a two-region mean square error threshold configuration scheme. Accordingly, we propose two resource allocation algorithms with closed-form solutions. Simulation results show that under different network and data distribution conditions, strategically manipulating over-the-air distortion can efficiently adjust the learning rate to improve SemiFL’s convergence. Moreover, energy consumption can be reduced by using the proposed algorithms.

title: A guided residual search for nonlinear state-space identification
authors: Merijn Floren, Jan Swevers
abstract: Identifying the parameters of nonlinear state-space models from input-output data typically requires solving a highly non-convex optimization problem, which is prone to slow convergence and suboptimal local solutions. This work improves the reliability and efficiency of the estimation process by decomposing the overall optimization problem into a sequence of tractable subproblems. Starting from a linear baseline model, nonlinear residual dynamics are first estimated using a guided residual search (GRS) and subsequently refined through multiple-shooting optimization. Experiments on two benchmarks show competitive performance with state-of-the-art black-box methods and improved convergence over naive initialization.

title: Adaptive Robust Predictive Direct Power Control With NN Compensation for 3L-AFE Rectifiers
authors: Zeyu Zhang, Jien Ma, Xing Liu, Chenghao Liu, Lin Qiu, Youtong Fang
abstract: Model predictive direct power control for the active front-end rectifier faces degraded control performance under parameter mismatches. To improve robustness to model uncertainties, this article proposes an adaptive robust predictive direct power control method. Specifically, the proposed design integrates the fitting capability of a super-twisting observer for unknown nonlinear dynamics to estimate aggregate disturbances, and simultaneously proposes a fast gradient method operating within the orthogonal complement of the state vector to update the input gain in real time, achieving the overall effect of the control process operating with adaptive capability and robustness against unknown system variations and model uncertainties. Furthermore, the developed solution takes actuator output deviations into account and uses a two-layer neural network for approximation, which corrects the real reference input. Moreover, by constructing a Lyapunov function, it is proved that the proposed control scheme ensures that the system error remains uniformly ultimately bounded. Finally, simulation and experimental results validate the findings.

title: Secure Federated Data Augmentation Empowered Efficient THz Semantic Communication for Agentic AI Collaborative Networking
authors: Zihong Li, Jinhao Zhou, Qianqian Pan, Jun Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Agentic artificial intelligence (AI) collaborative networking and secure federated learning are emerging as critical enablers for unmanned swarms with the advent of 6G and the proliferation of AI-enabled IoT devices, where autonomous driving and cooperative tasks require fast, reliable, and energy-efficient communication and learning. However, massive multimodal sensor data, the limited bandwidth of conventional mmWave V2X links, heterogeneous local datasets, privacy concerns over sensitive observations, and the strict energy budgets of UAVs, UUVs, and small robots create major bottlenecks for traditional federated learning. To address these challenges, we propose a cross-layer network framework integrating 6G THz-enabled efficient semantic communication with secure federated data augmentation for agentic AI collaboration. Semantic compression over short-range THz links reduces per-round transmitted payloads by extracting only task-relevant features, while semantic prototype-based data augmentation accelerates FL convergence by enriching non-IID local datasets without additional communication overhead; secure aggregation (SecAgg) further ensures that individual model updates are never exposed to the aggregation server. This co-design simultaneously reduces per-round transmission cost and the total number of rounds required for convergence, yielding a compound reduction in cumulative communication energy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system significantly outperforms a conventional mmWave-based federated learning baseline in transmission latency, tail-latency reliability, learning convergence speed, global model accuracy, and cumulative communication energy, validating its effectiveness for energy-efficient and privacy-preserving agentic AI collaboration.

title: Effects of the oxygen content of a-IGZO field-effect transistors on their density of states and dopant concentration lateral profile regarding oxygen scavenging
authors: SEong Hoon Jeon, Wonjung Kim, Seungki Kim, Seung Joo Myoung, Dong Hyeop Shin, Dong Myong Kim, Sung-Jin Choi, Yoon Jung Lee, Changwook Kim, Dae Hwan Kim
abstract: The oxygen flow rate (OFR) during sputtering deposition of amorphous indium–gallium–zinc–oxide (a-IGZO) was systematically adjusted to modulate the oxygen vacancy (VO) concentration in the channel, yielding O–poor, O–mid, and O–rich FETs. Under identical source/drain metal and annealing conditions, a pronounced oxygen scavenging (OS) effect was observed at lower OFRs, resulting in significant source/drain contact resistance (RSD) reduction due to the lowered VO formation energy. However, while reduced oxygen content effectively suppressed RSD, it simultaneously induced undesirable threshold voltage (VT) shifts caused by OS-driven carrier diffusion from the source/drain regions into the channel. Consequently, a clear trade-off between RSD reduction and VT stability was identified, wherein lower OFR enhanced RSD suppression at the expense of VT controllability. In contrast, higher OFR stabilized VT with limited RSD improvement. An optimal OFR was therefore proposed by decoupling the OS contributions to RSD and VT. In addition, the VT shift increased with decreasing channel length, and this tendency was more evident at lower OFRs.

title: Lightweight Multi-Modal Transformer for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting with Sky Imagery and Meteorological Data
authors: Peng Gao, Ying Li, Wenzheng Song
abstract: The increasing penetration of photovoltaic generation systems into modern power grids necessitates accurate short-term power forecasting to ensure reliable grid operations and optimal energy management. Traditional forecasting approaches relying solely on meteorological measurements often fail to capture rapid atmospheric changes that critically affect solar irradiance. This paper proposes a lightweight multi-modal transformer architecture that integrates sky imagery with meteorological time-series data through cross-attention fusion mechanisms for enhanced photovoltaic power forecasting. The framework employs parallel convolutional and transformer encoders to extract complementary spatial and temporal features from sky images and weather measurements, enabling the model to capture both gradual meteorological trends and abrupt cloud-induced irradiance fluctuations. Experimental validation on a real-world dataset, averaged over five independent random seeds, demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves a normalized mean absolute error of 0.0999 ± 0.0037 and a weighted mean absolute percentage error of 11.81 ± 0.44%, corresponding to a 16.4% improvement over the best unimodal baseline. The cross-attention mechanism enables weather-adaptive visual feature selection, with recurring high-response bands on the visual time axis indicating that the model concentrates on a limited subset of historical sky observations rather than weighting all visual tokens uniformly. The lightweight architecture with only 32×32 pixel image resolution achieves superior performance compared to higher resolutions while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for operational deployment in photovoltaic forecasting systems.

title: Wavelet-Based Bayesian Random Forest Classifier for High-Voltage Insulator Pollution Severity Detection: A Hybrid Approach
authors: Zakaria Trachi, Abdelouahab Mekhaldi, Abderrahmane Beroual
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The reliability of high-voltage insulators in power transmission systems is often compromised by pollution-induced flashovers, especially in coastal and industrial regions. Traditional methods for assessing contamination levels are labor-intensive and prone to inaccuracies, requiring automated solutions. This study proposes a Wavelet-Based Bayesian-Optimized Random Forest Classification (WB-BRFC) method for classifying pollution severity on high-voltage insulators. The approach integrates wavelet decomposition for feature extraction with Bayesian-optimized random forest classifiers to enhance accuracy. Wavelet transforms enable multi-resolution analysis of leakage current signals, isolating transient and steady-state features indicative of contamination. Bayesian optimization systematically tunes hyper-parameters to maximize classification performance. Experimental validation using real-world data evidences that WB-BRFC achieves superior accuracy (0.9825), precision (0.9821), recall (0.9880), and F1-score (0.9812) compared to conventional methods including SVM (Support Vector Machines), Fuzzy Granular, and wavelet-based 1D-CNN. This hybrid approach leverages wavelet-based multi-resolution feature extraction, Bayesian hyper-parameter optimization, and ensemble classification, enabling unprecedented robustness for high-voltage insulator pollution assessment.

title: Semantic Region-Guided Transferable Attacks on Vision-Language Pretraining Models_supp1-3701225.pdf
authors: Chen Wan
abstract: Vision-language pre-trained (VLP) models have achieved strong performance on multimodal tasks, but they remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks. In black-box settings, transferability is often limited because perturbation generation relies on surrogate-specific saliency cues that generalize poorly across VLP architectures. In this letter, we propose Semantic Region-Guided Attack (SRGA), a transferable attack framework that replaces such cues with detector-derived semantic regions to provide more consistent cross-model guidance. Based on these regions, SRGA performs coordinated perturbation generation in both image and text modalities with lightweight regional transformations and spatially weighted optimization. Experiments on Flickr30 K and MSCOCO show that SRGA achieves competitive black-box transferability across diverse VLP architectures under multiple settings, with modest additional cost.

title: Single Platform, Dual Readout: Comparing EIS and EGFET Modes for Quercetin Sensing
authors: Giulia Elli, Ahmed Rasheed, Shamim Torkian, Sahira Vasquez, Paolo Lugli, Luisa Petti, Pietro Ibba
abstract: Quercetin, a polyphenol found in fruits and vegetables, is well known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and therapeutic properties, with potential roles in preventing cardiovascular, oncological, and neurodegenerative diseases. Its detection could be crucial in novel foods validation. However, its effective detection in complex food matrices remains a challenge due to limitations of conventional analytical methods, which are often labor-intensive and lack portability. In this work, we present a novel dual-mode biosensing approach integrating electrolyte-gated carbon nanotube field-effect transistors (EG-CNTFETs) with electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) for the label-free detection of quercetin, employing Horseradish peroxidase (Hrp) as biorecognition element to enhance specificity. EG-CNTFETs enabled real-time monitoring of the sensor response, showing a decrease in drain-source current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf>) upon quercetin addition, with a sensitivity of −13.6%/mM in the 0.1–2mM range. Complementary EIS measurements confirmed the detection of quercetin via a frequency-specific impedance drop at 1 kHz, yielding a sensitivity of −15.7Ω/mM. This synergistic approach not only improves detection accuracy but also offers a deeper understanding of the interfacial processes during analyte recognition, paving the way for robust, portable sensing systems for phenolic compounds in food and biomedical applications.

title: Energy-Efficient Complexity-Aware Scheduling in Cloud-Edge Computing
authors: Thilagavathy Sakthivel, Rajasekar Murugesan, Anitha Gopalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Cloud-edge computing has become a revolutionary paradigm to assist the design of latency-dense and computationintensive applications like Internet of Things (IoT), smart healthcare, autonomous systems, and real-time analytics. Nevertheless, methods of managing workloads, energy efficiency, and computational complexity, as well as the heterogeneity of both cloud and edge resources, create a considerable problem in the task scheduling. The current paper suggests an Energy-Efficient Complexity-Aware Scheduling (EECAS) system in cloud-edge scenarios, which is used to combine forces to minimize energy consumption/ usage, execution time and computational overhead. The suggested model describes the tasks according to the complexity of calculation, time constraints, and the resources used and allocates dynamically to the edge or cloud node with the help of a hybrid optimization strategy. Complexity-sensitive decision engine reduces the redundant offloading and optimizes the distributed resources. The structure is a combination of adaptive workload balancing, energy-aware resource scheduling, and latency-sensitive scheduling to increase overall system performance. The simulation outcomes have shown that the suggested EECAS method contributes to a substantial decrease in total energy consumption and scheduling overhead with the enhancement of the rate of task completion and system throughput in comparison with the traditional methods of scheduling. The model offers a next-generation sustainable and scalable approach to cloud-edge infrastructures to support the emerging real-time and AI-driven workloads.

title: Green accounting and firm performance: PRISMA review
authors: Huu Anh Nguyen, Thi Kim Ngoc Nguyen, Thi Cam Van Nguyen, Phan Minh Hoa Nguyen
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: The growing global emphasis on sustainability and corporate accountability has positioned green accounting as a crucial mechanism for integrating environmental considerations into business operations. This study aims to systematically synthesize empirical evidence on the relationship between green accounting and firm performance using the PRISMA 2020 framework combined with bibliometric analysis. Data were collected from two major academic databases, Scopus and Web of Science, covering publications from 1999 to 2025. A total of 407 peer-reviewed studies were selected through a multistage screening process following the PRISMA protocol. The bibliometric analysis, conducted using the Bibliometrix R-package and VOSviewer, reveals a strong global interest in this research area, with Asia - particularly China - emerging as the leading contributor. Core publication sources include Journal of Cleaner Production, Business Strategy and the Environment, and Sustainability. The findings indicate that green accounting plays a significant role in linking financial accountability with environmental stewardship, helping firms achieve operational efficiency, cost savings, and improved stakeholder trust. While most studies confirm a positive association between green accounting and firm performance, results varied across industries, regions, and regulatory contexts, suggesting the influence of mediating factors such as governance quality, environmental regulation, and stakeholder engagement. This review contributes to the academic discourse by mapping the intellectual structure of the field and identifying emerging themes such as ESG integration, carbon disclosure, and sustainable finance. The study underscores that green accounting should be understood not merely as a reporting instrument but as a strategic management framework for achieving sustainable competitiveness. The findings provide valuable implications for scholars, policymakers, and practitioners seeking to strengthen the link between environmental responsibility and long-term corporate performance.

title: A novel heterogeneous limit analysis framework for the collapse assessment of curved masonry constructions under different loading scenarios
authors: Mohammad Pourfouladi, Natalia Pingaro, Gabriele Milani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative computational framework for the collapse assessment of masonry constructions, which seamlessly integrates advanced parametric modeling with Upper Bound Limit Analysis (UBLA). The methodology is built upon the PoliBrick plugin, implemented within the Rhinoceros/Grasshopper environment, which enables the automatic generation of three-dimensional brick assemblages that accurately represent the stereotomy of complex geometries, including arches, vaults, and walls. This geometric output is directly interfaced with a home-made heterogeneous Upper Bound Limit Analysis (UBLA) code implemented in MATLAB. The code models masonry blocks as infinitely resistance units. All nonlinearities are concentrated at the interfaces. These contact surfaces are governed by a rigid-perfectly plastic Mohr-Coulomb criterion. The versatility and accuracy of the proposed framework are demonstrated through its application to two distinct loading scenarios and structural typologies. First, it is validated against an in-situ tested masonry arch subjected to a vertical concentrated load, showing excellent agreement with experimental collapse loads. Second, its efficacy for seismic assessment is proven by analyzing a cross-vault, where the critical influence of stereotomy is systematically investigated across four different brickwork patterns. The results quantify the profound impact of brick disposition on the structural capacity, revealing performance variations of up to 84% in the collapse multiplier. The proposed framework thus provides practitioners with a practical, efficient, and accurate tool for the rapid prediction of collapse mechanisms in a wide range of masonry constructions under diverse loading conditions.

title: VulnCloud Scanner: Lightweight Multiengine Scanning for Container Security
authors: Divas A S, Barath S, Geethanjali D, Umasankari N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Container security has become a major issue for organizations adopting cloud-native architectures. Existing scanner reports lack exploit context, deployment-specific applicability and knowledge of whether the vulnerable function is ever executed. This leaves security teams overwhelmed by alerts that are theoretically flagged but practically irrelevant. This paper presents VulnCloud, an open-source Kubernetesnative framework that turns inconsistent scanner results into actionable and deployment-aware risk signals. The VulnCloud orchestrates many scanning engines through a confidenceweighted consensus model. Enriches findings with exploitlikelihood and threat intel, context-aware applicability filtering, and validates exploitability through hybrid static call-graph and runtime reachability analysis. An admission controller enforces the composite risk score through policy-as-code to block images. On evaluation over 100 diverse container images, VulnCloud achieves a decision accuracy of 96.8 %, a precision of 99.7 %, and a false-positive rate of 0.5 %. This offers security teams a fully auditable, CI/CD native pipeline that is low-noise and highassurance.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Multiband Fractal Antenna Based on Koch Curve Structure
authors: Sakthisudhan Karuppanan, Nandhini N, Prakash N, Vidusha S P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: A triangle fractal antenna was proposed in this research article. It was designed, simulated and fabricated for application in wireless communication. The design of antenna concept is based on a fractal geometry, specifically the subsequent iteration Koch fractal patch implemented on an FR-4 substrate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=4.4$</tex>, thickness = 1.6 mm) with substrate dimension of 70 × 60 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, partial ground plane dimension of 25 × 60 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. When the geometric coordinates of an electrical length of patch surface increases (iterations) without affecting the overall dimensions, this design enables compatibility. The proposed fractal coordinates were simulated in CST Microwave Studio 2025. Based on the simulation results, there was a resonance frequency of 2.031 GHz with a fractional bandwidth of 44.79%, which indicates wideband characteristics. The fabricated prototype shows multiband performance at 1.03 GHz, 1.55 GHz, 1.84 GHz and 2.47 GHz respectively. Among the resonances at 1.84 GHz and 2.47 GHz the demonstration satisfied impedance matching performance with return losses of -41.9 dB and -41.2 dB. Moreover, these VSWR has satisfied the transmission line characteristics. Simulated versus measured results are in reasonable agreement indicating the proposed fractal antenna design is effective. The results indicate that the achieved antenna has good wideband and narrowband Characteristics, which is useful for modern-day wireless applications.

title: Multimodal Model Based on Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining for Micro-Expression Recognition
authors: Peng Yang, Yanyang Zhou, Qilin Wei, Zhifeng Zeng
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Micro-expressions, fleeting facial expressions lasting 1/25 to 1/5 s, are characterized by low intensity and involuntary occurrence, making them critical in applications such as psychological evaluation and forensic lie detection. This study proposes MECLIP, a dual-modal micro-expression recognition framework built on Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (CLIP). The MECLIP introduces three key innovations. (1) Visual stream: The original CLIP image encoder was reformulated as a temporal transformer equipped with a hierarchical spatiotemporal feature extractor that employs multi-scale spatiotemporal attention to jointly capture local muscle movements and global expression dynamics. (2) Text stream: Large language models (e.g., ChatGPT) generate fine-grained physiological behavior descriptions to replace traditional categorical labels. Learnable, context-aware prompt tokens further tighten visual-text semantic alignments through contrastive learning. (3) Multimodal fusion: A learnable cross-modal fusion module combines visual and textual representations, enabling the model to jointly reason about facial muscle dynamics and their semantic interpretations throughout micro-expression evolution. On the CAS(ME)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> dataset, MECLIP attained an unweighted average recall of 39.6% and an unweighted F1 of 40.0%, outperforming the previous best baseline by 3.8% and 5.9% in relative terms. Given that micro-expression recognition is an intrinsically difficult task where accuracy remains limited even under controlled conditions, the consistent gains of the proposed multimodal approach highlight the potential of language-augmented contrastive learning for micro-expression analysis.

title: AI Powered: Gamification Strategy for Dyslexic Children
authors: Harshith Leburu, Kishore Kumar, Senduru Srinivasulu, Suganthi. L, Jeberson Ratna raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Online Resource for Child Education Games that Help Children with Dyslexia Learn. Developed and delivered by a series of tests that evaluate the mental capabilities of children with dyslexia; these tests identify both strengths and weaknesses; the results of these tests enable us to create customized games for children that improve their reading skills, memory and attention span. All games are designed to maximize the participants' involvement in the process through an entertaining, engaging and cognitive approach; our continuous support through guiding children allows us to help them develop their strengths to the fullest potential. The reinforcement and progress tracking are interactive and provide insights which are helpful for both children and parents, showing the areas of growth and the ones that need assistance. Furthermore, its online accessibility makes it easy to reach from any device, thus allowing flexible and convenient learning. The project which integrates adaptive assessments, customized games, and real-time monitoring develops an engaging and supportive environment that not only enhances skill acquisition but also boosts self-esteem, thereby providing a fun and effective alternative to the old-fashioned methods of learning for dyslexic students.

title: Improving Skin Cancer Detection Using Deep Learning Methods
authors: Aisha Singh, Moresh M. Mukhedkar, Vivek P. Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Skin cancer is a worldwide health issue because of its relatively common and onerous histopathology unless diagnosed early. Recognizing malignant skin lesions in a timely manner is essential for increasing survival rates and minimizing the complexity of treatment. In this paper, a deep learning-enabled system is proposed for automatic identification of several types of skin cancer from dermatoscopic images. The proposed framework aims to recognize seven types of lesions: Actinic Keratoses and Intraepithelial Carcinoma (akiec), basal cell carcinoma (bcc), benign keratosis-like lesions (bkl), dermatofibroma (df), melanoma (mel), melanocytic nevi (nv) and vascular lesions(vasc). CNNs are chosen, as they have demonstrated the ability to learn discriminative features from image itself, without employing any hand-crafted feature designs. The model is trained on the publicly available HAM10000 dataset. Additionally, a comparative perspective between classical machine learning approaches and deep learning models is considered. The proposed system demonstrates strong potential for real-world clinical deployment and scalable diagnostic solutions. The experimental results show that the proposed method achieves satisfactory performance with a training accuracy of 96.00% and validation accuracy of 97.00%. These results demonstrated that the designed deep learning model has a potential to be used as an assistive tool for early detection of skin cancer and also decision making by dermatologists. In addition, this study also demonstrates the importance of deep learning methods in medical image analysis and helps to promote the application of public datasets like HAM10000 in facilitating future research on automatic dermatological diagnosis.

title: Enhancing Design Control Compliance in Medical Devices Leveraging AI, Blockchain and Real-Time Monitoring
authors: Ranga Swamy Sirisati, Kotari Sridevi, Kanusu Srinivasa Rao, Ratna Kumari Challa, Pradeep Venuthurumilli, Swapna Kasarla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Design controls are a requirement mandated to optimize the safety, performance, and regulatory compliance of medical devices, especially part 820.30 of the FDA. However, despite the critical nature of these controls, repeated FDA 483 observations can be traced back to substantial margin of discrepancy in implementing these requirements, which result in recalls, compliance exposure and delay in time-to-market. Recent works of their functions and FDA 483 observations compare any gaps in the early stages (before and during the initiative implementation and late-stage control and support design elements; and finally. To demonstrate how the method/analysis can be used to improve medical device development practices. Through an examination of historical data trends and common design non-compliances, this survey presents implementable best practices and information to manufacturers to help them reduce design control inadequacies. The rationale behind the findings is to strengthen product quality, increase the respect of regulation, and achieve better results for the manufacturer and the patient. This paper will also cover how to leverage new approaches and technologies that can be implemented to overcome these challenges, specifically related to empowering design control systems with the use of automation, data analytics, and real-time compliance monitoring.

title: Biologically-Inspired Networking: Exploring Nature-Inspired Algorithms in Wireless Sensor Network Design
authors: Narender Chinthamu, Vipashi Kansal, Krishna Kant Dixit, Anurag Shrivastava, Arun Pratap Srivastava, Akhil Sankhyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper explores the application of biologically inspired algorithms in the design and optimization of Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). By drawing parallels between biological systems and technical challenges, this study delves into how principles of swarm intelligence, genetic algorithms, and other nature-inspired approaches can enhance WSN performance. These algorithms are particularly suited to address issues such as energy efficiency, robust network architecture, and adaptive communication protocols in dynamic environments. The paper provides a thorough examination of various biologically inspired algorithms, their implementation in WSNs, and evaluates their effectiveness through simulation and real-world testing. The findings suggest significant improvements in WSNs’ adaptability, efficiency, and scalability, promoting a shift towards more resilient and autonomous network infrastructures.

title: Forecasting Analytics for Lean Supply Chain Management: A Scoping Review
authors: Rostyslav Pietukhov, Mujthaba Ahtamad
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Lean manufacturing focuses on waste reduction and operational flow but increasingly requires predictive analytics to navigate volatility and uncertainty in data-driven industries. Integrating forecasting models into lean frameworks offers businesses a pathway to improve decision-making, enhance responsiveness, and sustain competitive advantage. This scoping review examines how forecasting integrates within lean supply chain systems, with emphasis on models used and delivered value. Using PRISMA-ScR guidelines, 10 studies (2009–2024) were analysed across diverse industries and regions, indicating narrow but focused scope. Three key themes emerged: (1) forecasting acts as a strategic enabler of lean, supporting proactive planning, waste minimisation, and Just-in-Time operations; (2) classical statistical models (e.g., ARIMA, Holt-Winters) remain prevalent despite advances in AI and Machine Learning, reflecting a pragmatic preference for simplicity; and (3) integration challenges, such as data quality issues and proprietary software reliance, highlight the need for scalable, context-aware analytics solutions. This study clarifies forecasting’s role as a lean enabler and provides practical value by guiding practitioners in aligning forecasting approaches with lean frameworks to improve operational performance. Future research should focus on developing data-driven integration frameworks, assessing forecasting model trade-offs and success factors, and exploring how IoT and agentic AI could enable real-time, autonomous lean supply chains in data-rich environments.

title: An Overview of Analyst Forecast Research: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Phan Minh Hoa Nguyen, Thi Kim Ngoc Nguyen, Hoang Tu Nhi Truong
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: This paper aims to provide a comprehensive overview of research on analyst forecasts by applying bibliometric techniques to identify influential contributors, collaboration patterns, and key research themes. A dataset of 1,995 documents published between 1975 and July 2025 was extracted from Scopus using the keywords "analyst forecast" and "analyst prediction". Bibliometric analysis was performed with VOSviewer and Biblioshiny to examine publication trends, influential authors, journals, and countries, as well as co-authorship networks, keyword co-occurrence, and citation structures. The results show a steady growth of publications, with Accounting Review and Review of Accounting Studies being the most influential journals. Gerald J. Lobo and Lawrence D. Brown emerge as leading authors, while the United States plays a dominant role in international collaboration. Keyword analysis highlights themes of forecast accuracy, market efficiency, behavioral biases, and corporate governance, with recent attention to uncertainty and asset pricing. Highly cited works form the intellectual foundation of this field. The study provides valuable insights for academics by highlighting key contributors, thematic clusters, and emerging trends, thereby guiding future research directions.

title: Research on Energy Optimal Path Planning Algorithm for UUV Considering Dynamic Constraints
authors: Meng You, Zhanfeng Ying, Hao Ge, Dingcheng Peng
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (UUVs) play an increasingly important role in marine resource exploration, underwater detection, and other marine applications. However, existing path planning algorithms generally lack systematic consideration of the coupling effects between ocean currents and UUV dynamics. To address this issue, this paper proposes an energy-optimal path planning method for UUVs in three-dimensional (3D) ocean current environments, which integrates dynamic constraints and realistic thruster characteristics. First, a UUV dynamic model is established with the influence of relative current velocity taken into account, enabling the dynamic evaluation of path feasibility. Second, a power characteristic model is constructed based on the actual performance curve of the propulsion motor, which converts dynamic thrust requirements into an accurate energy consumption calculation model. On this basis, an improved path planning algorithm suitable for 3D complex flow fields is developed. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm increases the ocean current utilization rate by 56.74% and reduces total energy consumption by 25.56% while ensuring path dynamic feasibility.

title: IoT Based Smart Cart for Automated Billing in Supermarkets
authors: Sumit Srivastava, Sushanta Mahanty, Krishna Kant Singh, Amit Kumar Das, Shraddha Shreyanshu, Akash Sharma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The challenges that modern retail stores are continually experiencing include queues in the billing counters, heavy dependence on manpower, and increasing cases of shoplifting and billing fraud. To address these constraints, this study presents a prototype of the IoT-based smart cart that would be used to automatize billing, improve the efficiency of shopping, and decrease labour expenses. The smart cart includes a unique QR code. When scanned shopping session is initiated that links the cart to the user's profile. Products are added to the cart by scanning their barcodes through the application. To validate and provide security, weight and ultrasonic sensors, as well as ESP32-CAM module, are offered to the cart to scan the cart with an integrated camera to run a YOLO-based object detection algorithm to detect unscanned or fraudulent entries. Products added by the customer is instantly reflected on the web app and if any mismatch is found then store manager is alerted. The customer then goes to a special checkout point where he or she pays and leaves the store. The system ensures secure and customer friendly shopping with instant billing updates, fraud detection, and reduces labour needs.

title: Improving Brand Interaction by Integrating AI into Marketing Communication
authors: Vasudha Jaggula, Gitanjali Shrivastava, Naim Shaikh, Vivek Veeraiah, Ankur Gupta, Arpit Namdev
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the present digital age, AI is changing way research helps customers. These are some of biggest changes in how people interact with brands. Research examines utilization of AI in marketing communication, focusing on chatbots, and recommendation systems, with delivering data-driven, customized, and efficient consumer experiences. Research performs literature analysis to analyze and compare traditional advertising with AI-driven alternatives. Researach shows that AI helps organizations build stronger relationships with their customers, that lead to more engagement, and trust. AI can be helpful in more ways than merely providing customer support, according to the research. As a result of making it simpler for people to communicate with one another in real time and by targeting clients based on the products they are most likely to purchase, it also assists individuals in getting to know one another better. The essay teaches us that it is important to establish a strategy for dealing with any potential issues that may arise during the installation of artificial intelligence. Because it provides the marketing industry with new communication methods that are scalable and centered on the client, this initiative provides the marketing sector with both theoretical and practical benefits.

title: Learning with Machines: What Shapes the Use of ChatGPT in Universities?
authors: Delenvya Savinka Frensya, Razib Chandra Chanda, Miao Ling Zhang, Priscilia Calista Kowirya, Anderes Gui, Suryanto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The rapid advancement of technology has empowered classroom instructors to evolve into tech-savvy educators. Among recent innovations, Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) has emerged as a transformative force with the potential to reshape teaching and learning. However, despite its promising capabilities, students’ intention to use GenAI in higher education remains relatively low. This study aims to examine the key factors influencing students’ intention to adopt GenAI in academic settings. Focusing on five core variables, Perceived Usefulness, Perceived Trust, Perceived Risk, Moral Obligation, and Perceived Intelligence, the research investigates how these elements shape students’ behavioral intentions. A quantitative approach was employed, with data collected from 224 students via an online survey. Importantly, participants who did not previously use GenAI for educational purposes. The data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) to test the hypothesized relationships. The findings reveal that Moral Obligation, and Perceived Usefulness positively influence students’ intention to use GenAI, while Perceived Risk has a significant negative effect. In contrast, Perceived Trust and Social Influence were not found to have a significant impact on students’ intentions. These results suggest that students are more inclined to adopt GenAI when they perceive it as morally relevant, and useful, and when associated risks are minimal, rather than being influenced by trust or social endorsement. The study offers both theoretical and practical implications for stakeholders involved in the development and integration of AI-driven tools, such as chatbots, within higher education.

title: Real-Time UAV Swarm Autonomy Using Edge-Deployed Small Language Models
authors: Kruthik Narasimhamurthy, Prajwal Mahesha Sethurani, Aryan Anand, Prabha Sundaravadivel, H. Allen Torbert, Lakshman Tamil
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This work demonstrates an edge-enabled autonomous UAV swarm framework in which onboard intelligence enables real-time decision-making without reliance on cloud connectivity. The video demonstration showcases autonomous drone operation driven by a small language model (SLM) optimized for deployment across heterogeneous hardware platforms, including NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, edge boards, and companion laptops. The system highlights how lightweight language-based reasoning can be integrated directly into drone autonomy pipelines to support mission execution, coordination, and adaptive behavior. In the demonstrated setup, each UAV performs onboard inference to interpret mission context and execute autonomous actions while maintaining low latency and energy efficiency. Hardware-aware optimization allows the same SLM architecture to run consistently across embedded and non-embedded devices, enabling seamless transitions between onboard autonomy and ground-based supervision. The demonstration illustrates realtime responsiveness, stable execution, and decentralized intelligence, which are critical for scalable UAV swarm operations. By validating autonomous behavior through an end-to-end video demonstration, this work emphasizes the practicality of deploying language-driven reasoning models directly at the edge. The results highlight a viable pathway toward robust, scalable UAV swarm autonomy using embedded AI, supporting applications that require rapid decision-making in communication-limited environments.

title: Medicinal Plant Disease Analysis by Using CNN-based Approaches
authors: Yamini Singh, Sanjay Kumar Dubey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The fast evolution of artificial intelligence has greatly revolutionized the concept of identifying plant diseases especially by the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). In recent years, the change in research in this field has shifted to simple image-level classification of diseases into more complex hybrid learning models, real-time object detector models, architectures founded on attention and solutions at the system level. The current paper offers a systematic review of 30 peerreviewed articles published in 2022-2026 and it is dedicated to the gradual development of methods applied to the analysis of plant diseases and medicinal plants. The recent methodological advances studied in this paper comprise CNN-only models, transfer learning, hybrid models, object detection systems, attention mechanisms, and realizations at a scale of a system. Technique-wise distribution (pie chart), year-wise growth of publications (stacked bar graph) and task-evolution flow diagrams are used to perform analysis of a trend. The research review draws attention to the transition in the research priorities to deployable, real-time, and large-scale solutions of monitoring. The results can be seen as the synthesized view of the research tendencies and can be utilized as a framework of reference in further studies of AI-driven plant and medicinal disease analysis.

title: Earliest Failure Localization in Pipelines Via Quantum Search
authors: Alexander Fisher, Christophe Bobda
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
