Nevertheless, as 6G architectures evolve toward large-scale, distributed architectures, existing rule-driven, centralized paging mechanisms face severe challenges, including inefficiencies due to massive mobile-device access, incompatibility with distributed network architectures, and significant privacy concerns. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a privacy-preserving and personalized federated intelligent paging mechanism for 6G Networks (6G-PFIP). 6G-PFIP introduces a novel intelligent decision model to enhance paging efficiency for large-scale user equipment (UE). A personalized hierarchical federated paging architecture (HFPA) is further constructed to improve architectural compatibility and privacy by decomposing the centralized paging process into distributed layers. HFPA effectively addresses the challenge of Non-IID user data in 6G by designing region-level and local-level personalization and fairness-aware aggregation strategies. Furthermore, HFPA integrates local differential privacy (LDP) on demand to perturb shared model parameters, thereby mitigating the risk of privacy leakage. Experiments on a 6G Telecom Dataset show that 6G-PFIP achieves significant signaling resource savings (71.33%) and delay reductions (34.78%), while rigorous analysis confirms its privacy guarantees.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network-Based Fast Computation Method for Two-Dimensional Space Charge in Oil-Paper Insulation
authors: Shuo Jin, Guoping Nie, Yongjing Xiang, Lin Zhu, Xiaoxing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: To improve the computational efficiency of simulating space charge density in two-dimensional oil-paper insulation, this paper proposes a fast space charge calculation method based on Physics-Informed Neural Networks. By embedding the Poisson equation and the current continuity equation into the loss function in the form of residuals, this approach integrates physical laws with deep learning. A parameter sensitivity analysis is conducted to identify dominant factors, and a two-stage optimization strategy combining Adam and L-BFGS optimizers is employed to enhance fitting accuracy in regions with strong gradients. Under the optimal combination of hyperparameters, the predictions of the PINN model closely align with those from the Finite Element Method (FEM), achieving a space charge density RMSE of 0.1420 C/m³, MAE of 0.1063 C/m³, and an R² of 0.9992. The average relative error of the synthesized electric field is below 1%. Moreover, the model maintains high accuracy even outside the training sample range and in complex geometries such as U-shaped structures, demonstrating good generalization capability. This method significantly reduces computation time while maintaining accuracy, providing an efficient tool for space charge simulation and structural design in oil-paper insulation under DC voltage.

title: A Review of Deepfake Detection Techniques: Approaches, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Jasmeet Kaur, Shivali Chopra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Deepfake has its name made out of deep learning and fake, which mean synthetic images, videos, and audio synthesized by AI based on advanced architectures like generative adversarial networks (GANs). Such realistic forgeries create more and more challenges on how to differentiate between the authentic content as opposed to the media manipulation. Deepfakes are beneficial in the areas of entertainment, gaming, marketing, and education; yet, misinformation, identity theft, financial fraud, and harassment are the most serious dangers. This paper reviews the conventional feature-based approaches, machine learning approaches and deep learning approaches that use CNNs, RNNs, and transformer models. The important datasets that are used to train and test deepfake detectors are pointed out. Recent research efforts indicate that Vision Transformer (ViT)-based models have over 90% AUC on benchmark datasets such as DFDC, and this is a significant performance improvement. This review gives a summary of the detection methods, performance measurements, data sets, current issues, and research areas.

title: Green AI: Reviewing Strategies for Waste Sorting
authors: Ravneet Singh, Tanima Thakur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Automated waste classification solutions have become increasingly significant when it comes to the need to promoting sustainability. The proposed systematic literature review considers the works that utilize artificial intelligence driven learning methods to classify waste based on trash images. The review focuses on three main aspects: use of datasets, algorithm methods or models designed, and preprocessing methods used in the studied literature. The evaluation of the use of datasets indicates the dependence on the commonly used datasets with different augmentation methods. Transfer learning using pre-trained CNNs architectures is dominant in algorithmic approaches. The most used preprocessing technique consists of geometric transformations and data augmentation. The research also presents the key research gaps and future directions of the study to enhance the area of study towards stronger waste classification systems.

title:
Influence of CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Sampling Techniques on
                    <sup>14</sup>
                    C-Based Biogenic Carbon Quantification in Waste-to-Energy Emission
authors: Luigi Sabino, Mauro Rubino, Carolina Allocca, Anna Di Palma, Beatrice Papa, Lorenzo Rambaldo, Fabio Marzaioli
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The alteration of the carbon (C) cycle by fossil fuel burning has climatic implications, requiring accurate partitioning of the fluxes of fossil vs biogenic (=renewable) carbon in various applications (eg: Carbon in bioplastics, Carbon Dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) emitted by waste to energy plants).The most reliable and sensitive tool to do that is the usage of <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">14</sup>C combined to Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS) high precision measurements. The ASTM D7459 and UNI EN 13528 standards set the methods to quantify the CO2 emissions from waste-to-energy plants by using 14C analyses. One of the crucial points in these methods is the sampling techniques, that can be divided in two categories: "time integrated" sampling, using NaOH or KOH or soda lime (i.e. a mixture of CaO, NaOH and KOH) to trap CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> for 6-48 hours, and "instantaneous" sampling, using glass (flasks) or plastic (tedlar bags) canisters to trap flue gas for only a few tens of seconds. The methods accept both sampling techniques without discussing the potential differences that may arise when comparing sampling techniques of different durations.To address this concern, we have measured the <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">14</sup>C-CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emitted by a waste-to-energy plant (Acerra, southern Italy). Sampling campaigns has been performed monthly for c.a. 6 years (from 2019 to 2025), from 3 gas emission lines using plastic Tedlar bags and glass flasks, or NaOH and soda lime, respectively, for continuous or instantaneous sampling. The <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">14</sup>C content has been measured by AMS.Preliminary results showed that the percentage of biogenic C ranged from 43 to 53 % (49 % on average), with an uncertainty associated with the sample preparation procedure (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> extraction and analysis) of 0.3 %. No significant differences were found among the 3 emission lines in terms of <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">14</sup>C inferred biogenic fraction suggesting homogeneity in the flue gas production by the plant. An increase in the biogenic fraction in gaseous samples taken during 2023 was observed when sampling with soda lime. Work is in progress to investigate the influence of the sampling technique on the measured fraction of biogenic vs fossil Carbon.

title: Enhancing Airport Security and User Experience through Integrated Facial Recognition and Emotion Analysis
authors: Chinmai Aditya Baddepuram, Zainb Dawod, Mhd Saeed Sharif
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This paper focuses on the application of facial recognition technologies to analyze passengers’ emotions during airport travel. Although the application of facial recognition for emotion analysis enhances the speed and accuracy of security screening, it also improves the overall passenger experience at airports. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), a specialized deep learning model designed for image recognition and processing, is utilized to detect passengers’ reactions during airport procedures such as check-in and security screening. The facial emotion detection model was trained for 50 epochs, demonstrating progressive improvement in accuracy. The final training accuracy reached 61.49%, while the final validation accuracy was 59.57%. The results indicate that integrating facial recognition with emotion analysis can enhance the passenger experience at airports.

title: A Framework for Mental Health Support Using Text Analysis and Screening Tools
authors: Vani Tiwari, Anishika Singh, Saru Dhir, Nitasha Hasteer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Mental health plays an important role in well-being and affects all spheres of life, both private as well as professional. However, social stigma and an insufficient availability of resources hinder many people from seeking help for their emotional health. This paper discusses the potential for digital supports to provide non-diagnostic and broadly accessible support for emotional well-being, particularly where traditional services may be difficult to access. The proposed research introduces a hybrid framework that combines methods from psychometric assessment, NLP, and ML to capture and analyse emotional states through natural linguistic communication inputs by the user. Using established mental health questionnaires such as PHQ-nine, GAD-seven, and PSS, and via sentiment analysis and emotion classification algorithms such as VADER, DistilBERT, and RoBERTa, the approach seeks to offer feedback on an individual basis. This reflective feedback raises self-awareness and recommends actionable tools for stress, anxiety, and other forms of emotional distress, aiming to promote early insight in a private, stigma-free manner.

title:
Robust ℋ
                    <sub>∞</sub>
                    Pitch Control for Wind Turbine via Average Dwell-Time Switched Systems Approach
authors: Fang Li, Pengyu Wen, Shujuan Mu, Jiahui Li, Hongli Dong
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper investigates the ℋ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> pitch control problem for wind turbine systems operating in the rated power region to mitigate output power fluctuations caused by wind speed variations. Traditional linear-parameter-varying (LPV) modeling approaches often exhibit significant conservatism when operating across wide wind-speed ranges. To overcome this limitation, a novel switched system modeling and control framework is proposed. Specifically, the nonlinear wind turbine model is linearized across wind-speed steady-state operating points, constructing a switched linear model that more accurately captures the abrupt changes in system dynamics. Consequently, a mode-dependent switched ℋ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> output-feedback controller, whose switching rule is scheduled by the real-time wind speed, is designed under the average dwell-time (ADT) switching scheme. Based on Lyapunov theory, sufficient conditions in the form of linear matrix inequalities (LMIs) are derived to guarantee the exponential stability of the closed-loop switched system while achieving a prescribed ℋ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> performance level. Finally, simulation results on a 5 MW onshore wind turbine (OWT) demonstrate that, compared with conventional non-switched LPV methods, the proposed switching control strategy effectively maintains the rated power and significantly reduces theoretical conservatism.

title: Significant Impact of Biomarker-Driven OCT Analysis for Improved Detection of Retinal Pathology with Visual Overlays
authors: Uday Arunrao Mande, Prashant Dhotre, Shafi Pathan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Purpose: Diabetes can lead to complications such as diabetic nephropathy, diabetic neuropathy, and diabetic macular edema (DME). Similar to DME, AMD-related vision loss can be prevented through early detection and prompt treatment. Optical coherence tomography (OCT) is the most widely used imaging technique in ophthalmology for the diagnosis these conditions. While OCT is effective for early screening, the increasing volume of OCT scans adds to the workload of ophthalmologists, who must interpret each image. Method: To address this challenge, automated diagnostic screening systems are being actively developed to reduce the burden on eye care professionals. Prolonged diabetes may lead to Macular Edema (DME), choroidal neovascularization (CNV), or DRUSEN. This causes the aberrant development of blood vessels from the choroid layer into the retina. DME, CNV can cause to vision loss. Early detection and correct treatment are crucial to addressing the issue of vision loss. Artificial intelligence techniques have been widely adopted in many studies to overcome these limitations. This paper contributes to development of textual reports that are helpful for ophthalmologists. Results and Conclusion: This review was developed for detect and analyze DME, DRUSEN, CNV using AI techniques over the last decade. In Addition, we attempted to convert the OCT findings into a textual report, That was assessed by an ophthalmologist. This system automates the process of analyzing OCT images with biomarkers and visual overlays, eliminating the need for manual interpretation by ophthalmologists. This significantly reduced the time required for diagnosis. The system processes the images and generates textual reports using deep learning methods.

title: Under Ground Power Cable Fault Detection Based on Hybrid SPF and TFDR Method Compared to SPF Method
authors: E. Kannan, K. Meenatchi, R. Sacithraa, A. Bharath, M. Ragul, P. Vishwayoghadharshini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: To design a hybrid SPF - TFDR system is the primary objective. It combines GPS besides IoT components to find faults in underground cables. When the environment is controlled, the system identifies more than 90 % of faults. For those tests the distance errors are less than 2 % of the total cable length. Materials & Methods Group 1 - Existing Method the Simple Pulse Reflection Technique is the current SPF method. By measuring the time between sent and returned pulses, it calculates where a fault exists. It is successful in 85 % of cases. There is an average distance error of 7 % with this tool. Group 2 (Proposed Method - Hybrid SPF - TFDR) A hybrid SPF method is the new approach - in this system, wavelet analysis processes the signals. It also uses STFT analysis to examine data. There is a GPS component with a 3 m precision range. With IoT cloud monitoring, the system tracks the cable status. The method classifies faults correctly in 94 % of attempts. Due to the features, the error level is 1.6 %. When locating faults, the hybrid approach is 97 % accurate. As a result, the average error is 1. 3 %. If the SNR is 5 dB, the accuracy remains at 88 %. By using this design, the classification of faults reaches 93 %. On average the system sends a report in 1.5 seconds. The proposed system is better at finding faults than previous versions. And the distance errors are smaller. For underground cable networks, it provides data in real time.

title: CamelGuard: AI-Powered Camel Detection System for Road Safety Using YOLO and IoT Sensors
authors: R. Albastaki, I. Hussain, R. Alrais
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) faces critical road-safety risks from camel-vehicle collisions on its desert highways, with camels accounting for 83% of all animal-related road accidents in the country. Existing passive measures, such as roadside fencing and warning signs, have proven insufficient, particularly in remote areas and at night. This paper presents CamelGuard AI, an autonomous, offline-capable, edge-computing detection and driver alert system. The system integrates a YOLOv11-tiny deep learning model, an HC-SR04 ultrasonic proximity sensor, a Raspberry Pi NoIR camera with 940nm infrared LED illumination, a DHT11 environmental sensor, and a Flask web monitoring dashboard, all running locally on a Raspberry Pi 5 within a custom 3D-printed PLA+ enclosure. Two specialized models (day.pt and night.pt) were trained on a Roboflow-managed camel dataset of 230 images with 2,040 labeled instances, achieving mAP@0.5 of 0.869 and 0.842, respectively. Prototype testing confirmed 94.7% daytime and 91.0% nighttime detection accuracy with sub-200 ms alert latency. The complete prototype was assembled for 834 AED (~$227 USD), representing more than 180× the cost reduction compared to commercial radar alternatives. Stakeholder validation from Sharjah Police further confirmed operational feasibility and national relevance.

title: Gen Z Engagement with BYOND: Sustaining Islamic Finance Heritage in Bank Syariah Indonesia
authors: Teuku Aufa Abthal, M. Shabri Abd. Majid, Teuku Muhammad Syahrizal, Maulidar Agustina, Yunisha Muslina, Raudhatul Jannah
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustaining Heritage: The Future of Conservation: Embracing Technology and Innovation for Sustainable Heritage (ICSH)
abstract: Islamic finance extends beyond its role as a financial mechanism; it embodies an intangible cultural heritage grounded in justice, ethics, and collective responsibility. With the rise of digital technologies, this heritage faces both new possibilities and emerging challenges as services transition onto online platforms. This study examines how Gen Z interacts with BYOND, the digital banking application of Bank Syariah Indonesia, focusing on the influence of security, convenience, and product features on their intention to discontinue usage. Employing survey data analyzed through SEM-PLS, the results indicate that while digital banking enhances accessibility and revitalizes Islamic financial practices, limitations in trust, ease of use, and innovation may threaten sustained adoption. The findings highlight the significance of community-driven digital participation, showing that Gen Z engagement is vital to preserving Islamic finance as a living heritage. Framed within cultural sustainability, the study contributes to global discussions on SDG 9 and SDG 11.

title: Global Trade Nexus: An AI-Powered Intelligence Platform for Democratizing International Commerce
authors: Kishlay Roy, Anushka Dagar, Akul Verma, Ravi Khurana, Meghna Sharma, Jimnee Deka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Fragmented tariff structures, volatile logistics prices and complex import-export regulations across 195 economies hinder SMEs' access to global markets. This paper introduces Global Trade Nexus, a next-generation AI-powered platform that simplifies landed cost estimation, automates cross-border compliance and identifies strategic export markets. The system integrates machine learning for cost forecasting with natural language processing of regulations and AI-driven market intelligence. Although the platform has not undergone real-world simulation or deployment yet, this paper puts forward the complete conceptual architecture, methodology and expected impact. The analysis draws on the contemporary literature in AI-driven trade transformation <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1], [10]</sup> for a theoretical basis of how AI can democratize global commerce for SMEs.

title: Environment-Adaptive Safety-Aware Path Planning via Weighted BFS for Autonomous Navigation
authors: Jiayong Li, Ying Liang
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Long-range autonomous navigation in unstructured outdoor environments presents significant challenges due to large-scale maps and complex obstacle distributions. Traditional geometric search methods often generate collision-prone trajectories that tightly hug obstacles, while sampling-based approaches suffer from path instability and low success rates. This paper proposes an EnvironmentAdaptive Safety-Aware Weighted Breadth-First Search (WBFS) framework for safe and efficient path planning. By employing spatial convolution to estimate local obstacle density, the algorithm constructs a dynamic cost potential field that autonomously modulates safety weights, prioritizing path optimality in open areas while maximizing obstacle clearance in cluttered regions. To further enhance efficiency in expansive environments, an extended strategy couples Bidirectional RRT (Bi-RRT) for topological skeleton extraction with Dynamic Narrow-Band W-BFS for local optimization. Experimental results on large-scale outdoor maps demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> success rate, compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> and 44% for RRT on the two test maps. Compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{*}$</tex>, WBFS delivers a 34.6- to 58.9-fold speedup and improves obstacle clearance by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 2 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 0 3 \%}$</tex>. Ablation studies confirm that the adaptive mechanism effectively eliminates hazardous boundary-hugging behaviors with negligible computational overhead. The Bi-RRT enhanced strategy further reduces computation time by 5.05 to 5.22 times compared to global search, providing a robust solution for time-sensitive autonomous missions.

title: Smart Trash Bin: Waste Management with AI-Powered Sorting, IOT Monitoring
authors: Ashish Sharma, Abhishek Yadav, Rinku Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: This idea is proposed as a Wi-Fi enabled smart trash bin to change the way of waste management in India especially in the areas inhabited by wildlife and high tourist traffic. The system is designed to promote the correct disposal of waste by offering free Wi-Fi connection to the users after entering a one time password (OTP) while the device is equipped with IoT sensors and AI driven waste classification (YOLOv8) as well as real time connectivity. In the wildlife zones the bins are made animal proof, the units are solar powered for off grid sustainability and the units have multilingual LED displays for tourists. The waste is sorted into recyclables and non recyclables and the fill level information is sent to the authorities through the IoT for on time collection. This solution is in conformity with the Swachh Bharat Abhiyan and incorporates digital rewards (behavioral nudges), environmental protection (reduced litter in forests) as well as smart city goals (effective waste management). The revenue model is scalable and consists of advertisement displays and government partnerships to ensure it is a low cost, tech based solution to India’s urbanization and ecological issues.

title: A Low-Complexity Two-Stage Robust Beamforming Scheme for ISAC Systems with Imperfect CSI
authors: Xiaodong Zhao, Menglong Wu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper proposes a low-complexity robust beam-forming scheme for downlink integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) under channel uncertainty. The sensing beam is generated by projecting the target steering vector onto the null space of the estimated communication channel, while the communication beam is optimized via second-order cone programming (SOCP) under a conservative leakage-aware robust signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) constraint. A one-dimensional search determines the power split. Simulation results show that the proposed design improves sensing gain while preserving reliable communication.

title:
GammaPod
                    <sup>TM</sup>
                    : A Novel Approach to Stereotactic Breast Cancer Radiotherapy
authors: Gaetano Gagliardo, Marcello Serra, Valentina d’Alesio, Gianluca Ametrano, Francesca Buonanno, Cecilia Arrichiello, Rocco Mottareale, Sara Falivene, Piera Ferraioli, Vincenzo Ravo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: GammaPod™ is an innovative radiotherapy platform specifically developed for noninvasive stereotactic treatment of early-stage breast cancer. Designed to overcome the geometric and dosimetric limitations of conventional Accelerated Partial Breast Irradiation techniques, it delivers highly conformal ablative doses via 25 fixed <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</sup>Co sources arranged in a hemispherical configuration. The system relies on a vacuum-assisted breast immobilization cup embedded with a wire-shaped fiducial marker, enabling the creation of a stereotactic coordinate system for submillimeter target localization.As of July 2025, GammaPod™ has been implemented in only 5 centers worldwide, with 2 units currently operational in Italy. The forthcoming installation at our institution will represent the 3rd in the country and the 6th globally, underscoring the pioneering nature of this clinical deployment. Commissioning activities will be conducted in preparation for clinical deployment of GammaPod™ at the Istituto Nazionale Tumori IRCCS Fondazione G. Pascale (Naples, Italy) scheduled for January 2026. Planned procedures will include mechanical reproducibility tests of the motorized couch, assessment of vacuum seal stability, and absolute dose calibration using PMMA phantoms combining the AAPM TG-51 protocol and the IAEA TRS-483 code of practice. Radiation protection assessments will be performed to verify the shielding effectiveness of the device, given the presence of fixed radioactive sources and the extended patient setup times required, which may involve staff proximity to the treatment unit. The clinical deployment of GammaPod™ will require the establishment of dedicated commissioning procedures, institution-specific infrastructure adjustments, and its systematic integration into established radiotherapy workflows. This system may represent a paradigm shift in stereotactic breast radiotherapy, offering precise treatment delivery with reduced toxicity and patient burden. Furthermore, by condensing treatment into a limited number of sessions, GammaPod™ may contribute to the sustainability of radiotherapy services through optimized scheduling and improved patient throughput. The upcoming commissioning phase will define the framework for its safe and effective clinical integration.

title: Developing a Structural Model for Empathetic E-commerce Customer Experiences: Using ISM & MICMAC Analysis
authors: Abu Bashar, Sherif Badran, Irfanullah Khan, Mohammad Noor Alam, Mohammad Nadeem Akhtar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: In this world of hyper personalization, the customer experience is one of the crucial aspects for ensuring that the customers are delighted. The element of empathy is a critical factor in understanding and developing effective strategies to offer super immersive and unforgettable customer experience on e-commerce platforms. This paper explores the interplay between emotional intelligence and empathy in human-AI collaborative e-commerce environment. Customer service is the most important component of the e-commerce dynamics that contribute to customer satisfaction, trust, and loyalty towards the brand. Interpretive Structural Modeling (ISM) is used to identify the key factors that enable empathetic interactions in digital commerce practices. To identify dependency and relationship among variables, MICMAC analysis is conducted. The study contributes to theoretical understanding and provides a comprehensive framework for designing & offering emotionally intelligent customer experiences. This study also highlights few ethical implications in AI-driven empathy on digital platforms.

title: Preliminary Analysis of Trunk Electrical Bio-Impedance in Professional Apple Orchards
authors: Federico Cum, Mattia Barezzi, Danilo Demarchi, Umberto Garlando
abstract: Accurate, low-power and reliable plant monitoring systems in smart agriculture are crucial to better resource management, such as water usage. Direct plant monitoring, particularly trunk electrical bio-impedance monitoring, is a promising solution to precisely monitor plants’ health conditions without relying only on environmental measurements. This paper showcases data acquisition and a preliminary observation campaign on apple trees from a professional orchard using a previously developed trunk bio-impedance sensor. The system operated in the field for one year, continuously sampling data every 10 minutes. The implanted electrodes exhibited good biocompatibility after an initial wound recovery period from the plants. The preliminary results revealed trunk bio-impedance trends generally consistent with existing literature regarding vegetative states and astronomical seasonal variations in apple trees; moreover, the strength of daily components ranged from high values of seasonality intensity index (4.94, 6.37) during spring (vegetative growth) to low values during summer (0.29, 0.41), likely due to high temperatures and limited water availability.

title: Low-Carbon Economic Dispatch of the Microgrid Based on Multi-Objective Beaver Behavior Optimizer
authors: Youzhong Wu, Qingqing Pang, Biao Shi, Feiyan Li, Jinzhong Li, Wei Ma, Yuguang Xie
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To address the challenge of coordinating economic operations with multi-constraint optimization in microgrids while balancing core objectives, such as minimizing operational costs and maximizing environmental benefits, this study proposes an economic dispatch strategy based on the Multi-Objective Beaver Behavior Optimizer (MOBBO). First, a mathematical model for microgrid scheduling is constructed, aiming to minimize both system operating costs and pollutant emissions. Second, strategies such as non-domination sorting, external repository, and congestion distance are introduced to transform the BBO into MOBBO. Finally, simulation analysis is conducted using a typical microgrid comprising photovoltaic, wind power, diesel generators, micro gas turbines, and energy storage systems, with comparative analysis against classical multi-objective optimization algorithms. Results demonstrate that the proposed MOBBO effectively balances economic and environmental objectives. The generated optimal dispatch scheme reduces operational costs by 12.45% and pollutant emissions by 2.06% compared to traditional algorithms, while achieving higher optimization accuracy and more uniform solution distribution. This approach provides reliable technical support for the low-carbon economic operation of microgrids.

title: Intelligent Fine-Grained Classification and Severity Assessment of Wind Turbine Blade Faults Based on YOLOv11+HPA-Biformer
authors: Qunhao Zhou, Chuanbo Wen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The existing YOLOv11 model can only perform fault localization on wind turbine blades and faces difficulties in characterizing fine fault features under complex textured backgrounds. Therefore, this paper proposes an integrated diagnosis method for wind turbine blade fault detection, classification and grading by fusing Hybrid Pooling Attention (HPA) and BiFormer sparse Transformer. The proposed method optimizes the backbone and neck networks: in the P5 branch from the neck network output to the detection head, the original C3k2 module is replaced with the C3k2-HPA module, and the C2PSA module at the end of the backbone network is replaced with C2PSA-BiFormer. Fault region ROI cropping is performed using the optimized YOLOv11, and a lightweight multi-scale classification network is designed to achieve fine-grained identification of four types of faults including cracks and corrosion. Experimental results fully verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The optimized YOLOv11 model achieves a mAP50 of 88.7%, which is 2.5% higher than that of the previous model; the fine-grained fault classification accuracy is 90.9%, and the severity grading accuracy is 88.1%. With improved detection accuracy, this work provides a practical technical solution for the intelligent operation and maintenance of wind turbine blades.

title: Adaptive Battery Cooling System for Small Ev Startups
authors: Ramya T, Pranesh M, Ritheev K S, Savitha S, Sowntharya A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Efficient thermal management of lithiumion battery packs is crucial for ensuring safety, performance, and lifespan in electric vehicles. Small electric vehicles developed by startups face limitations in cost, power availability, and system complexity, restricting the use of liquid or refrigerant-based cooling systems. This paper presents a low-cost and energy-efficient predictive-adaptive battery thermal management system using variable-speed air cooling. Unlike conventional fixed-speed or threshold-based fan control, the proposed approach enables proportional cooling based on real-time thermal conditions, reducing unnecessary energy consumptaion and thermal stress. An LSTM network is employed to capture temporal temperature behaviour influenced by load history and charging patterns, allowing short-term temperature prediction and preventive cooling. In addition, a Random Forest model evaluates battery thermal health using features such as temperature level, fluctuation rate, and cooling effort. The integrated framework improves thermal stability, enhances battery longevity, and ensures safe operation, making it suitable for small electric vehicle applications.

title: Finance Analytics for SMEs in Emerging Markets: From Dashboards to Generative AI
authors: Alexey Nuzhnyy
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are the backbone of emerging economies, yet they often lack the advanced financial tools routinely employed by multinational corporations. While practices such as rolling forecasts, scenario-based planning, integrated KPI dashboards, and risk-adjusted performance metrics have become standard in large industrial groups, SMEs face structural barriers to adoption, including limited expertise, resource constraints, and cultural resistance to data-driven decision-making [1], [2]. This paper explores how corporate-grade financial tools can be simplified and adapted for SMEs in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), CIS, and African regions. Drawing on consulting experience with SMEs in Russia, Kazakhstan, UAE, KSA, and African countries—as well as teaching experience in executive education programs—the study demonstrates how data analytics can transform traditional financial planning and control practices into more agile, accessible, and cost-effective solutions. Case insights include work on SME financial education, the adaptation of dashboards and scenario planning in industrial settings, and internal audit transformation projects in mid-sized companies. The contribution of this research lies in offering a practical framework that bridges the gap between corporate finance sophistication and SME realities. By leveraging data analytics, SMEs can achieve better financial visibility, strengthen risk management, and align strategic decision-making with dynamic market conditions [5], [7], [11].

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Blood Cancer Identification: Evaluating CNNs and Vision Transformers
authors: Prashanti Sharma, Satyendra Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Early Detecting and diagnosing blood cancers like leukemia, myeloma and lymphoma early is essential for better patient outcomes. Deep learning, especially using the two deep learning models, CNNs i.e Convolutional Neural Networks and Vision Transformers (ViTs), is improving how medical images such as blood smears and bone marrow samples are interpreted. This paper explores how these two technologies can be used together for blood cancer diagnosis and classification, compares their effectiveness, and suggests a way to include them in clinical practice. Hybrid models that are built with combine CNNs and ViTs show clear improvements in accuracy, generalization, and efficiency.

title: Quantum-Rag: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Quantum Circuit Code Generation
authors: Nicholas Stapleton, Sharmin Afrose, Ethan Seefried, Vicente Leyton Ortega, Travis S. Humble, Tirthankar Ghosal
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Generating correct and executable quantum circuit code remains a significant barrier for both newcomers and experienced practitioners in quantum computing. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise in classical code generation, their application to quantum programming is hampered by limited domain-specific training data and the rapidly evolving nature of quantum software frameworks. In this work, we present Quantum-RAG, an extended Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework that couples a domain-specialized language model with a curated, multi-source knowledge corpus to produce Qiskit-based quantum circuit code. Our three-stage pipeline ingests documentation, educational notebooks, and research literature into a dense retrieval index and augments the generation model with the most relevant context at inference time. Evaluated on the Qiskit HumanEval benchmark, QuantumRAG achieves a Pass@1 of 0.4132, a Pass@3 of 0.6291, and a Pass@5 of 0.7020, surpassing the domain-adapted GraniteQiskit baseline by 101%, 72%, and 47 % at each respective threshold. These results demonstrate that retrieval-augmented approaches can substantially improve quantum code generation without expensive model retraining.

title: A Hybrid Attention-Based Dual-Backbone Framework for Breast Cancer Histopathology Classification
authors: M. Bindu Priya, M. Jyothi Swaroopini, P. Supraja, M. Shanmukha Sri, D. Manoj Kumar, Akurathi Ravi Raja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The classification of breast cancer based on the image obtained through histopathological analysis is difficult owing to the complex patterns present in the image, the high level of similarity between the classes, and the high level of variation within the same class. Though the results obtained through the use of deep learning algorithms are satisfactory, the classification results obtained through the use of the single-backbone model are not effective in capturing the overall tissue structure and the detailed texture features necessary for the diagnosis of the disease. Hence, the hybrid attention-guided dual-backbone model is proposed, which combines the benefits of the ConvNeXt model and the EfficientNet-B0 model. The model is effective in capturing the global structural features and the texture features necessary for the diagnosis of the disease. The model is tested based on the patient-wise splitting of the data and is found effective in obtaining 99.30 % accuracy and 99.86% AUC.

title: RDB-MTL: A Parameter-Efficient Architecture for End-to-End Trustworthiness in 6G Service Chains
authors: Yuanyuan Zi, Tianyi Geng, Changyu Li, Xinli Zhou
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The vision of intelligent 6G service chains is confronting a critical reliability degradation issue at the physical layer: under high-bias channel conditions, conventional feedback models degenerate into static predictors—a phenomenon termed "Mean Collapse"—thereby nullifying the end-to-end trustworthiness of the air interface. To resolve this systemic vulnerability, we propose RDB-MTL, a lightweight architecture that endows edge nodes with intrinsic self-assessment capabilities. Unlike passive autoencoders, this framework leverages Residual Dense Blocks to enhance feature reuse and proactively predicts a Trust Proxy (Error Map) from the latent codeword. Through a stop-gradient-based self-governing optimization strategy, computational focus is dynamically redirected toward low-confidence regions with high reconstruction complexity. With Compression Ratio (CR) 1/16 (M = 128), RDB-MTL consistently improves reconstruction accuracy over CsiNet while using substantially fewer parameters, and is competitive with Transformer-based baselines (e.g., TransNet) under the same training and evaluation protocol, yielding a favorable accuracy–complexity trade-off. By embedding intrinsic uncertainty quantification into the feedback loop, this work establishes a self-aware, trustworthy failure-aware foundation for autonomous orchestration of 6G network service chains.

title: A Conceptual Modeling Framework for Multi-Product Cement Production Scheduling Using Influence Diagrams
authors: Nurhadi Siswanto, Ahmed Raecky Baihaqy, Ferry Aditya Nur Hidayat, Yasyfi Maulidina Nur Rohmah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the current competitive market of the cement industry in Indonesia, cement producers like the company under study must optimize operations to reduce costs amid stagnant sales and rising expenses. This research aims to optimize production scheduling across three production units: the Raw Mill, Kiln, and Finish Mill units, to minimize various costs, including total production costs, variable costs, setup costs, and transition costs. The research employs a Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) approach. The primary phase, which is also the focus of this paper, involves developing a conceptual model using an Influence Diagram. This model structures the system's components by defining uncontrollable inputs, system variables, control inputs, and system outputs, establishing a coherent foundation that accurately reflects real-world operations. The conceptual framework serves as the essential first step in constructing a robust and extendable MILP mathematical model for informed production decision-making.

title: Speed fluctuations suppression strategy for PMSMs based on improved EBADRC
authors: Zhengjie Hao, Tianzhen Liu, Taiyun Cui, Long Lei
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: An improved error-based active disturbance rejection control (EBADRC) is proposed for suppression of speed fluctuations in permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) subject to both periodic and aperiodic disturbances. The EBADRC eliminates the tracking differentiator compared to conventional active disturbance rejection controller structures while preserving equivalent disturbance rejection capabilities with simplified architecture. Subsequently, the integrator within the extended state observer (ESO) is replaced by a low-pass filter (LPF) to enhance disturbance estimation performance. Furthermore, the control law of the EBADRC is modified through integration of a repetitive controller. The repetitive controller provides high-gain compensation at fundamental and harmonic frequencies of periodic disturbances, enabling effective suppression of periodic interference components. The improved EBADRC can achieve concurrent attenuation of periodic and aperiodic disturbances, thereby improving PMSM control system robustness. Experimental validation is conducted on a 750 W PMSM setup, confirming the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning-based Dynamic Resource Allocation for Wireless Ad Hoc Networks
authors: Shengchun Liu, Xingyu Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: In this paper we investigate intelligent dynamic transmission resource scheduling in Ad Hoc networks with Multi-Frequency Time Division Multiple Access (MF-TDMA) transmission mechanism. We design a distributed signaling interaction and resource allocation scheduling strategy based on Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). Through reservation and contention among nodes, this strategy achieves distributed and dynamic on-demand allocation of transmission time slot resources. Furthermore, the dynamic contention process of time slot resources in the network is modeled as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), which is solved by using MARL. On this basis, we propose an intelligent time slot scheduling algorithm based on centralized training and distributed execution to realize intelligent on-demand allocation of transmission resources in the network. Simulation results demonstrate that compared with a number of baseline algorithms, our proposed algorithm can effectively improve the transmission performance of the network.

title: Large Language Models in Real-World Applications: Benefits and Risks
authors: Divyani V. Chatarkar, Reena S. Satpute
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Large language models finally received the ability to understand and generate language similar to human language; hence, they revolutionized human-machine communication. Nowadays, AI-driven models have been employed in lots of fields of activity: customer service, education, health, creative industries, etc. Huge volumes of data and state-of-the-art machine learning technologies were the basis for their development. They are so valuable for modern civilization because they speed up the activities, provide a lot of useful information, and make automation possible in the laboratory. Surely, several drawbacks are present in LLMs: they can give biased results and propagate false information. Security and privacy concerns may be raised by them.The huge processing power requirement causes environmental damage. Different governments and agencies are making new ethics and rules for responsible usage. Ethics questions need to be combined with this development so that maximum utilization could be made with much lesser risks. This paper reviews the evolution, practical applications, benefits, and disadvantages of LLMs, ethics, and probable developments in the future. Of importance is the fact that a comprehensive understanding of pros and cons will further guide development toward ethical AI techniques that ensure maximum positive impacts and minimum negative ones.

title: Hybrid Deep Adaptive ErrP-Aware Reinforcement Learning Framework for BCI Applications
authors: Swarna Surekha, Pushpakumar R, S. Rukmani Devi, J. Balamurugan, Yalamanchili Saroja, N.Dhasarathan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The implementation of reliable Brain-Computer Interface platforms for assistive technologies is impeded by limitations such as non-stationary EEG signals, session variability, and categorization errors. An enhanced Deep Adaptive ErrP-Aware Reinforcement Learning (DAERL) method is introduced in this research to address these shortcomings. The NIT Raipur P300 collection is used in developing this installation. By adding error-related potentials (ErrP) to the reinforcement learning (RL) loop, DAERL makes it easier for policies to change quickly after failed classes. To develop effective BCI models, the approach uses a set of computer models that work together to do tasks like improving rewards, changing policies based on feedback, extracting P300 features, and preprocessing signals. The results of this research indicate that the DAERL's classification accuracy (92% accuracy, 28 bits/min ITR) as well as adaptability index (0.88) outperform the deep baselines (CNN 85% accuracy) and LSTM (0.88), as well as the classical models (SVM, 75% accuracy). In repeated trials, DAERL is stable with minimal accuracy loss, according to session-wise testing that demonstrate robustness over EEG non-stationarity. Results show that using ErrP improves communication and assists to correct faults. DAREL is an innovative platform to empower assistive devices such as spelling devices, robotic arms, wheelchairs and smart-home control. It achieves this by bridging the gap between experimental research and practical life application.

title: The Relationship Between Customer Experience, Customer Satisfaction, and Customer Loyalty towards Digital Banking Services
authors: Phuoc Cuu Long Le, Than Ai Van Ho, Thi Minh Chi Nguyen
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: Today, competition for businesses is becoming stronger globally in general and for Agency businesses in particular. That makes product pricing and price for customers an extremely important factor to promote customer purchasing behavior. Based on this concern, it is necessary to implement specific strategies to offer prices that are suitable for consumers while bringing maximum benefits to the company. A pricing strategy that is often used but rarely studied is the decoy effect. This study aims to determine the motivation of the Decoy Effect on consumer purchasing decisions. The theory of the Decoy Effect has been proven to have an impact in marketing and influence on customer behavior as demonstrated in research in 1982, but in the context of the Agency service industry, it has not been studied yet. Therefore, this study will explore the impact of the Decoy Effect in enhancing customers' purchasing decisions. The collected data was processed using confirmatory factor analysis and exploratory factor analysis. To evaluate the initial standard of the data, the author determined the reliability of the data through the Cronbach's Alpha coefficient of the scales, all of which were greater than 0.7. The research results showed that the Decoy Effect promotes purchasing decisions and creates consumer intentions.

title: Random Forest-Enhanced Madelung Model for High-Precision Hysteresis Characterization in Piezoelectric Actuators
authors: Rui Li, Zijian Wang, Gang Li, Kairui Cao, Jinmei Xie
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Piezoelectric actuators are widely utilized in ultra-precision positioning applications. However, their intrinsic hysteresis nonlinearity significantly degrades positioning accuracy. Although the traditional Madelung model effectively captures history-dependent characteristics through its wiping-out mechanism, it encounters a precision bottleneck regarding the mathematical characterization of the Current Hysteresis Curve (CHC). This paper presents an enhanced Madelung hysteresis modeling approach synergistically integrated with Random Forest (RF). The proposed approach retains the analytical framework and wiping-out mechanism of the Madelung model, facilitating the accurate identification of trajectory turning points and motion directions. To enhance the fidelity of the CHC characterization, the traditional analytical mapping is replaced by a data-driven RF algorithm. By constructing a 13-dimensional feature space, independent regression models for the ascending and descending branches are systematically developed to ensure high-precision performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the RF-Madelung model can precisely reconstruct complex asymmetric hysteresis loops. Furthermore, under variable-amplitude sinusoidal excitations, the proposed model demonstrates superior predictive precision, yielding a relative root-mean-square error of 0.19% compared with 0.38% for the conventional model.

title: Explainable AI-Driven IoT Intrusion Detection: Human-Interpretable Threat Identification Under Extreme Class Imbalance
authors: Yazan Adnan, Mohamed Taleb, Noor Aldeen Alawad
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The number of Internet of Things (IoT) devices being deployed in smart cities, smart hospitals, and smart industrial facilities is increasing at an impressive rate. However, the number of attacks on these devices is increasing at a rapid rate as well. Most conventional IDS solutions fail to detect rare attacks due to the extreme class imbalance that exists in the training data. In this paper, we propose GRW-LGBM, a novel human-AI collaborative IDS that can detect rare events and explain its decisions to human users. The use of inverse-square-root frequency weighting reduces the class imbalance from 840:1 to 29:1. The use of DART (Dropout Additive Regression Trees) regularization helps to prevent overfitting in the model. The use of SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) adds the capability to explain each model decision. The proposed model achieves 69.9% accuracy, 0.585 Macro F1, 0.652 Macro Recall, and ∼ 0.61 Macro AP on the UNSW-NB15 dataset. The performance is statistically better than the conventional models (p < 0.05). The F1 score of GRW-LGBM is the highest for the critical classes of attacks, such as Fuzzers (+0.12), DoS (+0.11), and Analysis (+0.09). Additionally, the model can process 345 samples/s of data on a Raspberry Pi 5, making it suitable for real-time IoT deployment in smart infrastructure projects around the world.

title: Data-Driven Diagnosis of Building-Envelope Thermal Behavior in Real Time
authors: Lahoucine Ouhsaine, Damien Ali Hamada Fakra, Mohammed El Ganaoui
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: This work presents a data-driven methodology to identify the thermo-physical properties of building envelope components and to perform real-time diagnosis under realistic, time-varying boundary conditions. The approach combines a dedicated state-space heat-transfer model with a non-dimensional reformulation that replaces classical RC coefficients by compact parameter sets (p, q) that separate internal conduction from surface exchange. Treating identification as an inverse problem, we adapt Dynamic Mode Decomposition with control (DMDc) to estimate discrete-time state and input matrices directly from temperature and heat-flux snapshots. The resulting low-order models are fast to identify, suitable for in-situ characterization and model calibration of single- and multi-layer walls and remain physically interpretable. On top of the identified model, we define two complementary diagnostic indicators: (i) a residual-based score that tracks persistent inconsistencies between model predictions and measurements, and (ii) a parameter-drift score that exploits the (p, q) structure to distinguish internal wall alterations from changes in boundary exchange. The framework is assessed on a single-wall assembly under dynamic climatic excitations, using both numerically simulated data and experimental measurements. Results show that the proposed methodology accurately reconstructs surface temperatures and heat fluxes and yields compact state-space models that are well adapted to real-time envelope monitoring, model calibration, and energy-aware operation of buildings.

title: A Scalable Federated Deep Learning Architecture for Privacy-Preserving Intrusion Detection in Distributed Industrial IoT Networks
authors: Prabhat Purushottam, Sweta Mukherjee, Nainika Anand, Rajesh Kumar Tiwari, Smita Dash, Jeevan Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Smart manufacturing and critical infrastructure implements the rapid Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) technologies, which has increased the attacker-target field of distributed industrial networks significantly. The conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are traditional centralized systems that need to aggregate sensitive operational data, which increases the privacy, scalability, and regulatory issues. This paper suggests a Federated Deep Learning (FDL) architecture to overcome these problems by providing a scale-able architecture to privacy-preserving intrusion detection in distributed Industrial IoT systems. It is proposed that the proposed framework will allow several edge nodes to cooperatively train deep neural network models without exchanging raw data, which will not only guarantee data confidentiality, but also enhance performance of global detection The architecture is a combination of the secure model aggregation, adaptative client selection and communication efficient updates and anomaly based deep learning models in order to detect known and zero-day cyberattacks. The model used is a hybrid CNN-LSTM that is used to extract the spatial and temporal attack patterns on an industry traffic stream. Benchmark IIoT intrusion evaluation provides evidence of higher detection accuracy, low false-positive rate and high scalability than centralized and standalone IDS methods. Besides, the framework has very low communication overheads and strong convergence properties in case of heterogeneous data distributions. The proposed solution offers a privacy-aware, secure, and scalable intrusion detection mechanism in accordance with the Industry 4.0 cybersecurity requirements.

title: Robust Cluster Tomato Detection via Attention-Enhanced Yolo for Greenhouse Uav Inspection
authors: Xiaopeng Du, Shaocong Wang, Hang Chen, Kai Li, Yilei Yin, Xiaoming Ding
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: To address the challenges of dense small targets and complex backgrounds in cluster tomato detection under greenhouse conditions, this paper presents an accurate detection method built upon an enhanced YOLOv11 architecture. By integrating the iterative attention feature fusion (iAFF) module into the feature pyramid network and deeper layers of the backbone, we construct a C3k2_iAFF fusion unit to enhance the adaptive fusion capability of multi-scale features, and systematically evaluate its placement effectiveness at different network locations. On a drone-collected cluster tomato dataset, the proposed method achieves 89.8 % precision, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95.3 \% \text{AP}_{50}$</tex>, and 89.6 % recall-improving precision by 2 %, recall by 2 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A P}_{\mathbf{5 0}}$</tex> by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 \%}$</tex> over the baseline model-while only adding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 4 ~ M}$</tex> parameters and 0.1 GFLOPs. This demonstrates a significant enhancement in small-object detection performance with minimal computational overhead. Furthermore, ablation studies reveal that deploying iAFF in high-level feature stageswhere semantic content is rich—yields optimal performance. In contrast, indiscriminately extending iAFF to shallow layers degrades detection accuracy due to instability in low-level feature representation.

title: Calibration Repeatability of Soil Moisture Sensors in Biochar Amendments under Changing Salinity
authors: Maria Jose Gomez-Astorga, Karolina Villagra-Mendoza, Federico Masis-Melendez, Renato Rimolo-Donadio
abstract: Soil moisture sensors play a key role in the efficient management of irrigation systems, contributing to the optimization of water. They often require initial calibration, and their accuracy over time is a valid concern, considering sensor drift and aging, in particular for low-cost sensors. This paper evaluates two types of soil moisture sensors —reflective and capacitive— after different calibrations at different times, using soil amended with biochar and different salinity levels. Sensor readings were compared against a direct measurement method, and accuracy and repeatability were assessed using three statistical criteria. Results show that the calibration procedure needs to consider the material composition of the soil to ensure reliable performance. Reflective sensors demonstrated better stability, maintaining errors below 10% while capacitive sensors showed errors approaching 30%, as biochar and salinity increased.

title: Exploring the Viability of Multi-Paradigm Transfer Optimization
authors: Yi Lu, Chenming Cao, Kai Zhang, Liming Zhang, Xiaoming Xue, Kay Chen Tan
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Knowledge transfer has emerged as a promising approach for improving the search performance of optimizers on various complex problems, also known as transfer optimization in the literature, which consists of three main transfer paradigms: sequential transfer, multitask and multiform optimization. Despite a variety of knowledge transfer techniques that have been developed over the years, most of them are tailored to a specific paradigm. That is, advances in algorithm design of the three transfer paradigms have largely proceeded in isolation. However, none of the knowledge from the three paradigms can guarantee its superiority over the others, as such superiority is generally problem-dependent. A paradigm-specific transfer optimization algorithm is incapable of releasing the full potential of a database containing different types of knowledge, i.e., previously-solved tasks, potentially related tasks to be solved concurrently, and alternate formulations. In light of this, this paper explores the viability of multi-paradigm transfer optimization by proposing a unified framework that integrates the three paradigms, wherein different types of knowledge are assessed from a consistent view and the most promising one is identified for knowledge transfer. Preliminary experiments conducted on representative benchmark problems demonstrate the feasibility of our proposal.

title: Green metrology for power integrity: PSRR characterization via compressed sensing
authors: Ioan Tudosa, Francesco Picariello, Eulalia Balestrieri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The accurate characterization of Power Integrity (PI) metrics, such as the Power Supply Rejection Ratio (PSRR) and Power Delivery Network (PDN) impedance, is critical for validating the reliability and energy efficiency of modern electronic systems. However, traditional Nyquist-rate metrology necessitates electronic instrumentation (e.g. oscilloscopes, data acquisition boards, etc.) that embed high-speed Analog-to-Digital Converters (ADCs), leading to significant energy consumption, massive data throughput, and high hardware costs—factors that conflict with the principles of green engineering. The paper presents a novel method for high-fidelity PSRR measurement based on Compressed Sensing (CS), directly addressing the resource-intensity of conventional approaches. By exploiting the inherent sparsity of the PDN’s impulse response and employing a standard Biased Pseudo-Random Binary Sequence (PRBS) as a broadband sensing mechanism, the measurement problem is transformed into an underdetermined linear system solvable via the Orthogonal Matching Pursuit (OMP) algorithm. Through simulation, the proposed method demonstrates that the full-bandwidth PSRR profile can be accurately reconstructed using only a small, randomized subset of samples. This study quantitatively shows that the proposed measurement method maintains Safety Integrity Level (SIL)-compliant accuracy while achieving a 70% reduction in the data acquisition duty cycle. This efficiency enables the abstraction of hardware, allowing low-power, embedded ADCs to perform diagnostics. This work ultimately facilitates continuous, in-situ health monitoring, significantly contributing to the predictive maintenance and extended lifespan of sustainable electronics.

title: Financial Statement Fraud Detection in the Automobile Sector: Employing the Beneish Model
authors: Fatima Harun, Latifah AlAjmi, Gagan Kukreja
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This study evaluates earnings manipulation in five significant automobile companies (Toyota, Honda, MercedesBenz, BMW, and Volkswagen) from 2019 to 2023 using the Beneish M-Score. To assess possible fraud risks, the study calculated and examined multiple financial ratios and the MScore. The results indicated that there was a minimal likelihood of manipulation for Mercedes-Benz, BMW, and Toyota because their M-Scores were consistent and negative. Volkswagen had a significant rise in leverage in 2023, while Honda displayed considerable volatility, especially in 2020, which raised questions but did not prove manipulation. Although several oddities call for more investigation, the study’s overall findings support the null hypothesis that these companies did not manipulate their revenues. The results validate that the Beneish M-Score offers a useful and straightforward framework for early fraud detection and can facilitate future integration with more sophisticated forensic or machine learning models for improved precision.

title: Experimental Evaluation of Cryptographic Models for Blockchain Security and Privacy in Smart Environments
authors: Arvinder Singh, Mandeep Singh, Jyoti Rani, Sakshi Chauhan, Amarpreet Kaur, Deepak Juneja
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Blockchain technology into the healthcare sector, logistics, finance, and massive IoT system, this technology is making its way into it. With the issue of privacy becoming much more critical than before in the adoption phases. Even though blockchains provide integrity and resistant storage against tampering, numerous implementations still expose behavioural evidence, time-based data, besides other forms of metadata, which may be compromised by inference attacks. These breaches become more apparent when there is an exchange or near real-time processing of sensitive information. This paper will analyze the performance of three widely discussed privacy-saving methods Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP), Homomorphic Encryption (HE), not to mention Differential Privacy (DP) in a practical blockchain architecture and not in isolation. A testbed made of Hyperledger Fabric and Ethereum instances privately owned was constructed to enable each of the methods to be tested under the same conditions. The experiments made trade-offs of transaction suspensions, added computational load, and exposure to metadata-based attacks.The results demonstrate that ZKP-based settings store observable metadata substantially and restricted the amount that an adversary would deduce regarding transaction patterns. HE was a good implementation in areas where safe computation was needed but with significant latency overhead. The least significant communication impact was on DP, which was, however, to be carefully tuned in terms of its privacy parameters to prevent the decline in protection. We further conducted simulated attacks to research into the reaction of each approach to pressure and detail how validation can be extended in the future with the aid of open datasets through the MIMIC-III, OpenAQ, and TII cybersecurity logs. On the whole, the findings provide useful insights to teams, who want to embed cryptographic privacy tools in production-scale blockchain systems in particular in those industries where confidentiality and data protection cannot be jeopardized.

title: AI in Modeling Host-Pathogen Interactions for Antiviral Drug Discovery
authors: Chaitanya Tumma, Supraja Ayyamgari, Saicharan Kadari, Vinay Kumar Kasula, Sonali V. Patil, Bura Vijay Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: With the ambition to accelerate antiviral drug development, there is a need to advance the understanding of host-pathogen interactions; however, traditional methods often lack sufficient accuracy or generalizability across diverse pathogens. This project aims at the development of a state-of-the-art AI framework for improving the prediction of HPIs in response to pressing pandemic needs for scalable, accurate, and interpretable approaches. We have developed a hybrid deep learning model that incorporates CNNs, GNNs, and attention for developing complex biological patterns across protein sequences and structures. Comparative results prove that our proposed hybrid deep learning model outperforms both traditional machine learning and single-modality deep learning models by achieving 91.3 % accuracy, 89.5 % precision, with an AUC-ROC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 3. 1 \%}$</tex>. The results suggest that the fusion of spatial, structural, and contextual learning styles can be helpful for deep learning model development. Therefore, the proposed framework constitutes a significant step toward AI-powered therapeutic discovery that has the potential to bring advances in pandemic preparedness, precision medicine, and opens ways for more ethical and interpretable AI models for the biomedical world.

title: Early Detection of Hardware Trojans Using Neural Controlled Differential Equations and Analysis of Power Traces
authors: Hasala Senevirathne, Rahul Vishwakarma, Amin Rezaei
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Evolving Hardware Trojans pose a serious threat to modern digital systems by evading traditional detection through stealthy, adaptive behavior. Even recent methods that leverage advances in machine learning can only detect them after activation, leaving a critical window for potential security breaches. To address this gap, we propose a novel approach for hardware Trojan detection and prediction using Neural Controlled Differential Equations (NCDEs) and analysis of power traces. Our method leverages an NCDE model trained exclusively on Trojan-free data to learn nominal power behavior, combined with a Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) classifier calibrated on labeled data, to distinguish between three scenarios: no Trojan, dormant Trojan, and active Trojan. Our method uses a sliding window to process side-channel measurements, enabling detection of subtle power consumption deviations that indicate Trojan presence, even when dormant. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed NCDE-based method achieves superior accuracy compared to traditional machine learning approaches, with the additional advantage of handling dormant Trojans above a sensitivity threshold. We validate our approach on standard hardware Trojan benchmarks, showing robust detection and prediction performance.

title: Asymmetric Effects of Oil and Geopolitical Risk on Indonesia’s Equity Market: A Nonlinear ARDL Approach
authors: Tran Trong Huynh, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Financial markets play a central role in capital allocation and policy transmission, yet they are highly exposed to exogenous shocks (from energy prices and geopolitical risk (GPR)). These forces rarely act in simple linear ways: investor responses differ between good and bad news, depending on the size and persistence of shocks, and interact through both cashflow and discount-rate channels. Capturing such nonlinear, regime-dependent dynamics is therefore crucial for understanding equity returns in emerging economies.This study provides the first Indonesia-specific evidence on asymmetric oil-GPR transmission by estimating a nonlinear autoregressive distributed lag (NARDL) model using monthly data on the Jakarta Composite Index (JKSE), WTI crude oil prices, and the Caldara-Iacoviello GPR index from January 2010 to July 2025. Unlike prior studies that focus only on insample relationships, we explicitly evaluate out-of-sample forecast performance against a univariate ARIMA benchmark under a fixed train/test design (2010-2019 vs. 2020-2025). The results show that the asymmetric NARDL delivers $\mathbf{3}-\mathbf{5} \boldsymbol{\%}$ lower forecast errors than ARIMA, indicating incremental predictive value from sign-specific, long-run oil and GPR components. These findings highlight that sustained runs of “good” or “bad” external news matter more than one-off shocks, offering practical insight for investors and policymakers in energydependent emerging markets.

title: An inertia estimation method for power generation units based on weight-adaptive integration model
authors: Zhen Li, Jinfan Xie, Julong Chen, Yuanping Guo, Jianfeng Yang, Xia Xiao, Si-Zhe Chen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: In the new type of power system, accurately estimating the inertia of each power generation unit is crucial for maintaining system stability and compensating for weak inertia areas. The average-weight integrated hybrid neural network for inertia estimation overlooks performance differences between distinct neural networks, failing to fully leverage the unique advantages of each architecture. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a power generation unit inertia estimation method based on a weight-adaptive integration model. A two-stage hybrid neural network composed of LSTM-MLP and TCN-LSTM is constructed as the integration model for power generation unit inertia estimation. Building on this, a global optimal weight integration strategy is designed to achieve a reasonable allocation of the integration weights of sub-models, fully exploiting the complementary advantages of different sub-models in time series feature extraction and nonlinear mapping. The method was verified on the IEEE 9-bus system, and the results show that compared with the average-weight integration model, the proposed method can effectively improve the accuracy and robustness of power generation unit inertia estimation.

title: Optimization Method for Multi-UAV Cooperative Localization of Multiple Emitters
authors: Xiaoting Ji, Bo Le
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: This paper studies the time difference of arrival (TDOA) localization problem using multiple unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) for multiple emitters. An improved adaptive stochastic learning particle swarm optimization algorithm (ARLPSO) is proposed to optimize the deployment configuration of multiple UAVs in order to enhance the localization accuracy, while it also takes energy consumption, communication conditions and safety constraints into consideration. The proposed algorithm effectively balances the global exploration and local exploitation by introducing an adaptive inertia weight adjustment mechanism, and avoids falling into local optima by a random neighborhood learning strategy. In addition, the mixed integer linear programming is utilized to achieve task allocation for multiple emitters. Through experimental simulation, compared with several classic PSO algorithms and genetic algorithm, the effectiveness and stability of the proposed method have been verified. The research results have certain practical application potential.

title: Parameter Identification of Fractional-Order Time-Delay Systems Based on Improved Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Chao Chen, Zhengsheng Chang, Yang Shi, Shikun Shao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Fractional calculus accurately describes "memory" and "hereditary" dynamic processes, but fractional-order time-delay system identification faces challenges of non-integer order, increased parameter dimension, and noise sensitivity. Existing methods rarely consider time delays, which are common in industrial processes. This paper proposes an improved particle swarm optimization (IPSO) algorithm integrating dynamic inertia weight and chaotic mutation for joint identification of "coefficients + fractional order + time-delay constant". Key innovations include: a hierarchical strategy of "exponential inertia weight + adaptive mutation triggering" to adapt to fractional parameter characteristics; a "matrix multiplication dimension expansion method" solving implicit dimension incompatibility in MATLAB fractional calculus calculation; and a weighted fitness function balancing dynamic fitting and parameter numerical accuracy. Simulation results show the optimized IPSO reduces identification errors of α and τ by over 30% and improves K/T parameter stability by 40% compared with traditional PSO. It maintains stable performance under SNR=30, meeting industrial-grade fractional-order system modeling requirements.

title: Intelligent Feedback Analytics: Hybrid LSTM-GPT Framework for Educational Improvement
authors: Sali Radha Pranav, Sonali N. Jadhav, S. V. Gumaste, Sahil Patil
