publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In today’s educational landscape, institutions are recognizing the importance of student feedback in improving teaching methods and learning experiences. However, traditional analysis methods, like manual reviews, surveys, and basic statistical tools, often fail to reveal the complex patterns within qualitative feedback data. To tackle this issue, this work introduces a new analytical approach using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) algorithms for sentiment analysis. LSTM models can handle sequential and contextual data well, making them ideal for capturing the flow, tone, and deeper meanings in student responses. This helps us extract relevant insights into student emotions, concerns, and trends over time. By using LSTM, we move beyond superficial evaluations, leading to a better understanding of student sentiment. This helps educators and institutional leaders pinpoint areas for improvement and reinforce successful strategies. Besides LSTM, we also use a Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT) model to provide dynamic, personalized suggestions that support individual student development. GPT’s language capabilities allow it to generate constructive, context-aware recommendations based on each student's feedback. By combining these two machine learning techniques, our system analyses student feedback with greater depth and accuracy while offering actionable, individualized recommendations. This intelligent approach improves both student outcomes and institutional effectiveness, making feedback a key tool in modern education.

title: A Knowledge Graph and Large Language Model Approach for Detecting Overclaims in Halal Certification
authors: Ganendra Aby Bhamakerti, Nur Aini Rakhmawati
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Obtaining halal certification can be subject to errors and frauds. This research attempts to detect errors in halal certification applications submitted by business actors by using Knowledge Graph (KG) with Large Language Model (LLM) and is expected to assist the public in determining whether products and food providers with genuine halal certification are free from any indication of error or fraud. This research is conducted by collecting data sources from HalalWave and GoFood sites, with additional data from Google Maps API. Next, the GoFood dataset is preprocessed by dropping unused columns, deleting duplicate rows, dropping non-halal products and restaurants that have non-halal products, joining tables, and creating new ID columns, resulting in two new datasets that are used in the KG construction stage which involves defining the KG schema, such as determining which nodes and relationships need to be created, and then constructing the KG. Then, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is conducted by using LLM and Google Maps API to find alternative restaurants, KG usage as a knowledge source to find similar “Restaurant” nodes, Jaccard Similarity calculation is conducted to determine whether the restaurant is overclaim or not, and confusion matrix-based metrics is used to evaluate the constructed system performance. The results show that the system demonstrated strong detection ability with an accuracy of 0.91, a precision of 0.908, a recall of 1.0, and an F1-Score of 0.952, which indicate the approach is effective at identifying overclaims on halal certification submissions.

title: Multi Gas Source Localisation through Candidate Sampling and Gated Cluster Selection
authors: Muhammad Talha Yasin, Rafiqul Islam, Matt Garratt
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: Localising multiple gas sources from sparse measurements remains a fundamental challenge in turbulent flow environments where plumes exhibit intermittent behavior and spatial overlap. This paper presents a two-stage probabilistic framework that addresses false-positive suppression through candidate-guided particle filtering and interpretable cluster gating. A Boids-based swarm executes structured sampling across a coarse grid to identify high-concentration candidate cells, focusing subsequent measurements on regions with maximum information content. A Particle Filter (PF) performs Bayesian inference exclusively on in-cell measurements, while an adaptive Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DB-SCAN) extracts source hypotheses from the weighted posterior. Four interpretable gating criteria—minimum particle count, local weight fraction, spatial spread, and inter-lock separation—reject transient clusters arising from plume intermittency. The complete pipeline is validated through extensive simulation using PomPy puff-plume dynamics and real-world arena trials with VICON-tracked Sphero RVRs. Experimental evaluation across varying wind directions, source counts, and challenging spatial configurations demonstrates detection rates exceeding 85% with substantially reduced false positives compared to ungated baselines, while maintaining sub-meter localisation accuracy and real-time execution on resource-constrained platforms. The framework operates without wind-direction estimation and scales linearly with source count, establishing practical feasibility for multi-source gas detection on low-cost mobile robots.

title: Blueprint Based Campus Navigation System with QR Scanning
authors: Chiranjeevi Aggala, B. Raghavaiah, M.L.S.N.S Lakshmi, G. Bindu Pavani, D. Hema Latha, K. Ramya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The proposed campus navigation system assists in locating buildings within the campus like canteens, libraries, hostels, playground and departmental buildings. It uses a pop-up map that uses X-Y coordinate system to guide the routes accurately. It is created using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and QR code technology and it allows to save time navigating the campus and be particularly useful during events, admission seasons, and orientation programs. This innovative and expandable product upgrades campus navigation and makes it hassle-free and simple to all users.

title: Electromagnetic Noise Analysis and Research on 10kV Medium-Voltage 36-Pole Circulating Pump Motor for a Nuclear Power Project
authors: Wenlei Zhang, Deyang Feng, Bin Xie
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The circulating water pumps of Units 5 and 6 in a domestic nuclear power plant adopt the scheme of direct drive by metal mixed-flow pumps and motors. According to the process calculation requirements, the project plans to use 10kV medium-voltage 36-pole vertical asynchronous motors. The noise level of a motor is an important index to evaluate its quality, and how to effectively reduce motor noise is a key issue in motor design. This paper deeply analyzes the main sources and formation mechanism of the electromagnetic noise of this motor. In view of the causes of electromagnetic noise, specific noise reduction suggestions and control measures are proposed, which can significantly reduce the motor noise level and provide strong support for the stable operation of the circulating pump transmission system and environmental protection.

title: Natural Language Processing Techniques for Analysing Consumer Sentiments Toward Time-Honoured Brand Innovations
authors: Na Wang, Lusi Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Text Classification, Opinion Mining, and Decision Making have become a requirement for businesses and researchers to understand what's hidden behind the lenses in the textual data set. Hence, Sentiment Analysis has taken forward its reach and importance as an important tool to classify the textual data, effectively extracting meaningful information from it. With this study, the traditional machine learning (ML) models, such as Naïve Bayes, Support Vector Machine, and Random Forest are evaluated alongside with the deep learning (DL) models, such as Long ShortTerm Memory, Convolutional Neural Networks and Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. According to the results observe that in IMDB and Yelp reviews, traditional ML models were moderately successful and the best model was SVM at 83.1 % and 80.5 % respectively. Despite that, deep learning models performed much better, managing to surpass ML approaches by a large margin: BERT was able to achieve 91.2 on IMDB and 89.1 on Yelp (on the latter two, both theoretically and in practice). Finally, our best results were obtained from the BERT + LSTM hybrid model, which had 0.938 degree of accuracy on IMDB, 0.914 on Yelp, 0.881 on Financial News and 0.863 on medical reviews, thus it enhanced the sentiment classification via contextual embeddings and sequential learning. It is demonstrated in the study that hybrid models improve sentiment classification accuracy under each domain. Future work will be on computational efficiency, application of multilingual adaptation, and domain specific tuning for best performance on business, finance, healthcare applications.

title: Granger-Inspired Predictive Structure Test for RBG/RNG Evaluation
authors: Joshua H. Sylvester, Micah A. Thornton, Mitchell A. Thornton, Eric C. Larson
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: New standards for post-quantum cryptography rely on new methods to evaluate the quality of random bit generators. Existing approaches largely rely on correlation-based tests, which are limited in their ability to capture dependence. In particular, they do not directly assess independence or reveal potential cause-and-effect relationships within the sequence. To address this gap, we introduce a methodology for evaluating random generators that draws on the concept of causality as a measure of predictability. We introduce the Granger-inspired Predictive Structure Test, which evaluates subsequences of a bitstream by comparing a logistic regression model against a null model, enabling the detection of predictive dependencies that correlationbased methods overlook. We show that, under assumptions aligning our method with a Granger causality test, the method detects long-term predictive dependencies and identifies nonrandomness at a significantly higher rate than widely used statistical tests.

title: Design of Periodically Connected Root Protograph LDPC Codes for Block-Fading Channel
authors: Yejin Lee, Minseok Han, Hosung Park
abstract: Existing root protograph (RP) low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes are constrained to a code rate of 1/L or slightly less for specific values of L, the number of fading blocks, while achieving full diversity in the block-fading (BF) channel. To overcome these limitations, we propose a systematic method to construct periodically connected RP (PCRP) LDPC codes of various rates 1/n for n ≤ L. The PCRP LDPC codes are designed to have diversity order n by their basic structure and a large coding gain by employing a new BF outage estimation algorithm. It is shown via sum-product decoding simulation that the PCRP LDPC codes achieve a diversity order of at least n and have better frame error rates than conventional RP LDPC codes.

title: IAM-YOLO: A Multi-Dimensional Fusion Perception Network for Rapeseed Pest Identification
authors: Qiangmin Zhou, Hang Zhang, Yongcheng Jiang, Fanzhen Wang, Tonghai Liu, Na Liu, Jinghan Cai, Meng Wang, Xiaomin Kang
abstract: As an important oil crop in China, rapeseed (Brassica campestris) relies heavily on effective pest control to ensure both yield and quality. However, traditional detection methods suffer from high labor dependence, low efficiency, and poor real-time performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes an improved pest detection method based on YOLOv10s, named IAM-YOLO. Specifically, we propose a multi-scale fusion attention module (IAM), and further integrate existing modules, including the Convolution and Attention Fusion Module (CAFM) and the Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module, to enhance the model’s feature extraction capability and detection accuracy. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed improvements, we constructed an image dataset containing multiple categories of rapeseed pests and designed a series of experiments. Experimental results demonstrate that our improved model outperforms the original YOLOv10s in terms of precision, recall, and mAP. In particular, the model incorporating IAM, CAFM, and ECA modules achieves a precision of 89.7%, representing a 3.9 percentage point improvement over the baseline model, showcasing excellent detection performance. These findings indicate that the proposed method holds significant potential for rapeseed pest detection and provides strong technical support for intelligent pest and disease monitoring in agriculture.

title: NeuroScan AI: Deep Transfer Learning and Integrated Web-Based Diagnostics for Multiclass Alzheimer’s Disease Staging
authors: Meena Jha, Reesh, Animesh Mishra
abstract: While deep learning has emerged as a powerful tool for automated medical image analysis, existing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) often struggle with limited datasets and the "vanishing gradient" problem when scaled to deeper architectures. In this study, we propose a robust, high-precision Deep Transfer Learning framework designed specifically for the multiclass staging of Alzheimer’s disease using structural Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI). We utilized a data set of T1-weighted magnetic resonance imaging scans from the Open Access Series of Imaging Studies (OASIS), rigorously balancing the distribution across four classes: Non-Demented, Very Mild Demented, Mild Demented, and Moderate Demented. To overcome the limitations of training deep networks on small medical datasets, we employed a ResNet50 architecture pre-trained on ImageNet. This extensive backbone leverages residual skip connections, allowing the network to learn identity mappings (F(x)+x) and effectively propagate gradients through 50 layers without degradation. We further customized the architecture by replacing the final classification head with a specialized block containing Global Average Pooling, discrete dropout layers (p = 0.5), and dense layers to prevent overfitting and enhance feature extraction capabilities. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a state-of-the-art overall classification accuracy of 94.56% and a macro-averaged F1-score of 94% on a held-out test set. Crucially, the model exhibits high sensitivity in distinguishing the ’Very Mild Demented’ class from ’Non-Demented’ controls, a task where traditional shallow networks and human radiologists frequently underperform. This superior performance validates the efficacy of deep residual learning in capturing non-linear, hierarchical patterns of cerebral atrophy. By automating the extraction of these complex biomarkers, our proposed model offers a reliable, scalable, and objective second opinion for clinicians, potentially varying the trajectory of patient care through earlier and more accurate diagnosis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves an overall multi-class accuracy of 94.56%, yielding a performance improvement of 1.38% to 5.16% over comparable state-of-the-art multi-class baseline models. Significantly, the model attains an exceptional specificity of 0.98 and high sensitivity for the ’Very Mild Demented’ stage, addressing a critical clinical need for reliable, early-stage Alzheimer’s detection when therapeutic interventions are most effective.

title: A High-Precision Power Amplifier with Weighted Multi-Objective Current-Loop Tuning method for High-Precision Linear Propulsion
authors: Sihang Cui, Mingyi Wang, Jiaxing Ye, Mengcui Bi, Haohang Zhai, Junchi Li, Liyi Li
abstract: High-precision linear propulsion systems require power amplifiers (PAs) that simultaneously provide ultra-low current distortion and high-bandwidth current tracking. However, in conventional switched PAs, dead-time effect and current zero-crossing distortion severely degrade current linearity, while existing controller tuning approaches often fail to explicitly balance closed-loop peaking, robustness, and bandwidth in digitally implemented high-frequency systems. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a high-precision switched PA featuring two coordinated innovations at both the power stage and control stage. First, an auxiliary-current commutation mechanism is introduced into the switched PA so that the MOSFET current is forced to reverse in every switching period. As a result, dead-time effect and current zero-crossing distortion are eliminated at the source, while ZVS-ON is achieved and the equivalent switching frequency is increased. Second, to fully exploit the improved power stage, a weighted multi-objective controller parameter tuning method is proposed. The method establishes a discrete-time current-loop model consistent with the practical implementation and jointly constrains the closed-loop peak, phase margin, sensitivity, complementary sensitivity, and bandwidth, so as to achieve a balanced tradeoff between tracking accuracy, dynamic response, and robustness. Experimental results verify an equivalent switching frequency of 1 MHz, a minimum current THD of 0.03%, a closed-loop tracking error within 0.34dB below 10kHz, and a maximum achievable bandwidth of 14kHz. These results demonstrate that the proposed PA provides a practical solution for precision-oriented linear propulsion applications requiring both ultra-low distortion and high dynamic current tracking.

title: Adaptive Gated Multimodal Fusion for Explainable and Uncertainty-Aware Tumor Grade Assessment
authors: B Uthra, R Sivashankari
abstract: Deep learning models hold potential progress for automating glioma grading, but their limited interpretability and unreliable confidence estimation hinder clinical adoption. In this work, a trustworthy multimodal deep learning framework is proposed that integrates clinical demographics and genomic mutation profiles to predict glioma grade. A Dynamic Gated Fusion Network is introduced to learn patient-specific weighting between clinical and molecular modalities. To reduce overfitting and derive a clinically meaningful gene panel, Mutual Information-based feature selection is employed, retaining the ten most informative mutations. A systematic pipeline consisting of Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation, Temperature Scaling for probability calibration, and SHapley Additive exPlanations for post-hoc explainability was performed to enhance the trustworthiness. The model was evaluated on a publicly available glioma grading dataset obtained from the University of California Irvine repository, derived from The Cancer Genome Atlas, using five-fold stratified cross-validation. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 0.864, an F1 score of 0.843, and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.919, while producing calibrated confidence estimates with an expected calibration error of 0.184. These results highlight the capability of patient-adaptive fusion and reliability-enhancing strategies to bridge the gap between algorithmic performance and real-world clinical deployment.

title: Phase-Aware Cardiac Edge Recognition for Physiologically Informed CI Estimation in AIoT Sensing Systems
authors: Chiung-An Cheng, Tsung-Yi Chen, Mei-Ling Chan, Yu-Hung Fang, Po-Lin Huang, Chen-Wei Chang, Yuan-Jin Lin, Patricia Angela R. Abu, Wei-Chen Tu
abstract: Energy-efficient bio-integrated sensing systems for edge AI must operate under strict constraints on sensing availability, computational resources, and hardware area. This work presents Phase-Aware Cardiac Edge Recognition (PACER), a physiologically informed framework addressing these challenges through software contributions and a hardware-efficient edge implementation for non-invasive cardiac index (CI) estimation in AIoT sensing systems. In this study, data were acquired using Terumo and PhysioFlow platforms for rigorous validation. First, under resource-constrained conditions, a single- stage neural model is optimized to minimize input dimensionality. Compared with state-of-the-art approaches requiring multiple parameters, this proposed model achieves CI estimation using only two physiological parameters, demonstrating a 30% performance improvement with 96% accuracy and a low RMSE of approximately 0.29. Second, for scenarios with insufficient measurements, the PACER architecture introduces a calibration-based strategy. Despite reduced sensing information, PACER maintains a high prediction accuracy of 96% with an RMSE of approximately 0.29, significantly outperforming a multivariate linear regression baseline which achieves only 90% accuracy with an RMSE of 0.42. On the hardware side, the PACER framework is fully implemented on an FPGA platform using fixed-point arithmetic. The design achieves high-speed, low-latency inference with approximately 96% hardware-level accuracy, exhibiting close agreement with software results. Notably, it reduces logic and memory resource usage by nearly two-thirds compared with existing designs. These results validate PACER as a practical and scalable solution for energy-efficient bio-integrated sensing in edge AIoT healthcare applications.

title: ResNet-Driven Channel Spreading Function Estimation for High-Mobility OFDM Systems
authors: Xuebo Pang, Yiyan Ma, Jingli Li, Hao Zhou, Xiaosong Liu, Daiming Guo, Ruiqing Cai, Yun Zhao, Yujia Zhai, Guoyu Ma, Bo Ai
abstract: Channel estimation in high-mobility orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) systems is often challenged by limited accuracy, high computational complexity, and a heavy reliance on prior channel knowledge. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel delay-Doppler (DD) domain channel estimation approach based on deep residual networks (ResNet). By exploiting the stationary property of the channel spreading function (CSF) in the DD-domain, we transform the estimation of the time-varying channel into a two-dimensional (2D) image reconstruction task. Specifically, a framework integrating ResNet with image processing techniques is developed to accurately reconstruct DD-domain channel images, effectively mitigating the impact of off-grid Doppler shifts. The time-frequency (TF) domain channel transfer function is then derived from the estimated CSF. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms the linear minimum mean-square error (MMSE) and shallow CNN-based estimators with respect to mean-square error (MSE) and bit error rate (BER), while offering a substantial reduction in computational time.

title: Interface-Based Design and Implementation of the Master Control System for ITER AC/DC Converters
authors: Jihyun Choi, Inho Song, Jong-Seok Oh, Jaehak Suh, Chungsan Lee
abstract: The master control system (MCS) for ITER AC/DC converters plays a key role in supervising converter operation and coordinating plant-level control while serving as the interface between the ITER central system and plant subsystems. As installation progresses at the ITER site, integration between the MCS and circuit controllers (CCRs) becomes an essential prerequisite for commissioning. The MCS is currently under preparation for integration and commissioning. This article presents the design and implementation of the MCS based on an interface-driven approach, focusing on consistent interaction between central and local controllers in a multisupplier environment. The proposed design emphasizes systematic interface definition, including real-time communication, coordinated state machine operation, and structured data exchange, thereby reducing ambiguity and improving overall system consistency. In addition, staged operational procedures enable safe and progressive validation of control and communication functions prior to full system commissioning. This work shows that systematic interface design is essential for achieving integration readiness in large-scale distributed control systems and provides practical insights to support subsequent integration and commissioning of ITER AC/DC converters and similar multisupplier fusion power supply systems.

title: OffsetCrust: Variable-Radius Offset Approximation with Power Diagrams
authors: Zihan Zhao, Pengfei Wang, Minfeng Xu, Shuangmin Chen, Shiqing Xin, Changhe Tu, Wenping Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Offset surfaces, defined as the Minkowski sum of a base surface and a rolling ball, play a crucial role in geometry processing, with applications ranging from coverage motion planning to brush modeling. While considerable progress has been made in computing constant radius offset surfaces, computing variable-radius offset surfaces re mains a challenging problem. In this paper, we present OffsetCrust, a novel framework that efficiently addresses the variable-radius off setting problem by computing a power diagram. Let R denote the radius function defined on the base surface S. The power diagram is constructed from contributing sites, consisting of carefully sampled base points on S and their corresponding off-surface points, displaced along R-dependent directions. In the constant-radius case only, these displacement directions align exactly with the surface normals of S. Moreover, our method mitigates the misalignment issues commonly seen in crust-based approaches through a lightweight fine-tuning procedure. We validate the accuracy and efficiency of OffsetCrust through extensive experiments, and demonstrate its practical utility in applications such as reconstructing original boundary surfaces from medial axis transform (MAT) representations.

title: A Bayesian joint inversion framework of Martian subsurface dielectric permittivity and loss tangent using SHARAD data
authors: Peng Fang, Jinhai Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The inversion of orbital radar sounder data for Martian subsurface dielectric properties is a fundamentally illposed problem, hampered by a strong ambiguity between the relative permittivity (ε′) and loss tangent (tanδ), as both affect the echo power. This obstacle complicates the accurate estimation of dielectric properties using radar echo power data, thereby impeding our understanding of subsurface composition and stratigraphy. Here, we propose a novel Bayesian framework of joint inversion of subsurface dielectric permittivity and loss tangent using sounding radar data. Our method utilizes a Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) framework and, crucially, constrains the loss tangent by incorporating a physics-based, informative prior derived from frequency-domain spectral ratio analysis. Synthetic data experiments demonstrate that the use of spectral ratio prior significantly improve the accuracy and convergence in the posterior probability distribution of ε′ compared to an unconstrained inversion. Tests on SHARAD data in Elysium Planitia show that the proposed method robustly distinguishes between layered subsurface material, characterized by a well-constrained upper layer with ε′ of 5.6±0.0012 and tanδ of 0.013±0.0002, and a lower layer with ε′ of 11.2±0.014 and tanδ of 0.0129±0.001. The proposed framework provides a rigorous tool for the quantitative analysis of the Martian subsurface, enabling more precise constraints on material composition, density, and the potential distribution of volatiles.

title: Cross-Modal Spatial-Temporal-Semantic Adaptive Network for Coal-Rock Cutting Events Localization
authors: Qian Xu, Yunxia Wu, Hailan Zhang, Zhengyang Zou, Zhihan Wang
abstract: The task of coal-rock cutting event localization is to locate the start and end time of both visible and audible cutting events through video sensors, as well as identify the coal-rock cutting categories. The development of video sensors equipped with functionalities to localize coal-rock cutting events is crucial for the intelligent development of coal mines. However, challenges such as coal dust and equipment noise compromise the discriminability of visual and audio features of cutting events, consequently diminishing the accuracy of event localization. To mitigate these challenges, we propose the Cross-Modal Spatial-Temporal-Semantic Adaptive Network (CM-STSAN). By introducing the Cross-Modal Spatial-Temporal-Semantic Adapter (CM-STSA) into the frozen pre-trained Swin Transformer (Swin-T) model, which enhances the representation of visual and audio features, thereby improving the accuracy of coal-rock cutting event localization. Specifically, initially, a modality-specific temporal relation module is employed to capture the temporal dependencies between the cutting sound source and the cutting action. Subsequently, a spatial audio-visual interaction fusion module is designed to utilize the clues of the opposite modality to achieve cross-modal feature alignment. Lastly, a semantic audio-visual interaction fusion module is devised to use the shared audio-visual semantic interaction to supports accurate inference of cutting semantic. In the latter two steps, an audio-visual attention interaction module dynamically adjusts the weighting of cross-modal features via an attention gating mechanism, effectively suppressing interference from coal dust and background noise. Our methodology verifies the accuracy of coal-rock cutting events localization on the self-bulit Audio-Visual Cutting Event (AVCE) dataset. Furthermore, validation on the publicly available Audio-Visual Event (AVE) dataset demonstrates that our CM-STSAN achieves state-of-the-art results in AVE localization tasks.

title: Combinatorial Admissibility in Control-Affine Networks
authors: Daniel Zelazo, Louis Theran
abstract: We study synchronization of heterogeneous control-affine nonlinear agents interconnected through diffusive (relative-output) measurements. We separate the design into an edge-space step—specifying a stabilizing model evolution for relative outputs—and a lift step—realizing the prescribed edge motion using the agents’ allowable input directions, constrained by the control-affine geometry of the agents. We introduce an admissibility notion that characterizes when an edge-driven diffusive design is feasible. We derive checkable combinatorial certificates that connect graph topology and actuation limits directly to admissibility, so that feasible edge dynamics can be verified in a practical and transparent way. The results are illustrated on synchronization of nonlinear oscillators.

title: From Traditional SLA Management to Blockchain-Enabled Smart Contracts: A Survey
authors: Riham Badra, Lazhar Hamel, Layth Sliman
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The rapid evolution of 5G and the growing complexity of digital services have increased the need for robust, transparent, and automated Service Level Agreement (SLA) management. Traditional management methods across different sectors like telecommunications and cloud computing frequently suffer from a lack of transparency and a heavy reliance on centralized third parties, which can lead to disputes and delayed enforcement. To address these challenges, smart contracts using blockchain technology offer a transformative solution, automating SLA execution and ensuring decentralized, tamper-proof enforcement. This paper provides a comprehensive survey and critical analysis of existing traditional SLA management systems and smart contract-based management. By studying and examining various frameworks across 5G, cloud computing, IoT, and telecommunications, we highlight common strategies, technical trade-offs, and existing gaps in current research. Through a structured classification and comparative analysis, this study offers an overview of blockchain-based SLA management current state while providing a strategic roadmap for the future of SLA representation and automated lifecycle management.

title: An Asymmetric Position/Force Cooperative Control Method for Dual-Arm Manipulators Based on Visual Feedback Under Multi-Source Errors
authors: Yi Wu, Xiaohui Jia, Jingzheng Zhou, Jinyue Liu, Yang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Autonomous dual-arm robotic systems often experience degraded position and force tracking accuracy during cooperative manipulation due to accumulated kinematic and hand-eye calibration errors. This study introduces a vision-based cooperative control approach for tightly coordinated tasks that ensures precise trajectory tracking and force regulation. First, kinematic and dynamic models of the system are formulated to decouple internal and external forces. Furthermore, an asymmetric control scheme is designed in which one arm employs visual feedback to compensate for object-level trajectory tracking errors, while the other is dedicated to internal force regulation; meanwhile, the object-level compliant motion generated by an external force controller is provided as feedforward to the dual arms with closed-chain consistency. Within this framework, the visual feedback adopts a specific design that takes the relative pose residual of auxiliary markers as the feedback signal to resist the influence of multi-source errors. Subsequently, Lyapunov stability theory is employed to validate the convergence of the proposed control methods. Finally, through simulations and physical experiments involving a board transport task with a dual-arm system, the effectiveness of the proposed approach in cooperative position tracking and force control is validated.

title: A Low-Power Hybrid BJT–CMOS Voltage Reference for 0 °C–170 °C Operation
authors: Yuyang Li, Jianyu Sun, Yun-Jae Won, Inhee Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper proposes a low-power voltage reference that generates 736 mV over a temperature range of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$170~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, targeting low-power, high-temperature miniature sensing systems. Operating in the subthreshold region, a bipolar junction transistor (BJT) diode produces a process-insensitive complementary-to-absolute-temperature (CTAT) voltage, while stacked complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) transistors provide temperature compensation by adding a proportional-to-absolute-temperature (PTAT) voltage. Measurements from 76 samples across three wafers fabricated in a 180-nm CMOS process demonstrate a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm 3\sigma $</tex-math> </inline-formula> inaccuracy of 3.64% from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$170~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C without trimming. The reference consumes 31 pW at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$27~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and 113 nW at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$170~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C from a 0.9 V supply. Compared with the state-of-the-art voltage reference covering <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$170~^{\mathrm {o}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, it consumes <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.36\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> less power, achieves a 50% lower minimum supply voltage (0.9 V), and reduces the average temperature coefficient (TC) by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.37\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> without per-chip trimming (27 ppm/<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sup>C).

title: Weather Uncertainty Prediction in Marine-Based Construction Project Using Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM)
authors: Muhammad Mu’az Amran, Nor Azuana Ramli, Sahimel Azwal Sulaiman
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Weather uncertainty has a significant impact on marine-based construction projects, leading to challenges such as schedule delays, increased costs, and safety concerns. Predicting sea phenomena is crucial because factors like tidal levels, rainfall, and wind speed contribute to uncertainty and risks. This study aims to apply the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model to predict wind speed, rainfall, and tidal levels for marine-based construction scenarios. The performance of these models is evaluated using standard metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) to assess their accuracy in capturing weather uncertainties. The dataset for this study focuses on weather conditions in the Langkawi area. A preliminary study was conducted using a tidal, rainfall, and wind dataset obtained from the Permanent Service Mean Sea Level (PSMSL) website and the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). The rainfall prediction model demonstrated limited accuracy, as indicated by a low R -squared $\left(\mathrm{R}^{2}\right)$ score and visible prediction errors. The same applies to the wind speed model. Nevertheless, it offered practical value for planning wind-sensitive operations. In contrast, the tidal model achieved high prediction accuracy and provided consistent and reliable risk assessments, with an $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ Score of 0.9891, which demonstrates that the model explains almost 99% of the accuracy in the tidal level heights. This high $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ value confirms the model’s ability to capture the essential patterns in the data. This study has the potential to serve as an alternative approach in weather forecasting research, particularly in predicting tidal uncertainties during operations in offshore and coastal construction projects.

title: Coupled Multiphysics Simulation and Spatial Position Control of Water Hydraulic Manipulator Driven by High-Speed On/Off Valves
authors: Xing Yang, Guanghao Ren, Cong Xue, Xujun Ye, Defa Wu, Yinshui Liu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: Water hydraulic high-speed on/off valves (WHSVs) feature excellent sealing performance, strong contamination resistance, and rapid dynamic response. WHSV can withstand the impact and wear caused by using water as the working medium. When WHSVs are employed as control elements for the joints of a hydraulic manipulator, the overall reliability and service life of the manipulator can be significantly enhanced. In this study, a four-in-one WHSV group is designed to replace the conventional four-way three-position servo valve, enabling precise motion control of the actuator. Based on the configuration of the water hydraulic manipulator, a coupled multiphysics simulation model integrating electromagnetic, hydraulic, structural, and control domains is established. The proposed model not only provides a digital-twin visualization of the manipulator in three-dimensional space but also captures its real physical characteristics, including the dynamic behavior of the hydraulic system and manipulator joints. Spatial trajectory planning of the manipulator end-effector is carried out, and each joint of the manipulator is driven along the planned path using a PID controller to achieve spatial position control. The simulation results show that the radius of the working space reaches 2.5 meters and the positioning accuracy reaches ±17.4 mm. The developed model effectively represents the manipulator’s physical behavior and verifies the feasibility of the proposed WHSV-controlled water hydraulic manipulator.

title: Embedding-Free Image Steganography Based on Diffusion Models
authors: Meiqi Liu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: In recent years, neural network-based image steganography technology has made significant progress. Currently, neural network-based image steganography typically uses an encoder-decoder network framework. That is, the secret information is embedded into the cover data through the encoding network, and then the hidden secret information is extracted from the steganographic image through the decoding network. However, this type of scheme still faces issues in practical applications, such as decoder exposure and poor visual quality of the steganographic images. This paper proposes a diffusion model-based embedding-free image steganography framework. Specifically, we use a diffusion model to construct the probability density function of the cover image and embed the secret information into specific positions of this distribution function through a deterministic mapping relationship with a fixed noise map (generated by an embedding key), rather than embedding it into the cover image itself. Here, the diffusion model is used to learn the local feature distribution of the cover image. By establishing a deterministic mapping relationship from the fixed noise map (generated by the embedding key) to the secret information during the local feature learning process, the secret information is hidden. The original diffusion model, as public information, can be accessed by the public, while only the recipient with the embedding key can extract the secret information. We validated the feasibility of this method from three dimensions: image quality, extraction accuracy, and model security. The results show that this scheme can effectively improve the accuracy of secret information extraction, and at the same time, it ensures model security without the need to train or transmit any decoding network.

title: Comparison and Simulation of power system harmonic and reactive current detection methods
authors: Qiangwei Chen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper investigates three detection methods for grid harmonic and reactive currents, and proposes a comparison of the detection efficiency of the instantaneous reactive power theory algorithm, the Fryze theory algorithm, and the Fourier series algorithm by analyzing the variance of the difference between detected values and actual values. Based on the harmonic and reactive currents generated by a three-phase bridge fully-controlled rectifier circuit, MATLAB/Simulink was used to simulate and analyze the aforementioned three detection methods. The simulation results indicate that, under the premise of no distortion in the grid voltage, all three methods can accurately detect grid harmonic and reactive currents. However, the instantaneous reactive power theory algorithm and the Fryze theory algorithm demonstrate better detection efficiency compared to the third method.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Sentiment Analysis System for Public Opinion Monitoring
authors: M. Arunmozhi, P. Nithya, Sridhar Yerram, Nayani Sateesh, Vinod Bagga, Mahendra Singh Yadav
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: This research shows a sophisticated machine learning system for amazingly real-time opinion monitoring for the public using sophisticated sentiment analysis. The system is based on transformer-based architectures and, in particular, fine-tuned versions of BERT and of RoBERTa in tandem with attention mechanisms to capture morphologic expressions of emotions as well as contextual dependency in content of social media data. We adopt a hybrid an appliance of integrating large language models (LLMs), such as GPT-4, for the zero-shot classification and PyTorch and Hugging Face Transformers for efficient model deployment. The framework consists of the utilization of aspect-based sentiment analysis with BiLSTM-CRF networks and is based on the streaming analytics service with Apache Kafka for real-time analysis. Research validation in tweet and reddit data 94.2% accuracy in Multi-class sentiment classification. The system has an interactive dashboard using Streamlit, which enables dynamic visualization of sentiment trends, topic modeling using BERTopic, and negroes anomalies of the emerging opinion shifts in the public discourse.

title: Design of the Bahrain Primary Healthcare Centers Clinical Notes Dataset
authors: Hasan Abdulla, Nabil Hewahi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: To address the paucity of publicly available clinical text corpora from the Middle East, this study presents the Bahrain PHC Clinical Text Dataset. The dataset comprises de-identified free-text electronic health record (EHR) notes from Primary Healthcare Centres (PHC) within Bahrain’s ISeha system, with each note mapped to one or more International Statistical Classification of Diseases and Related Health Problems 10th Revision (ICD-10) diagnostic labels. A reproducible, three-stage preprocessing pipeline systematically de-identifies patient information, normalizes text, and harmonizes relevant structured fields. Exploratory data analysis characterizes the distribution of diagnoses and patient demographics, supporting the dataset’s quality and representativeness. The corpus is suitable for a broad range of ML/AI applications, including multi-label diagnosis prediction, information extraction (e.g., symptom/problem detection), comorbidity and co-occurrence modelling, concept normalization and terminology mapping, and pre-training or domain adaptation of clinical language models for primary-care settings, with natural support for long-tail and class-imbalance research. The dataset is publicly available in the Harvard Dataverse (DOI: 10.7910/DVN/ZZXKKH) under the Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 4.0 International (CC BY-NC 4.0) license, with documented versioning and a changelog to enable transparent and reproducible research in clinical NLP.

title: An Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Hybrid Quantum-Classical Learning Framework for Drug Discovery
authors: A. Santhosh Nantha, M. Jayalakshmi, K. Maharajan, Hui KaiSu, Sanmugasundaram R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Quantum machine learning (QML) has attracted significant interest in drug discovery due to its potential to model complex molecular interactions and improve prediction accuracy for protein-ligand binding affinity. However, most existing hybrid quantum-classical approaches rely on static architectures that apply quantum computation uniformly across all samples, regardless of molecular complexity or prediction confidence. Such static execution strategies lead to inefficient quantum resource utilization and limit scalability on near-term noisy intermediatescale quantum (NISQ) devices. In this paper, we propose an uncertainty-aware adaptive hybrid quantum-classical learning framework for protein-ligand binding affinity prediction that treats quantum computation as a selectively invoked resource rather than a constant component of the learning pipeline. The proposed framework first employs a classical ensemble-based regression model to generate binding affinity predictions along with an associated uncertainty estimate derived from ensemble variance. This uncertainty information is then used by a decision mechanism to dynamically select between classical-only inference, shallow quantum circuit execution, or deep quantum circuit execution. By adapting the quantum circuit depth and invocation frequency to the predicted uncertainty, the framework enables decision-centric allocation of quantum resources. Experimental evaluation of binding affinity data demonstrates that the recommended adaptive framework utilizes around 76 % less quantum resources than static hybrid quantum learning methods that use fixed-depth quantum circuits for all samples. The framework also preserves decision-level functionality and a steady inference workflow. These findings highlight the effectiveness of uncertainty-guided adaptivity as a practical strategy and offer a scalable foundation for future quantum-enhanced molecular learning.method for incorporating QML into drug development pipelines while taking hardware constraints into consideration.

title: Analysing Workplace Spirituality and Emotional Stability on the Commitment of Guest Lecturers
authors: K.S. Sivakumar, Madhumita G
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This study discovers how workplace spirituality and emotional stability contribute to raising a sense of commitment among guest lecturers, thereby influencing their satisfaction, performance, and well-being. Workplace spirituality and emotional stability has an important role in shaping commitment of guest lecturers in government arts and science colleges. Workplace spirituality improves respect, trust, and association with personal values. When lecturers feel spiritually attached with their institutions, improves job satisfaction and creates more fulfilling work experience, which can lead to stronger commitment. Emotional stability is an ability to maintain composure, manage stress, and navigate workplace challenges effectively. One -way Anova and structural equation modelling was adopted this study to find the association between workplace spirituality and the productivity of guest lecturers. Lecturers who possess high emotional stability are better equipped to handle pressures connected with contract-based roles, such as lack of long-term benefits or job insecurity, leading to greater flexibility and a stronger emotional investment in their work. These factors raising a positive work culture, increase job commitment, improving lecturers’ performance and retention in government colleges.

title: Magnetic Induction Tomography of Unidirectional CFRP Delamination Defects Based on Projection Tikhonov
authors: Honggui Cao, Bo Ye, Yangkun Zou, Siqi Luo, Hongbin Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Delamination defects generated during the production and service process of unidirectional Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polymer (CFRP) have the effect of seriously affecting the material properties. A planar coil array Magnetic Induction Tomography (MIT) method is developed to tackle the challenge of detecting delamination defects in unidirectional CFRP, an eight-layer unidirectional CFRP model is established using COMSOL and a planar electromagnetic sensor array containing 9 coils is designed, the model is used to calculate the voltage measurement value and the sensitivity matrix. In consideration of the physical significance of conductivity in the image reconstruction process, a projected Tikhonov regularization algorithm is proposed to perform MIT on delamination defects at varying positions. Simulation results demonstrate that the MIT approach can effectively visualize delamination defects in unidirectional CFRP. The proposed projected Tikhonov algorithm delivers better imaging effect, and producing reconstructed conductivity distributions that more accurately reflect the true defect patterns within the unidirectional CFRP.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Business and Industry: A Strategic Framework for People Analytics in Human Resource Management
authors: Ashutosh Verma, Abu Bashar, Md. Shabbir Alam
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence has become deeply embedded in modern organizations, serving as a major driver of change. Among its many applications, People Analytics is one of the most impactful in Human Resource Management. Traditional methods and managerial instincts give way to AIenabled tools that offer more profound insight into workforce dynamics. Companies today use these tools to align talent strategy with business goals more accurately and effectively. Decision-making across the employee lifecycle is now faster and more precise. This shift is helping HR evolve into a strategic business partner rather than a supporting function. This paper takes a global and strategic view of how AI is being woven into core HR practices. It examines the influence of key technologies like machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and predictive modeling on the various facets of HR. Practical insights and ethical issues are explored through real-world examples from companies such as Unilever, IBM, and Google. While AI brings many opportunities, it also raises concerns related to data privacy, fairness, and algorithmic transparency. The study presents a people-centered framework, offering guidance for organizations seeking to create more efficient, inclusive, and resilient workplaces.

title: Edge AI Based Smart Traffic Control System for Ambulance Prioritization
authors: Sadashiv Badiger, Aditi Umesh Mahale, Abhishek Jaisingh Yadav, Aditya Pradeep Jadhav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Traffic within and around large hospital campuses has become increasingly challenging due to rising vehicle density and the critical need to ensure uninterrupted movement for emergency vehicles. Conventional centralized traffic control systems often fall short because of slow response times, scalability limitations, and dependence on remote processing. To address these issues, we propose a decentralized, edgeintelligent traffic signal control framework tailored for healthcare environments. The system deploys edge computing devices at hospital gates and nearby intersections to perform real-time traffic analysis directly at the edge. Using computer vision and sensor-based intelligence, the system dynamically adjusts signal timings, prioritizes ambulances, and efficiently manages both internal and external traffic flows without relying on a central controller. By combining edge AI, adaptive signal control, and real-time decision-making, the proposed solution significantly improves emergency response times, reduces congestion, and can be seamlessly integrated into existing hospital traffic infrastructures.

title: A Smart Hydroponic System for Sustainable and Health-Centric Agriculture
authors: Víctor José Huilca, Alexandra Macarena Flores, Juan Pablo Haro, Miguel Angel Osorio, Omar Darío Delgado
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: This work presents the design and implementation of an intelligent monitoring and control system for nutrient solutions in hydroponic crops, based on analog pH, electrical conductivity (EC) and temperature sensors, along with an ESP32 microcontroller. By fitting double exponential mathematical models, the pH and EC values are accurately estimated from the measured voltage. The system integrates with the Blynk platform for real-time visualization, sending alerts through mobile notifications, and remote control of a recirculation pump. The system was tested in real world conditions, demonstrating stable operation, low energy consumption, and ease of operation. This solution aligns with the principles of sustainability and digitalization proposed by the 5.0 Industry and Health paradigms, offering an accessible, efficient, and user-centered tool for smart agriculture.

title: Detection and regulation of residual stress in electron beam welded titanium alloy using ultrasonic approaches
authors: Xin-yan Wang, Jie Zhang, Cen-cheng Li, Peng-fei Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Titanium alloys are critical materials in the aviation industry due to their high strength-to-weight ratio and excellent corrosion resistance. Joining technologies are essential for the fabrication of large-scale critical components used in service. Electron beam welding is one of the most widely used techniques for joining metallic parts. Thermal gradients during welding typically lead to the formation of residual stresses. Residual stresses are self-equilibrating stresses that exist in materials when no external load is applied. These stresses may cause dimensional instability, deformation, or even cracking of the welded component during subsequent processing or service. The presence of undesirable residual stresses, such as tensile stresses, can significantly reduce the fatigue life of a component. Therefore, accurately determining and reducing the stresses is crucial for the manufacturing and performance of welded titanium components. In this study, ultrasonic nondestructive methods are employed to detect and regulate residual stresses in electron beam-welded Ti-6Al-4V components. Firstly, the mechanisms of ultrasonic detection and ultrasonic stress regulation are described. The ultrasonic longitudinal critically refracted wave detection method is applied to measure the average residual stress within a specific depth range of the component. High-energy ultrasonic stress relief method is employed to quickly and efficiently reduce residual stresses. After each ultrasonic treatment, the stresses in the welded component are reduced and homogenized. The experimental results demonstrate that, after several stress regulation treatments, the component reaches a low-stress state.

title: Hardware-Efficient Quantum Grid Sampling for Low-Latency Amplitude Estimation
authors: Yu-Ting Kao, Hao-Yu Lu, Yeong-Jar Chang, Jason Gemsun Young, Chao-Hung Wang, Darsen D. Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: High-performance computing heavily relies on Monte Carlo (MC) simulation, but achieving high precision imposes massive computational loads due to its low convergence rate. To overcome this classical bottleneck, Quantum Amplitude Estimation (QAE) has emerged as a powerful algorithmic alternative, theoretically offering a quadratic speedup by offloading these workloads to quantum accelerators. However, mitigating Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT) discretization errors typically require Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE). MLE introduces prohibitive iterative latency and struggles with shot-noise in low-measurement regimes. To address this, we propose a comprehensive, hardware-efficient framework that integrates uniform state preparation with a novel Peak Estimation Refinement (PER) readout technique. PER exploits the symmetric conjugate eigenvalues of the quantum operator to provide deterministic, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${O}(\mathrm{1})$</tex> probability extraction. This eliminates classical iterative optimization, achieving an approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$600 \times$</tex> reduction in post-processing latency while inherently amplifying the signal-to-noise margin to resist stochastic fluctuations in low-shot environments. Validated via an RTL-to-quantum compilation flow and a 10 -qubit <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi$</tex>-estimation synthesis benchmark, we demonstrate an estimation error of 0.007 %, proving the viability of migrating complex probabilistic workloads to real-time quantum accelerators

title: IoT-Enabled Internal Temperature Monitoring and Prophet-Based Forecasting for Outdoor Hydraulic Control Cabinets
authors: Yuxin Wu, Wei Liu, Gang Du, Xiaojia Xu, Yingxin Shen, Luyue Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: During the operation of outdoor hydraulic control cabinets, excessively high temperatures can easily cause equipment failure or malfunction, making temperature prediction essential. In recent years, severe fluctuations in ambient temperature and unstable climate conditions have increased thermal risks for outdoor equipment, making temperature management inside the cabinet even more critical. Therefore, this paper proposes a new IoT-based remote temperature prediction scheme for outdoor control cabinets. Using LTE Cat.1 wireless networking, the collected temperature data are transmitted quickly and reliably, addressing the traditional problems of weak field signals and difficult maintenance. The internal temperature of the cabinet is modeled using the Prophet time series model, which identifies the daily and seasonal variation patterns of temperature. Experimental results show that the proposed Prophet model achieves high prediction accuracy, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of about 3.17 and a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of about 16%. The model can help temperature control devices prevent overheating and avoid equipment failure caused by excessive temperature. Compared with traditional methods, this approach offers better accuracy and engineering applicability, providing reliable support for the intelligent operation and maintenance of outdoor hydraulic control cabinets.

title: Digital twins as a tool for data-driven decision making in pasture-based livestock production
authors: Diego Fernando Quiroga, Juan Pablo Franco, Yazmin Bentancour, Alicia Waller, Rocio Martinez
abstract: This study explores the role of Digital Twins (DTs) in transforming pasture-based livestock production through data-driven decision-making. A digital twin acts as a virtual real-time replica of physical pasture systems, integrating continuous data from IoT sensors. In pasture management, DTs provide a user-friendly interface where farmers can visualize spatial variability, monitor pasture growth, and simulate grazing results. This usability supports timely decisions regarding rotation schedules, forage allocation, and stocking rates. By incorporating predictive analytics and machine learning, DTs enable scenario testing for climate events, resource limitations, or herd movements. The result is improved efficiency in feed utilization, enhanced animal welfare, and better adaptation to environmental challenges. The study highlights the potential of DTs to make precision agriculture accessible at the field level, and a case study is presented and modeled using SysML; challenges remain in data integration, model calibration, and farmer adoption. Also, usability emerges as a key factor in ensuring meaningful, actionable insights that support sustainable livestock systems.

title: Predicting Stock Volatility in the Context of Digital Trade Barriers: Leveraging Big Data and AI in Financial Market Forecasting
authors: Jiyuan Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Digital trade barriers have been a major factor which has impacted the international financial markets leading to the volatility of the prices of stocks and unstable exchange rates minted. This study used machine learning techniques based on trading stock volatilities using machine learning algorithms like Random Forest algorithm Regression, Support Vector Machine algorithm (SVM), and XGBoost in the context of digital trade barriers that help in predicting stock volatility. The research employs simulated data for the prices of stock, trade barriers and volatility across five years to predict stock volatility using machine learning models with the features of stock prices, trade barriers, and volatility. According to the findings, SVM is outperformed by both Random Forest and XGBoost in terms of prediction accuracy with Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.3524 and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.4576. The time series graphs of stock price, trade barriers and volatility are visualised. This study further concludes that SVM is the most suitable model for predicting volatility in such a context. The only thing it suggests is to consider further features that may lead to higher prediction accuracy such as economic indicators. The implication is practical, and it suggests that financial institutions can leverage machinelearning models to more predictively predict stock volatility to adopt a risk management strategy. Nevertheless, the drawback of using simulated data as opposed to real-world market data is accepted, and it is suggested that the models be validated further with actual data.

title: AI-Driven Personalities and Consumer Behaviour: Understanding How Virtual Influencers Shape Gen Z’s Digital Engagement
authors: Lalrengpuii, Manish Kumar Srivastava, Jitender Bhandari, Harendra Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: As Virtual Influencers continue to be introduced into the Digital Marketing space particularly for GenZ, this study of 751 Gen Z Instagram Users in Bengaluru, India is an attempt to assess which Qualities (such as Trustworthiness, Attractiveness, Similarity, Human-Like Appearance) will affect both user Engagement and Purchase Behaviour when it comes to virtual influencers. The results found that users tend to engage with and make purchases from virtual influencers who they perceive as trustworthy, attractive, and similar to themselves. The sense of a personal (parasocial) connection appears key in driving this engagement. So far, trust and relational connection matter more than the influencer’s authenticity or expertise. This research helps marketers understand how virtual influencers can foster meaningful engagement and influence purchase decisions among younger audiences.

title: Unsupervised Time-Series Anomaly Detection with Hyperdimensional Computing
authors: Alaaddin Goktug Ayar, Martin Margala
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Unsupervised time-series anomaly detection is essential for monitoring complex systems where labeled fault data are scarce and system behavior evolves over time. This work investigates the effectiveness of Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) for unsupervised anomaly detection in multivariate time-series data, with a focus on degradation monitoring. We propose a similarity-driven HDC framework that encodes multivariate sensor measurements into high-dimensional binary hypervectors using random feature and value representations. Normal operating behavior is modeled through a bundled prototype hypervector constructed from early-life data, while incoming time windows are encoded with the same mapping and compared to this prototype using a bitwise similarity measure to produce a continuous health score. We evaluate the method on a benchmark turbofan engine degradation dataset (C-MAPSS FD001), which provides multivariate run-to-degradation trajectories. Results show that the HDC similarity signal captures degradation trends over time and provides strong anomaly separability without supervised labels or deep neural architectures. The study demonstrates that Hyperdimensional Computing offers a lightweight, interpretable, and scalable approach for unsupervised monitoring of multivariate time-series degradation processes.

title: Optimizing Grocery Shelf Displays through Deep Feature Extraction and Ensemble Classification
authors: Anju B. Nandrajog, Sahil Nayyar, Amritpal Kaur, Abhishek Bhattacherjee, Sargam
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The displays in shelves are extremely important in retail performance, where most of the decisions involving purchases are made at the point of sale. The use of manual planogram audits is expensive and tedious, and might have many errors, which explains the necessity of automated solutions. The given work presents a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Random Forest (CNN-RF) model of grocery shelf optimisation, which combines the visual feature extraction with the interpretable ensemble decision-making. It was aimed at identifying planogram compliance and anticipating the sales uplift in the store conditions. An 8,000 annotated shelf image dataset, in combination with $\mathbf{6}$ months of sales data of $\mathbf{5 0}$ outlets, was employed. CNN (ResNet-50 backbone) features were spatial product and layout features, and compliance (binary) and sales uplift (low, medium, high). The model was trained on 70/15/15 store-independent splits and then was validated with paired statistical tests. The proposed hybrid had compliance accuracy $94.3 \%$ which beats CNN-only (91.7) and RF-only (88.5) baselines. It was robust and its precision $(93.5 \%)$, recall $(92.8 \%)$, and macro-F1 (92.6) were confirmed, and AUC $=0.95$. Practical implementation in 10 locations showed a $12.8 \%$ average response in sales, with beverages (14.3) and cereals (12.1) having the largest increase.These results make CNN-RF model a statistically validated, interpretable and practically effective tool to optimally organize shelves and allow retailers to improve compliance, product placement, and sales results on a large scale.

title: Object Detection Using Dynamic Channel with Multiscale Attention Enabled Generative Adversarial Network Model
authors: Preeti, Shashidhar Sonnad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Background: Object detection plays a significant role in pinpointing objects in images and videos, which drives applications across diverse industries by enabling automation, enhancing safety, and improving user experiences. The existing object detection frameworks have struggled with various drawbacks, including the lack of ability to detect tiny or hidden objects, restricted datasets, and computational complexities. Objective: The research proposes an Optimized Dynamic Channel with Multiscale Attention enabled Generative Adversarial Network (ODMGAN) model to perform effective object detection. In essence, the ODMGAN model demonstrated refined ability to locate and detect objects that are hidden. Besides, the utilization of the Prairie Dog Search Optimization (PrSO) algorithm effectively finetunes of the hyperparameters of the model. Additionally, the dynamic channel with multiscale attention (DCMAtt) mechanism enables the detection of hidden objects effectively. Finding: The experimental accomplishments indicate that the ODMGAN accomplished greater performance through achieving a greater accuracy of 97.59 %, 0.98 Intersection over union, 97.88 % sensitivity, and 97.30% specificity, which is extremely higher when compared with the existing frameworks.

title: Integration of Machine Learning Algorithms in Predictive Maintenance of Mechanical Systems
authors: Jyoti Prakash, Mohammed Asad Bilal, Somadatta B. Karanjekar, Muhammad Saqib, Subhash Malik, K K Dhande
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Predictive maintenance has emerged as a crucial strategy in controlling mechanical systems to reduce maintenance cost and losses. Machine learning algorithms are needed in this sphere since it is possible to predict the occurrence of equipment failures. This research aims at developing a new framework based on the latest technologies, including self-attention mechanisms, machine learning, and the Internet of Things. The predictive maintenance mechanism that uses the data was a data-driven mechanism and the tools were compared using the conventional and sophisticated tools. To monitor continuously three industrial motors, forty datasets were collected at the power plant. The results of the study revealed that in all the 8 experimental scenarios, the proposed framework resulted in a high-performance measure of 88.6. The rate was attributed to the optimisation mechanisms, self-attention mechanisms, and the long short-term memory architecture. The results of this research show the validity of using this process to predict the imminent equipment failure, as well as developing the right management strategies to handle the industrial motor failure. Among the key aspects of the framework development, there was the use of advanced tools. Predictive management on equipment failure relies on the use of scaled tools and optimisation methods as they allow the frameworks to uncover optimal characteristics on the sensor data.

title: IoT-Based Crop Health Prediction Using Hybrid CNN–LSTM Models
authors: Ritvik Dubey, Ekta Rao, Gurinder Singh, Naina Chaudhary, Anamica Singh, Rajneesh Kler
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The combination of Internet of Things (IoT) and deep learning devices is transforming the current agriculture by making it possible to make decisions promptly based on the data provided in real-time. The paper consists of the description of a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) model to predict crop health based on multimodal IoT data. The framework fuses spatial characteristics of images obtained by drones with their time-related environmental data, including soil moisture, temperature, and humidity, obtained using IoT sensors. The CNN isolates visual representations indicating disease presence whereas the LSTM isolates dynamic changes in field conditions thus allowing the effective prediction of crop stress and disease outbreak. As experimental assessments on real-world datasets show, the proposed hybrid model is more accurate and convergent as opposed to single CNN or LSTM architectures. This works with precision agriculture in that early intervention is made possible, a decrease in the use of chemicals, and better predicting the yield all of which results in a sustainable and intelligent farming ecosystem.

title: Sustaining Culinary Heritage: Communicating Brand Heritage to Build Trust and Consumer Loyalty in an Indonesian Heritage Restaurant
authors: Michael Christian, Henilia Yulita, Theresia Fransisca, Oktafalia Marisa Muzammil, Kurnadi Gularso, Yudhiet Fajar Dewantara
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustaining Heritage: The Future of Conservation: Embracing Technology and Innovation for Sustainable Heritage (ICSH)
abstract: Brand heritage is a crucial aspect of brand management that highlights historical significance, continuity, and cultural symbols throughout generations. This study investigates the impact of brand heritage on brand trust and purchasing behavior, using Pagi Sore restaurant as a case study reflecting Indonesia’s culinary heritage. A quantitative methodology was employed through an online survey of 211 participants, with data analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) via SmartPLS 4. The findings indicate that brand heritage significantly influences brand trust and purchasing behavior. Moreover, brand trust has been demonstrated to moderate the impact of heritage on purchasing behavior, while heritage enhances the correlation between trust and buying behavior through slope analysis. The R-squared values indicate explanatory capacity of 31.3% for brand trust and 40.3% for purchasing behavior. This study theoretically reinforces the concepts of brand heritage, commitment-trust, and signaling. The findings underscore the necessity of preserving historical narratives, ensuring service consistency, and leveraging digital media to enhance consumer trust and loyalty. Beyond managerial and theoretical implications, this study contributes to Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities, by showing how heritage restaurants act as custodians of intangible cultural heritage and play a role in safeguarding cultural identity as part of sustainable urban development.

title: Enhanced Multi-Area Load Frequency Control using Hybrid Monarch-Wolf Optimization (HMWO) Algorithm
authors: Harendra K. Pushpat, Sanjiv Kumar Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Hybrid Monarch Wolf Optimization (HMWO) is a novel approach to LFC problem solving that has been developed for use in three-area interconnected power systems, including those including thermal generating, solar PV systems, electric cars, and energy storage. In order to effectively limit frequency deviations and tie-line power oscillations, the controller settings can be optimized using the suggested HMWO. The integration of ESS and EVs provides immediate power supply, which is great, but the system has oscillations due to its dynamic reaction. HMWO resolves all of these problems by making the system more stable and better at controlling itself. systematically examine the suggested methodology's performance to that that of conventional optimize techniques, we observe that the suggested methodology does better in terms of settling time, maximum overshoot, and integral error. The simulation results demonstrate that using the suggested HMWO-based control method reduces the changes in frequency caused by changes in demand and changes in renewable power. Studies show that the suggested strategy cuts the settling time by 25% and the peak overshoot by 30% compared to the usual approaches.

title: A Hybrid 6-DoF Pose Measurement System Using String Potentiometer and Dual IMUs
authors: Zhendai Huang, Zuyan Chen, Ameer Tamoor Khan, Yu Gan, Fei Hu, Juha Röning, Shuai Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: With the advancement of technology, six-degree-of-freedom (6-DoF) pose measurement plays an essential role in spatial metrology, yet existing methods often involve trade-offs between accuracy, cost, and deployment complexity. In particular, optical and inertial systems may require line-of-sight, extensive calibration, or high power consumption, limiting their use in resource-constrained settings. This paper proposes a novel hybrid 6-DoF measurement system that integrates a string potentiometer with dual inertial measurement units (IMUs). By combining cable extension sensing with orientation data through geometric modeling, the system enables real-time pose estimation without the need for external infrastructure. To validate the approach, experiments were conducted on objects with diverse geometries, including linear, angular, and curved features.
