The results show that the system consistently achieves millimeter-level spatial accuracy under standard indoor conditions, which suggests that the proposed method offers a practical and sustainable alternative for portable 6-DoF tracking. It may also inform the development of low-cost measurement tools in applied metrology.

title: Influencer Marketing and Its Impact on Sales
authors: Gaurav Wankhade, Divyanshi Goswami, Jagjeevan Kanojjiya, Smruti Patre, Anand Bhattad, Shailesh Rastogi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Influencer marketing is one of the most important forms of marketing in the 21st century and continues to influence the way consumers purchase products. In this study we want to understand how an individual’s social media presence influences the way their followers perceive products and ultimately make purchasing decisions based on the social media influences they have. In order to study this, we will collect data via a survey using Google Forms. The survey will collect information about how visual appeal affects purchases, how reliable the influencer's content is perceived to be, how responsive the influencer is to their audience's needs, how assured the audience feels about a product being endorsed by an influencer and how well a connection can be made between an influencer and their followers. We will analyze the survey results to better understand how effectively influencer marketing is creating sales. We anticipate providing valuable insights to marketers seeking to enhance consumer trust in influencer marketing, as well as to create better sales growth. This research will add to the body of digital marketing knowledge and enhance understanding of how influencer marketing fits into this category.

title: How does a Key Opinion Leader influence customers' booking intention at Online Travel Agencies? The Intermediary Role of Customer Trust
authors: Thi Thu Hoa Mai, Hung-Trong Van
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: The integration of Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) has emerged as a critical marketing strategy for promoting hotel services, yet empirical research examining their influence on booking intentions within Online Travel Agency (OTA) platforms remains limited. Therefore, this study focuses on investigating how KOLs gain trust from customers and thereby influence hotel booking intentions on OTA. Adopted the theoretical framework of S-O-R and tested through two stages of group discussion and online questionnaire survey with 273 valid votes.Based on a theoretical basis, this study examines KOL features: Reputation, Perceived Fit, and Production Involvement. The results after analyzing the CBSEM model show that Production Involvement with KOLs has the greatest impact on customer trust and creates motivation for booking intentions on the online travel agency platform, followed by the KOL's reputation factor. It is worth noting that perceived fit has no impact on customers'trust in KOLs in the hotel industry.

title: Osteoporosis Inference Using ResNet50 from Dental X-Rays
authors: Nikita Lad, Shruti Thakur, Archana Raut
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This study proposes an opportunistic screening framework using dental panoramic radiographs combined with a deep learning model based on ResNet50. A dataset of 474 radiographic images was utilized, comprising normal and osteoporotic cases. The methodology includes preprocessing, feature extraction using transfer learning, and classification using fully connected layers. Detailed training configurations such as batch size, dropout, and optimization strategies are incorporated to enhance reproducibility. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 96%, outperforming other deep learning architectures including VGG16, VGG19, DenseNet, and InceptionV3. The results demonstrate that dental radiographs can serve as an effective, non-invasive, and cost-efficient screening tool for early osteoporosis detection.

title: Pruning for Precision: Leveraging Feature Selection to Cultivate High-Performance Thyroid Diagnostic Models
authors: Sravanthi Goalla, I.V. Shashikala, T. Aditya Sai Srinivas, K. Dora Babu, M. Ramu, S. Rakshitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This paper examines the diagnostic barriers, in relation to thyroid-related diseases, namely hypothyroidism, which often exhibit non-specific clinical features, thus putting the patient at risk of delays in the treatment process. And to this, we have come up with a detailed machine-learning model to support accurate, early-detection. We test the effect of the three feature-selection methods, namely Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE), Univariate Feature Selection (UFS) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on the predictive performance of various classifiers, which are XGBoost, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine. Our experimental findings are that the synergistic approach of ensemble classifiers and the feature selection approach has a significant positive effect on accuracy, and the XGBoost-RFE configuration can achieve the highest accuracy (99.35) and the highest recall (94.74). The paper, therefore, provides a reproducible, high-accuracy diagnostic pipeline, which underscores the critical role of feature engineering in the development of reliable, interpretable tools to screen people with thyroid disease.

title: The Moral Margin: Why Ethics is the New Bottom Line in Financial Decision-Making
authors: Pranshu Sinha, Akansha Kaur Dhanjal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The implementation of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) and data-driven technologies into the financial systems has transformed the nature of conducting the financial decision-making processes itself. Dependence of Financial institutions on AI-based models to conduct their operations including credit scoring, fraud detection, algorithmic trading, investment advisory services, and risk management are increasing at an alarming rate. Even though these technologies have been in a position to enhance performance efficiency, forecasting, and cost minimization, several ethical issues are majorly involved with their adoption. Algorithms bias, a scarcity of transparency, the risks of data privacy, the absence of accountability and systemic financial instability challenges have all become a major concern to the policy makers, financial institutions, and researchers. The paper dwells upon the ethical dimension of the AI-based financial decision-making and introduces the concept of the Moral Margin, i.e. the additional economic value created through the ethical practices, responsible governance and socially conscious financial practices. Using the mixed method research design involving literature analysis, conceptual modelling and statistical analysis of AI adoption trends, this research examines the impact of ethical considerations on financial performance, stakeholder trust and risk management which reduces the technological risks and also creates sustainable long-term value. The paper concludes by stating that ethical governance and regulatory oversight is important to ensuring that AI technologies contribute towards inclusive and stable financial systems.

title: An AI-Integrated Framework for Early Autism Detection and Emotion Analysis Using a Deep Learning-Based Multi-Modal System
authors: Anupam Das, Prasant Kumar Pattnaik, Anjan Bandyopadhyay
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Neurodevelopmental disorder Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is characterized by a follow-up of social communication, interaction, and repetitive behaviors. A 2021 study published in the Indian Journal of Pediatrics suggests that about 1 in 68 children in India have autism which is about 1 in 100 children below 10 years old. This is a drastic rise to what was recorded in the 2011 Indian census as 1.3%. Due to the ever-increasing popularity of ASD, research in new methods and available tools of diagnosis has become urgent. To help address this need, we present "Autemo" artificial intelligence emotionally intelligent application that would help in the identification of people with autism and assess their emotional status. "Autemo" uses a pipeline of multiple stages: the VGG19 deep learning model will be used to determine the probability of having autism, then a linear regression analysis will be performed using answers given to a structured questionnaire to estimate the severity of their condition, and lastly it will use a convolutional neural network (CNN) to understand the emotional state of the user utilizing audio as the data source.

title: Early Detection of Pancreatic Cancer Using Machine Learning
authors: Neeraj Yadav, Sanket Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: The late diagnosis of pancreatic cancer becomes it most dangerous cancer. It has less survival rate due to late-stage diagnosis. This research was done to build a predictive model for early diagnosis of pancreatic cancer using certain biomarkers found in blood and urine. The model is developed using machine learning techniques to train models, such as logistic regression, decision tree, and an artificial neural network were used. The ANN model is a sophisticated architecture that can classify benign and malignant cases with an accuracy score and F1 score of 0.77 and 0.83 respectively, when its hyperparameters are optimized. This study demonstrates how ANN-based methods can improve pancreatic cancer detection, enable prompt intervention, and ultimately lead to better patient outcomes.

title: Towards Quantum-Safe TLS in Healthcare Systems
authors: Farhath Zareen, Jasmin Kaur, Deepti Joshi, Arpit Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: The increasing use of cloud-based Electronic Health Record systems and Internet of Medical Things workflows in the healthcare sector exposes classically protected, long-lived Protected Health Information to Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) attacks from future quantum computers. We present a framework that (1) identifies HNDL-exposed data flows using passive TLS metadata and assigns risk scores and (2) quantifies remediation cost through post-quantum (PQ) hybrid key exchange (KEX). Using a service-level testbed emulating key clinical and IoMT interfaces, we collect passive TLS metadata to label flows by quantum risk, and quantify the operational overhead of migrating high-risk flows to hybrid PQ KEX. Our findings indicate that PQ cryptographic signatures improve authentication but do not make TLS traffic HNDL-resistant when KEX remains classical. Controlled A/B testing demonstrates minimal overhead (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{3 ~ m s}$</tex> latency and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{2. 2 ~ K B}$</tex> per handshake), indicating that risk-prioritized PQ KEX migrations are operationally feasible in healthcare environments.

title: Explainable AI for Ethical Decision Support in Humanitarian Robotics
authors: Kumaran Ramaswamy, Ramy Riad Al-Fatlawy, G. Durga Sowjanya, Yogendra Kumar, Ravi Kalra, Sorabh Lakhanpal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The integration of robotics into humanitarian operations, encompassing search and rescue, disaster response, and aid delivery, presents a profound opportunity to mitigate human risk and enhance operational efficacy. However, the deployment of autonomous systems in such high-stakes, ethically sensitive environments necessitates decision-making processes that are not only robust but also transparent and accountable. Opaque "black-box" artificial intelligence models pose a significant barrier to trust and adoption, as they preclude critical oversight from human operators and stakeholders. This paper argues for the critical integration of Explainable AI (XAI) methodologies as a foundational component of ethical decision support systems in humanitarian robotics. We posit that XAI can bridge the trust deficit by providing interpretable rationales for autonomous decisions, thereby enabling human-in-the-loop oversight, facilitating post-hoc accountability, and ensuring that robotic actions align with established humanitarian principles and international law. By examining current XAI paradigms—including model-agnostic techniques, inherently interpretable models, and human-centric explanation interfaces—this research delineates a framework for embedding ethical reasoning into the autonomy stack. The ultimate objective is to foster the development of humanitarian robots that are not only operationally capable but also morally competent and trustworthy partners in safeguarding human dignity during crises.

title: Cybersecurity Analysis of a Modern Power Network
authors: Rajasi Mandal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Due to the digitization of the grid network, it has become vulnerable to cyberattacks. Consequently, the need for ‘cybersecurity’ has become essential to protect the power systems from cyberattacks. Therefore, the present work involves the design and validation of a cyber-attack-proof frequency controller. The system frequency of a power network gets diverted from the nominal value due to a discrepancy in generation and load, a reduction in the system inertia, fluctuations in weather conditions, etc. Therefore, a smart frequency controller has been proposed in this paper to keep the system frequency within acceptable limits. The working of the designed controller has been assessed against mismatches in loads, fluctuations in weather circumstances, sudden connections of solar and wind plants, etc. Additionally, the functionality of the controller proposed has been demonstrated against cyberattacks to ensure a reliable and resilient environment for the modern digitized grid-network.

title: Identification of Metabolites in Root Exudation Pattern of Efficient and Inefficient Genotypes of Groundnut –A design Thinking Approach
authors: K. Radhika, S. Meena, N. Vijayalakshmi, A. Sumithra, B. Sivashankari, K. Lokesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Groundnut is regarded as one of the major oilseed crops grown worldwide, with cultivation extending across nearly a hundred countries. Among the micronutrients required for its growth, zinc (Zn) is particularly vital because it supports key physiological processes that directly influence pod formation and overall yield. The present paper deals with root exudation pattern of Zn efficient and inefficient groundnut genotypes, sand culture experiment was carried out for 21 days with four genotypes of groundnut (VRI 8, ALR 2, VRI 2 and CO 2) with-three treatments (100% Zn (3 μM), 50% zinc (1.5 μM) and control). Results revealed that Distinct chromatographic pattern were found in the root exudation for the four different genotypes under different levels of Zn stress accumulating 19 metabolites consisting of carboxylic group alone. The amount of carboxylic acid-based metabolites has increased in the root exudates of efficient genotypes under stress condition. Under control condition, 11 metabolites pronounced, 8 under 50% zinc and 7 metabolites under 100% zinc stress condition. Among all the carboxylic group metabolites, acetic acid, propionic acid, and butanoic acid are commonly present in all genotypes.

title: Comparative Analysis of U-Net Regularization Strategies for Crop Weed Semantic Segmentation
authors: Vaibhav Dhore, Simant Patil, Vijay Sambhe
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Precise crop and weed segmentation is one of the challenging tasks in precision agriculture. Among various state-of-the-art models, UNet performs better. For further improvement of UNet, a regularization technique is implemented in the work. The dropout rate regularization technique is applied on UNet. The different values of dropout rate used for experimentation are 0%, 20%, 40%, and 60%. As we increase the dropout rate, performance is improved; the mean intersection over union of 91.77% is highest for dropout rate 60%. For other evaluation metrics, a dropout rate of 0% performs better. The dropout rate of 0% has highest for most of the performance metrics. The randomness in neuron selection makes the model robust. In the future, further improvement of UNet can be made using other regularization techniques.

title:
Phantom-Based Image Quality Assessment of the ImagingRing Scanner for Image-Guided
                    <sup>192</sup>
                    Ir High-Dose-Rate Brachytherapy planning
authors: Francesca Buonanno, Rocco Mottareale, Valentina d’Alesio, Cecilia Arrichiello, Gianluca Ametrano, Marcello Serra, Gaetano Gagliardo, Federica Gherardi, Eva Iannacone, Mario Petrazzuoli, Vincenzo Ravo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Standard brachytherapy treatment planning typically relies on simulation computed tomography (CT) images to delineate the tumor target and organs-at-risk, and to calculate the radiation dose distributions. Performing 3D image-guided planning for each treatment fraction is crucial to ensure accurate dose delivery, particularly in cases with anatomical variability or organ motion, such as those encountered in gynecological brachytherapy. To support this approach, a dedicated mobile cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT) scanner, the ImagingRing, was installed at our institution in November 2023. This system enabling image acquisition directly on the treatment couch, thereby eliminating patient transfers and facilitating real-time planning in high-dose-rate <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">192</sup>Ir brachytherapy workflows. To evaluate the system performance and monitor long-term stability, we established quality assurance (QA) protocol. Since installation, image quality assessments have been conducted at six-month intervals. During each session, CBCT images of a CatPhan phantom were acquired using dedicated protocols. Quantitative image quality metrics were analyzed, including Hounsfield Unit linearity, uniformity, noise, contrast-to noise ratio and spatial resolution. The latter was defined by the modulation transfer function (MTF) and reported as the spatial frequencies corresponding to 50% and 10% MTF. The ImagingRing system demonstrated stable image quality across all QA sessions. HU linearity and uniformity remained consistent, and spatial resolution was preserved over time. This innovative CBCT scanner may represent a key asset for adaptive gynecological brachytherapy planning. The ability to perform in-room imaging at the time of applicator insertion positively impacts patient comfort, minimizing workflow efficiency and supporting a more sustainable clinical resources. Future work should focus on the quantitative analysis of clinical images, in order to fine-tune acquisition parameters and optimize them for the anatomical and procedural variability of gynecological brachytherapy, beyond the limitations of standard preset protocols.

title: Optimizing Deep Learning Models for Real-Time Edge Computing Applications
authors: Mahadev, Anil Kumar, Rahul Kumar, Ashok Kumar, Jitendra Kumar Chaudhary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Recent advances in the convergence between deep learning and edge computing have presented new possibilities of intelligent decisions in real-time and nearby data sources. Nevertheless, the implementation of the complex neural networks on edge devices with limited resources is still a significant technical issue as it has high computational requirements, memory constraints, and energy inefficiency. In this paper, the author offers a detailed review of the new optimization principles that enable deep learning models to be applicable in the edge environments. It discusses the main methods, including pruning, quantization, distillation of knowledge, and hardware-constrained neural architecture design, which have the overall effect of decreasing latency and resource utilization without sacrificing accuracy. This paper identifies feasible solutions to scalable and efficient edge artificial intelligence (AI) systems by combining existing practices and considering the trade-offs of those practices. The results indicate the experiences of how the combination of algorithmic and hardware-level optimization can provide edge intelligence in real-time, allow privacy, energy saving, and sustainable functionality of AI implementations in contemporary uses.

title: Agentic AI Framework for Adaptive Distributed Computing Architecture
authors: E. Afreen Banu, Abhilash Nagilla, K. Pavithra, K. Nithya, P. S. Latha Mageshwari, Nellore Manoj Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Dynamic workloads, the heterogeneity of resources, and scalability are becoming more of a challenge to adaptive management of distributed computing systems. The proposed paper is an Agentic AI Framework of Adaptive Distributed Computing Architecture, which integrates an autonomous decision-making process with the use of data-driven intelligence to optimize system performance. The framework uses the sliding-window normalization and missing-value imputation to guarantee the reliability of the processing of real-time telemetry data received by the distributed nodes. Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR) is used to pick up the most informative system features which minimizes computational overhead yet maintains predictive accuracy. A model of system behavior is then created by an XGBoost classifier to predict resource contention and allow resource proactive and adaptive control. Kubernetes is used to implement and assess the framework with the help of dynamic orchestration, scaling, and fault recovery. The outcomes of simulation show that the resources are used better, the response time is shorter, and the system is less prone to failure than using the traditional methods. The given solution provides a scalable and pragmatic intelligent distributed computing environment architecture.

title: Distributed Virtual Impedances for DC Bus Stabilization of Bidirectional Multiport Power Electronics Interface Systems
authors: Pengyuan Chen, Mariana Haddadin, Mohammad Qasem, Mahesh Krishnamurth
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: As power supply systems for advanced propulsion, energy storage, and computation become increasingly complex and versatile, their internal DC buses are often noisy and prone to unstable dynamic behavior. This issue is well recognized in applications ranging from grid-tied and islanded energy systems to data center power plants, electric vehicles (EVs), and electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft. To ensure stable and reliable DC bus operation in such complex power supply systems, this study proposes a distributed virtual-impedancebased DC bus stabilization strategy. The proposed approach is inherently suitable for both system expansion and system downscaling. It is fully derived from analytical formulations of power converters' state-space models according to their innetwork roles and operating regions, while explicitly avoiding heuristic tuning and arbitrary control parameters. To decouple local device dynamics from overall bus dynamics, dedicated innetwork state-space models for bidirectional converters are developed. The instability mechanisms of the bus dynamics are then analytically examined. Two worst-case transient simulation scenarios are conducted for the distributed virtual-impedancecompensated system. The results clearly demonstrate stable bus behavior before, during, and after severe transients, thereby validating the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Generative AI-Based Smart Energy Meter with Consumption Prediction and Optimization
authors: Geetha Ramani J, Jenisha S, Ilancheran B, Mohammed Kaif M, Rahul R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The continuous increase in global electricity usage, along with the accelerated adoption of renewable energy, has created a strong demand for intelligent and predictive metering systems. Traditional energy meters are limited to simple measurement and logging functions, offering no support for forecasting demand or optimizing consumption. To overcome these restrictions, this paper proposes an advanced Smart Energy Meter enhanced by Generative AI. The system integrates IoT-based sensing, solar energy analytics, and a cloud–edge collaborative architecture to provide accurate load forecasting and automated energy-flow regulation. Unlike deterministic forecasting techniques such as LSTM, ARIMA, or Prophet—whose performance depends heavily on historical data—the generative model produces synthetic weather and load variations. This capability improves resilience and maintains forecasting accuracy even under uncertain or highly fluctuating conditions. The proposed architecture includes real-time sensing, microcontroller-level control, cloud-assisted analytics for large-scale prediction, and automated demand-side management to minimize peak loads. The system also performs intelligent coordination between solar energy and the main grid, increasing renewable utilization and reducing reliance on conventional power sources. Secure data handling and AI-driven anomaly detection further strengthen operational safety. Experimental analysis confirms improvements in prediction accuracy, peak shaving, solar efficiency, and cost reduction. Overall, the generative framework offers a scalable and sustainable solution for future smart energy-management systems.

title: Continuance Intention to Use AI as Writing Assistants Among Indonesian University Students and Its Implications for Academic Literacy
authors: Ralph Jonathan Balthazar, Joshua Halim Kunadi, Keisha Dean Tan, Anderes Gui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: University students are increasingly turning to AI-based writing assistants such as ChatGPT, QuillBot, and Grammarly to support various aspects of their academic writing, such as idea development, and proofreading. Although these tools have contributed to improving the quality of formal and academic writing, their use remains controversial due to ethical concerns, potential over-reliance, and the possible decline of essential skills. This study explores the effects of such tools on academic literacy and investigates students’ continued intention to use AI writing assistance through a hybrid framework that integrates the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), incorporating the construct of AI literacy. This study also offers insights and recommendations for Indonesian universities and policymakers to design AI literacy programs and educational strategies that enhance the advantages of AI tools while mitigating their risks, promoting responsible and equitable AI use in academic contexts.

title: Design and Implementation of Low Profile CRPA Array for Antijamming and Interference Mitigation in NavIC Multi-Band Navigation Satellite System
authors: Kavitha T, Malathi M, P. Nagarajan, Ashokkumar N, Bharathi M, Rahul Krishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Every Navigation Satellite System (NSS) aims to provide accurate and reliable positioning and timing information to the user. Navigating Satellite Systems (NSS) signals do, and should, provide that information. There are, however, Navigation Satellite System (NSS) signals that face unintentional signal noise and intentional signal disruptions. Jamming signals can significantly reduce available signals to Navigation Satellite Systems (NSS). Because of this, low power jamming signals have enticing, and often, greater than signal jamming interferences. Of all the intrinsic elements of a Navigation Satellite System (NSS) receiver, the antenna has the most significant impact on the receiver's performance. An objective of this study is the design and realization of an indigenous Controlled Radiation Pattern Antenna (CRPA) for NavIC Satellite System receiver's selective interference mitigation. The focus of this study is on assisting the proposed systems to realize the simulated outcomes and the assessment of the system under different traffic conditions to improve the system. Unpaired effort will go towards assuring effective communication across the traffic, the channels, and the receivers.

title: A Comprehensive Approach to Strengthening Data Privacy and Cybersecurity in Edge AI-Driven Smart Healthcare Infrastructure
authors: Rohith Varma Vegesna, G. Sahoo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Edge AI-driven smart healthcare infrastructures are systems that produce large amounts of sensitive medical data, which will require effective privacy and cybersecurity measures. This paper includes a unified strategy that incorporates federated learning, lightweight encryption, zero-trust, and real-time anomaly detection to protect patient data. The suggested framework will provide greater data confidentiality, the ability to resist cyberattacks, and reliable data source-level decision-making to deliver reliable and scalable healthcare services and maintain privacy in the emanation of intelligent medical settings today.

title: Deep Learning-Based Satellite Image Analysis for Monitoring and Detecting Deforestation Trends
authors: K B Jayanthi, Susee Karthika A, Tarunika R S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Deforestation has become a major environmental concern due to its impact on biodiversity, climate stability, and ecosystem sustainability. Continuous monitoring of forest cover is essential for understanding environmental degradation and supporting conservation planning. Traditional forest monitoring approaches based on ground surveys are time-consuming and difficult to implement across large geographic regions. In this study, a satellite-image-based framework is proposed for detecting and analyzing deforestation trends using deep learning and environmental parameter analysis. Multi-temporal satellite images are processed to identify forest and non-forest regions through a U-Net convolutional neural network designed for pixel-level segmentation. Following segmentation, several environmental indicators-including Vegetation Density Index, Canopy Loss, Soil Exposure Index, and Fragmentation Indexare extracted to evaluate forest degradation patterns and spatial variations. To enhance interpretability and analytical accuracy, three complementary analytical models-Rule-Based Classification, Randomized Classification Tree, and Local Context Anomaly Scoring-are applied to the derived parameter maps. These models help identify abnormal deforestation regions and highlight spatial patterns associated with forest degradation. The framework generates visual maps and comparative outputs that allow clear interpretation of forest cover changes across different time periods. The proposed approach provides a scalable and interpretable solution for large-scale environmental monitoring and can support decisionmaking processes related to forest management, conservation planning, and sustainable land-use strategies.

title: Optimized Deep Learning Framework for Early Detection of Rice Leaf Diseases Using CNN-SVM and FRRSA-LeNet Models
authors: Uddagiri Sirisha, K. Meghana, M. N. V. V. Sai Babu, Sk. Fahmida Thabassum, M. Pranavi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: This paper suggests an improved deep learning model to predict the early symptoms of rice leaf diseases with hybrid models. Two architectures were constructed and tested: a CNN SVM hybrid model, a combination of convolutional feature extraction and Support Vector Machine classification, and an FR-RSA optimized LeNet model, a combination of the Fractional Remora Reptile Search Algorithm to optimize the parameters of the model. The models were trained and evaluated using a data set of 5,932 rice leaf images of ten disease classes. CNN SVM model reached an accuracy of 86.9 percent which is high in class-specific classification especially in Bacterial Leaf Blight and Neck Blast. FR-RSA LeNet model had better overall performance with 97.5% accuracy, 0.978 precision, 0.980 recall and 0.977 F1-score. It provides a solution to diagnose agricultural disease in real time by deploying a user-friendly web application connected to a MobileNet-based irrelevance detection unit, which allows deploying the application in the field which is practical and scalable.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Architecture for Liver Cancer Detection: Combining Convolutional and Transformer Networks
authors: Satyendra Sharma, Pradeep Laxkar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Timely Abstract Timely and accurate identification of liver cancers, such as hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) and cholangiocarcinoma, is necessary for better treatment results and improved patient survival. Deep learning approaches such as convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs) have shown promise in interpreting medical imaging data, including MRIs, CT scans, and histopathological slides. This study examines how combining CNNs and ViTs can improve liver cancer detection, classification, and staging. The hybrid CNN–ViT model reached 94.8% accuracy, 92.5% sensitivity, and 96.3% specificity, outperforming both traditional CNN and standalone ViT models. The hybrid model’s strength in capturing both local and global features led to its better performance on public datasets. These results show that the given framework helps detect liver cancer earlier and more accurately, which can lead to faster treatment and better patient outcomes.

title: Endurance Optimization and Charging Path Planning for Agricultural UAV Swarms Based on an Energy-Constrained Model
authors: Dianyi Zhou
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Affected by limited battery capacity, dispersed plots, and the complexity of coordinated operations, the continuous operational capability of agricultural drone swarms in precision agriculture is still constrained by both insufficient endurance and inefficient energy replenishment scheduling. Based on existing models for energy-constrained coverage path planning, multi-drone collaborative coverage, and energy replenishment coordination scheduling, this paper constructs a hierarchical spatiotemporal coupled optimization model: the upper layer jointly accomplishes plot decomposition, task clustering, swarm allocation, and candidate charging node selection, while the lower layer establishes a mixed-integer nonlinear programming model that simultaneously considers takeoff and landing, cruising, turning, hovering, ascent and descent, payload decay, energy evolution, charging queue, and time window penalties, and integrates column generation, adaptive large neighborhood search, and rolling re-planning for dynamic solution implementation. Simulation results show that the proposed model outperforms comparative methods in terms of task completion time, effective endurance utilization, and charging resource balance.

title: DBO-Optimized Deep Hybrid Framework for DDoS Attack Detection in Vanets
authors: K. Saranya, S. Ramakrishnan, A. Harees, E Manikandan, E S Gowthamraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) are important components of intelligent transport systems because they facilitate real-time communication between vehicles, they are also susceptible to DDoS attacks because of their dynamic and open nature. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning-based Intrusion Detection System (IDS) to improve the security of VANET environments. The Hybrid IDS uses Alex Net and Inception networks to extract spatial features from network traffic data and improve the detection of DDoS attacks by learning the temporal dependencies of the traffic flow using a Bi-directional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network. Additionally, Dung Beetle Optimization (DBO) is used to enhance the critical hyperparameters of the Hybrid IDS, resulting in increased performance and decreased false positive rate. The Hybrid IDS performance is evaluated using the UNSW-NB15 dataset and deployed via a Stream lit-based web application to allow for real-time single and batch predictions. The proposed system will serve as a userfriendly, scalable solution for securing VANET communications.

title: Research on the Design and Verification of Civil Aircraft Broadband Satellite Communication System
authors: Sijing Liu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: As an important component of the cabin system, the broadband satellite communication system is a significant aspect that reflects the competitiveness of modern advanced civil aircraft. This article provides a detailed introduction to the composition, functions, interfaces, architecture, and system verification methods of the broadband satellite communication system, offering guidance for its design and verification.

title: Diagnosis-Driven Model Evolution for Pavement Distress Detection: Enhancing Robustness Under Dynamic Illumination
authors: Qingfeng Tong, Limu Chen, Chongyi Zhou, Ru An, Lei Jia
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: Automated pavement distress detection is crucial for intelligent infrastructure maintenance. However, existing model-centric approaches, typically optimized for static datasets, often suffer from poor robustness under dynamic illumination in open-road environments. To bridge this adaptation gap, we propose a diagnosis-driven model evolution framework incorporating two key modules: (1) OvercastIndex, a quantitative diagnostic metric derived from failure case analysis to pinpoint photometric factors degrading performance; and (2) a targeted sampling augmentation strategy that leverages this diagnosis for iterative model optimization. Validated on a vehicle-mounted edge system utilizing a YOLO11-based detector, our approach reduces false positives by 26% per image while simultaneously improving the F2-score by 2.3% compared to the baseline. These results demonstrate that the framework effectively enhances operational robustness, successfully suppressing illumination-induced artifacts while maintaining high detection sensitivity for real-world deployment.

title: Influence of Rotor Slot Wedge Material on Temperature Distribution of Dual-Excited Synchronous Condenser during Asynchronous Operation
authors: Guangliang Yang, Guorui Xu, Zhiqiang Li, Wenmao Liu, Weili Li
abstract: A dual-excited synchronous condenser (DESC) with two sets of field windings can actively regulate the rotor speed through AC excitation control to provide inertial support for the power grid. However, the DESC would operate asynchronously after absorbing or delivering the inertial power, its inertial support capability is depended on the duration of asynchronous operation and finally can be restricted by the stator and rotor temperature rise. In this paper, an electro-magnetic-thermal coupling model of DESC is established and validated by the experiments of a small power DESC. Taking a 50-Mvar DESC as an example, the loss density and temperature distribution of the DESC are studied under different slips, and it is obtained that the rotor maximum temperature is the main limitation of the DESC during the asynchronous operation. Further, the influence of rotor slot wedge material on the loss and temperature rise is analyzed, and the duration of asynchronous operation is revealed along with the variation of slot wedge conductivity under different slips. The research can provide a theoretical basis for the asynchronous operating ability improvement for the DESC.

title: A Weather-Driven Machine Learning Framework for Fault Classification and Predictive Maintenance in Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Ali Hamza, Zunaib Ali, Sandra Dudley, Komal Saleem
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) systems are widely deployed renewable sources, yet their performance and reliability are often undermined by operational faults. This paper presents a novel weather-driven fault classification framework for PV predictive maintenance (PdM), addressing a key limitation of existing studies that analyse faults under fixed, short-term or simplified weather assumptions. The major contribution of this work lies in explicitly modelling variability in both solar irradiance and temperature across four distinct weather conditions, enabling the separation of weather-induced variations from true fault signatures. Features are extracted from IV characteristics under varying weather scenarios and processed through a regression-based residual analysis with a pre-feature assessment stage, generating deviation patterns and feature contribution signatures. These enhanced feature vectors are then classified using a Bayesian-optimised gradient boosting model within a two-stage architecture that (1) distinguishes normal vs. abnormal operation and (2) identifies either the prevailing weather condition (if normal) or classifies the fault type together with its corresponding weather scenario (if abnormal). The robustness and generalisability of the framework are validated using cross-validation, high-fidelity simulations, and independent laboratory experiments, achieving 99% classification accuracy. By directly linking each detected fault to its prevailing weather condition, the proposed approach provides a robust pathway for weather-aware PdM scheduling, improving diagnostic reliability and operational resilience across diverse environments.

title: Vibration of Soft, Twisted Beams for Underactuated Quadrupedal Locomotion_supp2-3686693.mp4
authors: Daniel Aukes
abstract: Underactuated compliant robotic systems offer a promising approach to mitigating actuation and control challenges by harnessing predesigned, embodied dynamic behaviors. This article presents Flix-Walker, a novel, untethered, centimeter-scale quadrupedal robot inspired by compliant underactuated mechanisms. Flix-Walker employs flexible, helix-shaped beams as legs, which are actuated by vibrations from just two motors to achieve three distinct mobility modes. We analyze the actuation parameters required to generate various locomotion modes through both simulation and prototype experiments. The effects of system and environmental variations on locomotion performance are examined, and we propose a generic metric for selecting control parameters that produce robust and functional motions. Experiments validate the effectiveness and robustness of these actuation parameters within a closed-loop control framework, demonstrating reliable trajectory-tracking and self-navigation capabilities.

title: From Slice to Sequence: Autoregressive Tracking Transformer for Consistent 3D Lymph Node Detection in CT Scans
authors: Qinji Yu, Yirui Wang, Ke Yan, Dandan Zheng, Dashan Ai, Dazhou Guo, Zhanghexuan Ji, Yanzhou Su, Yun Bian, Xiaowei Ding, Yi Guo, Kuaile Zhao, Le Lu, Na Shen, Xianghua Ye, Dakai Jin
abstract: Lymph node (LN) assessment is an essential task in the routine radiology workflow, providing valuable insights for cancer staging and treatment planning. Identifying scatteredly-distributed and low-contrast LNs in 3D CT scans is highly challenging, even for experienced clinicians. Previous lesion and LN detection methods demonstrate the effectiveness of 2.5D approaches (i.e., using 2D backbone with multi-slice inputs), leveraging pretrained 2D model weights and showing improved accuracy as compared to separate 2D or 3D detectors. However, slice-based 2.5D detectors do not explicitly model inter-slice consistency for LN as a 3D object, requiring heuristic post-merging steps to generate final 3D LN instances, which can involve tuning a set of parameters for each dataset. In this work, we formulate 3D LN detection as a slice-by-slice tracking task along the z-axis and propose LN-Tracker, a novel LN tracking transformer, for joint end-to-end detection and 3D instance association. Built upon a DETR-based detector, LN-Tracker decouples transformer queries into distinct track and detection groups with independent matching, enabling comprehensive LN detection while maintaining trajectory consistency. A masked attention mechanism further separates learning between these query groups, and a similarity loss promotes robust interslice LN association, particularly in low-contrast scenarios. Extensive evaluation on four LN datasets shows LN-Tracker’s superior performance, with at least 2.49% gain in average sensitivity when compared to top 3D/2.5D/tracking detectors. Further validation on public lung nodule and prostate tumor detection tasks confirms the generaliz-ability of LN-Tracker as it achieves top performance on both tasks. Code is available at https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/LN-Tracker.

title: SynPole: Zero-shot Recognition of Pole-Like Objects using CAD-derived Synthetic Point Clouds
authors: Zezheng Zhang, Davood Shojaei, Kourosh Khoshelham
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Pole-like objects (PLOs) are critical for urban infrastructure mapping but remain challenging to detect in mobile LiDAR scans without large, annotated datasets. We introduce SynPole, the first synthetic zero-shot framework for PLO classification that requires only a single CAD model of the target pole. SynPole consists of a lightweight, object-centric LiDAR simulator to generate realistic synthetic scans from a 3D model, and a training-free, non-parametric encoder that extracts fixed-length features from support samples. At inference, object proposals can obtained via any unsupervised, class-agnostic segmentation and classified by the similarity to support features. Experimental evaluation shows that SynPole achieves a 71.4% Intersection-over-Union (IoU) on PLO, demonstrating that our method matches the performance of state-of-the-art, fully supervised baselines while reducing huge annotation effort.

title: Pulse Repetition Frequency Tuned Low-Intensity Focused Ultrasound Neuromodulation for Tibial Nerve Targeted Bladder Function Modulation_supp1-3700985.docx
authors: Sung-Min Park
abstract: Objective: This study aims to investigate whether the neuromodulatory efficacy of low-intensity focused ultrasound (LIFU) for peripheral nerve stimulation (PNS) can be systematically tuned by the temporal stimulation parameter, pulse repetition frequency (PRF). Methods: LIFU-mediated tibial nerve stimulation (LIFU-TNS) was applied with multiple PRF conditions to evaluate PRF-dependent neuromodulatory effects, with micturition inhibition as the primary outcome. Intra-bladder pressure (IBP) was continuously monitored to evaluate changes in micturition intervals. Neural involvement was confirmed using (1) a lidocaine-induced sciatic nerve (tibial branch) block, and the stimulation paradigm was additionally evaluated in (2) an acetic acid–induced overactive bladder model and (3) freely moving, unconstrained animals to assess robustness across physiological and behavioral conditions. Results: A distinct PRF-dependent neuromodulation was observed, where 100 Hz stimulation elicited the most potent inhibition of micturition, whereas 10 Hz and 1 kHz showed comparatively less effective results. Quantitative analysis of normalized micturition interval (post-stimulation / pre stimulation baseline) confirmed this trend. (10 Hz: 0.98±0.16; 100 Hz: 1.72±0.81; 1 kHz: 0.95±0.35). Importantly, consistent inhibition was replicated across the nerve-block, overactive bladder, and freely moving models, indicating robustness of the effect under diverse physiological and behavioral conditions. Conclusion: The clear PRF-dependent modulation observed in LIFU-TNS establishes PRF as a feasible and tunable control parameter and highlights its potential for guiding precise neuromodulation. Significance: Unlike conventional intensity dependent strategies, this study demonstrates that PRF is essential for effective ultrasound neuromodulation in overactive bladder. These findings establish a systematic parameter-based framework for developing robust peripheral nerve interfaces.

title: Characterizing and Accelerating Spacecraft Onboard Workloads on RISC-V Platform_supp1-3701272.pdf
authors: Boul Chandra Garai
abstract: This paper studies 28 production spacecraft workloads from ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) missions to find out which computations actually limit performance on resource-constrained space processors. Spacecraft onboard processors face strict power and thermal constraints while performing critical real-time calculations for navigation, control, and data processing. Using a Basic Computational Element (BCE) breakdown, we find that software trigonometric calls (up to 36% of execution time) and small matrix operations (13%–41%) are the main bottlenecks across navigation, attitude control, guidance, and remote-sensing workloads. To check whether these findings translate into useful hardware, we build two domainspecific accelerators—a CORDIC (COordinate Rotation DIgital Computer) trigonometric unit and a 3 × 3 systolic matrix accelerator—integrated into a RISC-V processor through custom instruction set extensions (RVTrig, RVMatrix) and evaluated using cycle-approximate gem5 simulation. Across 18 representative workloads, the combined accelerators give a 3.13× geometric mean speedup and 37.2% energy reduction, reaching up to 9.29× on the most accelerator-friendly workload. Section VI introduces a timestep-based roofline model that tracks algorithmic progress rather than raw operations, so that an accelerated system no longer looks slower under the standard FLOPs-based view.

title: Efficient Monolingual Pretraining in Low-Resource Settings through Morphology-Aware Tokenization, Principled Corpus Denoising, and Benchmark-Driven Evaluation
authors: Nahid Hossain, Md Faisal Kabir
abstract: Training effective language models for morphologically complex, low-resource languages in resource-constrained environments is hampered by four largely unaddressed bottlenecks: the representational dilution imposed by massively multilingual pretraining, the inadequate token representation caused by multilingual tokenizers not being trained on language-specific data, the absence of a standardized high-quality evaluation benchmark, and the lack of a compact, language-specific pretrained model capable of competitive performance without prohibitive computational cost. We address all four deficiencies in a unified framework, taking Bengali as our target language. First, we introduce B-CORE, a rigorously curated 52GB Bengali monolingual corpus of 16.5M documents (4.32B tokens), constructed via a multi-stage pipeline integrating quality filtering, expert validation, and context-aware segmentation, achieving a 22.4% reduction in corpus volume relative to raw source data through systematic quality filtering and cross-corpus deduplication, among the largest and most carefully curated Bengali pretraining corpora to date. Second, we design morphology-aware WordPiece tokenizers (30.5K and 120K vocabularies) trained exclusively on Bengali text, yielding 96.9% script coverage versus 28–95% for multilingual baselines, with 20–26% fewer tokens per word and 37–41% faster processing. Third, we introduce BLUGE, the first balanced, expert-validated Bengali evaluation benchmark spanning 7 diverse tasks with 536K samples. Fourth, we pretrain BnLM, a suite of three compact Bengali-specific encoder models (66–135M parameters), which establish new state-of-the-art results on BLUGE and cross-lingual and Indic benchmarks while requiring 44–91% fewer FLOPs and achieving 2× faster inference than larger multilingual baselines. Our results demonstrate that the bottleneck in low-resource NLP is not model scale, but principled alignment of corpus quality, tokenization, and evaluation infrastructure with the target language’s linguistic properties.

title: Complexity-Reduced Optimal Switching Sequence Model Predictive Control for Grid-Connected Three-Level ANPC Inverters
authors: Euntaek Nam, Suyong Chae
abstract: This article proposes a complexity-reduced optimal switching sequence model predictive control (CR-OSS-MPC) for a grid-connected three-level active neutral-point clamped (3L-ANPC) inverter with an LC filter. The proposed method reformulates the three-level switching sequence selection problem into an equivalent two-level one and restricts the switching boundary. Thus, the number of switching sequences for optimization is reduced from 24 to 3, resulting in an 82.47% reduction in MPC computation time. DC-link capacitor voltage balancing is simultaneously achieved by regulating the zero-vector dwell time in the reformulated two-level switching sequence, without weighting factors or additional predefined switching sequences. Simulation and experimental results from a 3kW grid-connected 3L-ANPC inverter prototype verify the effectiveness of the proposed CR-OSS-MPC. The proposed method improves computational efficiency while maintaining accurate current tracking and balanced DC-link capacitor voltages under both steady-state and dynamic conditions.

title: REACTS: Robust Ensemble Approach with Comprehensive Temporal and Spectral Features for TTS Speech Detection
authors: Komal Mathur, Aditya Goutam Shah, Parva Chowdhury, Mamatha Balachandra, Shaoning Pang
abstract: The rapid advancement of AI voice synthesis technologies has led to significant security and authentication concerns, as synthetic speech can be used to impersonate individuals, spread misinformation, or commit fraud. Detecting AI-synthesized speech is crucial for maintaining trust in digital communication. The detection of synthetic speech has become increasingly critical as Text-to-Speech (TTS) technologies advance in naturalness and accessibility. This research introduces a comprehensive framework for distinguishing human from synthetic speech using an extensive set of acoustic features and an ensemble learning approach. The methodology combines prosodic elements (pitch, energy), spectral characteristics (MFCCs, chroma, spectral contrast), and voice quality metrics (jitter, shimmer, harmonics-to-noise ratio) with gradient boosting ensemble models. The feature extraction process carefully captures subtle temporal and spectral differences between human and synthetic speech. The research provides insights into the most discriminative features for synthetic speech detection and demonstrates the effectiveness of ensemble learning in this domain.

title: TACS: Decentralized per-Task Micro-Slicing for Deadline-Aware Provisioning in Distributed Computing Continuum Systems_supp1-3701061.pdf
authors: Zhenli He
abstract: Distributed Computing Continuum Systems (DCCS) unify cloud, fog, edge, and Internet of Things (IoT) into a single execution fabric. At scale, heterogeneity and bursty arrivals make it hard to meet per task deadlines. Prior approaches based on learning, optimization, market mechanisms, or class level slicing depend on global state or iterative coordination. Decisions lag arrivals, isolation is scoped to coarse classes rather than individual tasks, and the deadline violation ratio (DVR) rises. We present Task-level Adaptive Computing Slicing (TACS), a fine grained slicing paradigm that delivers task aligned resource governance through autonomous shard management. For each arriving task, TACS executes decentralized scheduling at the shard level to instantiate an ephemeral micro-slice governed by an autonomous shard formed exactly by the task's participants. Within the shard, participants apply closed form rules to make local resource allocation decisions. This design achieves strict task level isolation and precise, scalable matching between supply and demand without global coordination or model retraining. In simulations with 100 to 1000 heterogeneous nodes and a range of loads and heterogeneity levels, TACS maintains DVRs below 1% and provides steadier, higher throughput than advanced baselines. Under adverse conditions, representative baselines exceed 20% DVR and in several cases require retraining when device populations change.

title: Cortical Activity Associated with Phantom Leg Movements
authors: Natalie Mrachacz-Kersting, Cristian Pasluosta, Britta Meyer, Omar Feix Do Nascimento, Thomas Stieglitz, Dario Farina
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: We tested the feasibility for amputees to control artificial limbs using non-invasive electroencephalography (EEG). Thirteen participants engaged in attempts of isometric ankle plantar-flexions using their phantom or intact limb at slow or ballistic speeds. EEG data were analyzed for movement-related cortical potentials (MRCPs), the slow negative potentials related to the planning and execution of movements. We focused on temporal profiles and single-trial classification at electrode location Cz where MRCPs are most prominent. Distinctly different MRCP morphologies were observed for both movement speeds and phantom versus intact limbs. Crucially, time since amputation correlated significantly with classification errors for distinguishing tasks performed with the intact limb from those of the phantom limb (R = 0.36, p =0.004) and movement speed during trials of only the phantom limb (R = -0.33, p = 0.01). Here we show the persistent capacity of amputees to plan and attempt to execute limb motions at varying speeds using their phantom limb. This has implications for understanding neural adaptations over extended post-amputation periods and for the practical implementation of the MRCP in the design of brain-computer interfaces to control prosthetic devices using single-electrode EEG recordings.

title: Low-Elevation Source Pattern Effects in Evaporation Duct Propagation and Their Low-Dimensional Representation
authors: Yihao Xu, Yuqiang Zhang, Xiaofeng Zhao, Hao Cha, Bin Tian, Chen Zhou
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The transmitting source in evaporation duct propagation modeling is often approximated by an ideal Gaussian pattern, but the influence of fine low-elevation radiation-pattern details near the horizon remains insufficiently quantified. This letter investigates this effect using a three-level source model composed of an ideal Gaussian source, an engineering reference source, and a low-elevation perturbed source. Parabolic equation simulations are performed in range-independent and range-dependent evaporation duct environments to compare source-induced propagation differences and their relative importance with respect to duct parameter uncertainty. The results show that the discrepancy between the ideal Gaussian and engineering reference sources is limited, whereas subdegree low-elevation perturbations can produce pronounced propagation loss variations. Although duct uncertainty remains the dominant contributor, source uncertainty accounts for 29.7%-36.4% of the full field error variance and 25.2%-32.9% of the receiver-height error variance. A two-metric coupling representation further characterizes the source-induced errors with coefficients of determination of 0.934-0.969.

title: MSAM: Multi-Semantic Adaptive Mining for Cross-Modal Drone Video-Text Retrieval
authors: Jinghao Huang, Yaxiong Chen, Ganchao Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: With the advancement of drone technology, the volume of video data increases rapidly, creating an urgent need for efficient semantic retrieval. This work presents one of the early studies on the drone video-text retrieval (DVTR) task. Drone videos feature overhead perspectives, strong structural homogeneity, and diverse semantic expressions of target combinations, which challenge existing cross-modal methods designed for ground-level views in effectively modeling their characteristics. Therefore, dedicated retrieval mechanisms tailored for drone scenarios are necessary. To address this issue, we propose a novel approach called Multi-Semantic Adaptive Mining (MSAM). MSAM introduces a multi-semantic adaptive learning mechanism that models dynamic changes across frames and extracts region-level semantic information. This design improves fine-grained cross-modal alignment. This method relies on fine-grained interactions between textual tokens and drone video frames. It integrates an adaptive semantic construction module, a distribution-driven semantic learning term, and a diversity-aware semantic constraint to enhance cross-modal interactions and improve the robustness of feature representations. To reduce the interference of complex backgrounds in drone videos, we introduce a cross-modal interactive feature fusion pooling mechanism that focuses on feature extraction and matching in target regions, minimizing noise effects. Extensive experiments on two self-constructed drone video-text datasets show that MSAM outperforms other existing methods in the DVTR task. The code and data are accessible at https://github.com/huangjh98/MSAM.

title: TraCPro-BDPC: Trajectory Cluster-based Probabilistic Big Data-driven Predictive Control for Nonlinear Processes
authors: Shuangyu Han, Yitao Yan, Jie Bao, Biao Huang
abstract: A trajectory cluster-based probabilistic big data-driven predictive control (TraCPro-BDPC) approach is proposed for nonlinear processes in the behavioral systems framework. With a big dataset of input-output data trajectories, the nonlinear process behavior is partitioned into multiple linear sub-behaviors (characterized by the trajectory clusters) offline via clustering. To control the nonlinear process outside these linear sub-behaviors, a probabilistic system behavior interpolation method is proposed for online control based on the known trajectory clusters. In each receding horizon, the interpolation method quantifies the stochastic uncertainty of the online interpolated linear subbehavior by interpolating a probability density function of the data trajectory. Based on polynomial chaos expansions, an online big data-driven predictive control approach is developed to deal with the stochastic uncertainty propagation of online linear sub-behavior interpolation. The proposed approach is illustrated by an example of the control of an aluminum smelting process.

title: Modeling OTH Radar Sea Clutter in Range-dependent Atmospheric Ducts_supp1-3699523.docx
authors: Yuqiang Zhang
abstract: The accurate modeling of sea clutter for over-the-horizon (OTH) radar constitutes a classical multi-scale physical problem, necessitating the precise coupling of kilometer-scale propagation links with meter-scale sea surface roughness characteristics. In order to remediate the physical inconsistencies engendered by the decoupled calculation of radio wave propagation and sea surface scattering in prevailing methodologies, this paper puts forth a proposed integrated propagation-scattering physical simulation framework. The framework's foundation is predicated on the implementation of a bidirectional propagation-scattering coupling mechanism, which integrates the propagation loss correction induced by sea surface roughness with a high-fidelity scattering kernel incorporating two-stage foam evolution and multipath effects into a unified framework. At the implementation level, a heterogeneous parallel architecture based on C++/CUDA is constructed, which effectively resolves the challenge of electromagnetic scattering calculation for massive time-varying sea surface facets. Using measured data from the South China Sea, the model's validity in describing the statistical distribution and spectral characteristics of sea clutter is substantiated. Moreover, a series of numerical experiments are conducted based on this model to investigate the impact of range-dependent atmospheric ducts on the characteristics of long-range sea clutter. The simulation framework under consideration provides critical data support and a theoretical basis for the performance analysis of OTH radar systems and the development of high-precision atmospheric duct inversion algorithms.

title: An Efficient Signcryption for Internet of Health Things over Satellite Terrestrial Integrated Networks
authors: Laila Alqahtani, Haralambos Mouratidis, Insaf Ullah, Fahad Algarni
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Health Things (IoHT) devices has introduced serious security issues in healthcare settings, where securing data confidentiality, integrity, and authentication is essential. The proliferation of Satellite Terrestrial Integrated Networks (STINs) further amplifies these security concerns, as IoHT devices increasingly rely on hybrid satellite-terrestrial links for remote patient monitoring in underserved and emergency response environments, where channel heterogeneity and long propagation delays make traditional cryptographic overhead particularly costly. In addition, managing computational complexity, communication overhead, and memory usage presents a further challenge for resource-limited IoHT devices. This paper presents a novel certificateless signcryption scheme that integrates Certificateless Public Key Cryptography with Hyperelliptic Curve Cryptography (HEC) to address these challenges. The proposed scheme operates over a genus-2 hyperelliptic curve with an 80-bit security parameter, achieving a key size of only 240 bits 50% smaller than leading ECC-based schemes. Experimental evaluation on a MacBook Pro (Intel Core i5, 1.4 GHz) as sender and Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM Cortex-A72, 1.5 GHz) as receiver demonstrates that the complete signcryption and unsigncryption cycle completes in 0.001934 s, representing a 99.5% reduction compared to the best competing ECC-based existing scheme and a 99.99% reduction compared to the best pairing-based existing scheme. Communication overhead is reduced to only 2448 bits 14% lower than leading ECC-based approaches and 80% lower than pairing-based schemes. We conduct thorough security assessments through both formal and informal analyses, demonstrating that our scheme satisfies IND-CCA2 confidentiality and EUF-CMA unforgeability under the DDH and CDH hardness assumptions, and resists forgery, impersonation, replay, and man-in-the-middle attacks. Formal verification using the AVISPA tool with OFMC and CL-AtSe back-ends confirms the protocol’s security. Performance evaluation confirms our scheme is particularly suitable for resource-constrained IoHT devices operating over STINs, offering an optimal balance between security assurance and computational efficiency.

title: Zero-Shot Cross-Modal AI-Generated Content Retrieval via Diffusion Process on Graphs
authors: Xingshi Wan, Hanjun Gao, Wei Feng, Shenghao Liu, Wei Xiang, Xianjun Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The rapid proliferation of AI-Generated Content (AIGC) has fundamentally changed the distribution and semantic structure of multimedia data, posing new challenges to existing cross-modal retrieval systems. In traditional Zero-Shot Cross-Modal Retrieval (ZS-CMR), unseen categories are typically assumed to share similar semantic manifold structures with real data. In contrast, AIGC exhibits complex semantic entanglement and heterogeneous manifold structures, while also lacking explicit supervision, which jointly aggravate manifold distortion in cross-modal feature spaces. Existing ZS-CMR approaches largely overlook the manifold distortion introduced by AI-generated content, which undermines the alignment between different modalities in unseen domains. To address this challenge, we propose a novel graph-based framework termed Diffusion Process on Tensor Product of Heterogeneous Graph (DTHG). Specifically, a heterogeneous graph is constructed to model the shared semantic space by integrating similarity relationships from unimodal and cross-modal feature extractors, enabling effective representation of cross-modal relationships between real and AI-generated contents. We further perform a cross-modal regularized diffusion process on the tensor product graph to capture intrinsic manifold structures and alleviate distortion in zero-shot scenarios. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing approaches, highlighting its effectiveness for zero-shot cross-modal AI-generated content retrieval.

title: Toward Enumeration of Common Operational Processes: The Water Sector
authors: Javier Roasso, Jaidip Kotak, Aviad Elyashar, Robert Moskovitch, Asaf Shabtai, Rami Puzis

title: Research on insulator surface crack detection technology by laser array
authors: Bichuan Xu, Tao Lai, Zhenhua Xu, Guilai Yin, Yanglin Li, Hui Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: In order to meet the demand for non-destructive testing of insulator surface cracks in power systems, this paper constructs a two-dimensional axisymmetric finite element model to systematically study the effects of crack depth and array parameters on the detection effect. It is shown that, under the simulation conditions, the echo amplitude increases with the depth when the crack depth is in the range of 0.1-0.4 mm, and the amplitude decreases due to the energy loss and interaction saturation of the surface wave when the crack depth exceeds 0.4 mm. The increase in the number of laser arrays significantly improves the detection sensitivity, the increase in the line half-width reduces the amplitude and narrows the spectrum, and the increase in the wavelength increases the detection depth. Through experimental verification, the reliability of the above law is further confirmed, and the research results provide a theoretical basis and experimental support for the parameter optimisation and engineering application of insulator laser ultrasonic detection system.

title: Experimental Study on Public Warning Information Broadcast Based on BeiDou System
authors: Qiang Chai, Wei Wang, Ji Guo, Ying Chen, Lei Yao, Jian Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: For the people’s livelihood demand of disseminating early warning information through BDS (BeiDou navigation satellite system) navigation messages, this paper proposes a scheme to broadcast early warning information by using B2b-PPP signal of Beidou Geo Satellite, without changing the design of the BDS satellites. Based on the ground test and verification bed for BeiDou navigation satellite system, we have completed multi-scene tests for the technical scheme, with using B2b-PPP to broadcast earthquake public early warning information. The technical parameters, such as the capacity and timeliness of broadcasting early warning information, are verified. Those provide a technical reference for the promotion and application of the BDS in the national earthquake prevention and disaster reduction.

title: Comparative Performance and Security Analysis of Encryption Techniques in Cloud Infrastructures
authors: Priyam Srivastava, Sanjay Kumar Dubey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Although cloud infrastructures provide for scalable and economical data processing and storage, its dispersed and multi-tenant architecture poses serious security risks to data integrity and confidentiality. Sensitive cloud data is fundamentally protected via encryption, although the effectiveness, security, and operational complexity of various encryption methods vary. The Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), Rivest-Shamir-Adleman (RSA), Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), and Blowfish are popular encryption methods. Paper compares their security and performance in cloud infrastructure settings. Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS), Software-as-a-Service (SaaS), Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS), resistance to cryptographic attacks, computational overhead, encryption and decryption efficiency, and suitability across cloud service layers are just a few of the evaluation parameters that are compared. The study shows that while ECC delivers robust security with reduced key sizes for secure key exchange, AES offers better performance and scalability for bulk data encryption. Although RSA is more computationally expensive, but well for authentication. Study also underlines the necessity of hybrid and post-quantum encryption techniques in upcoming cloud security architectures and draws attention to new issues raised by quantum computing.

title: An Automatic Integrity Detection Method for Substation Equipment Profiles based on Ordered Hash Chains
authors: Le Liu, Lei Wei, Xiaolei Huang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: During remote authentication processes, traditional verification methods prove cumbersome and inefficient, failing to meet practical demands for file integrity checks. This paper proposes an automated integrity verification method for substation equipment configuration files based on ordered hash chains. Leveraging the distributed management characteristics of substation equipment configuration files, changes to server-side files are treated as a sequence of hash nodes. By iteratively computing hash values for files or directories using a hash function in chronological order, an ordered hash chain is formed. This chain records all operational states within the file repository. Clients need only synchronize file updates based on hash chain nodes, thereby ensuring the integrity of files within the repository. Iterative optimization using the SHA-256 algorithm shortens the critical path by precomputing shift results stored in lookup tables and merging adjacent cyclic shift operations, thereby accelerating hash computation. Experimental results demonstrate that this method rapidly synchronizes substation equipment configuration files while enabling detection functionality, achieving the goal of high-precision automated file integrity verification.

title: A Review of ResNet-Based Content-Based Image Retrieval Systems
authors: Haibaoui Mohammed, Omari Mohammed, Kaddi Mohammed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Content-Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) has become increasingly important due to the rapid growth of large-scale image repositories in domains such as healthcare, remote sensing, multimedia databases, and intellectual property management. Conventional CBIR systems based on handcrafted visual descriptors often fail to bridge the semantic gap between low-level image features and high-level human perception. Recent advances in deep learning, particularly Residual Networks (ResNet), have significantly improved feature representation and retrieval performance. Unlike existing surveys that focus on general convolutional neural networks or single-domain applications, this paper presents a ResNet-focused review of CBIR systems with an emphasis on cross-domain analysis and a reproducible evaluation framework. We systematically analyze ResNet-based methods in terms of feature extraction strategies, similarity measures, dimensionality reduction techniques, and indexing mechanisms. A structured comparative analysis highlights performance trends, methodological differences, and design trade-offs across multiple domains. Finally, key challenges and future research directions are discussed to guide the development of accurate, scalable, and interpretable next-generation ResNet-based CBIR systems.

title: Explanatory Transformer-Based Sequential Recommendation: Assessing BERTRec with SASRec for Customer Behaviour Prediction
authors: J. Balamurugan, M. Bhuvaneswari, Avinash Reddy Aitha, S. Nagaraju, R.Viswanathan, N. Dhasarathan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Digital platforms are evolving at a high pace, and recommender systems with advanced technology are used to forecast user behavior accurately, taking into account consecutive interactions. Because transformer-based models, in particular, BERTRec and SASRec, are good at modelling longdistance relationships based on self-attention mechanisms, as now-famous models. Yet, they have been studied in terms of their relative effectiveness and explainability, which is essential to improve user confidence and feasibility, even though they have demonstrated the capability of prediction. This paper compares BERTRec and SASRec in terms of prediction accuracy and interpretability for sequential recommendation. Measures such as inference delay, attention-based explainability scores, normalized discounted cumulative gain (NDCG at k), and hit ratio (HR at k) are applied to assess the two models on standard e-commerce datasets. According to the results, SASRec can be better applied to real-time use due to its faster inference, whereas BERTRec has a higher likelihood of generating recommendations due to its two-way encoding. Moreover, information shows consumers pay great attention, which throws light on consumer decision-making. The findings demonstrate the need to combine explainability techniques with transformers to provide recommendation systems that are clear, scalable, and easy to use. This research offers a useful structure on how to proceed with the implementation of transformer-based sequential recommenders in the industry in the future.

title: FEAGI: A Deterministic and Composable Neuromorphic Framework
authors: Soumya Swaraj Mondal, Mohammad Nadji-tehrani, Hritom Das
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
