abstract: Deploying brain-inspired architectures in physical AI systems introduces challenges in predictability and reliability across robotics, safety-critical, and embedded applications. We present FEAGI, a modular neuromorphic cognitive architecture that emphasizes composability and deterministic replay. In FEAGI, the neural architecture is represented as a modular data structure encoding cortical morphologies, connectivity rules, and physiological parameters, and is instantiated into a connectome to support reproducible execution given identical configuration, initial state, and input traces. FEAGI employs a fixed-phase burst schedule to reduce timing variability for an instantiated brain model across runs and deployment targets. Modality-specific sensorimotor encoders and decoders translate external signals into standardized neural representations using machine-readable interface definitions, supporting end-to-end traces from sensory input through neural processing to motor output. FEAGI's modular design also provides a basis for hardware-algorithm co-design, including potential mapping of modules to custom ASICs and other specialized platforms.

title: IPR in the Digital Era: Navigating Legal Challenges of Innovation and Technological Advancement
authors: Vartika Saxena, Reenu Chauhan, Stuti Lal, Mohd Danish Faraz, Rohit Mahajan, Ajay Kumar Dogra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the era of the virtual world, the quick pace of innovation like blockchain, big data, and artificial intelligence (AI) has created a sea change in the intellectual property rights (IPRs) dynamics. Whose intellectual property right (IPR) is it, how does one protect them, and who protects them are increasingly becoming problematic issues for the legal fraternity of the new world. In this article, a review of the finer details is made which arise due to advancements in technology, in particular in relation to the pressures imposed on flexible legal frameworks that can potentially create a synergy between the interest of inventors, artists, and the general public. There are several issues that have been raised, such as the copyrightability of works created by artificial intelligence, international harmonization of intellectual property laws regarding intellectual property rights, and the role that emerging technologies play in protecting intellectual property rights. The study emphasizes the importance of building strong legal reforms for guaranteeing strong intellectual property protection in the digital economy and for fostering innovation.

title: Trajectory Segment Association Based on Multi-Motion Features of Maritime Targets
authors: Yuqi Fan, Wei Zhang, Long Li, Bingdong Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Maritime target tracking via radars is often interrupted due to the complex and dynamic maritime traffic environment, making trajectory segment association a fundamental problem. Deep learning has demonstrated strong capabilities in processing time series data and has been increasingly applied to trajectory segment association. Trajectory data exhibit rich motion features, which are reflected by the various temporal dependencies and motion element correlations. However, existing deep learning-based methods for trajectory segment association are inadequate to capture the latent temporal dependencies and motion element correlations. In this paper, we propose a trajectory segment association model based on Multi-Motion Features (MMF), which explores the intrinsic motion characteristics of trajectory data from four perspectives, i.e., local motion features, periodic features, trend features, and motion element correlation features. After embedding each trajectory point, the model performs multi-scale convolution on the embedding differentiation to extract local motion features, leverages multi-scale decomposition to obtain trend and periodic features, and exploits an edge-learnable graph convolutional neural network to capture the correlations among motion elements. The model finally determines trajectory segment association by evaluating the similarity between the multi-motion features of old and new trajectory segments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model significantly improves the accuracy of trajectory segment association and reduces both false and missed associations.

title: An Efficient Dynamic Wireless Charging Methodology with Load Forecasting and Improved Gray Wolf Optimization for Electric Vehicles
authors: M. Tamilselvi, Ghassan Samara, Pushplata Dubey, D. Anitha Rexalin, Sorabh Lakhanpal, Kolisetti Giridhara Sravani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are powered by electric motors, which draw energy from rechargeable batteries. The vehicles can provide a greener and more energy-efficient mode of transportation as compared to traditional gasoline engines. The EDWLIG model incorporates a combination of EV charging and grid communication through state-of-the-art wireless control, forecasting, and optimization. It brings adaptation of Constant Voltage (CV), Constant Current (CC), and Constant Voltage-Constant Current (CVCC) modes to battery conditions to regulate the safe. The model features an EV Load Forecasting Module that helps to predict charging requirements, a VPP-Based incentive willingness model that aims at getting users to participate in grid services, and an Improved Gray Wolf Optimization (I-GWO) algorithm as a tool for the productivity enhancement. The Energy Management Strategy (EMS) acts like a traffic controller for energy flowing from different sources to satisfy power demand while still observing the set limits. This analysis focuses on charging efficiency, energy management, power rating, voltage support, and temperature management.

title: Operational Design Domain Exit Detection and Risk Assessment for Roadworks Scenarios in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Ramakrishnan Subramanian, Kiran Bhaskar Sajikumar, Ulrich Büker
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: Operational Design Domains (ODDs) are increasingly used as a foundation for arguing the safety of autonomous vehicle (AV) deployments, yet concrete workflows for monitoring ODD compliance and handling ODD exits in complex environments remain scarce. This paper proposes a camera-based workflow for detecting ODD exits caused by roadworks and assessing the associated risk in order to support the selection of an appropriate Minimum Risk Maneuver (MRM). A CNN-based object detector is fine-tuned on the ROADWork dataset to identify roadwork-related objects, from which three frame-wise metrics are derived: the distance to the nearest detected object, an area-weighted per-frame probability of roadworks existence, and a risk metric that incorporates both distance and existence probability information, including the presence of dynamic actors such as workers and work vehicles. To increase robustness against detection noise and misclassifications, these signals are temporally smoothed using exponential moving averages. The workflow is evaluated qualitatively on two representative scenarios, where the AV either approaches a work zone along a straight road or encounters it suddenly after a turn. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework can detect ODD exits in advance, generate interpretable risk profiles, and provide a structured basis for integrating MRM decision logic into an ODD monitoring system.

title: Combining AI and Blockchain for Secure and Ethical Medical AI Application
authors: Roshan D. Suvaris, Riddhi Chawla, Bhavani R, Prasanta Chatterjee Biswas, Swapan Banerjee, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The combination of blockchain with the application of AI results from the growing need for data safety and confidentiality in the healthcare industry. The article is concerned with the integration of these systems in the clinical diagnostics, health records, and pharmacological supply chain leadership. To investigate how blockchain guarantees data durability via transferred storage methods and how AI uses this data for making decision systems by means of deep learning and immediate analysis, it reviews the literature and analyses case studies. The findings demonstrate that although AI improves diagnostic accuracy and care decision-making, blockchain distributes data for patient confidentiality and integrity. There is a lot of promise for intelligent and individualised growth in the healthcare sector with the combination of blockchain and AI technologies.

title: Markerless Craniofacial Repair Surgery Navigation Method Based on Depth Vision and Projection Mapping
authors: Yuze She, Jingqi Li, Qiushi Dai, Guo Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: This paper presents a markerless navigation method for craniofacial repair surgery based on depth vision and projection mapping. The method acquires multi-view depth images of a skull phantom. These images are combined with static scanning and offline registration to generate a high-precision point cloud. A coarse-to-fine registration strategy is employed. It uses the Iterative Closest Point (ICP) algorithm to precisely align the point cloud with the preoperative CT-derived 3D model. A camera and a projector are jointly calibrated. Then, the surgical cutting boundaries are projected as a 2D image onto the physical skull surface. This creates an intuitive visual guide for the operative region. This technique offers a novel intraoperative guidance scheme for craniofacial repair surgery. It demonstrates significant advantages in intuitiveness and operational simplicity. Thus, it provides a reference for the advancement of precision in craniofacial surgery.

title: Sustainability of Telemedicine: A Comparative Analysis with In-Person Visitation
authors: Leopoldo Angrisani, Mauro D’Arco, Egidio De Benedetto, Luigi Duraccio, Immacolata Esposito, Annarita Tedesco
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Telemedicine is often regarded as a sustainable alternative to in-person consultations, primarily due to the reduction in patient travel. However, this is only a partial perspective, as it overlooks other sources of environmental impact, such as energy consumption from devices, data transmission, and digital infrastructures. This study introduces a method to compare the environmental impact of remote and traditional consultations in telemonitoring scenarios. The proposed method integrates Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) principles with the Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement (GUM) and Monte Carlo simulations, in order to provide probability distributions of the impact. This approach differs from traditional ones, which typically report sustainability estimations as single-point values. The case study shows that the sustainability of medical consultations is influenced by variables such as travel distance and the duration of the visit. The findings indicate that, under specific circumstances, either remote or in-person consultations may represent the more sustainable option. Consequently, telemedicine should not be regarded as intrinsically more sustainable, but rather as context-dependent.

title: Blockchain-Based Evidence Authentication Systems: Enhancing Security and Integrity in Criminal Justice
authors: Lakshmi Priya Vinjamuri, S Jenifer Stella, Aakriti Shukla, Mirzayev Giyosbek, Saranya A, M C Janaki
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: To find the definition of smart justice, criminal justice should be effective, fair, and technologically advanced. The national security, fiscal security and population security are highly dependent on the early detection of adverse behavior. However, certain undesirable parts of society are encouraged to commit criminals due to the inequality in the standards of livelihood that may put a strain on the balance of civilization and psychological tranquility. These problems can now be addressed and produce a holistic smart criminal justice system that unpredictably identifies such criminal acts because of the progress in artificial intelligence (AI). The present paper is based on a model of using AI technologies such as deep learning (DL), blockchain, and security strategies to analyze, infer, and propose how criminal justice management can be arranged. Specifically, the architecture offers a secure and inseparable platform to deal with legal documents and operations and preserve data privacy owing to the integration of blockchain. In this study, the authors create a convolution neural network (CNN)-based Xception algorithm named BlockCrime, which is used to detect criminal acts and improve community security. The blockchain innovation securely keeps the known details of criminal activity and alerts the nearest judicial departments. Due to the lack of databases, the transfer learning was chosen, and CNN-oriented Xception approach was used. The Xception new model is evaluated based on a number of evaluation metrics and surpasses the earlier CNN systems in terms of accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{9 6. 6 0 \%} )$</tex>. The advantages of the suggested paradigm are in its effectiveness and rapidity, lower risk of getting an error, consistency of applying criminal justice, and additional security and confidentiality.

title: Greenhouses for Future Homes with Blockchain-IoT Implementation
authors: Santiago Cortés Tovar, Jhon F. Gutierrez Rodriguez, Luis F. Leal Baute, P.hD. Alexander Pérez Ruiz, P.hD. Carlos A. Gonzalez Amarillo
abstract: This project proposes the design and implementation of modular greenhouses enhanced with IoT, blockchain and AI technologies to promote sustainable urban agriculture. By integrating real-time environmental monitoring, smart contracts and traceable digital transactions, the system enables efficient crop management and secure peer-to-peer exchanges. It aims to improve trade security, reduce import dependency and foster digital trust in urban communities.

title: Technological Impact of Media Dynamics on Public Perception and Political Influence
authors: Boola Choudhary, Ashutosh Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Media plays an important role in modern life and specifically in framing public perception. Overtime media in it extend forms has become one of the most approachable means of reaching the public at large. Now a days public perception is shaped by media platforms and it is increasingly used by corporates to drive customers in their favor. Though many studies have been performed to analyses the role of media on public opinion but specific quantitative analysis with in Indian context remains in research gap. This study explores the multifaceted relationship between media dynamics and public opinion, emphasizing how various forms of media impact political behavior, decision-making, and societal attitudes. The study examines the intricate relationship between media's role and its impact on public perception, focusing on demographic preferences and media's influence on political and societal narratives. The findings highlight the evolving media landscape, with a clear shift towards digital and social media platforms. These insights will provide actionable guidance for media practitioners and policymakers, emphasizing the media's multifaceted role in shaping societal narratives, trust, and political engagement.

title: Precise Online Soft Measurement Method for Tobacco Leaf Moisture Content Based on Temporal Reflectance Method and Adaptive Learning
authors: Xuedi Pan, Changhe Cheng, Ronghua Bin, Xiaozhong Zhao, Qi Chen, Xinguang Shao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: To address the challenge of achieving highprecision online detection of moisture content in tobacco processing, this study proposes an online soft measurement algorithm framework for tobacco leaf moisture that integrates time-domain reflectometry (TRM) modeling and an adaptive learning mechanism. First, based on the theory of electromagnetic wave propagation in multiphase media, this study constructs a mapping relationship between the complex permittivity of tobacco leaves and moisture content. Simultaneously, at the theoretical level, the round-trip propagation time of the TRM signal is used to physically represent moisture information. Building upon this, this study designs an I/Q complex signal temporal representation model. Furthermore, a sliding window and noise perturbation mechanism are introduced to enhance feature robustness. For indepth model optimization, a CNN-BiGRU hybrid deep network is constructed to achieve multi-scale feature extraction and temporal dependency modeling of the reflective signal. Finally, to address the model drift problem caused by environmental fluctuations and raw material differences in industrial scenarios, an adaptive online learning strategy is proposed. The experiment was conducted on a tobacco re-drying production line in Zhejiang Province, and a dataset containing 3286 batches of samples was constructed at the database level. This dataset collected TRM reflectance signals, ambient temperature and humidity, and laboratory standard moisture data. Results showed that the proposed method achieved an RMSE of 0.82%, MAE of 0.59%, a coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.964, and RPD of 3.98 on the test set. This indicates a significant improvement over PLS (RMSE 1.84%, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.862) and CNNLSTM (RMSE 1.18%, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.931). In experiments with different tobacco varieties, the model's average error was controlled within 1%. In temperature and humidity robustness tests, the model's RMSE at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 32^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and 38° C were 0.78%, 0.83%, and 0.94%, respectively. 30 consecutive days of online experiments showed that after introducing the adaptive mechanism, the model's RMSE only increased from 0.83% to 0.92%. Meanwhile, the model's single-sample inference time is 8.1 ms.

title: A KAN-Improved Dual-Branch Model for Synchronous Motor Fault Diagnosis Based on Time-Frequency Fusion
authors: Wei Liu, Tao Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: To overcome the limitations of conventional dual-branch fault diagnosis models in feature extraction completeness, robustness, and interpretability, this paper proposes a Kolmogorov–Arnold Network (KAN) enhanced dual-branch fault diagnosis framework for synchronous motors. Rotor current signals are processed using Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) to construct a 5×1024 multimodal feature matrix. The proposed architecture consists of a spatial branch based on a KAN-enhanced MLP Swin Transformer and a temporal branch employing a KAN–ECA–TCN network. A KAN-based fusion layer is further introduced to achieve nonlinear deep feature integration between the two branches. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves 100% diagnostic accuracy on clean datasets and a precision of 97.84% under 0 dB mixed Gaussian and impulse noise. Compared with existing methods, the proposed approach demonstrates superior diagnostic accuracy, noise robustness, and interpretability.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Underwater Acoustic Target Recognition
authors: Archana Ghate, Gajanan Birajdar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Recognition of underwater sound targets has made significant progress. Researchers have mainly focused on identifying the best features and improving classification methods. The most common ways to extract information from acoustic data are waveform, time-frequency, and auditory features. A ShipEar acoustic dataset is used to train and evaluate the models to determine their perform in real-world classification scenarios. Experimental findings show that by capturing both spectral and temporal relationships in the data, hybrid deep learning models outperform conventional methods in classification accuracy. A new approach is now being proposed that uses a CNN together with LSTM and GRU models running in parallel for underwater auditory target recognition. The proposed method achieved accuracy of 92.82% on the ShipsEar dataset. It performed better than the CNN-LSTM and CNN-GRU models by 2.31% and 1.51%. This result suggests the method is more effective at modeling temporal dependencies for underwater acoustic target detection. The results demonstrate the potential of the suggested framework for practical acoustic monitoring applications and indicate its scalability for use in intelligent audio-based detection and classification systems.

title: Physics-Informed Deep Operator Network Surrogate for Lithium-Ion Cell Dynamics
authors: Rahmat Adesunkanmi, Yassine Hafiane, Adel Alaeddini, Mahesh Krishnamurthy
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Physics-based lithium-ion cell models, such as the Doyle-Fuller-Newman (DFN) pseudo-two-dimensional (P2D) model, provide high-fidelity predictions of voltage and temperature, but their computational cost limits deployment in real-time and embedded battery-management workflows. We present a physics-informed operator-learning surrogate that maps timevarying current profiles, ambient conditions, and parametric variations to voltage and lumped-temperature trajectories while simultaneously learning latent electrochemical fields. The proposed model uses an encoder-trunk architecture to condition on the current history and cell parameters jointly. To improve generalization and physical consistency without requiring internal P2D states as supervision, we introduce collocation-based physics regularization that enforces selected P2D residuals together with boundary and initial constraints on the predicted internal fields. Numerical results show that the proposed surrogate can achieve accurate trajectory prediction across varying current profiles and parameter settings, offering a significant computational speedup relative to full-order physics-based simulation, making it promising for battery digital twin and control-oriented applications.

title: A Hierarchical Model Predictive Control Strategy with Gradient Direction Correction for T-Type Three-Level Grid-Connected Inverters
authors: Wei Liu, Ziliang Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced model predictive control (MPC) strategy for LCL-type grid-connected inverters. To address the inherent limitations of conventional MPC algorithms—namely, directionally unguided candidate solution generation and low computational efficiency—a gradient-direction-rectified hierarchical candidate generation (GDR-HCG) mechanism is introduced. A sixth-order state-space model is established to characterize the system dynamics, and a multi-objective optimization function is formulated, integrating dynamic reference trajectory tracking, integral error compensation, and control increment constraints. Results demonstrate that, compared to traditional enumeration-based methods, the proposed approach significantly reduces the number of candidate solutions within a 100-μs control cycle, enhances neutral-point voltage regulation in grid-connected inverters.

title: Apple Strain(ge) Measurement: Bringing Printed Electronics into the Orchard
authors: Michele Gullino, Albert Heinrich Lanthaler, Pietro Ibba, Sahira Vasquez, Mohammad Yusuf Mulla, Niko Münzenrieder, Giuseppe Cantarella, Paolo Lugli, Walter Guerra, Luigi Manfrini, Luisa Petti
abstract: Monitoring apple fruit growth directly in the orchard is essential for precision orchard management. By accurately estimating the final fruit size, growers can fine-tune agronomic practices such as fruit thinning and irrigation to optimise harvest quality. This study focuses on tools used for fruit growth measurement. Traditionally, this has been performed manually using vernier calipers. More recently, fruit dendrometers have allowed for continuous monitoring. However, neither method is widely adopted: the former is labor-intensive, and the latter is often expensive and bulky. In this work, we explore a stretchable, printed resistive strain sensor as an innovative alternative to current state-of-the-art solutions. A stretchable carbon-based ink was printed in a meander pattern on thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU). This approach aim to address key limitations of existing methods by offering a lightweight, low-cost sensor that adheres directly to the fruit surface. By adopting a field-oriented perspective, the lab characterisation was designed to closely replicate real-world operating conditions. Under these conditions, the sensor, tested using a custom-made stretching setup, demonstrated a sensitivity of 0.153 ± 0.0032/%. These preliminary results are promising and suggest that this approach can offer a scalable, maintenance-free solution for smart orchard monitoring.

title: Effect of Compaction Processing on Residual Stress in Molded Sulfur-Based Components
authors: Lin Wang, Chunguang Xu, Xiaowei Guo, Zekai Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: To address crack initiation and deformation in sulfur-bonded propellant rings caused by concentrated residual stress during compression molding, high-energy ultrasonic vibration was integrated into the compaction process for in-situ stress modulation. Residual stress was non-destructively quantified using the critical refracted longitudinal (LCR) wave time-of-flight method. The effects of molding parameters and ultrasonic excitation on stress distribution and structural integrity were systematically examined across specimens with varying densities. A calibrated stress coefficient of 0.254 was obtained for the batch, with all density groups showing no detectable cracks or porosity, confirming the efficacy of the optimized formulation and processing route. Ultrasonic-assisted molding reduced peak residual stress by 25-40%, while higher-density specimens exhibited more uniform hoop stress distributions with elevated magnitudes. The results reveal a synergistic interaction between material density and process parameters in tailoring the internal stress field, offering mechanistic insights and practical strategies for precision molding sulfur-based energetic composites. The proposed approach provides a viable framework for residual stress management in thermoset polymer systems for defense applications.

title: Robotics in Sustainability: Opportunities and Challenges for a Holistic Environmental Assessment
authors: Leopoldo Angrisani, Zuyan Chen, Egidio De Benedetto, Luigi Duraccio, Immacolata Esposito, Zhendai Huang, Shuai Li, Fabrizio Lo Regio, Annarita Tedesco
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Robotics offers significant potential for advancing sustainability across various sectors, including environmental monitoring, precision agriculture, and manufacturing. By improving efficiency and enabling more precise interventions, robots can help meet critical global challenges, including those outlined by the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) of the United Nations. However, the environmental benefits of robotics must be balanced against the challenges they introduce, such as high energy consumption, material waste, and the depletion of resources, particularly in the case of multi-robot systems.This paper examines the dual impact of robotics on sustainability, focusing on both the positive contributions of robotics and the environmental issues that arise from their production, use, and disposal. The paper aims to lay the groundwork for overcoming the current fragmented research, which often addresses robotic subsystems in isolation rather than considering the integrated systems as a whole. For a comprehensive framework that evaluates the full environmental impact of robotic technologies, the paper draws attention to the need for an integrated, holistic approach to sustainability in robotics, ensuring that technological advancements align with environmental goals.

title: NeuroLingo – Smart Brain Health Test in Indian Languages
authors: Vijay K, Thilak Kumar V, Vishalini E, Shivani S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Early detection of cognitive reduction is essential in directing the neurodegenerative conditions like dementia and Alzheimer's disease. Although conventional screening tools such as the Mini-Mental state Examination (MMSE) and Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) were mostly restricted to English, limiting their applicability among the rural and the low-literacy populations in India. To overcome these obstacles, we recommend NeuroLingo, a multilingual, AI-driven brain health assessment platform that uses natural language processing (NLP), The automatic speech recognition (ASR), and machine learning models were comprehensively cognitive screening. The system provides simple games like exercises to check to evaluate memory, attention, understanding, and language abilities using both spoken and written responses in Indian languages. The system uses a mix of rule-based scoring and machine learning models, while adjusting the scores according to a person's age and education level so the results are fair for everyone. Reports generated include domain wise scores and overall brain health index (BHI), offering actionable insights for individuals, caregivers, and clinicians. By combining multilingual accessibility with AI- powered analysis, NeuroLingo provides a scalable, cost effective, and inclusive solution for early brain health screening in underserved populations.

title: SEFM: A Self-Evolving Framework for Multirobot Embodied Agents with Semantic Frontier Assessment and Collective Long-Term Memory
authors: Junning Guo, Zhenyuan Li, Zibin Lin, Dahai Lin, Hexiang Zheng, Tingyun Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Symposium on Intelligent Robotics and Systems (ISoIRS)
abstract: With advances in robotics and artificial intelligence, autonomous embodied agents are increasingly deployed in large-scale dynamic settings, including smart manufacturing, emergency response, and urban digital twins. These applications demand system-level capabilities such as cross-domain collaboration, scalable coordination, and longterm adaptability. However, many multi-robot exploration and navigation methods still require expensive multi-agent training, generalize poorly, and lack reusable shared experience or reliable correction when environments evolve. This study presents SEFM, a self-evolving framework for multi-robot embodied agents with semantic frontier assessment and collective long-term memory. In pre-exploration, agents run simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) to build occupancy maps and extract geometric frontiers. A visionlanguage model interprets the visual context near each frontier to estimate semantic utility, and then performs global task allocation to reduce overlap and prioritize informative regions. Observations about places, objects, and attributes are consolidated into shared memory for open-vocabulary retrieval in downstream object search. During object retrieval, if memory provides no valid cue or the target is absent at a memorized location, a large language model (LLM) proposes relocation hypotheses and dispatches agents to verify them in parallel. New evidence is written back to memory to refine subsequent search. On HM3D-OVON with TurtleBot3 Burger using RGB-D and 2D LiDAR, and with Qwen-VL-Plus and DeepSeek-R1, SEFM achieves a success rate of 70.6% and a success weighted by path length of 30.6% with two agents, and a success rate of 74.0% and a success weighted by path length of 35.9% with three agents. SEFM outperforms representative HM3D-aligned baselines such as Co-NavGPT. Results demonstrate robust closed-loop collaboration without dense semantic maps or multi-agent reinforcement learning, supporting continual adaptation in dynamic indoor environments.

title: Hardware Implementation and Verification of Early Image Signal Processing Stages Using OpenISP
authors: Muhammad Furqan, Karsen Price, Muhammad Awais Bin Altaf
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This work presents a hardware-efficient implementation of early Image Signal Processing (ISP) stages for portable devices, addressing strict area and power constraints. Four key modules, Dead Pixel Correction (DPC), Black Level Correction (BLC), Anti-Aliasing Filter (AAF), and White Balance Control (WBC), were implemented in synthesizable RTL and validated against the open-source OpenISP framework using 30 RAW images. The proposed design achieved over 99% pixel-wise accuracy, confirming functional equivalence with the software reference. These results establish a reproducible foundation for low-power ISP hardware targeting mobile, IoT, and edge-vision systems.

title: A Metrological Framework for Sustainable Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Maurizio Giacobbe, Salvatore Distefano
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: Metrology is pivotal in "smart" ecosystems where digital technologies combined with physical processes sampled by sensors enable monitoring and controlling the world in real-time, namely cyber-physical systems (CPS). The integration of metrology principles into CPS is crucial for the assessment and optimization of its efficiency in terms of energy consumption, resource utilization, costs, as well as environmental and social impacts. This paper presents a novel framework that integrates sensing, edge processing, artificial intelligence, and system-level assessments of CPS sustainability bridging measurement science and sustainable computing practices towards environmentally responsible digital transformation. To demonstrate this framework suitability and effectiveness, the sustainability assessment of a case study based on a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model deployed on a CPS is reported in the paper.

title:
LINACWatch
                    <sup>®</sup>
                    : An advanced patient and machine quality assurance system based on logfile
authors: Gaetano Gagliardo, Caterina Muto, Ferdinando Francomacaro, Ivona Zlatkova, Vincenzo Iorio, Marcello Serra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The increasing complexity of radiotherapy techniques such as IMRT and VMAT demands highly accurate yet time-efficient quality assurance (QA) procedures. While conventional phantom-based systems remain the reference standard, they are often resource-intensive and decoupled from real-time treatment conditions. LINACWatch<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> (Qualiformed, La Rochesur-Yon, FRA) is a log file-based system designed to complement traditional QA workflows by enabling fast, non-invasive verification of beam delivery parameters. LINACWatch<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> automatically reconstructs the delivered fluence and compares it to the planned distribution, calculating quantitative indicators such as the γ-index without requiring physical detectors. Its integration into clinical practice has allowed session-specific QA in real time, reducing the number of phantom-based measurements in selected cases and improving operational efficiency without compromising safety. This study aims to assess the error-detection capability of a log file–based platform LINACWatch<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> in view of its potential integration into routine clinical practice for both machine and patient quality assurance (QA). Its performance was compared against the Delta4<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> measurement system in the context of QA for VMAT treatment deliveries.

title: Integrating Data Science into Sustainable Heritage: Predicting Water Scarcity Severity Using Seasonal and Demographic Indicators
authors: Fuad Musleh, Ranyah Taha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustaining Heritage: The Future of Conservation: Embracing Technology and Innovation for Sustainable Heritage (ICSH)
abstract: Water scarcity is a critical global challenge, especially in arid and semi-arid regions where climate variability and increasing demand strain limited water resources. Consequently, reliable forecasting of water scarcity severity is essential for proactive management and sustainable development. This study investigates the application of machine learning (ML) techniques to forecast water scarcity severity using seasonal hydro-climatic indicators collected from 405 river basins worldwide. Four models were evaluated: Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC), RF, DTC, and SVM–OvR. The results revealed that ensemble methods consistently outperformed single classifiers. GBC achieved the most balanced performance, with the highest recall (95.68%) and F1-score (94.66%), reflecting its ability to capture complex nonlinear relationships while minimizing false positives and false negatives. Although RF achieved the highest accuracy (82.72%), its lower recall slightly reduced its robustness compared to GBC. The novelty of this study lies in its integration of seasonal indicators as predictive features for classifying water scarcity severity, providing a practical and data-driven forecasting framework. This approach enhances the ability to generate actionable severity classifications, enabling early warning and decision support for policymakers and water managers. Future work will expand the feature space to include socio-economic indicators, apply hybrid ensemble–deep learning approaches, and evaluate scalability across larger basins to strengthen real-world applicability.

title: Impact of IOT &AR on Customer Engagement in Interior Design Retail
authors: Mona Chaudhary, Sumita Mukherjee, Namrata Pancholi, Kavita Thapliyal, Siya Sikka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This study investigates the extent to which IoT and AR technologies influence consumer behaviour and brand perception within the retail interior design sector. Employing a quantitative research methodology, structured surveys were administered to 100 participants across three key groups: (1) consumers interested in purchasing interior design products, (2) interior designers and architects utilizing AR/IoT tools, and (3) furniture retailers implementing smart technologies. The data were analysed using SPSS, applying descriptive statistics, correlation, regression, and ANOVA techniques to explore relationships between technology adoption and purchasing behaviour. The findings indicate that AR significantly boosts consumer confidence and improves decision-making by offering real-time product visualization within personalized environments, thus reducing purchase uncertainty. Meanwhile, IoT facilitates automation, enhances customer tracking, and enables smart, data-driven retail experiences, contributing to higher convenience and customization. However, the study also reveals that immersive technological experiences do not necessarily lead to long-term customer retention, challenging the assumption that innovation alone ensures brand loyalty. The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) and Augmented Reality (AR) allow brands to offer enhanced product visualization, personalization, and consumer engagement, effectively bridging the gap between physical and digital retail environments.

title: AI and Blockchain for Preventing Deepfake-Based Cybercrimes
authors: Pawan Madanan, Christabell Joseph, C. Vijai, Poonam Bhargav, Maddikera Kalyan Chakravarthi, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The artificial intelligence (AI) has led to the increased popularity of deepfakes, or fake media, which imitates human appearance, voice, and behaviour. These inventions are very dangerous to perpetrating cybercrime, yet they are of both negative and positive application in mobility and cinematography. Techniques of dealing with this problem are both active (liveness checking, watermarks, blockchain-assigned content check) and passive (physiological identification of cues, deep learning, artefact and frequency measurement) detection methods. Even so, there is still the adaptation of attackers to remain invisible in the fight of deepfakes to be generated and identified. In order to ensure ethical and safe utilization of AI-generated content, the paper emphasizes the importance of hybrid methods of detection, powerful rules and regulations, and international cooperation. Meticulously Searching Hyperledger Fabric blockchain technology, known as Smart Contracts, may come in handy to deal with the deepfake problem. The paper suggests a blockchain-dependent architecture to stop deepfakes and attempts to compile current studies on blockchain's potential to safeguard internet-based verification.

title: Study on the Deformation Mechanism and Numerical Simulation of Power Transmission Tower Foundations in Cold Regions under Multi-Factor Coupling
authors: Yang Zhang, Boyu Jiao, Haichao Peng, Cong Tan, Qi Xu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Under the background of climate change, the warming of global permafrost areas has accelerated the degradation of permafrost engineering properties, severely threatening the long-term stability of power transmission tower foundations in cold regions. To accurately predict the long-term deformation behavior of power transmission tower foundations in freeze-thaw environments, a refined three-dimensional numerical model based on "water-heat-stress" multi-physics coupling is developed. Through a systematic full-factor experimental design and large-scale parametric simulations of five key factors, global sensitivity analysis using the extended FAST method reveals that annual average temperature and groundwater table depth are the dominant factors influencing foundation deformation. For final settlement, their first-order effect indices are 0.48 and 0.20, while their total-effect indices reach 0.68 and 0.35, indicating significant interaction effects. The simulation results show that the foundation deformation follows three typical patterns: stable (≈65% of cases), continuous development (≈25%), and destructive development (≈10%). The maximum settlement increased from approximately 18 mm at the 5th year to 32 mm at the 10th year under adverse conditions, accompanied by the expansion and coalescence of the plastic zone (up to ~2.8 m in diameter). The synergistic effect of elevated temperatures and high water levels exacerbates water migration and ice lens growth, leading to progressive foundation deformation. To optimize the design, a strategy of 'thermal management and hydrological control' is proposed, recommending measures such as lowering the groundwater table, using insulation, and enhancing drainage. Future research could further integrate regional climate prediction models to provide precise decision support for the foundation design under long-term climate change.

title: Situational Awareness-Driven Intrusion Detection Framework Using Optimized Machine Learning Models
authors: Vinothkumar U, Dharshini V, Gokul Hari S, Luxmanasamy P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11546717/ (Caused by ReadTimeoutError("HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=15)"))

title: A Health Monitoring System for Fatigue Damage of Port Quayside Container Cranes
authors: Na Li, Xu Zhang, Guang Chen, Li Chen, Zhijie Wang, Keqin Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Aiming at the fatigue damage monitoring and maintenance requirements of key structural components of quayside container cranes, a data-driven intelligent monitoring system is proposed. Firstly, the structural mechanics model is established by finite element simulation, and the stress concentration area is identified as the potential fatigue failure risk point. On this basis, a real-time stress monitoring system is constructed by using distributed optical fiber sensor network to realize the dynamic acquisition of stress time- history data of key parts in the process of crane lifting operation. Then, a data-driven diagnosis algorithm is developed by combining rain flow counting method, stress spectrum statistical analysis and Miner linear cumulative damage theory, and a structural health status assessment model is established to predict the residual fatigue life and damage evolution trend of each monitoring node. The research results can provide quantitative decision-making basis for preventive maintenance of port machinery, and effectively improve the whole life cycle management level of equipment by dynamically optimizing maintenance cycle and maintenance focus.

title: Tourists' Attitudes Toward Low Carbon Tourism in the Eastern Economic Corridor of Thailand
authors: Rapeepun Siriwatpatara, Sitarawee Nithinanthakul, Thitirat Thavornsujaritkul
publish: 2025 Conference on Digital Economy and Fintech Innovation (DEFI)
abstract: Global warming remains a pressing global concern in 2025, driven by human activities across sectors such as population growth, economic expansion, industrialization, and the rapid development of tourism. In response, the Thai government has promoted "Low Carbon Tourism" as a strategic initiative to reduce carbon dioxide emissions. This study investigates tourists’ attitudes toward low carbon tourism within Thailand’s Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC).Data were collected from 286 tourists visiting the EEC using structured questionnaires. Statistical analyses included percentage, mean, standard deviation, one-way ANOVA, and Scheffé’s post hoc test, with significance determined at the 0.05 level.Results indicate that most respondents were female, aged 22–38, held a bachelor’s degree, worked in private-sector companies, and earned a monthly income between 30,001 and 50,000 baht. Overall, tourists expressed a high level of importance regarding low carbon tourism in the EEC. Specifically, attitudes were strongly positive in areas such as green mindset, green attractions, green communities, green activities, green services, and green plus. However, attitudes toward green transportation were rated at a moderate level.Significant differences in attitudes were found based on demographic factors. Gender differences were observed in perceptions of green attractions, while educational background influenced attitudes toward green mindset and green transportation, with statistical significance at the 0.05 level.

title: Cognitive e-KYC: A Convergence of Biometric Forensics and Digital Identity Validation
authors: Vedant Kanoje, Abhiyank Yadav, Vinit Karlekar, Avadhesh Kumar Shah, Tejas Verma, Saravanan S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: e-KYC checks are of great importance for secure and efficient identity authentication in financial and regulatory services that are slow, error-prone and fraud-sensitive to traditional KYC methods. This study suggests an AI-driven e-KYC system that extracts important details such as AADHAAR number, name, date of birth, address, and integrates optical character detection (OCR) with Tesseract.js to achieve a processing time of 23 seconds per document with 90% accuracy of Aadhaar cards. The system uses deep learning-based facial recognition to compare it with 5% false negative rates in 1.5 seconds compared to live images and ID photos. Machine learning models recognize data inconsistencies to prevent fraud, but Uidai Aadhaar - API users details are detailed against official state records to improve reliability. Documentary security is guaranteed through ReportLab and Pypdf2 encryption. This adds 1.2 seconds of overhead per PDF with controlled file approval to meet data protection standards. End-to-end latency with stable internet remains under 10 seconds. This scalable and secure framework combines image improvements, document analysis, biometry, ML checks and API authentication to significantly improve the accuracy and recognition of fraud via traditional methods. Ideal for financial institutions, government agencies and digital platforms, it offers the latest automated AI improvement approach to reduce workloads, accelerate onboarding and check digital identity for manual reviews.

title: Microstructure variation characterization using magnetic Barkhausen noise image under tensile stress
authors: Jia Liu, Gui Yun Tian, Kun Zeng, Chun Jun Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: Stress is the crucial factor of ferromagnetic material failure origin. However, microscale visual analysis of ferromagnetic material property variations under stress using non-destructive testing methods has not been achieved so far. In this study, in order to scan the sample, magnetic Barkhausen noise (MBN) signals under in situ tensile tests is detected by high-spatial-resolution magnetic probe. Root-mean-square (RMS) is extracted from MBN to quantify the variation of MBN on different locations. The 2-dimension (2D) images of MBN are established by a spatial mapping of the RMS value. The domain-wall (DW) motion, grain, and grain boundary (GB) are detected using a magneto-optical Kerr (MOKE) image to verify the accuracy of the MBN image. the difference of the DW distribution caused by tensile stress and microstructure affect the RMS of MBN, which further affect the results of MBN images. The results of the MBN image demonstrate the capability of the method to visualize the variation of microstructure with high-resolution probe, which can be used to evaluate and predict the unstable area of the early damage in the future.

title: Research on the Thermal Design of a Certain Electronic Device Structure
authors: Xuesong Liang, Wangwei Qiao, Lei Hang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the rapid development of integrated circuits, electronic devices have become increasingly compact and more powerful in modern technological environments. As the brain of electronic devices, integrated circuits rely on thermal design as their temperature regulation system, which directly determines the lifespan and reliability of these devices. This study focuses on the thermal design of a specific electronic device structure, aiming to balance production costs while ensuring efficient and reliable operation. The research proposes a design approach for thermal design in electronic devices. First, it investigates theories related to the thermal design of this scheme, then designs heat dissipation pathways for the electronic device. Subsequently, thermal analysis and design are conducted on these pathways, leading to the proposal of a forced-air cooling solution using heat pipe finned radiators. In the design of the cooling solution, emphasis is placed on optimizing finned radiators, analyzing the thermal resistance network of T-components, and selecting heat pipes and fans. In terms of simulation analysis, the focus is on verifying whether the simulated results of the electronic device, heat pipes, and inlet/outlet air temperatures meet thermal design requirements. Based on the analysis results, the cooling solution for the electronic device is finalized.

title: Exploring Factors Influencing AI Adoption in Accounting and Auditing Practices
authors: Anderes Gui, Angela Wangsadjaja, Celine Bineva Chandra, Yuvaraj Ganesan, I Gusti Made Karmawan, Riki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: AI is radically changing the accounting and auditing profession across the globe these days. However, few countries— including Indonesia—have extensively studied the readiness and acceptance of AI by accounting professionals, auditors, and students. This gap will be filled in the study by exploring the antecedents impacting the adoption of artificial intelligence in accounting and auditing in Indonesia. Therefore, we develop a conceptual framework to investigate the intention to use AI in accounting and its underlying factors such as performance expectancy, task crafting, facilitating conditions, security, and trust. Data were retrieved from a questionnaire distributed to accounting practitioners and students who are familiar with AI in Banten, Jakarta, and West Java. A purposive sampling procedure was used, leading to 402 valid responses. Data were characterized by Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM). Facilitating conditions, performance expectancy, security, and trust affected intentions to use AI in accounting and auditing significantly, while task crafting had no significant effect on usage intention. It will also advance our knowledge of AI acceptance in emerging markets and have importance to educational institutions, policymakers, and developers adopting AI in accounting through better training, trust-building programs, and infrastructure projects in localities.

title: Prompt - Based Transfer Learning for Handwritten Character Recognition with Reduced Character Rate
authors: Latha B, Nandakumar R, Vinothkumar B, Indresh S, Shifana Barveen J, Sri Subeetchanya B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Handwritten Character Recognition is a notable research area due to its extensive practical and financial benefits. Aim: The aim of this study is to perform the character recognition in the handwritten character using the PromptBased Transfer Learning approach and EfficientNet. A comparative performance analysis against VGG-16 and ResNet architectures demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed model. Materials and Methods: The study is classified into two groups: Group 1 (Comparison Method) uses the model of VGG-16 and ResNet on the model of handwritten Epochs. This research has been executed in the Google Colab. Result: The metrics used to analyze the performance include accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The Prompt-Based Transfer Learning model achieved better performance with an accuracy of 98% whereas the ResNet accuracy gives 91.89% using and VGG-16 produces <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 9. 2 5}$</tex> %. Conclusion: The PromptBased Transfer Learning approach is superior and statistically significant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) than the standard CNN approach, which confirms the appropriateness of the approach.

title: Learning@Edge: Model-based Edge Learning through Prompt Engineering for Personalized Education
authors: Laxmisha Rai
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: The growth of Large Language Models (LLMs) and the influence of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) tools have given rise to novel learning paradigms for educators. In this paper, we propose Model-based Edge Learning (MbEL), a framework that shifts learning from centralized, human-centric institutions to the "edge" of the network, where individual users interact conversationally with a pre-trained foundational model to acquire knowledge and skills. Unlike traditional edge computing, which focuses on data processing, MbEL emphasizes cognitive offloading and personalized knowledge acquisition. This paper conceptualizes MbEL, illustrating its principles through case studies conducted with the DeepSeek model using prompt engineering methods such as zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting. As this study focuses exclusively on personalized learning through prompt engineering, to ensure response quality, we established a bench-marking system using LLM-as-a-Judge framework with structured prompt templates. This allows learners to systematically evaluate and verify the answers generated by the model. The proposed method is tested for generation, and evaluation of standard algorithms taught in the undergraduate Data Structures course serving as the evaluation domain. Furthermore, we discuss its profound implications for education, including the shift from knowledge retrieval to reasoning development, and challenges such as model bias and academic integrity. However, ethical considerations such as model hallucinations and trusting incorrect answers remain critical, underscoring the continued necessity of human oversight in guiding the student learning process.

title: Interpretable Fault Prediction for Industrial IoT Using a Lightweight Bidirectional LSTM
authors: Zhihui Ran
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things(IoT) systems generate a large amount of operation sensor data, which makes early failure detection more and more important,especially in low-power edge devices.This paper proposes a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory(Bi-LSTM) framework with an effective aggregation strategy based on summary statistics of the final hidden states for capturing degradation patterns of the system units. Experiments are performed in two benchmark datasets: C-MAPSS dataset and NASA battery data set. The results show that the proposed method performs well, requiring few thousand parameters to achieve about 0.95 F1score.Compared with traditional Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model, it keeps the same prediction precision but reduces its scale and maintains almost the same real-time performance. Additionally,an interactive visual analytics tool for analyzing the sensor importance as well as assisting in identifying the faulty components,making it applicable to a real world industry IoT application.

title: Data-Driven Classification of High-Risk Typhoid Areas in Jharkhand via Machine Learning
authors: Sonal Tudu, Anna Shree Kujur, Muskan Pandey, Gitanjali Gupta, Ravi Kumar Burman, Abhishek Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: It used hospital Widal test data during the year 2022-2025, rainfall data during the year 2024 published by the India Meteorological Department, and population data during the year 2011 Census to develop a machine learning system with multiple data sources to make classifications and predict typhoid risks and their occurrence in the ten administrative blocks in the Ranchi district in India. The blocks-month panels to simulate the lag effects of rainfall incorporated both spacetime and hydrology dimensions i.e. Season, Month_Num, Lag_rainfall. Even though the scores of Bero, Burmu and Silli blocks were low-risk at any time, the spatiotemporal patterns were realized to some degree in the exploration analysis, such as the high surges of monsoons in Ratu and Ormanjhi blocks. While the XG Boost classifier worked good for the test set and give an Accuracy of 0.92, Precision of 0.33, Recall of 0.50, and F1value of 0.40, the Random Forest classification fared well, estimating an Accuracy of 0.96, Precision of 1.00, Recall of 0.25, and F1 score of 0.40.. For regression modeling of incidence (Cases_per_1000), Random Forest attained MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.045$</tex>, RMSE =0.113, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.136$</tex>, whereas XG Boost achieved MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.038$</tex>, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.103$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.053$</tex>. According to the SHAP analysis, Cases_per_1000, Population and Number_Tested were the most impactful predictors, while rainfall based variables played a minor role. The K-Means clustering with K <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=3$</tex> effectively mapped out high-, moderate-, and low-risk areas - it also highlighted the monsoon season as the most important episode for intervention within the field of public health management.

title: Online Gaming: An Interdisciplinary Analysis of Legal Frameworks and Engineering Systems
authors: Garima Dhaka Sangwan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The paper discusses the intricate dynamics of online gambling in India using a multimodal approach spanning many areas. We therefore use methods including system engineering, legal studies, and behavioral psychology. A variety of factors, including the spread of artificial intelligence technology, the availability of mobile internet, and rising young people's engagement which have resulted in significant obstacles, are driving great advancement in the Indian gaming sector. The study looks at the many legal systems every state of India has in place, the technology allowing the construction of scalable, safe platforms, and the behavioral impact artificial intelligence games have on young people living in India. Empirical evidence highlights important issues such gaming addiction, academic drop-off, algorithmic influence, and players' legal knowledge awareness. Surveys, statistical analysis (including ANOVA), descriptive metrics, and awareness distribution help us to achieve this. This article tries to provide a legal framework allowing ethical integration of artificial intelligence, centralized law, responsible game development, and educational outreach among other things. When these factors are considered, the study provides legislators, parents, developers, and teachers with a strategic platform from which to minimize the negative consequences of online gaming while maintaining its chances for expansion, education, and creativity.

title: Cloud Computing-Powered HR Data Management System for Organizational Efficiency
authors: R. Venkatesh, M. Arunmozhi, Rabichand Thongam, Sharmila Fernandes, N Kesava Sri Jagrut, Niravkumar R Joshi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Implementation of Cloud based Recruitment System in Human Resources (HR) will increase the efficiency of the organization by simplifying the recruitment process. A cloud-based Applicant Tracking System (ATS) allows the HR teams to automate the most important areas, including the screening of resumes, communication with the candidates, and the scheduling of the interviews. Such automation does not only minimize the human resources but it also speeds up the recruitment process enabling qualified candidates to be placed more quickly. The cloud characteristics of the system enables real-time cooperation between the HR managers and the hiring teams regardless of the location, and this fosters easy communication and decision-making. In addition, analytics tools in the ATS provide significant information on the performance of the candidates, the tendencies of the recruitment process, and the efficiency of the different methods of hiring. Consequently, there will be more informed hiring choices, a decrease in the costs of hiring, and improved candidates experience. Cloud-based recruitment system is vital to the contemporary HR departments, and it enhances the scalability, flexibility, and general effectiveness of recruitment.

title: Design of a High-Precision Conductivity Meter Based on an Improved Adaptive Algorithm
authors: Yide Yan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of fixed threshold, weak anti-interference ability and gain switching jitter in the adaptive gain algorithm of traditional conductivity measurement, a high-precision aqueous solution conductivity meter based on an improved adaptive algorithm is designed. The instrument adopts the mature four-ring electrode structure and Pt100 temperature compensation scheme, with core optimizations focusing on three key technologies: a dynamic threshold adjustment strategy to adapt to different conductivity ranges, the introduction of moving average filtering to suppress random noise interference, and the adoption of a hysteresis control mechanism to avoid frequent gain switching. Experimental test results show that the improved algorithm enables the instrument to have a relative measurement error of ≤0.3% within the range of 1~ 100000μS/cm,, and the number of false gain switches is reduced by 75%. It is suitable for high-precision application scenarios such as complex water quality monitoring and industrial process control.

title: Commissioning of RadCalc Monte-Carlo IDC model for clinical plan verification and EPID-based in vivo dosimetry in radiotherapy
authors: Rocco Mottareale, Valentina d’Alesio, Francesca Buonanno, Marcello Serra, Gianluca Ametrano, Cecilia Arrichiello, Gaetano Gagliardo, Vincenzo Ravo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: RadCalc is a commercially available software developed by LifeLine Software Inc. for independent dose verification in radiotherapy. Originally designed for point dose calculations using conventional algorithms, RadCalc has been widely used in clinical practice as a secondary check system for treatment planning calculations. With the recent integration of a three-dimensional Monte Carlo (MC) algorithm, the software now offers enhanced accuracy and the ability to recalculate full volumetric dose distributions, making it suitable for more advanced applications such as in vivo dosimetry and independent plan verification.RadCalc is intended to be integrated into our institution’s in vivo dosimetry program through the use of Electronic Portal Imaging Device (EPID)-based techniques. EPID-based in vivo dosimetry encompasses a set of computational methods that, by analyzing the signal recorded by the portal imager after transmission through the patient (transit dosimetry), aim to identify clinically relevant deviations before the therapeutic efficacy of the treatment is adversely affected. The validation of the in vivo dosimetry program using RadCalc necessarily requires an initial tuning and validation phase of the software.The present work focuses on the validation of the new RadCalc software, equipped with a three-dimensional Monte Carlo module for the recalculation of radiotherapy treatment plans previously optimized within a Treatment Planning System (TPS). This process has been carried out by verifying the dosimetric accuracy of treatment plans recalculated with RadCalc Monte Carlo algorithm, with the aim of achieving a virtual Dose Quality Acceptance (DQA) passing through an Independent Dose Calculation (IDC) algorithm.Despite their current prominent level of accuracy, radiotherapy treatment plans remain subjected to various sources of uncertainty, arising both from limitations inherent to the TPS dose calculation algorithms and from physical variabilities associated with the treatment delivery system. Consequently, this work represents not only a crucial initial step in the implementation of an EPID-based in vivo dosimetry program, but also an effective strategy to streamline conventional phantom-based DQA procedures for treatment plans.

title: MindScan: Transformer Models for Early Mental Health Detection from Social Media
authors: P. Anitha, S. Ramya, J.K. Kanimozhi, C.A. Kandasamy, K. Jeevitha, M. Venkatesan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The goal of the research is to establish a BERTweet Considering transformer model and the performance is compared with the BiLSTM for early mental health detection from social media text. In this study Group 1 refers to BiLSTM and Group 2 refers to BERTweet both examined with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0, 3 5 6}$</tex> text samples. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{G}$</tex> Power value is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0 \%}$</tex> and threshold is 0.05 % with confidence interval of 95 %. The review takes into consideration the following success metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. The above BERTweet model performs better than BiLSTM with respect to accuracy. The accuracy of the BERTweet is 93.87 % and the BiLSTM is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 4 7 \%}$</tex>. In Mental health detection, the BERTweet effectively captures contextual and emotional patterns in social media texts. It produced the highest accuracy with a statistical significance of 0.5. In this work, it is observed that BERTweet has significantly better accuracy than BiLSTM.

title: A Decentralized Blockchain Framework for Access Management in Precision Agriculture IoT
authors: Ramesh Mailapalli, A.C. Santha Sheela
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies into precision agriculture has transformed farming practices through real-time monitoring, automated irrigation, and data-driven decision-making. However, centralized access control mechanisms remain vulnerable to single points of failure, scalability issues, and security breaches such as spoofing and replay attacks. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a decentralized blockchain-based framework for access management in agricultural IoT environments. The architecture integrates IoT devices, edge gateways, smart contracts, and a distributed ledger to provide secure identity management, automated authorization, and immutable audit trails. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) are combined to enable context-aware authorization, while Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs), Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), and end-to-end encryption strengthen resilience against identity theft and unauthorized access. Performance evaluation demonstrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 5 \%}$</tex> reduction in authentication latency and an access control accuracy of 98.5 %, confirming the framework's scalability and security effectiveness for large-scale precision agriculture deployments.

title: Prediction of Cerebrovascular Accident Risk Using the XGBoost Algorithm and Electronic Health Records
authors: E. Loganathan, G. Jegan, N. Prakash, M. Venkatesan, R. Geetha, P. Anitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The main objective of this research is the risk assessment of cerebrovascular accidents based on the electronic health record and the comparative study for the predictive capabilities of the two algorithms Support Vector Machine and XGBoost. The study was conducted on 820 pregnancies in 681 women who were being treated for heart diseases in a hospital in South India. Information on medical history, pregnancy details, maternal complications, and outcomes was collected and examined using routine statistical analysis. About 14.9% of the pregnancies, and 12 mothers died with heart related problems. Women who had a history of heart problems, severe mitral valve narrowing, previous valve surgery, or pulmonary hypertension were found to have a much higher risk. The proposed risk score showed good predictive ability. Heart problems often occur during pregnancy in women who have rheumatic heart disease. The present study helps in the early identification of stroke risk factors, which aids in better clinical decisions and preventive health care.

title: The Research on Fashion Design Intelligent Evaluation System Integrating XR and AI
authors: Xiaoyun Zhao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: In view of the problems of low evaluation accuracy and long evaluation time, in order to improve the security of the system operation, this paper conducts in-depth research on the fashion design intelligent evaluation system integrating XR and AI. Firstly, the application method based on integrating XR and AI is proposed to build an efficiency mapping from the efficiency of the fashion design intelligent evaluation system to each operation plate, and to extract the features of each operation plate in the fashion design intelligent evaluation system. Then, the fashion design information is evaluated through the advanced XR and ai integration technology, and the operation effect of the fashion design intelligent evaluation system integrating XR and ai is comprehensively analyzed. The experimental results show that the proposed method in this paper can effectively improve the accuracy and stability of the fashion design intelligent evaluation system, to improve the security of the system operation.

title: Optimizing File Classification: A Hybrid Approach Using Natural Language Processing and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Madhura K, Shweta S Aladakatti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In the computerized time, the corporate business has seen a change in perspective from customary in-person cooperations to advanced correspondence mediums, enveloping business conversations, profiles, gatherings, buying, and settlements. This progress has prompted a remarkable expansion in the volume and intricacy of records and texts, requiring progressed AI methods for productive language distinguishing proof across assorted applications. Late progressions in Computerized reasoning, especially in handling regular dialects, have shown noteworthy capacities in understanding complex examples and nonlinear connections inside huge datasets. This review centres around outfitting Normal Language Handling (NLP) and Crossover Profound Learning approaches for the distinguishing proof and grouping of different sources of info like text, voice, and video records. As organizations progressively depend on advanced correspondence, the improvement of refined NLP programs has become basic to meet the developing necessities of client communications through messages, calls, computerized records, and chatbots. Our exploration investigates the reconciliation of message, voice messages, and sound inside NLP and Mixture Profound Learning structures to handle inputs in view of client collaborations, instant message reactions, and sound record recognizable proof. This approach upgrades the adequacy of computerized correspondence as well as essentially influences client inclinations, goals, and requests, consequently reclassifying contemporary help conveyance through email, informing, calls, advanced records, and chatbots as essential contact focuses.
