title: Neuro-Marketing Impact on Consumer Behavior in Search Engine Optimization: A Case Study of Chennai City
authors: Thasneem I, Maheswari G S, Kavitha M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: A lot of businesses can't fully use search engine optimization (SEO) because as it fails to know the optimized content affects the brains. People from Chennai used structured data, keywords, and meta tags to improve their interactions with web pages through search engine optimization. The information was collected and analyzed based on how well the people thought, felt, and remembered things. Content with a lot of keywords and headers that made people feel good got more attention (as measured by eye-tracking) and made people feel more strongly (as measured by EEG data). When neuro-marketing principles were used in search engine optimization (SEO) strategies, engagement, recall, and click-through rates went up by 22%, 30%, and 17%, respectively, compared to traditional SEO methods.

title: Research on Laser-Electromagnetic Ultrasonic Detection Method for Rifling Wear in Gun Barrels
authors: Xin Wang, Wei Wei, Jin Zhang, Xiao-Hu Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE Far East NDT New Technology &amp; Application Forum (FENDT)
abstract: During firing, the inner rifling of a gun barrel is subjected to high-temperature and high-pressure gas erosion as well as projectile cutting friction, leading to severe ablation and wear of the rifling, which significantly affects the service life and combat effectiveness of the artillery. To address the inefficiency and low accuracy of traditional rifling wear detection methods, a laser-electromagnetic ultrasonic detection method is proposed, utilizing a laser as the excitation source and an electromagnetic acoustic transducer (EMAT) as the receiver. The principles of laser-electromagnetic ultrasonic detection for rifling wear in gun barrels are derived, and the effects of laser source parameters and EMAT parameters on ultrasonic signals are analyzed to optimize the probe’s parameter combination. A finite element model of laser-electromagnetic ultrasonic propagation in a gun barrel with rifling wear is established using Comsol Multiphysics software, and the interaction mechanism between ultrasonic longitudinal waves and rifling wear with varying degrees is investigated. A laser-electromagnetic ultrasonic detection experimental system is constructed to excite ultrasonic longitudinal waves for rifling wear detection, and experimental results are compared with simulation results for validation. The analysis demonstrates that the laser-electromagnetic ultrasonic method can effectively detect rifling wear in gun barrels. Through parameter optimization, the optimal probe parameters are determined as follows: laser spot radius of 1mm, laser energy of 700mJ, rise time of 8ns, magnet height of 15mm, magnet width of 10mm, magnet spacing of 2mm, wire diameter of 0.2mm, coil half-width of 5mm, and coil spacing of 4mm. Under these conditions, the quality of the excited and received ultrasonic signals is optimal. Additionally, the method enables detection of rifling wear as small as 0.1mm in the inner rifling. The amplitude of the reflected longitudinal wave increases linearly with the growth of rifling wear, and experimental results align with simulations. This research lays a foundation for quantitative detection of defects and lifespan evaluation of gun barrels.

title: Power System State Estimation: Current Research Status and Future Perspectives
authors: Yuhao Guo, Yu Wang, Fan Tang, Zhenlin Ni, Yan Mei, Dafu Liu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of new energy into power grids, the operating characteristics of power systems have become increasingly complex, posing multiple challenges to state estimation, including missing measurements, noise interference, and topology changes. Focusing on the problem of power system state estimation under high new energy penetration, this paper reviews the current research status of both traditional model-driven methods and data-driven approaches, and analyzes the limitations of existing methods in terms of estimation accuracy, robustness, and adaptability to diverse operating scenarios. On this basis, in light of the demand for online perception in complex operating conditions, future research directions are proposed from the perspective of coordinated modeling of measurement data recovery and state reconstruction. In particular, it is suggested to explore state estimation methods that integrate physical constraints with data-driven techniques, so as to improve the accuracy and robustness of state estimation under complex operating conditions. The findings of this study may provide a useful reference for the development of state estimation methods for next-generation power systems.

title: On the Viability of Optimized K-Means Clustering and Autoencoders for Detecting Network Anomalies
authors: Thani Alriyami, Safa Otoum
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The technological evolution of the Internet of Things (IoT) has expanded the cyberattack landscape, resulting in new attack vectors. To keep up with these threats, we need to utilize technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), particularly Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL), to detect anomalous attacks. This study introduces PCA-IGKM, a novel ML algorithm that detects network anomalies in IoT networks. The study uses the IoT-TON-19 benchmark dataset and compares it with autoencoders, a DL algorithm. The findings demonstrate comparable results for PCA-IGKM to the AE-based model, with accuracy rates of 79% and 77%, respectively. Furthermore, this study marks the first implementation of autoencoders in the benchmark dataset, providing a baseline for future research. Although the results are promising, we still need to compare the novel model with other DL algorithms and test it across different datasets.

title: AI-Native 6G Networks: Adaptive Resource Allocation Through Edge-Integrated Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Zaid Alsalami, Rallabandi Venkata Santoshi Saraswati Swetha Nagini, Aman Sharma, Irfan Khan, Manish Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper investigates AI-Native 6G networks that integrate deep reinforcement learning (DRL) agents at the network edge to achieve adaptive, low-latency, and energy-efficient resource allocation. We present a conceptual architecture—Edge-Integrated DRL (EIDRL)—that combines lightweight on-device policies, edge-hosted value models, and a hierarchical orchestration layer that mediates model updates, inference placement, and safety constraints. The proposed framework explicitly addresses 6G-era characteristics: massive heterogeneous devices, semantic and sensing-aided communications, integration of sensing/communication/computation (ISCC), and stringent reliability/latency requirements. We detail the learning formulation (state, action, reward, multi-timescale update rules), introduce techniques for sample-efficient training (model-based rollouts, transfer from foundation WLAMs, and federated/split learning hybrids), and propose mechanisms for safety, fairness, and compute-aware scheduling. Through analytical arguments and representative numerical scenarios (multi-tenant edge slices, ISAC nodes, and mobile user clusters), we demonstrate that EIDRL reduces regret relative to static baselines, lowers average latency under load, and improves spectral and energy efficiency when the edge controller coordinates inference placement and action aggregation. Finally, we identify open research directions—privacy-preserving multi-agent coordination, certifiable robustness of learned policies, semantics-aware reward design, and operational considerations for O-RAN/OAI integration—thereby providing a focused research agenda for adaptive resource allocation in AI-native 6G systems.

title: Energy Efficiency Comparison Between Pulsed and AC Power Supplies for Nitrogen Fixation in Dielectric Barrier Discharge
authors: Jiaxinyi Huang, Yashuai Zhang, Zhuo Liu, Aiguo Tan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) plasma-based nitrogen fixation offers a promising alternative to the Haber-Bosch process, yet energy efficiency remains a critical bottleneck. The choice of power supply plays a decisive role in both energy consumption and fixation efficiency. This study compares pulsed and AC power supplies for nitrogen fixation in a water-film DBD (WF-DBD) reactor, investigating the effects of pulse frequency (200–800 Hz) and duty cycle (20%–80%) on discharge characteristics and fixation performance. Under optimized conditions (17 kV, 200 Hz, 80% duty cycle), the ${\text{N}}{{\text{O}}_3}^ - $ synthesis rate reached 112.4 μmol/min with an energy consumption of 67.0 MJ/mol. Compared with AC drive using the same electrode configuration, pulsed drive reduced energy consumption by 36% while maintaining 100% selectivity for ${\text{N}}{{\text{O}}_3}^ - $. Frequency exhibited a negative correlation with fixation performance, while duty cycle showed a positive correlation. These findings demonstrate that pulsed power supply significantly improves energy efficiency in DBD nitrogen fixation, providing a basis for optimizing plasma-based fixation processes.

title: Research on Hardware Circuit Design of Hub Motor Drive Controller for New Energy Vehicles Based on STM32
authors: Le Luo
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Guided by the objectives of the "dual carbon" initiative, new energy vehicles have emerged as a pivotal focus in the global transformation of the automotive industry. As a critical component of the powertrain system, the real-time performance and reliability of hub motor drive control significantly influence vehicle dynamics and safety. This research presents the design of a comprehensive hub motor driver hardware circuit centered on the STM32F407GZT6 microcontroller. The design encompasses power conversion through multi-stage voltage regulation, a minimal STM32 system, a three-phase inverter power stage, high-precision signal acquisition, CAN communication, and protection modules addressing overcurrent, overvoltage, and overtemperature conditions. The implementation of multi-stage voltage regulation ensures a stable power supply, while optocoupler isolation effectively segregates high-and low-voltage circuits, thereby enhancing electromagnetic interference resistance and operational reliability within the complex vehicular electromagnetic environment. This approach offers a robust and dependable hardware framework conducive to the advancement of power systems in new energy vehicles, promoting the integration and refinement of hub motor technologies.

title: Steiner-based Federated Aggregation Chain Deployment for Distributed LLM Inference
authors: Yuhang Zhao, Hailing Wu, Yufan Chen, Danyang Zheng, Xiaojun Cao, Chengzong Peng
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed to support intelligent perception, decision-making, and autonomous reasoning across distributed edge and cloud environments. Due to the extreme computational cost and privacy sensitivity of LLM inference, a single node is often incapable of executing the entire inference process independently. Instead, modern LLM inference tasks are decomposed into multiple functional stages and collaboratively executed as distributed inference service chains. However, exposing intermediate inference representations and partial reasoning results across heterogeneous nodes introduces significant security and privacy risks, while suboptimal deployment decisions may lead to excessive communication overhead and latency degradation. To address these challenges, this paper investigates the problem of Federated Inference Chaining and Deployment (FICD) for large language model inference over resource-constrained networks. We model LLM inference as an ordered chain of inference services with heterogeneous computation, bandwidth, latency, and security requirements. To efficiently deploy such inference chains, we propose a heuristic algorithm termed Steiner-based Federated Aggregation Chain Deployment (SFACD). Extensive simulation results demonstrate that SFACD significantly outperforms representative baseline approaches.

title: Detection of Traffic Incidents Using Ensemble SVM and KNN Algorithm
authors: Matheswaran M, Sumathi B, Omar Isam Al Mrayat, Shuvo Dutta, Obaidur Rahman Mohammed, Mangal Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: For intelligent transport cadres, detecting traffic accidents is an important research topic. Many techniques have shown excellent results in identifying traffic accidents. In any case, it is unacceptable to make these techniques stronger. In a specific context, it is not always the case that a method's efficiency improves when applied against another generating index; in fact, it once sounded rather fantastic in a set of data. The suitability of the KNN ensemble and support vector machines (SVM) for traffic incident identification is demonstrated in this work. Due to their excellent neural network performance and ability to generate a nonlinear classifier with the highest generality, SVM and KNN have been proposed as solutions to the traffic incident detection problem. Understanding ensemble learning (EL) is the main goal of this study. The EL approach is being employed to detect traffic accidents. SVM and KNN techniques are used separately at first and then combined to get the intended outcome. For a better final demonstration, a methodology is therefore needed to combine them. The findings of the investigation are shown to show that, out of all the thinking techniques, the best performance is obtained. Better representations of each of its consistency can be found in the suggested approach.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approaches for Sentiment Classification in Film Review Texts
authors: Adit Deshpande, S Deepa Nivethika, Aishwarya V, Pranav Murali Rao, M Senthil Pandian, Ojas M Agarwal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid deep learning approach for sentiment analysis of the movie reviews using Natural Language Processing (NLP). The study employs the IMDb dataset, applying preprocessing techniques like tokenization and normalization to prepare the data. A hybrid model combining BERT with CNN and BiLSTM layers is used to capture contextual and sequential features. The framework was further extended to multi-class sentiment classification (negative, neutral, positive) using a BERT-based sequence classification model fine-tuned on the Amazon US Reviews dataset. The multi-class system achieved competitive macro-F1 scores, demonstrating the scalability of the approach beyond binary polarity. The model achieved an accuracy of 94.5%, precision of 93.2%, recall of 94.1%, and F1-score of 93.6%, outperforming traditional machine learning baselines. Evaluation was supported by confusion matrices and accuracy graphs. The system also includes a user-interactive tool that predicts and explains sentiment, demonstrating practical utility. This research contributes to improving sentiment classification performance and has applications in recommendation systems, audience analysis, and opinion mining within the entertainment industry.

title: AI Automation Techniques for Optimized Scheduling and Resource Allocation in Genomics Data Pipelines
authors: Saket Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: Although managing large datasets and diverse computational resources is a significant challenge, pipelines for processing genomic data are essential to biomedical research and clinical diagnostics. In order to optimize scheduling and resource allocation, this paper presents a novel modular artificial intelligence framework that makes use of multi-agent coordination and reinforcement learning. Specialized agents that communicate via an orchestration bus portray tasks and compute nodes, continuously modifying their execution to optimize throughput and reduce latency. In comparison to conventional static schedulers, comprehensive analytical modeling based on actual genomics applications shows notable gains in processor utilization, job completion times, and queue wait times. Scalable and robust genomic analytics workflows are made possible by the solution’s support for multi-cluster and federated settings. This method proposes a novel framework for orchestrating high-throughput genomics data that is in line with developing computing capabilities.

title: External Dosimetry Audit IAEA/WHO for Validation of Megavoltage X-ray Beams
authors: Rocco Mottareale, Cecilia Arrichiello, Marcello Serra, Gianluca Ametrano, Francesca Buonanno, Valentina d’Alesio, Gaetano Gagliardo, Vincenzo Ravo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: In July 2025, an external dosimetry audit was conducted at the Radiotherapy Department of the National Cancer Institute IRCCS "Fondazione G. Pascale" (Naples, Italy), as part of the IAEA/WHO global dosimetry audit program for radiotherapy quality assurance. The objective of the audit was to independently verify the absorbed dose to water delivered by clinical megavoltage X-ray beams produced by a linear accelerator (LINAC) operating at 6 MV, 6 MV FFF, 10 MV, and 15 MV photon energies. The audit employed radiophotoluminescent dosimeters (RPLDs), which were supplied and later analyzed by the Dosimetry Laboratory of the Dosimetry and Medical Radiation Physics (DMRP) Section, Division of Human Health, at the IAEA Seibersdorf Laboratories, Vienna International Centre. Dosimeters were irradiated under reference conditions in a water phantom within the treatment room, following IAEA protocols. For each beam energy, dosimeters were positioned using clinical geometries in terms of source-to-surface distance (SSD), depth, and field size, ensuring standardization and traceability. Following irradiation, the RPLDs were returned to the IAEA laboratory for high-precision readout and analysis. The measured dose values were then compared against the stated institutional dose delivery to assess the accuracy of the beam calibration. Results for all beam qualities were found to be within the IAEA acceptance limits, thereby confirming compliance with international dosimetric standards. This external audit constitutes an essential component of independent quality assurance in radiotherapy, contributing to treatment safety and dosimetric accuracy. The positive outcome of the audit validates the local calibration procedures and supports the continuous implementation of high-quality standards in clinical photon beam dosimetry.

title: Fault-Tolerant Strategy for Nuclear In-Core Self-Powered Neutron Detectors Based on Virtual Twin Model and Data Repair
authors: Jiang Pan, Bo Fu, Shengqiu Jiang, Hongzhang Luo
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: The safe and stable operation of nuclear power plants relies heavily on the accurate monitoring of the core neutron flux. The Self-Powered Neutron Detector (SPND) is a key sensing device in the Core Neutron Flux Measurement (CNFM) system. However, SPNDs operating in harsh environments are prone to failures, which can lead to erroneous neutron flux information and compromise core safety. Existing data-driven fault diagnosis methods, such as those based on virtual twin models, experience significant performance degradation when multiple SPNDs fail simultaneously. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel fault-tolerant strategy. Firstly, a virtual twin model of the SPND signals is constructed using a Generalized Regression Neural Network (GRNN) to characterize the operational state of the detectors. When a large number of failures cause the model's performance to decline, a data repair strategy based on the Weighted K-Nearest Neighbor (WKNN) algorithm is introduced. This strategy identifies faulty signals, searches for similar healthy data segments from historical data, and replaces the faulty values. The Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence is employed to verify the similarity between the repaired data and the normal signals. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed fault-tolerant strategy can effectively restore the performance of the virtual twin model, increasing the fault detection accuracy to nearly 100% and reducing the output error, thereby ensuring the reliable implementation of the SPND fault diagnosis method.

title: Hardware-Accelerated NGAC Authorization for Real-Time Multi-Robot Systems
authors: Hassan Karim, Sai Sitharaman, Deepti Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Traditional authorization mechanisms such as RBAC and ABAC introduce non-deterministic latency and memory overhead incompatible with real-time multi-robot cyber-physical systems. This paper presents a hardware-feasible authorization primitive based on a compact NIST NGAC hypergraph representation compiled into deterministic bitmask structures. Implemented in C++, the approach achieves microsecond-scale authorization latency with a static memory footprint under 10 KB. Using a ROS 2 and Gazebo multi-robot simulation harness, we evaluate authorization overhead, worst-case observed latency, and embedded feasibility. Results show an average authorization latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.05 \mu s$</tex> with sub-10 KB memory usage, demonstrating suitability for deployment within real-time robotic control loops on resource-constrained edge controllers.

title: Saturated Power-Law Modulated Load Frequency Control of Multi-Area Interconnected Microgrids
authors: Md Saiful Islam, Rahul Bhadani
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: This paper presents a novel load frequency control strategy based on saturated power-law modulated global integral terminal sliding mode control (SPLM-GITSMC) to maintain stable frequency and tie-line power in multi-area interconnected microgrids with uncertainties. A graph-coupled multi-area model is developed to capture disturbances from load variations and intermittent photovoltaic and wind turbine generation. A saturated power-law modulated global integral terminal sliding surface with an adaptive exponential reaching law is designed to suppress chattering and ensure finite-time convergence. Closed-loop stability is established via Lyapunov analysis. Simulation results on the IEEE 39-bus system demonstrate improved frequency regulation and tie-line power stabilization compared with existing methods, reducing overshoot and undershoot by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}-\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex> and lowering integral error indices by upto 61 %.

title: Federated Learning-Based Healthcare Model Ensuring Secure Transmission Using Cryptographic Technique
authors: Arun Sanjay Dalvi, P. William
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in healthcare has led to exponential growth in medical data generation, enabling real-time patient monitoring and intelligent diagnosis. However, transmitting and processing such sensitive data raises serious privacy, security, and ethical concerns. This research proposes a Federated Learning (FL)-based healthcare model combined with cryptographic techniques to ensure secure data transmission and model validation across distributed IoT networks. In this model, patient data remains locally on IoT-enabled medical devices, while only encrypted model updates are transmitted to a central server using homomorphic encryption and Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC). The central aggregator then performs secure model aggregation and validation without exposing raw data. Experimental validation conducted using the IoT Healthcare Sensor Dataset (vitals such as heart rate, oxygen saturation, and body temperature) demonstrates a model accuracy of 94.6% with 96.8% encryption efficiency and minimal latency overhead (4.3%). The proposed framework ensures that sensitive patient data never leaves the device in unencrypted form, addressing both data leakage and compliance issues (HIPAA, GDPR). This approach establishes a secure, privacy-preserving, and trustworthy infrastructure for real-time IoT healthcare analytics.

title: Secure Federated Learning for Crop Mapping: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework Using Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
authors: Nayana S Kumar, R Abhishek, Sonu P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The agricultural sector in California's Central Valley faces increasing challenges due to climate variability, water scarcity, and soil degradation. Traditional decision-support systems rely on static datasets or computationally expensive real-time satellite processing, resulting in high latency and outdated recommendations. This paper proposes AgriVision Pro, a cloud-native precision agriculture framework that integrates Federated Learning with hybrid deep learning for privacy-preserving crop mapping. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is trained using the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm across three distributed organizational nodes, ensuring collaborative learning without sharing raw data. The system leverages Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), including Object Storage for a preprocessed Sentinel-2 tile repository and OCI Vault for secure model aggregation. A spatial reliability mechanism based on nearest-neighbor indexing ensures robust inference even with incomplete geospatial data. Additionally, real-time soil and climate APIs enhance decision-making. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a classification accuracy of 94.2% after five communication rounds, significantly reducing inference latency to under 800 ms. The proposed framework provides a scalable, secure, and efficient solution for real-time crop recommendation systems.

title: Graph Theory to Network Fairness: Sum 3-Equitable Labeling of m-pan Graphs
authors: Sharma Simron, Gupta Sangeeta, Srivastav Sweta, Gupta Deepti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: In this paper we have investigated a MATLAB program for the theorem which states that m-pan graphs preserve sum 3-equitable labeling. We see that this labeling pattern ensures that the edge labels are as equitable as possible. Equitability in resource allocation, bandwidth, and transmission power is essential in communication networks for efficiency and sustainability. Graph structures like that of m-pan graph closely mimic ring topologies commonly used in networks. Labeling patterns on graphs like sum 3-equitable labeling ensure equitable distribution of resources along a network since these mathematical structures can be mapped to communication topologies.

title: CrossDrone: Adaptive Cross-View Collaboration for Edge-Assisted Drone Video Analytics
authors: Zilong Wu, Kangli Zhao, Penglin Dai
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), or drones, has enabled flexible and dynamic video analytics, yet processing video data on resource-constrained edge devices remains challenging. Existing approaches, including Detection-Based Tracking (DBT) and conventional multi-camera collaboration frameworks, are inadequate for multi-UAV scenarios. DBT performs server-side detection only at key frames and relies on lightweight on-device tracking for the remaining frames, but its accuracy degrades over time due to tracking drift. Meanwhile, traditional multi-camera collaboration depends on static cross-view geometric mappings that become invalid under continuous UAV motion. To address these limitations, we propose CrossDrone, an adaptive cross-view collaboration framework for edge-assisted drone video analytics built upon the DBT paradigm. CrossDrone introduces two key components: (1) a Cross-Drone Collaboration (CDC) module that leverages lightweight optical flow to maintain a Dynamic Mapping Model (DMM), enabling UAVs to exchange tracking cues and correct drift in real time without repeated feature extraction; and (2) an Adaptive Joint Offloading Policy that dynamically determines offloading strategies based on tracking stability, balancing detection accuracy and end-to-end latency. Experimental results show that CrossDrone reduces end-to-end latency by 71.5% compared with full-frame offloading and achieves over an order-of-magnitude improvement in accuracy over static multi-camera collaborative baselines.

title: Multi-Targets Passive Localization via Dynamic Collaboration of Detector Array
authors: Jian-Jun Tu, Ding MA
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: To address the data association challenge in passive multi-target localization and overcome the limitations of existing methods regarding prior information, operating frequency bands, and signal characteristics, a passive localization scheme for multiple radiation sources using dynamic detection arrays is proposed, which is based on the Generalized Cross-Correlation with Phase Transform (GCC-PHAT) algorithm and Inverse Delay-Interval Region (IDIR) theory. The core innovations include: (1) a method for generating dynamic partitions from dynamic arrays; (2) a dynamic sensitivity indicator for radiation sources crossing partition boundaries. Simulation localization of multiple radiation sources was conducted in a 200 km × 200 km two-dimensional space. The results show that when the cross-partition dynamic sensitivity indicator reaches its maximum value, credible localization results are obtained. This scheme is suitable for multi-target scenarios involving large-range and long-distance conditions, providing a new technical approach for long-distance target search and perception.

title: Campus Safety Ranking Using Clery Act Reports and Multi-Layer Perceptron Regression
authors: Walaa Abo Elenin, Lei Chen, Yiming Ji, Atef Mohamed Shalan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: Ensuring campus safety is essential for secure and productive learning environments. Although Clery Act Reports provide the most reliable safety data, public rankings often correlate weakly with them, largely due to strong neighborhood influence. A campus with limited internal safety procedures may still receive a high rating if located in a safe area, revealing that current rankings underweight institutional efforts. To address this gap, we apply a data-driven method that classifies campuses based on their safety practices, reported ranks, and surrounding environmental conditions. By integrating Clery Act data into our framework, we highlight institutional safety measures over external factors to provide a more accurate evaluation. Using the extracted dataset, we compute indicators such as the Density of Considered Safety Measures (DCSM) and the Rank-Based Normal DCSM (RND) to measure safety class deviation. These classification results are then used to train an MLP model to predict safety rankings for campuses lacking public indices.

title: Voice Activity Detection in Background Noise by Feature-Driven One-Class Variational Autoencoder
authors: Nafisa Zarrin Tasnim, Nasser Kehtarnavaz
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Conventional supervised voice activity detection requires large amounts of labeled audio data across multiple noise types and signal-to-noise ratios, making them not effective when operated in newly encountered noise environments. To overcome this limitation, this paper presents a feature-driven one-class residual variational autoencoder solution for voice activity detection. The training is carried out exclusively on speech-innoise signals allowing detection of speech presence without relying on clean speech signals or explicit noise modeling. A feature study across different feature families is carried out showing that the combination of mel-frequency cepstral coefficients and spectral contrast features achieved an average accuracy of 92.8 % and F1score of 0.92 across different signal-to-noise ratios.

title: Integrating Role-Based Prompt Model Context Protocol with Large Language Models for Reliable Financial Market Recommendations
authors: Vikash Kumar Singh, Sonu Kumar, Manoj Kumar Rawat, Durga Sivashankar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Engineering Technologies and Applications (IC-EETA)
abstract: Financial forecasting has always been a critical task in finance, which influences decision-making in trading, investment, and risk management. As markets have always been highly dynamic, noisy, and nonlinear, making predictions have been inherently challenging. Among the recent LLM models for e.g. GPT-5 have introduced new opportunities for reasoning over financial data by combining advanced contextual understanding with improved computational efficiency compared to prior generative models like GPT-4. There have been multiple domain specific models been developed for sentiment analysis like FinGPT an open source framework for building financial LLMs, BloombergGPT a large scale model trained on proprietary financial data for industry scales. However, despite having models trained they still hallucinate, their outputs appear plausible but are not factually grounded as the operate without access to real-time market data. The advancement in GPT-5 model is notable as we have tested and it achieves 25% to 30% faster inference latency, 20% to 25% lower hallucination rates, and handling twice the context length of GPT-4, while consuming 15% to 20% less compute per query, which makes it particularly effective for efficient multi agent orchestration in financial forecasting. This paper introduces a Role Based MCP Framework for financial forecasting, which leverages multi agent orchestration and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to integrate real time, verifiable market data into the forecasting process. Our proposed framework will be consisting of coordinated agents each having specialized roles which includes a data retriever, analyst, risk evaluator and decision aggregator ensuring both structured reasoning and grounded predictions. Apart from this we validate our framework using seven equity indiceand FRED macro indicators as well as for commodities which includes gold, oil and cryptocurrencies Bitcoin, Ethereum. Where the tested results consistently shows a error reduction of 20% to 25% and point improvement by +9 to 10 in directional accuracy compared to baselines along with statistical significance where p < 0.05. Not only with equities, but the framework also demonstrates an asset class agnostic generalizability, performing robustly across diverse financial instruments.

title: Research Status and Development Trends of Multi-Sensor Data Fusion Technology in Robotics
authors: Zhenkang Ye, Xin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Multi-sensor data fusion technology effectively addresses issues such as low perception accuracy and poor robustness of single sensors by integrating information from heterogeneous sensors mounted on robots. It has become a key enabler for enhancing core robotic capabilities including environmental perception, localization and navigation, and task execution. This paper systematically reviews its current research applications across diverse robotics domains—including hazardous chemical warehouse patrol robots, pipeline inspection robots, and firefighting robots—by examining three core implementation approaches: pixel-level fusion, feature-level fusion, and decision-level fusion. It provides an in-depth analysis of the technical characteristics and applicable scenarios for each fusion method. Building upon existing research, this paper projects future development trends across three dimensions-algorithm optimization,hardware adaptation,and cross-scenario adaptation-to provide insights for further innovative applications of multi-sensor data fusion technology in robotics.

title: Incorporating Social Media Platforms and News: Leveraging Machine Learning with Hybrid LR PCA for Authentic News Verification
authors: Aparna R, Brinda Ramanujam, Roshini V, Shanthini R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Today's mass digital communication environment creates a situation where the speed of information dissemination during a crisis can be a double-edged sword “It has proven to be a blessing as well as a curse.” Social media networks and online news media have For real-time reporting that is critical to crisis management. Nevertheless, they are liable to disinformation, rumors, and This situation can be further exacerbated by deliberate attempts at disinformation. Crises and compromise sound decision-making. This study Provides a global machine learning framework for authentic news verification through social media information (tweets/posts) and more traditional news articles. By use of the CrisisMMD, TweetEval, and AG datasets. News - We compare MLP classifier, Decision Tree, Logistic Regression and Principal Component Analysis Regression analysis is an extension of (LR+PCA), and investigate how effective these are. The models also achieve high classification accuracy by applying Preprocessing, TF-IDF Feature Engineering, and Dimensionality Reduction. LR+PCA epitomizes a consistent and well MLP Classifier is guaranteed to be computationally efficient, and Decision Tree has expertise in similarity-based verification. The paper is an indication of the importance of multi-source integration. Data into machine learning for fast and accurate Misinformation detection to offer critical assistance to Emergency responders and officials during a crisis.

title: Research on Intelligent Assessment Method for Transmission Line Icing Based on Multi-source Data Fusion
authors: Hui Zhang, Jincan Lin, Yi Zheng, Jianxu Yang, Yu Li, Kangwei Chen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Reliable icing assessment for transmission lines depends on timely field perception and stable decision making under complex weather and operating conditions. Single-source methods and fixed warning rules often cannot capture the joint effects of visual appearance, meteorological evolution, line mechanical response, and local terrain. To improve practical assessment reliability, this paper presents a multi-source heterogeneous data fusion method for transmission line icing-state assessment. The proposed framework integrates micro-camera images, meteorological grid data, tension monitoring data, and geographic features. An improved YOLOv11 model with a CBAM attention module is used to extract visual evidence, while a Dempster-Shafer evidence fusion model with adaptive thresholds combines heterogeneous observations into an integrated icing-state judgment. Experimental results show that the image module achieves 88.3% recognition accuracy. After multi-source fusion, the overall icing-state assessment accuracy reaches 62.4%, the false alarm rate decreases to 7.2%, and the miss rate decreases to 11.8%. Engineering deployment on a transmission management platform further shows that, in the reported application scenario, the method can provide warning information 2-3 hours earlier than routine manual discovery. These results indicate that multi-source fusion improves the usability and stability of icing-state assessment, while further improvement is still needed in evidence mapping, parameter calibration, and rare extreme-weather samples.

title: An Integrated Ga-Taguchi Model for Parametric Optimization of Submerged Arc Welding Process
authors: Abishek Kumar, Prakash Kumar, Prabhat Kumar, Nisha Gupta, Yogendra Kumar, Shreyansh Prasad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: An optimization method influenced by biological evolution, a genetic algorithm (GA) is controlled by variables like mutation rate, crossover fraction, and population size. An integrated GA-Taguchi approach is used in this study to determine the ideal GA control settings for the SAW process. Following the optimization of these GA parameters, the SAW process parameters are further optimized using the same GA. A semi-automatic submerged arc welding setup is used for the experimental work. Weld bead width and weld bead hardness are taken into consideration as the output responses, and welding current, arc voltage, and welding speed are chosen as input variables. The objective function for GA optimization is created by combining regression models created for both output responses using a weighted-sum method. The ideal GA parameters, as determined by the suggested integrated methodology, are a mutation rate of 0.05, a crossover fraction of 0.7, and a population size of 100. The findings show that optimizing SAW parameters with the tuned GA performs better than optimizing with non-optimized GA parameters.

title: Qannas: Electromagnetic Authorization-Based Camera Detection System
authors: Budoor Alamoodi, Maha Albreiki, Rawan Aljaberi, Fatna Belqasmi
publish: 2026 17th Student Research Conference on Applied Computing (SRC)
abstract: The increasing availability of compact and easily concealed spy cameras has created serious privacy and security risks in restricted environments such as government facilities, military zones, and other sensitive locations. Many modern devices operate offline, avoiding traditional network-based detection methods. This paper presents Qannas, a passive electromagnetic leakage-based detection system designed to identify hidden and unauthorized cameras without relying on Wi-Fi traffic, optical visibility, or active probing. The system captures near-field electromagnetic emissions using a low-cost software-defined radio and analyzes power spectral density representations to detect periodic comb-like harmonic patterns generated by internal camera circuitry. This enables real-time detection and early alerts. A structured detection methodology is introduced, including spectral estimation, peak extraction, harmonic spacing analysis, artifact rejection, and signature generation. Experimental evaluation in realistic indoor environments demonstrates consistent identification of stable harmonic spacing patterns associated with active camera devices. The results confirm that unintentional electromagnetic leakage can serve as a potentially reliable indicator for passive camera detection and enable differentiation between authorized and unknown devices.

title: Research on the Information Visualization Design of Guangdong Intangible Cultural Heritage Herbal Tea Under the Digital
authors: Junwei Cai, Zhifeng Deng, Jingying Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The present research addresses the use of digital visualization tools like TouchDesigner to preserve and innovation of the culture of Guangdong herbal teas. This study will turn incomprehensible cultural data into interactive versions, which will improve transparency and interaction and accessibility by users. The paper discusses the ability to layout design, color scheme and digital platforms to unify old stories and new viewers. The three tools gamification, virtual museums, and AI driven instruments are identified to potentially contribute to cultural identity, economic models, and maintain tourism sustainability as this study demonstrates. The study relies on the qualitative multi-step examination of peer reviewed articles to offer information regarding theoretical and practical implications. The conclusions indicate the effectiveness of online tools in preserving the heritage of the cultural heritage both directly and indirectly and making it relevant and available in the digital era.

title: The Role of Blockchain in Transforming Web Applications: A Comprehensive Study
authors: Harsh Kumar, Mukhtiar Singh, Yash Kashyap, Arundhati Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: The growth of web applications has revealed several limitations of centralized systems, including security vulnerabilities, lack of transparency, and limited user control over data. Blockchain technology has emerged as a solution to these problems by providing immutability, transparency, and trust less consensus mechanisms. This paper presents a comparative study of centralized and blockchain-based web systems, evaluating key parameters such as security, transparency, scalability, cost, and data ownership. The study is based on an extensive review of existing research, real-life case studies, and current blockchain tools and platforms. The main contribution of this research is a comparison model that helps evaluate whether blockchain is suitable for modern web applications and identifies the conditions under which blockchain can be effectively integrated into web systems. The results show that blockchain-based systems perform better in terms of security, transparency, and user control, while centralized systems perform better in scalability and performance. The paper also discusses major challenges such as high energy consumption, regulatory issues, and interoperability between different blockchain platforms. Furthermore, the study suggests future research directions, including cross-chain communication, Layer-2 scalability solutions, and integration of blockchain with artificial intelligence. This study helps in understanding when blockchain technology should be used in web applications and serves as a guideline for researchers and developers working in decentralized web development.

title: Optimization of Edge AI Device for the Real-Time Monitoring of Physiological Signals
authors: Keya Acharjee, Sasikala Thangavel, Jonathan Roncancio, Premananda Indic
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: The purpose of this work is to explore the feasibility of implementing machine learning algorithms directly on an edge intelligent device to enable real-time physiological event detection and direct communication with remote servers, eliminating the need for a smartphone. To validate feasibility, we use an open-source wearable EmotiBit that provides full access to multimodal physiological data and supports both wireless streaming as well as local recording, making it ideal for edge-AI research. To assess performance and practical relevance, raw EmotiBit datasets are compared and cross-validated against data from the widely used commercially available Garmin Vivosmart 5. Synchronized recordings from both devices are used to map features, test performance on Garmin data, and benchmark results against EmotiBit outputs. Additionally, correlation analysis across accelerometer axes, linear regression modeling and feature mapping are used to evaluate cross-device relationships and results demonstrate that optimized edge-based algorithms can operate effectively. These findings underscore the potential of edge-AI wearables for scalable, real-time behavioral and physiological monitoring.

title: Tracing the Emotional Pathways of Facebook Marketplace
authors: Ahmed Al-Sa’Di, Swati Kumaria Puri, Richa Panjabi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: This paper examines the emotional dynamics of peer-to-peer (P2P) commerce on Facebook Marketplace. This study employed netnography, a qualitative research method rooted in ethnography and specifically adapted for studying online communities, to explore user behaviour, trust dynamics, and skill development on Facebook Marketplace. The methodological process comprises three key phases: data collection, data analysis, and ethical consideration. By focusing on publicly available user-generated interactions, this approach provides rich, contextual insights into the psychological and behavioural factors that shape P2P transactions on the platform. The study analysed 10,000 public user interactions from Australia and New Zealand using natural language processing (NLP) techniques for sentiment classification. The results reveal a balanced distribution of positive, negative, and neutral sentiments, reflecting the complex emotional landscape of digital marketplace exchanges. Positive sentiments were primarily associated with convenience and successful transactions, whereas negative sentiments were linked to trust issues and negotiation challenges. The findings advance social commerce research by offering a nuanced understanding of the affective dimensions of online marketplaces and provide actionable insights for platform designers to enhance user experience and manage emotional escalation more effectively.

title: A Comparative Study of Automatic Text Summarization: From Statistical Baselines to Transformers and Large Language Models
authors: Shalu Rani, Jyoti Vashishtha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Text summarization has become a significant task in natural language processing (NLP), which is dealing with the issue of the information overload in the digital age. This paper we presents a detailed comparative analysis of three paradigms of major trends in automatic text summarization: statistical extractive models, transformer-based abstractive models, and API-powered large language models (LLMs). The approach entails using nine representative models (TF-IDF, TextRank, LexRank, BART, T5, PEGASUS, Gemini Flash, LLaMA-3-8B and Mistral-7B) on three benchmark datasets CNN/DailyMail, XSum and PubMed. The same preprocessing and evaluation conditions are offered in order to offer fair cross-paradigm comparison on ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, ROUGE-L, and BERTScore. Findings indicate that statistical summarization tools like LexRank are still very relevant to fact-sensitive biomedical summarization with a ROUGE-1 retrieval score of over 0.43 on PubMed. Abstractive ability is higher in transformer models, and PEGASUS scored 0.91 on XSum. LLM-based APIs can have high semantic quality - Gemini Flash has ROUGE-L between 0.38 and 0.42 - and have low local computation requirements. These results show that the effectiveness of summarizing is extremely domain-specific: statistical extractive algorithms are superior in precision-based tasks, transformers in abstraction-based tasks, and LLM API in resource-based, but fluency-oriented, environments.

title: An Electro-Thermo-Mechanical Co-Design Framework for High-Power-Density EV Motors: Multidimensional Parameter Correlation and Multiphysics Coupling Modeling
authors: Xin Zeng, Julian Zhu, Song Liu, Yongyuan Wang, Huiling Jia
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: This paper proposes an Electro-Thermo-Mechanical Co-Design Framework (ETMFD) to address cross-domain coupling challenges in high-power-density electric vehicle (EV) motors. The framework integrates a Multidimensional Parameter Correlation Matrix (quantifying interactions among 12 key variables via sensitivity analysis) and multiphysics coupling models (electromagnetic, thermal, structural) to enable systematic trade-off analysis and collaborative optimization. A closed-loop architecture (Input-Coupling-Optimization-Validation layers) is constructed, with the NSGA-II algorithm solving Pareto-optimal solutions for power density, efficiency, and NVH. Simulation validation using a 1/8 symmetric finite element model (ANSYS Maxwell/Fluent/Mechanical) shows the framework achieves 1.85 kW/kg power density, 96.2% peak efficiency, and 84% NVH reduction versus single-domain optimization. The ETMFD framework provides a paradigm shift from siloed design to integrated co-design, offering a versatile tool for next-generation high-power-density motors.

title: Federated and Privacy-First Observability Architectures for Geographically Distributed Systems
authors: Prathusha Prakash, Naga Deepak Pothuraju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: One of the challenges of observability of geographically distributed systems is to allow the correct detection of anomalies of the network without having central access to sensitive traffic information. To handle this dilemma, this paper suggests the use of FP-FOA (Federated Privacy-First Observability Architecture) as a privacy-aware architecture, which conducts distributed learning without exposing raw data. Local node network traffic data are aggregated and analyzed using a client-side pipeline which includes missing value imputation, normalization of data types, IQR-based outlier treatment, SMOTE-based class imbalance treatment, and Z-score feature scaling. The scaled features are then trained on a small neural network (FedNet) on-client to obtain weights of the model. These weights that are learned locally are sent to a federated aggregation server where they are averaged together through the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) model to create a global model. The combined global weights are reallocated to customers and refined repeatedly through training cycles. The last global model is a binary classification model to show whether traffic over the network is anomalous or benign. The centralized deep neural network has been adopted as a performance baseline in the form of an upper-bound. The experimental evidence verifies that FP-FOA can achieve 98% accuracy, 98% precision, 98% recall, and 98% F1-score with high privacy guarantees, which demonstrates that FP-FOA is a proper privacy-first observability of distributed systems.

title: A Transient Performance-Oriented Optimization Framework for Virtual Inertia and Damping in Virtual Synchronous Generators
authors: Zhilin Dong, Haoqing Xiong, Qianfeng Shen, Jun Yang, Shangzhi Pan, Shaohua Zhang, Rongqiang Su, Taotao Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources, the inertia of modern power systems has significantly decreased, thereby motivating the development of grid-forming control strategies such as the Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG). The dynamic performance of a VSG is highly sensitive to the selection of its key control parameters, particularly the virtual inertia and damping coefficients. Conventional offline parameter tuning methods typically determine VSG parameters based on engineering experience or small-signal analysis. In practice, these parameters are often implemented as fixed values, which may lead to unsatisfactory performance under transient disturbances. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an offline parameter optimization framework for VSG control based on the Salp Swarm Algorithm (SSA). The optimization problem is formulated by minimizing a weighted performance index that incorporates both frequency overshoot and settling time. Through a population-based search process combined with time-domain simulations, the SSA algorithm is employed to identify optimal combinations of the virtual inertia and damping parameters. Simulation studies are conducted under three representative disturbance scenarios. The results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces frequency deviations and improves the transient response performance of the VSG system compared with conventional fixed-parameter and rule-based tuning strategies.

title: In-Depth Mining and Trend Prediction of Public Opinions on Sports Policies Based on Social Media Sentiment Analysis and Social Network Graphs
authors: Siye He, Xindi Jiang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Sentiment analysis and social network graphs represent new ways of mining, analyzing, and making predictions about trends of public opinion. This study advances the research problem of integrating sentiment analysis and social network graphs to comprehend and forecast the masses of people in respect to sports policies taking advantage of the huge volumes of data generated by social media networks. In the current digital era, social media has emerged as an important arena whereby contemporaneous athletes, fans, commentators, and the general masses have real-time discussions relating to sports governance, participation, equity, funding, and other policy-related issues. Since conventional means of collecting popular opinion, like surveys and focus groups, are usually too slow and inadequate in scale, this research would serve to offer a data-based means to policy-makers of tracking and reacting to real-time popular sentiment. The research presents the results of the social media posts analyzed using the sentiment analysis techniques, to read either positive, negative, or neutral reaction to the particular sports policies among the population. Through the use of more sophisticated machine learning models, that is, the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and the Bidirectional Encoder Representations of Transformers (BERT) analysis extends past simple text classification to recognize subtle feelings and dialectical contexts, even in debatable situations. Also, social networks graphs are created to trace the interactions and highlight major influencers during online discussions and how their views influence the masses and distribute the sentiment. The research also tends to realize changes in the public opinion in a long-term perspective, where trends in the sentiment scores and network interactions help to obtain future trends in sentiments. A combination of these approaches will give the intended research actionable suggestions that can enable more responsive and inclusive sports policy development. Finally, the results can assist the sports organizations and governing bodies not only to respond promptly to the popular moods, but also to actively address the influencers to gain more popular support to the policy initiatives.

title: Design and Development of a Real-Time Intelligent Dashboard for Predictive Train Fault Analysis and Maintenance Decision Support
authors: Emmanuel Jian Yuan Phua, Andrew Keong Ng
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Control, Automation and Robotics (ICCAR)
abstract: Subsystem fault trend analysis plays an important role in maintaining the reliability and availability of urban metro rail systems. In practice, fault data are often reviewed through periodic static reports, which limits their usefulness for timely assessment and proactive maintenance planning. This paper presents a real-time dashboard for predictive train fault analysis and maintenance decision support using rail fault records. The dashboard consolidates near real-time fault visibility with trend projections, abnormal behaviour indicators, fault pattern grouping, and maintenance advisory recommendations in a single desktop and mobile-accessible interface. Established analytical techniques are operationalised to support forecasting, anomaly identification, and pattern discovery, with analytical outputs presented as decision-support cues rather than automated decisions. Emphasis is placed on interpretability and practical usability to align with safety-critical maintenance workflows. Demonstrative evaluation using simulated near-real-time data from multiple anonymised metro lines shows that the dashboard provides timely situational awareness and structured insights beyond traditional static reporting. The results indicate that integrating analytics and visualisation into a practical dashboard can support more proactive, consistent rail maintenance decision making under realistic operational constraints.

title: Sustainability and Equity Challenges of Nuclear Fission and Fusion
authors: Vittorio Ciardiello, Daniele Davino, Vincenzo Paolo Loschiavo, Carlo Silano
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Sustainability (MetroSustainability)
abstract: The sustainability and long-term viability of nuclear fission and fusion as energy sources may be an important topic in view of the recent changes in the political scenario worldwide. This paper provides a framework for comparatively evaluating both technologies vs renewable sources towards a stable and sustainable energy future. We focus on a first quantification of risks, including technological requirements, strategic material demand, energy efficiency and performance metrics, fuel availability constraints, and inherent technical feasibility challenges. Special attention is paid to the scale of infrastructure and research efforts required, and the critical balance between net energy return and total environmental impact.

title: IoT-Enabled Framework for Smart Agriculture: Enhancing Crop Production with Environmental Sensors and Machine Learning
authors: Richa Vijay, Astha Gupta, Khusnidinova Nozima, Koteswara Rao Anne, Raj Kumar, Harjeet Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent &amp; Innovative Practices in Engineering &amp; Management (IIPEM)
abstract: The world population is increasingly growing which requires massive improvements in agricultural productivity and sustainability. The common traditional modes of farming have the failure of maximally utilizing resources and a failure to adapt to changing environmental features. This paper presents a new IoT-based system of smart agriculture that is aimed at streamlining production in crops. Such a network is a distributed system that gathers data on soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and pH levels as well as a utility managed with a cloud-based data analytics system. To process the sensor data, we use the algorithm of machine learning, namely, a Random Forest (RF) model and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. These models recommend real time irrigation schedules and forecast the possibility of crop stress. Our testbed deployment results indicate that the proposed framework can be used to enhance crop yield and also reduce water use as opposed to conventional methods.

title: Advancements in Speech Recognition for Regional Languages Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Geetali Tilak, Anuja Bagaitkar-Palkar, Aatif Sundke, Hemant Dattajirao More, Keshav Sathaye, Ujjwala Barve
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Scientists have been trying to identify and understand impromptu speech since 1970. Many solutions have been adopted to an automated voice identification structure. Users always use English in speech identification since it was the target of much research and implementation. There are several researches conducted to determine the demographic details of a person and his or her terms in the Bangla language but not even a single research has been conducted to find out the dialect of the person speaking. Thus, this work is aimed at identifying and classifying the generated Bangla language, by developing a database of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex> hours of Bangla language in five local Bangla languages. The production of Bangla voice was done through the process of first translating the syllables in the Bengali language into English phrases deviation, which is often used as a form of text, and then translating these phrases into an English sentence. The suggested approach was also used to differentiate the regional dialect of the speaker in the audio stream and determine its validity. The processing of this data with deep learning algorithms with convolution tiers can generate feature mappings. The suggested method was widely trained and tested on the basis of unsupervised statistics i.e. the development of new lines with the help of different English and Bangali abbreviated words. The algorithm factored the age category in language category tagging because as age advances physiological changes occur in the brain and it affects how the audio data is handled. This improved classifier accuracy to 95 percent without and with age categories respectively. In the case of generated Bangla language tags, the accuracy in classification is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5}$</tex> percent. The proposed solution is also very effective when dealing with audio collections that express English.

title: In-Line Grain Moisture Sensor for Vertical Silos
authors: Diego Barrettino, Liliana Roscaneanu
abstract: We designed, fabricated and tested an in-line grain moisture sensor that can measure at real time the grain moisture content at the input of vertical silos with a resolution of 0.05%, thus allowing to remotely monitor the state of grains just before storing them in vertical silos. Measurement results performed in vertical silos located in Villa Mercedes, Province of San Luis, Argentina are reported.

title: Optimized Real-Time Hazard Detection for Crane Operations Using YOLOv11 Object Detection
authors: E. Kannan, P. Manikandan, S. Ramesh, D. Devadharshini, N. Dhivya, N S. Varna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Safety from hazards in construction using cranes is very timely during construction. Traditional camera vision systems typically have a significant amount of latency and about the same amount of detail when calculating exact distances from people and moving loads to obstacles, and they cannot easily be customized for putting cameras in open-air locations. The proposed safety system uses YOLOv11 to find and approximate the location of workers, crane loads, obstacles and restricted areas using live video streaming. The speed of processing images creates features allow for more accurate inference and feature extraction. The crane operator will receive a warning immediately upon seeing a risk and will see the actual distance of each risk area. The proposed model reduces the latency and increases the stability as evidenced by the test results.

title:
CoG
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    RH-LLM: A Geometric Graph Attention Framework for Faithful Knowledge-Grounded Dialogue Generation
authors: Yuxuan Li, Xinzhi Wang, Ruishen Liu, Mingxuan Wang, Xiangfeng Luo
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Factual hallucination is a significant challenge in Large Language Models, limiting their practical applications for dialogue generation in specific, knowledge-intensive domains. A key mitigation strategy involves grounding dialogues in Knowledge Graphs, which provide reliable structured facts. However, this approach is constrained by two primary issues: structural heterogeneity and information noise. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel geometric graph attention framework to enhance dialogue fidelity. First, the framework addresses structural heterogeneity using a Geometric Weighted Average Graph Attention mechanism. It achieves precise fusion by applying component-wise attention to deconstructed KG triples, followed by a geometric mean aggregation. Second, a knowledge gating mechanism mitigates information noise by dynamically controlling knowledge integration to filter irrelevant facts. Finally, a graph-context contrastive learning objective aligns output representations with the relevant KG subgraph to enforce semantic consistency and ensure robust factual grounding. Experiments on the OpenDialKG dataset, a benchmark grounded in specific domains including movies, music, and sports, confirm the framework’s effectiveness, showing significant outperformance over strong baselines. A 6.14% improvement in ROUGE-L demonstrates effective noise suppression, as the model’s outputs align more closely with ground-truth responses. Furthermore, a high Entity Coverage Precision of 97.53% indicates the framework’s ability to resolve structural heterogeneity, confirming the accuracy of its fine-grained fusion. The model’s factual consistency is further corroborated by a 3.60% gain on QuestEval and superior human evaluations. These evaluations affirmed its ability to generate coherent and relevant responses while mitigating hallucinations.

title: AI-Based Predictive Maintenance for Induction Motors Using a Hybrid CNN-LSTM Architecture with Attention Mechanism
authors: Jackson Jacob L, Uma Maheswari KM
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: In Industrial rotating machinery, predictive maintenance plays a significant role in reducing downtime and the cost of operation. The proposed paper suggests a deep-learning-based fault detection of induction motors, utilizing the hybrid architecture, which combines a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network, enhanced with an attention mechanism. The model learns the multi-scale spatial features by using a multi-kernel CNN branch and the temporal relationship at the same time by using a stacked BiLSTM network. An attention layer provides selective emphasis on the most informative temporal instants and increases the classification accuracy. The suggested framework is tested on the Fault Induction Motor Dataset, where the test accuracy is 98.60 %, and the precision, recall, and F1-score are more than 0.986. Moreover, a real-time monitoring dashboard is adopted to visualize system status and predictions. The empirical results support the usefulness of the hybrid method in the correct classification of six different motor conditions.

title: Security Evaluation of Approximate Quantum Adders
authors: Bhaskar Gaur, Himanshu Thapliyal
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Quantum addition is a vital building block for many quantum applications, yet exact adder architectures exhibit long carry paths and higher depth, increasing their exposure to quantum attacks. This paper presents a security driven evaluation of quantum approximate adders with carryout, having constant depth approximation and benchmarks them against two exact quantum adders. We evaluate resilience against four attack models: (1) Stuck-at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\vert 0\rangle /\vert 1\rangle$</tex> qubit attack, (2) State preparation attack, (3) Gate attack, and (4) Crosstalk attack in multi-tenant scenario. We use an emulator provided by Quantinuum that models its H1 ion trap-based 20-qubit quantum computer to conduct attacks. Our results indicate that approximate adders show higher resilience to noise, state preparation, gate, and crosstalk attacks, with AQA3 outperforming AQA4, which outperforms AQA5, due to increasing gate counts across zero, single CNOT layer, and one Toffoli gate respectively. Exact adders remain more robust against stuck-at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\vert 0\rangle /\vert 1\rangle$</tex> qubit attacks, as their carry propagation structure acts as an error absorbing mechanism, highlighting a security accuracy trade off for selecting adders in error-tolerant quantum applications.

title: Optimizing Financial Decision-Making Using AI and Deep Learning Models: Applications in Risk Assessment and Portfolio Management
authors: Krishna Prasad. A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: The Deep Learning (DL) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) integration has become beneficial to investment practices introducing novel methods of risk management and increasing investment returns. Financial institutions have focused on assessing and managing the portfolio credit risks (CR) both at the portfolio level and the transaction level. The factors that make credit portfolio management (CPM) become a topic of greater interest are many. The study considers the role of the Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods in the issues of transparent financial decision-making. The present generation of deep learning models offers easy implementation, but the development of financial systems to a practical application is a cumbersome task. Due to the lack of transparency and explainability, the DL methods fail to produce reliable technology. The XAI framework design of the work is the key distinguishing factor that addresses the problem of transparency and establishes a framework according to which credit risks management rules are developed. This study introduces XAI algorithms that are used to assess the risks associated with the credit borrowing in peer-to-peer platforms as part of CRM applications. CNN and LSTM methods scored the highest accuracy of 98.7 and 94.6 respectively in an evaluation. The model was tested with increased data that proved that it was able to achieve steady levels of accuracy. Because of LSTM, the accuracy value was 96.4 and CNN made 98.7.

title: Distributed scheduling strategy for highway microgrid clusters based on improved ADMM
authors: Mengjun Chen, Haoyu Zhang, Mingxing Shao, Zhuangzhuang Liu, Xianfeng Xu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Low energy utilization and poor economy are issues with the highway microgrid's autonomous operation. The system's economic performance can be significantly enhanced by connecting nearby microgrids to create highway microgrid clusters for scheduling. A distributed energy scheduling approach based on the enhanced alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) is presented in this paper. In view of the difficulty in predicting the charging load of electric vehicles, the charging load during typical non-holidays and holidays is first modeled based on the Monte Carlo method to generate dynamic charging load curves under typical scenarios. An optimal model of the cooperative game between microgrids taking into account the equilibrium of benefits is established based on the Nash game model, and an optimal scheduling model of the integrated energy microgrid clusters taking into account carbon trading costs is established. The reasonable redistribution of benefits is realized by constructing an asymmetric energy mapping function. The improved ADMM method is finally proposed to realize distributed solution, and the coordinated optimization between multiple microgrids is realized. Simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed model for safeguarding user privacy and promoting the local consumption of renewable energy.

title: Enhancing Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Compliance for NICE Actimize using a Data driven Approach
authors: Pantham Vishnu, Kammara Mouneshwari, M Shiva Kumar, Mannem Saimanasa, V.Lathika
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Institutional finance must adhere strictly to Anti-Money Laundering compliance through complete detection protocols and combined risk management and active monitoring systems. The traditional rules-based detection methods fail to match the pace of money laundering technique advances which creates both inefficient operations and excessive incorrect alarms. The paper studies how the AML compliance system becomes more effective because of NICE Actimize's financial crime detection platform that utilizes data science methods to strengthen operations. The anomaly detection system along with risk assessment capabilities of NICE Actimize strengthen its transaction monitoring capabilities through advanced analytics and artificial intelligence and machine learning. Data-driven model effectiveness receives examination in this study to determine its impact on accuracy and established its capacity to reduce regulatory compliance expenses and enhance regulatory practices. The analysis also covers essential implementation hurdles for AML strategies grounded in data science that involve data quality control as well as model readability and regulatory framework restrictions. An analysis reveals that NICE Actimize serves as a transformative tool for AML compliance modernization to deliver better financial crime prevention by utilizing automated intelligent systems and data-based understandings.
