title: Estimating arcing ground fault current factor for LV systems
authors: Claudio S. Mardegan, Rasheek Rifaat, S. A. Saleh, Wendler L. N. Matos
abstract: IEEE official multiplying factor for arcing ground faults for 0.48 kV system is 0.38 and is documented in some IEEE papers and a couple of books. This factor was calculated for a specific voltage level (0.48 kV) and developed for a specific transformer size. During the development of IEEE Std 1584 second edition many tests have been done to reach a better probabilistic arcing current estimation. On this standard, the three-phase arcing current is considered, but no arcing ground fault current is mentioned. Thus, the main objective of this paper is to provide means of estimating arcing ground fault current to support protection engineers to better set phase and ground protective devices. This technique has been used by the author for decades, but with the second edition of IEEE Std 1584, the estimation of this factor based on this standard enhanced significantly this calculation since it is based on equations that are built on a more robust database achieved with tested values (about 1800 tests have been performed). This new approach evaluation is done, in this paper, for different electrode configurations (HCB, VCB and VCBB for in box, VOA and HOA for open air). This paper intends to support the 3004.6 and 3004.13 Overcurrent Coordination base book (buff book). With this new factor, protection engineers will have new guidelines for setting phase and ground protective devices, being able to ensure the arc current clearing times at the same time as the bolted fault, reducing incident energy and damage to equipment.

title: A Foot Resistive Force Model for Legged Locomotion on Muddy Terrains
authors: Xunjie Chen, Liuyin Wang, Xinyan Huang, Jerry W. Shan, Yantao Shen, Jingang Yi
abstract: Legged robots face significant challenges in moving and navigating on deformable and highly yielding terrain, such as mud. We present a resistive force model for legged foot-mud interactions. The model captures rheological behaviors, such as visco-elasticity, thixotropy of the mud suspension, and retractive suction. One attractive property of this new model lies in its effective, uniform formulation to provide underlying physical interpretation and accurate resistive force predictions. We further take advantage of the resistive force model to design a new morphing robotic foot for effective and efficient legged locomotion. We conduct extensive experiments to validate the force model and the results demonstrate that the morphing foot enhances not only the locomotion mobility in terms of reducing sinking/suction and preventing robot appendage entrapment, but also energy-efficiency of walking in mud. The new resistive force model can be further used to develop data-driven simulation and locomotion control of legged robots on mud.

title: HEIoT: A Novel Three-Factor Authentication Protocol for Enhanced Security in IoT and Next-Generation Networks
authors: Masoumeh Safkhani, Mohammad Reza Servati, Fatemeh Rezaei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: The Internet has a significant impact on contemporary society, enabling a wide range of applications, including advanced cellular networks such as 4G, 5G, and 6G. Since these communications occur over shared or open channels, ensuring secure data exchange is of critical importance, as any weakness in the communication infrastructure may compromise system reliability. Device authentication in the Internet of Things (IoT) and user authentication in smart environments, such as smart homes, remain fundamental security challenges. As the first line of defense, authentication mechanisms must be robust, since vulnerabilities at this stage can expose the entire system to serious threats. To address these challenges, numerous authentication schemes based on cryptographic primitives, including Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), have been proposed. In this paper, we present a comprehensive security analysis of an ECC-based three-factor authentication protocol proposed by Yuan et al. Our analysis shows that the protocol is vulnerable to desynchronization, user impersonation, traceability, and insider attacks, all of which succeed with probability 1 by exploiting at most two protocol phases. To mitigate these weaknesses, we propose an improved authentication scheme, called HEIoT. The proposed scheme is formally analyzed under the Real-or-Random (RoR) model to establish session-key security and is further verified using the Scyther tool. Moreover, a Python-based implementation is provided to demonstrate the practicality of the proposed protocol. Comparative results indicate that HEIoT achieves stronger security while maintaining acceptable communication, computational, and storage overhead.

title: Implementing a Dynamic Roadmap Management System by Incorporating Emerging Digital Platforms
authors: Clare Farrukh, Michele Routley, Dilek Cetindamar, Elif Baktir, Can Demir
abstract: Aligning research and development activities with a company's strategy, monitoring progress towards realising technology roadmaps, updating these with changes and developments, and making measurable results visible, are of ever-increasing importance. Many companies prepare their roadmaps through collective intelligence and collaboration, but they fall short in carrying out the subsequent activities to manage the implementation and update the roadmap. Roadmaps that cannot adapt to the rapid changes we experience today quickly become obsolete. The ability to efficiently manage roadmaps in a dynamic and cyclic manner depends on their integration with organisational practices and effective digital support. In recent years, there have been great advances in collaborative creativity platforms to brainstorm ideas and bring these together in digital environments. Additionally, low-code tools have been made available for everyone that allow even those with no coding knowledge to develop applications. With the emergence of these possibilities, it has become feasible to establish a dynamic roadmap management system, accessible to even small companies. This paper presents a case study on the creation and implementation of a dynamic roadmap management system. This system was integrated with company practices, ensuring institutionalisation through process definitions and securing the vitality of roadmaps with IT infrastructure support. Although the approach used was implemented in a large company, it could easily be scaled and adapted to different sized organisations.

title: Package-Embedded Coupled Inductor Arrays for High-Performance Computing Power Delivery
authors: Rami Rasheedi, Salma Abdelzaher, Inna Partin-Vaisband
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: A novel power delivery framework, comprising a package-embedded inductor topology and an inductance-island methodology, is introduced to maximize both inductance and current densities in vertical power delivery (VPD). The framework leverages multiple multi-phase converters, a common strategy in high-performance computing systems, to enhance efficiency and scalability. The proposed topology employs an array of tightly coupled spiral square inductors sharing a common magnetic rod, serving multiple converters operating in the same conversion phase. The array is optimized to maximize coupling and minimize conversion losses, achieving superior inductance and current densities of 250 nH/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and 10 A/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, respectively. At the system level, the inductance-island methodology partitions the power delivery network into multiple islands, each dedicated to a converter phase and supplying a portion of the load current, thereby enabling scalable and efficient distribution. To validate the framework, the inductor array is designed and simulated in ANSYS Maxwell 3D and Mechanical exhibiting an average quality factor of 23.6 and efficiency of 97.4% at 2 A load current, 6 V input, and 10 MHz switching frequency. The inductor array netlist is extracted from ANSYS and co-designed in Cadence Virtuoso with a distributed dual-phase power conversion system, ensuring joint optimization of passive and active components. The co-designed converter achieves a significant efficiency gain of 5.65% on average and up to 11.04% at 40 A load over a similar converter with uncoupled inductors, demonstrating the practical benefits of the approach.

title: Measuring the Topological Charge of Infrared Vortices via Nonlinear Interferometry_supp1-3700019.mp4
authors: Yuanying Zhang
abstract: We demonstrate a nonlinear Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI) designed for direct characterization of infrared (IR) optical vortices. By interfering an IR signal vortex with a dynamically phase-modulated reference beam, the MZI maps IR vortex modes into visible second-harmonic generation (SHG) interference patterns. Distinct from linear interferometers, the nonlinear characteristic of our MZI provides an inherent contrast enhancement, where the quadratic intensity response effectively sharpens the petal structures to improve fringe visibility significantly. These petal-like interference patterns allow for the simultaneous retrieval of the topological charge (TC) magnitude and sign, which are determined directly from the number of petals and rotation direction, respectively. By leveraging the high sensitivity of standard visible-light CMOS sensors, our scheme provides an efficient platform for IR vortex metrology and OAM-based optical communications.

title: IB-BPSE: A blockchain-assisted privacy-preserving data sharing scheme using searchable encryption
authors: Bo Yang, Xiaoguang Liu, Xinyan Wu, Peize Liu, Naixin Zhang, Yuxin He, Fagen Li
abstract: In a typical Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) system, it is common for users’ physiological data, collected by medical sensors, to be processed and shared in a third-party environment, such as a cloud server. Medical data sharing in a third-party environment must guarantee data integrity and source authentication, and robust privacy-preserving measures must be taken to enhance reliability. Searchable encryption (SE) has been leveraged in the IoMT to address the issue of exposing private data during storage and transmission. However, existing works suffer from shortcomings, such as vulnerability to data tampering. In this paper, we propose a novel privacy-preserving data sharing scheme with identity authentication based on SE that addresses the heavyweight public key management problem within a Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) framework. In IB-BPSE, a redactable signature (RS) assisted by blockchain and Merkle tree structure ensures authentication and integrity. Searchable proxy re-encryption encrypts private data, enabling efficient ciphertext search and multi-user data sharing. In addition, the scheme is provably secure in the random oracle model, and the performance analysis demonstrates that the system achieves ideal comprehensive performance. Finally, we propose an application scenario in IoMT based on IB-BPSE.

title: High-Selectivity D-Band Filtering Antenna Based on ESPPs for 6G IoT Applications
authors: Ling Yun Niu, Hongyi Li, Le Peng Zhang, Hao Yang Huang, Pei Hang He, Liangliang Liu, Jiaqi Han, Zhuo Li, Hao Chi Zhang, Long Li
abstract: An effective surface plasmon polariton (ESPP) filtering waveguide-fed D-band antenna is proposed for 6G Internet of Things (IoT) applications. Aiming at cross-band interference and link stability in 6G IoT, the design integrates an ESPP filtering structure with a waveguide slot antenna, achieving high selectivity and deep out-of-band suppression while maintaining stable radiation performance. Through ESPP dispersion engineering, the passband (135.9–143.2 GHz) and out-of-band suppression can be independently tuned. Benefiting from ESPPs’ steep asymptotic dispersion and extended modal bandgap, deep out-of-band suppression (>55 dB) and a stable stopband (147–170 GHz) are realized. Notably, the ESPP-based waveguide ensures robust performance against fabrication errors, maintaining frequency consistency between the filtering and radiation responses. The proposed ESPP-based design provides a high-selectivity and fabrication-tolerant solution, effectively mitigating complex interference in ultra-high-speed 6G IoT links and ensuring reliable operation for heterogeneous devices, such as Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) sensors and smart city terminals.

title: DBMC-aNOMAly: Asynchronous NOMA with Pilot-Symbol Optimization Protocol for Diffusion-Based Molecular Communication Networks
authors: Alexander Wietfeld, Wolfgang Kellerer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Molecular, Biological, and Multi-Scale Communications
abstract: Multiple access (MA) schemes can enable cooperation between multiple nodes in future diffusion-based molecular communication (DBMC) networks. Non-orthogonal MA for DBMC networks (DBMC-NOMA) is a promising option for efficient simultaneous MA using a single molecule type. This paper studies parameter optimization and bit error probability (BEP) reduction for asynchronous DBMC-NOMA. First, we analytically derive the associated BEP and compare DBMC-NOMA with time-division and molecule-division MA. We show that asynchronous offsets can improve performance, and the upper-bound performance can be approached under almost all considered conditions by avoiding a small set of worst-case offset configurations, for which we propose and characterize a dedicated avoidance mechanism. We then propose DBMC-aNOMAly, a pilot-symbol-based optimization protocol for asynchronous DBMC-NOMA, and evaluate it using Monte Carlo simulations. DBMC-aNOMAly provides robust BEP reduction across different network sizes and noise levels, under sampling jitter, and under changing runtime conditions, outperforming protocols from previous work. An end-to-end efficiency analysis further shows that these gains translate into increased net throughput after compensating for the pilot overhead. DBMC-aNOMAly uses simple operations such as comparisons and additions that are compatible with chemical reaction networks, motivating future realistic modeling of the protocol.

title: A 2-bit Ku-band Digital Metasurface with Two-Dimensional Scalable Capability
authors: Xiaocun Zong, Binchao Zhang, Hao Shi, Fan Yang, Yong Liu, Shenheng Xu, Maokun Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this letter, the design and implementation of a 2 bit digital metasurface operating in the Ku-band are presented, which is engineered to exhibit advanced polarization conversion characteristics. To overcome the scalability limitations in conven tional 2-bit metasurfaces caused by dense control routing, a novel radio frequency (RF)–direct current (DC) separation architecture is proposed. In this architecture, the metasurface and the DC control circuitry are integrated onto separate printed circuit boards (PCBs) and interconnected through pin cascading, enabling theoretically unlimited two-dimensional array expansion. To validate the pro posed design, a 32 ×32 metasurface prototype is fabricated and experimentally characterized. The measured results demonstrate that a peak gain of 29.2 dBi and an aperture efficiency of 26.46% can be achieved, confirming both the scalability and perfor mance of the proposed architecture. The developed 2-bit high-gain metasurface provides significant reference value for long-distance communication and radar detection applications. Moreover, the RF–DC separation architecture establishes a pioneering framework for large-scale metasurface deployment in practical engineering scenarios, offering enhanced design flexibility and scalability.

title: Transitioning O-RAN Toward Post-Quantum Security: A Layered Architecture, Performance Estimation, and Migration Strategies
authors: Pramitha Fernando, An Braeken
abstract: Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) introduce flexibility and multi-vendor interoperability through open and standardized interfaces. The emergence of programmable RAN Intelligent Controllers (RICs), along with the ability to deploy third-party applications as xApps and rApps, further accelerates innovation. However, these advancements also expand the attack surface of 6G infrastructures and introduce significant new security challenges. As quantum computing threatens classical cryptography, O-RAN security must evolve toward quantum-resilient mechanisms. This article outlines a layered migration roadmap for integrating Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) and hybrid schemes into O-RAN’s identity, control, and runtime layers. Building on O-RAN Alliance Work Group 11 and GSMA post-quantum telco network task force guidelines, we estimate the relative cost and performance trade-offs of classical, hybrid, and PQC-only protections across key interfaces. The results show that hybrid integration provides near-term quantum resistance with moderate overhead, while full PQC deployment requires architectural adaptations. The proposed approach offers a standards-aligned path toward secure, interoperable, and energy-aware O-RAN evolution for 6G.

title: : Flexible Data Management for Private Smart Contract_supp1-3701041.pdf
authors: Xiaoguo Li
abstract: Private Smart Contracts (PSCs) aim to settle the privacy protection issue for sensitive data on the blockchain (a.k.a., on-chain data) while inheriting the computation functionality of general smart contracts. However, most of the existing PSCs focus on secure computation and ignore the secure and flexible management of on-chain data, making it difficult to support flexible user permission modes efficiently. Further, although a few on-chain data management schemes have been proposed to manage data on the blockchain, they provide insufficient support for scenarios where data must be managed concurrently by managers with different privilege levels. This paper proposes <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MISC</small>, a novel solution that features efficient and flexible data management capabilities for managing on-chain data in private smart contracts. <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MISC</small> leverages a novel Double Trapdoor Homomorphic Proxy Re-encryption (DT-HPRE) scheme and Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to ensure (1) low-overhead, flexible permission control over on-chain data and (2) a harmonious mechanism for both privileged and constrained managers. We prove the security of <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MISC</small> under the Universal Composability (UC) framework to demonstrate its data confidentiality guarantees, user revocation, and computation integrity. Further, we implement <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MISC</small>, and the performance evaluation shows that on-chain data access for one hundred data points is practically achieved in 5.8 s (under privileged managers) and 9.7 s (under constrained managers) using its off-chain design.

title: Partial Discharge Characteristic of Cellulose-Contaminated Synthetic Ester-Based Nanofluid under Dynamic Flow and Non-Uniform Electric Field Conditions
authors: Hakim Subekti, Daniar Fahmi, I. M. Yulistya Negara, Arkan Fadhilah, D. Anton Asfani, I. G. N. Satriyadi Hernanda
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This study investigates the characteristics of Partial Discharge (PD) in synthetic ester-based nanofluids contaminated with cellulose particles at various concentrations (0, 0.5, and 1 g/L) and circulation velocities (0, 0.3, and 0.5 m/s). The base oil is filtered, dried, and degassed, while cellulose and TiO2 nanoparticles (anatase phase, 30 nm average diameter, 0.01 wt%) are dried to prevent agglomeration. Nanofluids are produced using a two-step method to ensure their stability. A custom system with a DC pump and flow sensors regulates oil flow, and PD tests are performed in a plexiglass chamber using needle-plane electrodes featuring variations in tip geometry (needle, square, blunt) to measure Partial Discharge Inception Voltage (PDIV) and Phase-Resolved Partial Discharge (PRPD) patterns. Results show that PDIV decreases with higher cellulose concentration but increases with faster circulation. Blunt electrodes yield the highest PDIV, followed by square and needle tips. PRPD analysis reveals that higher cellulose concentrations increase the PD activity and cause the cluster density to spread. Conversely, an increase in the circulation velocity reduces the PD activity and shifts the cluster to the left with a more dispersed density. The order of the PD activity based on the electrode configuration is needle, square, and blunt. The obtained PRPD patterns demonstrate a dominant positive cycle associated with PD activity owing to the non-uniform electric field. This study provides insights into the effects of cellulose contamination and dynamic conditions on the performance of synthetic ester nanofluid insulation under non-uniform electric fields.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Lung Disease Diagnosis: A Survey on Addressing Overlapping Radiographic Patterns
authors: Venkateswara Rao Kalidindi, Priyanth Dunna, Mounika Devi Paila, Likhitha Lavanya Bandi, Abhiram Tamarapalli, Siva Surya Varikooti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal, Systems, and Computing for Next-Gen Automation (ICSSCNA)
abstract: Lung diseases like pneumonia, tuberculosis, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, lung cancer, and COVID-19 have remained among the top ten major contributors to mortality across the globe. In this direction, medical images through modalities such as chest X-rays and CT scans have remained the preferred choice of diagnostic tools for various lung-based disorders. Although recent developments in deep learning-based approaches have led to monumental improvements in the accuracy of automatic detection of various lung diseases, it has been identified that such approaches are still ineffective in correctly handling images that have overlapping radiographic patterns for different lung-based disorders. Using different types of multimodal learning methods has been found to be a better way to improve how these approaches handle images with overlapping patterns. It has been identified that through the application of such approaches, it has been possible to effectively assess how such learning approaches demonstrate unparalleled superiority over their unimodal learning counterparts in effectively dealing with images through various modal analyses. These observations further reinforce the importance of multimodal learning as a crucial step toward achieving accurate and reliable diagnosis of lung-related disorders.

title: WheatNet: A Domain-Aware Attention-Driven Framework for Robust Wheat Disease Detection in Real-World Field Conditions
authors: Mandol Md Sharif, Jiangsheng Gui, Md Morshed Alam, Jubayer Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: Wheat diseases pose a persistent threat to global food security, as their visual symptoms vary widely across growth stages, environmental conditions, and plant organs, making reliable field-level detection particularly challenging. To address these limitations, this paper proposes WheatNet, a domain-aware deep learning framework for accurate and robust wheat disease detection under real-world agricultural conditions. The framework integrates leaf- and head-region imagery through an attention-enhanced DenseNet–FPN backbone to capture subtle lesion textures and spatial patterns. A disease-aware augmentation strategy models occlusions, illumination variability, and sensor noise, while an adaptive loss formulation mitigates severe class imbalance. Extensive experiments on enriched multi-source datasets show that WheatNet consistently outperforms classical CNNs and state-of-the-art detectors in mAP, F1-score, and robustness. Interpretability analyses further confirm that the model attends to biologically meaningful disease regions, supporting practical deployment in precision agriculture.

title: Secure Transmission in IRS-THz Systems via CNN-Transformer Based Joint Beamforming and Artificial Noise Optimization
authors: Mingyu Ma, Jingping Qiao, Jialei Wang, Zihan Yu, Chuanting Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th World Conference on Computing and Communication Technologies (WCCCT)
abstract: This paper focuses on physical layer security (PLS) in an intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-aided terahertz (THz) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system, where a multi-antenna eavesdropper may intercept the communication signals transmitted from the base station (BS) to the users. To counter this security threat, the BS transmits artificial noise (AN) to interfere with the eavesdropper. Accordingly, we formulate a joint optimization problem for the BS’s active beamforming, artificial noise beamforming, and IRS phase shift configuration to maximize the system secrecy rate. To address the challenges of high computational complexity and the difficulty in effectively solving the objective function with conventional optimization methods, this paper proposes a gated selective CNN-Transformer collaborative fusion network (GS-CFNet), enabling joint optimization of the BS’s active beamforming, artificial noise beamforming, and IRS phase shifts. Numerical simulation results demonstrate that, compared to traditional DL algorithms, the proposed method maintains comparable security performance while significantly improving computational efficiency, making it particularly suitable for real-time implementation in THz-band communication systems.

title: Machine Learning and Communication Devices for Data-Driven Mental Health Diagnosis and Care
authors: Shams Tabrez Siddiqui, Ziauddin Syed, Syed Ghyasuddin Hashmi, Jorair Ahmad, Abu Salim, Mohammad Alamgir Hossain
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: Mental health concerns impact all individuals, and yet the traditional methods of diagnosing mental health issues are so subjective that many disorders remain difficult to diagnose. The combination of machine learning and the latest communication devices is a potential solution because it means that the data-driven approach to the determination and treatment of the mental issues can be practiced. This paper explores the types of data used, the effectiveness of different types of machine learning models, and how these technologies can be used in the real world to improve the way we diagnose and treat mental health issues. The paper also explores the issues of bias, misdiagnosis, and the misuse of data that could be used with the help of communication devices and machine learning. The lesson is that using communication devices and machine learning, we are able to transform the manner in which we handle mental health problems.

title: Design and Implementation of an Intelligent Life Jacket System for Scenic Spots
authors: Xingchao You, Jiale Chen, Zhaoxian Huang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
abstract: Aiming at the passive protection and low rescue efficiency of traditional life jackets, an intelligent life jacket system for water scenic spots is designed. Centered on an STM32 microcontroller, the system integrates Beidou/GPS positioning, 5G communication and multi-sensor technology to realize functions such as wearing monitoring, drowning detection, automatic inflation and positioning alarm. The Kalman filter is used to optimize Beidou positioning data, and an adaptive weighted multi-sensor fusion algorithm is adopted to improve the accuracy of drowning detection. Field tests show that the system has a drowning alarm response time of ≤0.5s, a dynamic positioning error reduced to ±2.1m, and a drowning detection false alarm rate of only 2.3%, which can effectively improve the rescue efficiency and safety guarantee capability of water scenic spots.

title: FPGA Implementation of a Fractional Sample Rate Converter for Multi-GSPS Sub-THz Transmitters
authors: Shahriar Shahabuddin, K M Faisal, Mahmoud A. Albreem, Pejman Ghasemzadeh
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th Dallas Circuits and Systems Conference (DCAS)
abstract: Wideband sub-terahertz (THz) transmitters require very high sample rates at their digital front-ends, often reaching multi-gigasample-per-second (GSPS) levels. However, the maximum clock frequency of contemporary field programmable gate array (FPGA) fabric remains limited to a few hundred megahertz, creating a fundamental throughput gap between required sample rates and feasible hardware clocks. In this work, we present an FPGA implementation of a fractional sample rate converter (SRC) tailored for very high throughput transmitter applications. The proposed architecture is based on a generalized polyphase formulation that enables an arbitrary number of parallel output lanes independent of the interpolation factor, thereby removing the conventional constraint that ties the degree of parallelism to the conversion ratio. The method is evaluated for a 15/8 sample-rate conversion applied to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6 0 0 ~ M H z}$</tex> baseband signal using a 16-lane parallel configuration. A VLSI architecture of the proposed parallel fractional SRC is implemented on a Radio Frequency System-on-Chip (RFSoC) kit, and implementation results are presented.

title: FIDS: Scalable and Privacy-Preserving Intrusion Detection through Federated Learning
authors: Mohammad Waseem, Shafiqul Abidin, Adan Ul Abidin, Mohd Mustaqeem
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: The increased complexity of networked systems has led to the need to have intrusion detection techniques that do not affect the privacy of the data and still ensure accuracy and scalability. The conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are centralized, which is prone to privacy leakage, data imbalance, and high communication overhead. To solve these problems, the following paper is based on Federated Learning based Intrusion Detection System (FIDS) where the distributed nodes can be trained to generate a global model without sharing raw data. The suggested model has a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) architecture with class weighting and federated averaging (FedAvg) to manage data imbalance and enhance learning efficiency. The experiments involving the UNSW-NB15 and NSL-KDD datasets demonstrate that FIDS achieves more accuracy, recalls and F1-scores than centralized and classical machine learning models, which proves the scalability and robustness of this model. The findings indicate that FIDS is an effective detection tool that can be used to achieve high detection rates without exposing the data in distributed environments.

title: Instruction-Following Agents for Multimodal Human-AI Collaboration in Industrial Robotics
authors: Babasaheb Jadhav, Ashwini Mulekar, R. Sunder, Albin Kurian, Komuravelly Sudheer Kumar, Nazeer Shaik
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Recent Trends in Computer Science and Technology (ICRTCST)
abstract: It is clear that industrial robots increasingly require the theory of smooth human-AI cooperation to enhance productivity and safety. This study will create an adaptive system of instructions following, who can interpret multimodal messages such as speech, gestures, and visual information to attain accuracy and efficiency in the interaction. The suggested solution combines the transformer-based multimodal encoder, reinforcement learning of dynamically adjusted instruction, and a symbol-neural hybrid planner to run fault-tolerant tasks. The outcomes reveal significant improvements: after accuracy, instruction was at 90.0, the task success rate was increased to 91.3, multimodal fusion resistance was 88.2, and the error recovery was achieved at 84.7. There is a 80.9 percent decrease in cognitive load and improvement of response time by 29.7 percent compared to baseline models. Thus, the value that the paper brought to the field of collaborative robotics is the creation of a powerful and user-friendly system that can be used in the harsh industrial setting. The next step might be the implementation into practice and tackling the issue of ethicality and equity in the context of human-AI interaction.

title: AI-Augmented Cybersecurity Crisis Management: Leadership and Best Practices
authors: Adekunle Alabi, Elias Eze, Ameer Al-Nemrat
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Analytics for Business and Industry (ICDABI)
abstract: In an era marked by increasingly sophisticated and frequent cyber threats, effective cybersecurity crisis management has become a strategic imperative for organizations. Traditional incident response strategies, while foundational, often struggle to keep pace with the scale and speed of modern attacks. This review paper critically examines the emerging role of artificial intelligence (AI) in augmenting incident response within the broader context of cybersecurity crisis management, with a particular focus on leadership strategies and best practices. Drawing on a multidisciplinary body of literature from cybersecurity, AI, organizational leadership, and crisis management, this paper provides a novel synthesis that reframes incident response as a strategic leadership challenge in the age of AI. It explores how AI technologies such as machine learning, natural language processing, and intelligent automation are reshaping the way organizations detect, respond to, and recover from cyber incidents. It highlights the strategic decisions leaders must make to integrate AI tools effectively, manage human-AI collaboration, and ensure ethical and transparent deployment. The review also synthesizes real-world case studies to identify patterns in successful AI-enhanced responses, while outlining gaps in preparedness and leadership adaptation. Furthermore, the paper proposes a set of best practices that align technical readiness with organizational resilience, emphasizing crossfunctional coordination, continuous learning, and robust governance frameworks. Challenges related to AI maturity, data quality, regulatory compliance, and cultural resistance are also discussed. Finally, the paper outlines future research directions to support more agile, adaptive, and intelligent incident response ecosystems. By situating AI within the leadership-driven domain of crisis management, this work offers actionable insights for cybersecurity executives, policymakers, and researchers navigating the next generation of digital threats.
